El Metate|2406 Bryant St, San Francisco,.text: Got the green salsa chicken enchiladas and they were phenomenal! They have a salsa bar with about 5-6 different salsas and even a fruit salsa . There's something here for everyone. The woman at the counter was also very friendly and helpful.

The chipotle salsa alone gets them 5 stars. All those amazing colorful flavorful homemade salsa does, as do the chips which were freshly puffed in house and not out of a bag. Pozole was excellent and filling, and the veggie tacos were full with all kinds of goodies, though the refried beans could use some more spice and flavor. The salsas more than made up for it though.

There is so much to say about this place but here are some highlights:
- The decor of this place is beautiful. Down to earth yet thoughtful and colorful.
- The chips are so creative. The puffy ones are amazing with beautiful color. I usually discard most of chips but I need to hunt down for them this time.
- So much variety of salsa and sauce. It is my first time having a warm salsa as well. It is incredible! It is such a service mind to provide all accommodations for all tastebuds.
- The food itself is big and delicious. My quesadilla is packed with carnitas. I wish it can be a bit more crispy but it is also fulfilling. My friend got a seafood plate and it can feed 2 people. All dishes we got are under 20 dollars, which is a rare steal in SF.
In short, this is my new go-to spot for Mexican food in my beloved San Francisco!

The salsas and meats are excellent!  They taste fresh and falvorful.  I really liked the house red salsa and cucumber salsa, while my wife liked the tomatillo and chipotle salsas.  The interior is beautiful with nice music at a balanced volume.  The only aesthetic detractor is the AI-generated poster on the door.  Finally, the prices are high ($15.75 basic burrito and $18.75 tamales plate) plus 4% credit card fee, tip, and tax.  So it's up to you if the nice salsas and interior are worth 20-30% more than an average taqueria.

Staff is always friendly, the restaurant has a warm homey atmosphere and seating outside. The flakey chips they put at the table are amazing (and you get to pick any salsas/heat level they have out for the day!) I used to live closer but still make the journey for the chicharron burrito. It's my absolutely favorite, the texture/crunch is amazing, I hope I get to eat it for years to come (it's that serious for me).

La Taqueria|2889 Mission St, San Francis.text:
Messy but worth digging in.

Came on a Friday slightly past noon and there were already about ten groups ahead of us. Skipped the main line and went straight to the separate takeout window and our food was ready in 15 mins.

We got a couple super tacos and a burrito to share. Everything was grilled flawlessly, and our favorite was actually the most basic one (Carne Asada). Lengua and Carnitas didn't come through as flavorful as I'd hoped, and with toppings overflowing everywhere, it got hard to tell them apart.

The tacos were also so huge they layered two tortillas instead of one. We much preferred the crispy shell version over the plain double tortilla. Outer shell crisped up, inner one kept soft. Between the meat juices and the guac, things got soggy fast though.

The burrito was one of the best I've had in a while. No rice inside, and the beans and guac filling that space instead made the proteins feel way more satisfying. Gelatinized starch granules usually bind fat and trap volatile aromatics, so without the rice soaking them up, the meat's aroma came through louder. Also Glutamate (beans) + Inosinate (carne asada) supra-additive chemistry just works great all the time.

The burritos were really good. It definitely lived up to the hype. Make sure to make the burrito "super" it adds sour cream,cheese and avocado. And also get it crispy. They crisp the sides of the burrito on the grill making it that much better. The flour tortilla they use for the burrito was insanely good. A little thicker and softer than all the other flour tortillas I've had before, super good. 🙌
All the employees were super nice and the atmosphere was moderately noisy but something to be expected in a restaurant this popular and this historic.
The only down side is the neighborhood is a little sketchy. Be careful where you park and dont leave valuables in your car visible to the public. I hear break in are common, although thankfully nothing happened to our car.
Other than this, this was one of the best burritos I've had in a long time. Definitely a must try if your in the city.

I finally had the chance to taste what is arguably the most famous burrito in all of SF. I went close to noon on weekday, and the queue was about 15 minutes long. The line moves incredibly fast, and before I knew I was already placing my order. I got the burrito they are known for which is their carne asada super burrito. I got it toasted. You place your order at the front, and then you go inside and pay your bill, and then you wait for them to call your number. The inside of the restaurant is packed, and often you will be sharing your table with other Diners, making it a very communal form of dining. My burrito took about 10 minutes to arrive, and it was probably the best burrito I have ever eaten. It wasn’t as hefty as other burritos, given the lack of rice, which gives it volume, but the meat was so tender, the beans were so well seasoned, and the sour cream, and the guac was so creamy. This was such a satisfying burrito and also the perfect portion size for one person.

I will definitely be back, but I also want to try some other burritos in the neighbourhood.

Best burrito in the world nothing else compares and I’ve eaten a lot of burritos. I am not sure what it is but this place is just amazing. When my bf gets me from SFO I make him bring me this or if I’m visiting friends I always come here at least once. This place is always worth the wait and the amount of food they give for the price is a great deal too.

We came here today because we had often seen long lines outside and were curious to try it ourselves. For the burritos, I ordered the beef tongue, and the other dish (I can’t remember the exact name) was made with regular beef. Both were really delicious. The meat was flavorful, tender, and well-cooked.

The restaurant was clean, and even the restroom was very clean. The staff were friendly and polite, and the service was smooth.

Overall, we had a great experience, and in our opinion, this place definitely deserves five stars.

Taquería El Farolito|2779 Mission St, Sa.text:
🌯 A Reliable and Decent Burrito Option
This establishment serves up a very decent burrito that handles a midday hunger craving perfectly. In a city known for its legendary and highly competitive wrap scene, the version here holds its own with a solid assembly of ingredients, good structural integrity, and classic flavors. The meat is well-seasoned, the beans and rice are cooked thoroughly, and the salsa adds a nice kick without making everything soggy. It is a straightforward, satisfying meal that gives you exactly what you expect from a traditional Mission-style spot, with generous fillings that keep you full for hours.

🏙️ A Convenient and Good Lunch Spot When in the City
Finding a quick, satisfying meal in San Francisco can sometimes be a challenge, but this location serves as a fantastic solution.

Midday Convenience: It stands out as a highly practical and good lunch spot when in the city. The ordering process moves along quickly, which is essential if you are on a tight work schedule, traveling between meetings, or just taking a brief break from sightseeing.

Consistent Quality: The kitchen team is incredibly experienced at turning out orders efficiently, ensuring that your food arrives warm and freshly wrapped even during the peak afternoon rush when the line starts to grow.

Casual Environment: The setting is unpretentious and casual, providing a comfortable, low-key spot to sit down, refuel, and enjoy a quick break before heading back out into the bustling streets.

📈 Dispelling the Online Noise: Better Than Expected
It is always worth giving a historic spot a chance regardless of what the internet says, and this visit proved that point beautifully.

Exceeding Expectations: I would highly recommend giving this place a try because it's not as bad as the reviews suggest. Online platforms can sometimes skew negative or overly critical, but the actual dining reality here is perfectly enjoyable, clean, and reliable.

Great Value: Given the hefty portion sizes and the steep pricing found at many contemporary city eateries, the value proposition here remains very fair and approachable for an everyday midday meal.

🍱 Final Thoughts and Recap
To recap, this is a very decent 4-star location that deserves credit for keeping traditional, quick-service food alive. Between a decent burrito that satisfies your appetite and the fact that it is a good lunch spot when in the city, it ticks all the right boxes for a casual afternoon stop. Don't let the online critics dissuade you; it delivers a solid, tasty meal that I highly recommend picking up next time you find yourself navigating the neighborhood!

Good food here, never disappoints, remember they only accept cash. They do have a atm there as well. I ordered the Al pastor torta and it was very good. The area isn’t the best in terms of parking and there’s so much traffic/people in this area. If you have a chance to take it to go I’ll definitely recommend.

got the chicken super burrito and I wish it had more tomatoes inside to balance it out. it's certainly flavorful and VERY creamy (so much sour cream) but the only way I could cut through those flavors is with the two salsas they give you and i used a container and a half of salsa for only half of the burrito. still rlly good, would recommend 😸

Classic late night spot in the mission in SF. The portion sizes are out of this world and the prices are hard to beat. Cash only and no frills, you’ll have food for 2 days off one burrito. Dining in definitely guarantees a better taste but there’s not a ton of seating, maybe 5 tables. The line may look long but it’ll move fast.

The food here is incredible. The carne asada has the perfect amount of char and salt. The staff are super cool and helpful. Make sure to try the strawberry agua fresca, nice pieces of strawberry in it and it’s the perfect level of sweetness; It’s super refreshing!

Taqueria Cancun|2288 Mission St, San Fra.text: We were looking for the best taco spot in SF and this place kept popping up. it was just what everyone described. The food was amazing, and they served big portions. The meat was flavorful. If you dine in they give chips and salsa while you wait for your food. I got the carne asada platillo and my gf got the alambre platillo and it came with avocado which is really rare. we also got a taco to try it and it was really good. The service was fast and the people were really nice. The vibe is 10/10 couldn’t beat it. Would definitely recommend for an authentic Mexican restaurant experience

The food is hearty and affordable, but lacks flavor a bit for me. I was planning to try this place for a while as it’s a true SF staple but was disappointed by the quesadilla I picked. Just tons of cheese and bland chicken. Barely could finish a half of it. The wet burrito had more taste thanks to the sauce so that’s probably the safe choice if you can manage to eat that huge thing. The service was very friendly though and the ambiance of a lively Mexican spot was nice.

Was on my way home and just needed something to eat. I ordered the Chicken Enchiladas and a Lime Jarritos for here. The food was ready fairly quick. It came with tortilla chips, salsa, refried beans, and rice. The chicken was overcooked, dry, and burnt. I did enjoy the rest of the meal.
Always great authentic and fusion food. Very mixed clientele, which shows the food is really appreciated by widespread customer base.

First time coming here and it was great. Service is fast and to the point. Its a little on the spendier side for a burrito but it's 2025 so im not mad. The chips are thicc bois and the salsa is too. Can't wait to come back and try more meat options. Also the tortillas are like... crazy good.

Taqueria Los Altos|2830 Mission St, San.text:
Incredible food that is flavorful and warm. I ordered the quesabirria tacos and it was actually my first time trying them. I also ordered a horchata to go along with it. The tacos were so good I went home and learned how to make them.
My server was also incredibly friendly and nice. Highly recommend stopping by.
Came to eat across street but I think this is as good or better with no line and delicious stuff to enjoy.  First time trying quesabirria with soup and it was so good.
Cali burrito was gas, girl at front was helpful, back patio was cute as hell with the Christmas decoration.

Huge portions, extensive menu, great specials, delicious. I got the Taco Tuesday Special and a hibiscus drink for $11 (a steal these days), and my family got Birria burritos and tacos, chile rellenos, quesadilla, and rice and beans. We were all full and happy. Killer salsa bar too. The burritos are especially huge!

Only one month since its grand opening on October 25, 2025, Taqueria Los Altos is doing well with its food, service, and general cleanliness. We ordered an al pastor super burrito and a vegetarian taco--but I asked to have the taco topped with skirt steak (for a small extra charge). The pastor meat on the burrito was flavorful and crispy (as it should be) and mixed well with the other burrito ingredients. The vegetarian taco includes rice, beans, lettuce, cheese, salsa, crema, and guacamole. Adding the meat--which was tender and delicious--made it something like an open-faced carne asada burrito, but with two taco-sized corn tortillas instead of one large flour tortilla.
The taqueria's well-stocked salsa bar elevated everything. What's great about the super taco is that you can mix the ingredients together and add as much spice and flavor depth as you want from the different salsas.
The restaurant is spacious and open inside, and there's also an outdoor dining deck in the back.
Service was kind, quick, and efficient. We'll definitely be back--and bring friends. Note: The faces in the photos were blurred on purpose. It seems rude to post photos of strangers without permission.

Tacos El Patron|1500 S Van Ness Ave #100.text: Absolutely amazing and authentic Mexican food. The workers are extremely helpful, and are genuinely happy to be there. Before your food is served, they welcome you with a delicious chips and homemade salsa. I highly recommend their Birria Tacos with cheese. This is an absolute hidden gem found on 26th and South Van Ness.

I’m born and raised in Boston, and I thought we had some pretty decent Mexican food here, but boy was I wrong. I was visiting SF and a coworker recommended Tacos El Patron to me. I’m glad I made the journey here because I think it’s the best Mexican food I’ve ever had. I think it’s ruined my enjoyment of Mexican food at home, because now I know what I’m missing out on…I will forever compare everything I eat in MA, to this restaurant in SF.

Of course, in MA we don’t have the same choice and variety as you do in CA, but still! Everything was 10x more flavorful than what I’ve had at home in Massachusetts. The portion sizes are also great - usually I’m used to having to order 2 or 3 tacos or a couple tamales to be satisfied. But just one taco or tamale here is a pretty big portion (for me at least!). The horchata was also incredible! The birria tamale is so cool - it’s a must try for sure! I also had a birria taco and a taco al pastor with carne asada - also very good choices. The salsa that comes with the chips is also very flavorful, and actually a bit spicy. Nothing too hot for me, but I was surprised because in Massachusetts the free salsa is always zero spice hahaha. What a great place - I can’t wait to come back here again on my next trip to SF.

Tacos El Patron is fantastic. The birria tacos were incredible—rich, juicy, and packed with flavour. You can really taste how authentic everything is. Great portions, quick service, and a proper Mexican vibe. Definitely one of the best taco spots in San Francisco. 🌮

When I say these are the BEST birria tacos I have EVER had I am not exaggerating. The beef was cooked perfectly and was tender and juicy. Consomme had a lovely umami and rich flavor. The salsas were made in house, cold, and refreshing. My agua fresca was definitely a bit too sweet but that’s probably how it’s supposed to be. Will absolutely be back next time I’m in the Mission district.

Came here for the birria tacos, consomé, and ceviche and WOW. We were not ready for the flavor, in the best way possible. In a big city where spice and seasoning often get watered down to “appeal to everyone,” this place said nah🔥.

This is authentically Mexican, full of bold flavor, rich broth, and that homemade touch you don’t find everywhere. The birria was juicy, the consomé hit the soul, and the ceviche was fresh and perfectly balanced.

If you like real flavor, real spice, and food that doesn’t apologize for being good, this is your spot.
Muy auténtico. Mucho sabor. 100% recomendado. 🇲🇽

I’ll absolutely be back. 5/5 — vengan con hambre.

El Tepa Taqueria|2198 Folsom St, San Fra.text:
I've been coming here for years and have always gotten great food and service. The people running it are super nice and very helpful.

Here are some photos from when it was still Taqueria Tepatitlán in the 1970s & 1980s.

It once had a mural painted on the side depicting Parroquia de San Francisco de Asís in Tepatitlán, Mexico.

The parish is now about 250 years old. Construction began in 1742, took 33 years, and was completed in 1775.

Unfortunately, the mural and painted text on the outside of the building are no longer there.

📸: Max Kirkeberg

I think I've been to about 75% of the Mexican restaurants and Taquerias in the city, so keep that in mind when I say the El Tepa Taqueria put out the very best Tacos de Cerdo I've ever had.  The flavors of the pork and salsa verde melded together perfectly with the hand made tortillas!
5 stars and worthy of a two hour drive...no shit! ☆☆☆☆☆

This place easily beats out the famous Mission spots. You really can’t go wrong here, but my favorite is the super quesadilla with al pastor. It’s pretty much a riceless burrito with a generous serving of meat. The Al pastor is well seasoned, a little bit smokey, and very tender. The cherry on top is the open salsa bar with 3 excellent options (the orange is my favorite), and the prices are very reasonable.

El Tepa is a fantastic spot for authentic Mexican food. The flavors are fresh, bold, and satisfying, with generous portions that make every meal worth it. The tacos, burritos, and house specialties are packed with flavor, and the salsa adds the perfect kick. The staff is friendly, attentive, and welcoming, creating a comfortable dining experience. Whether you're stopping by for lunch, dinner, or takeout, El Tepa consistently delivers great food, excellent service, and outstanding value.

Had lunch here and it really stood out compared to other taquerias in SF because of how clean and well organized everything was, it made a great first impression. The ladies preparing the food were incredibly friendly and the cashier was also very kind and helped me choose the perfect sauces.

The burrito was absolutely delicious and the complimentary chips were a great bonus. Well deserved 5 stars for both the owner and the team!

Al Carajo|3224 1/2 22nd St, San Francisc.text:
Al Carajo Yucatán is a fantastic spot for breakfast, lunch, or dinner. While I've heard people criticize the restaurant for its small dining area, I think the food more than makes up for any lack of seating comfort.

As someone who has tried chilaquiles all over the Bay Area, I can confidently say that the chilaquiles here rank in my top three. The standout is the sauce—a delicious medium-spicy, tomato-forward sauce with just the right amount of tanginess. It's layered over crispy tortillas and topped with eggs, queso cotija, crema fresca, and pickled red onions, creating a flavorful and well-balanced dish.

The portion size was generous, and the presentation was impressive. In fact, my lunch companions commented on the appearance, aroma, and overall abundance of the dish when it arrived at the table.

I'll definitely be returning and bringing guests with me. Between the outstanding food and convenient location, Al Carajo Yucatán has earned a spot on my list of favorite Mexican restaurants in the area.

I'm not very familiar with Yucatan cuisine, but liked this place - the chicken tinga quesadilla was a personal favorite and I liked it more than their famous breakfast burrito, which was good and had a nice crunch, but was quite oily. Husband had their ceviche and it was sweeter than expected, but good. I also liked their hot sauce a lot. Dog friendly outdoor seating is a nice plus.

The Al Carajo Taqueria is about the best thing in this neighborhood, after looking around at all the barred up storefronts and windows the only thing I could think of was...this must be what it's like to be looking into a prison!
But...Let me yell you, I had about the best Tacos de Pollo con Mango Salsa I've ever had!  5 ☆☆☆☆☆'s for sure!

THE BEST BREAKFAST BURRITO. Al Carajo in the Mission makes the best breakfast burrito I’ve ever had. The hash browns are cooked perfectly, crisp and structured without any grease. It’s a meat-forward burrito, but not overwhelming the way a Deli Board sandwich can be. The chipotle sauce that comes with it is ideal for drizzling and pulls the whole thing together. The staff inside is always friendly, and when I order through DoorDash it consistently arrives very warm. Call outs for the chorizo, eggs, avocado and crispy potatoes are balanced, and I agree. This burrito earns its reputation.

Definitely a place for the quesabirria lovers, the pizza is enough for two and is a beauty. The burrito with poc chuc was also delicious, very unique citrus flavor and crispy texture. Service was friendly and good vibes all arouns.

Suavecito Birria & Tacos SF|882 Sutter S.text: Surprised how good the chicken tacos were here. Top tier. The lime hit so well with the marinated chicken. I gobbled them up quick. The chips and salsa were also top notch, along with the horchata and beef tacos. The shredded beef Birria tacos were also very good. Can't wait to come here again.

The food was top notch! Got the 24c burrito (the tots were soooo crunchy it made the burrito even better), taquitos (filling was moist and tasty), and birria tacos (delicious all aroundddd). Highly recommend eating here!
Absolutely killer birria tacos! We also got the guac, carnitas tacos, and carne asada tacos. All were so delicious. The service was super friendly and fast. 10/10 would come back.

What a great addition to the neighborhood and San Francisco. Finally a spot where you can grab high-quality, delicious food both during the day and late night. Already ordered from here twice in a week - highly recommend!

A cool new spot for fantastic Birria Quesatacos! The tacos were super delicious with plenty of Birria inside. The consume with chili oil and guac sauce are great add on for the tacos! Even the thing of coleslaw that came with it is nice; it's light and refreshing in between eating the tacos. There was no line when we went and the wait time was quick. Can't wait to go back!!

Cocina Mamá Cholita|3471 Mission St, San.text: What a great little place Cocina Mamá Cholita is!  And.. the parking is a dream come true, I parked within a half block, and I was pulling a horse trailer!  The food was amazingly authentic and crafted by a person that's been cooking family dinners all their life, it was perfect.  This little gem is highly recommended.  5 ☆☆☆☆☆'s!

Great experience from start to finish! The service was excellent. They offered us sample sauces and meats before ordering and helped us choose dishes to share between three people. We had the mole enchiladas, enchiladas divorciadas, and birria tacos. I’d definitely order all three again. Ice cream and churros for dessert. Great meal and great service. I’ll definitely be back!

The sauces here are elite!! Everything was so delicious! This place deserves 5 stars! The service was amazing and the plates are customizable. They gave us little sample platters to try since it was our first time in :)

My family and I had the yummiest food here! 3/5 of us are vegetarian, we were super happy with the veggie tacos and green enchiladas. My white elderly grandparents also gave a major thumbs up to the cheese & chicken quesadillas.
The staff were super welcoming and really lovely— there is a nice family vibe to the place. It is easy to understand all the 5 star reviews. A gem in the Mission district. Thank you for having us!

My wife and I came up to San Francisco from LA for Valentine’s weekend. She found this spot on Instagram and said we HAD to try it, and honestly, it did not disappoint. Food was 10/10. Service was 100/100. Soon as we walked in, the owner/server was offering samples to new customers, real welcoming vibes. I grabbed the carne asada wet burrito, my wife went with the quesa birria taco… both were 🔥🔥. No exaggeration and we’re from LA, this might be some of the best Mexican food we’ve had. Defiantly worth the train ride from Union Square to their location. Even the little 5-year-old girl sitting next to us yelled out “this food is good!” 😂 and she wasn’t lying. I’m not even the type to leave reviews, I don’t think I’ve ever written one for a restaurant before, but I had to speak up after seeing how a worker handled a difficult customer who ended up leaving a false review. There wasn’t some crazy long line, people were chilling, and the energy inside was good. Look, I get being frustrated, but putting false claims on a small business that’s really out here serving quality food ain’t right. If you’re in the Bay Area, pull up. Like my wife says… this place is “Foodie Approved.”

La Oaxaqueña|2128 Mission St, San Franci.text:
Super yummy and filling meal! As is typical for mission burritos, you get what could honestly be two meals for the price of one.

I got the California burrito with carne asada. The tortilla was excellent. The first few bites were okay but it got better midway through as I got to the cheese and the juicier meat. Unfortunately there was very little pico and avocado, but they didn’t skimp on the meat at all so I guess it balances out.

The chips and salsa were just okay. I thought I had to pay for them separately but they ended up bringing them out with my burrito anyway.

The Mexican hot chocolate was a winner. Huge size in a stylish cup. Just make sure you don’t burn your tongue so you can taste the rest of your meal! :)

Service was quick and easy. Pay at the counter. Credit card is fine. Music and atmosphere are great. Probably too loud if you want to have an intimate conversation but just right if you’re hoping to kick back.

I absolutely loved this place. I went there for lunch on a Saturday and when I got, I was able to find a a table, but just after 15 minutes, it got filled up completely, and there was a line to get a table.

The restaurant itself is pretty small and intimate, and it has a lot of very colourful decoration, which makes you feel like you in some carnival.

I want to try a bunch of things. Everything was fresh and they told me that it would take up to 20 minutes to make my food, which was such a refreshing change from places that pre-cook your food. I was more than happy to wait.

I got the tamales first. This was my first time having a actual tamale outside of the trader Joes frozen one, and it was so good. I was taken a bag by the beans and potatoes with the chicken filling, but it was so hot with the two salsas. Also, it was steaming hot.

I also got the quesa birra as that is one of my favourite things to get, and this one was honestly above average. The taco could be Chris up better, and the feeling could be a little more generous, but the taste was on point.

The last thing I got was the papusa and again, this was my first time trying one, so I am not sure if this was a good one, but it was very tasty. It was hot and hearty went well with the pickled vegetables.

The host was so nice and welcoming and made me feel really comfortable with the menu that I was largely unfamiliar with.

I will definitely be back and bring more people.

Went on a Sunday around 7pm. It was pretty busy and there was a guy playing the guitar so it was a pretty lively place.
I ordered a tlayudas with carne asada. It is a big crispy tortilla topped with refried beans, meat, cheese lettuce, tomatoes and avocados. They also provide you with red and green salsa, the red one is pretty spicy and the green is a mild sauce. It was really flavorful and the meat was juicy and tender. This dish can easily feed 2-4 people so it's great for groups.
I also got a pork tamale and it was well seasoned and had a good amount of filling and the masa was light and soft.
The chicken and cheese papusas were also really good. It had a crispy exterior and flavorful filling and it comes with salsa and pickled vegetables.
The food did take about 15 minutes to come out since it is made fresh but it is worth the wait. There is some seating but not too much since the place is kinda small. The staff were really friendly and welcoming. I would definitely recommend coming here.

Delicious and authentic Oaxacan food with gracious service and cheerfully colorful decor too near 17th & Mission. Recommend!

During my friend’s and my lunch visit in early September 2025, crispy roasted Oaxacan grasshoppers (chapulines) were in season, and La Oaxaqueña’s were crunchy and tasty just like I remember from my trips to Oaxaca in years past. We tried both shrimp and chicken tacos as well as the traditional Oaxacan “tlayudas” (extra-large crispy corn tortillas topped with refried beans, meat, melted Oaxacan string cheese, and salsa, big enough to serve two, for about $20). I enjoyed them all!

For dessert, we tasted sweet fried plantains with the traditional sour cream as well as Mexican alfajores (cookies).

The space was comfortable for meeting my friend, a new professional connection, for a networking lunch. And the colorful decor definitely brought back memories of Oaxaca and made for a lively and upbeat vibe for our first meeting as well.

One member of the couple sharing lunch at the table next to us ordered the chicken with black chocolate mole sauce traditionally served at Oaxacan weddings (mole negro fandango), and that looked authentic, too, though I did not taste it this visit. During our time in Oaxaca, this was the signature dish that was served in fine-dining restaurants. I definitely will take my wife to try La Oaxaqueña’s version of that dish for date night sometime.

And I think this would be a suitable and fun place to meet someone new, not just in a professional networking setting, but also for a first or second date.

The person at the counter when we were there was friendly, helpful, and welcoming, and they cheerfully accommodated my hanging out on the Wi-Fi with my laptop after lunch until the beginning of the dinner service.

I’ll be back for more at La Oaxaqueña!

First time here I’ve always passed by but never been inside, first thing as I walked in was the vibrant colors all over the walls and tables… such beautiful vibe! Next the cleanliness. All tables were clean and floor was spotless! Next was our cashier! Her spirit was jolly! Super happy and very helpful in explaining menu items including the tlayuda which we ended up purchasing! Also tried the panucho and huarache. Presentation and flavor was A1! shout out to our cashier Sarahi! We need more people like Sarahi in this world!

Tio Chilo's Grill|2872 24th St, San Fran.text: Solid Mexican breakfast spot! I had the machaca and coffee. Service was genuinely friendly and efficient, with plenty of coffee refills. The spicy salsa was perfect with the dish. Chips were forgettable, but appreciated. The midmorning vibe was great with an open front door letting in fresh air, chatty locals, and lots of sun. Looking forward to returning to try more.

I have been going to Tio Chilo’s for the last three years and I have to say they never disappoint! The food, drinks, ambiance and service is always top notch and on point!
This little family owned restaurant is a gem therefore if you’re in the Mission District area in San Francisco you must visit this spot! Make sure to ask for Liz, the owner so she can make you her famous margarita flights!

I had the Lamb Shank, served with refried beans and rice with plenty of tortillas. The Quesabirria was cheese and served with  broth that needed to be a bit hotter. Both were flavorful and served on time. The place is very clean and the bathroom is spotless. Great attentive service all around.

Best Mexican food I've had in a long time.
Family owned. Great customer service.  The portions are great for sharing.  I had quesabirria. omg. And my boy had nachos.

We ordered to go, burrito was fine however, our chicken enchiladas order had not only BONES in the chicken but a FEW end of the bone (large nerves).
Very dangerous to have this in a chicken order where you don’t expect this chicken breast enchilada order.
We will not return to eat or order at this place, even recommend this place since this was not a pleasant delicious dinner experience!

el Mil Amores|2780 21st St, San Francisc.text: We came here for brunch and sat outside, which was absolutely lovely. We ordered the tres leches pancakes and the naranja dulce de leche French toast — both were incredibly delicious. The servers were very kind and even spoke Spanish, which made the experience feel extra welcoming. The freshly squeezed orange juice was exactly what I needed. Portions were generous too — I even had leftovers to take home. Definitely a spot I’d come back to!

One of the best plates of food I've had in a while.

We tried the cdmx bowl and the chilaquiles. We also ordered the tres leches pancakes.

The savory dishes were 10/10. The pancakes were good but we felt they were short on the tres leches syrup

The only complaint I have is 3 tables were empty for a while but it took the staff a long time to seat us.

We went for breakfast at El Mil Amores and it was such a wonderful experience. The atmosphere was bright and welcoming, and the staff made us feel right at home.

My favorite part of the meal was the café de olla. It was sweet, spiced, and comforting, exactly how it should be. I also loved the CDMX Plate, their breakfast sampler that included house chilaquiles, my choice of meat, scrambled eggs, home potatoes, and the most delicious concha French toast. Every bite tasted fresh and full of flavor, and the portions were generous without feeling heavy.

It is the kind of place where you want to linger over your coffee and look forward to coming back to try more of the menu.

Came here on a Tuesday ~10:30am and waited around 15 mins. The food was so delicious. We ordered the Cdmx plate and tres leches pancakes. The cafe de olla reminded me of abuelita's coffee. The place is very clean and the staff is attentive and kind.
First time trying a Mexican brunch place; the food was incredibly flavorful. We got the tres leches pancakes and the vegetarian chilaquiles. I much appreciated that the pancakes weren’t too sweet!

Loló|974 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA.text:
Wow! The tropical taco was phenomenal! Also had the house N/A drink and it was surprisingly good. Service was very nice - bartender and waiters very kind.

Also really enjoyed the octopus and the chicken confit enchiladas.

The inside is stunning - decorations are very interesting and lively.

Great cocktail and food menu. The Taco Tropical and Chile Relleno worth go back, but the fried chicken taco I ordered was quite disappointing. The fried chicken breast were bone dry and tough to chew.

Extremely happy with all the dishes. Chile relleno, skirt steak, and pork carnitas entrees all are excellent. The cocktails were even more delicious - id recommend the easy cheesy, the rosita, and the pickle beer cocktail.

Their sauces and dressings are good. Fried avocado tacos are great with the habanero aioli. Young coconut appetizer was good if you like sour lemony sauce. Sweet Potato would have been better if it were baked or maybe bbqed - the dressing is good. Cauliflower tacos were good. Chiles Rellenos was okay - it felt too oily.

The fish tacos are incredible. If you’re a fish taco lover and have had them from all over like me, absolutely get these. I’m so picky and these were fantastic.  The chicken tinga quesadilla tasted good but was so disappointing small. And the beer and drink pricing is high. $9 for a can of beer and $6 for a Mexican coke. Cocktails were all $18. This place gets busy so try to reserve ahead of time!

Taqueria Los Mayas|331 Clement St, San F.text: Amazing food! The panochos were great, the flavor of the carne asada and cochinita pibil were to die for. The torta de carne asada was phenomenal as well, and the elote loco lived up to its name in the best way!

The 2nd and 3rd visits were as satisfying as the 1st. We tried Taqueria Los Mayas several months ago. We ordered different dishes but both had mole which was memorable. Months later, I remembered how delicious the mole was and ordered it in the burrito bowl.

The dish was aesthetically pleasing and colorful: against the dark mole were crumbles of queso surrounded by purple pickled onions, red rice, greens, black beans, and sprinkles of sesame seeds.

On the 3rd visit, I tried the breakfast burrito. The eggs were perfectly scrambled and the potatoes were comforting. My hubby ordered the tortilla soup - fitting for a cold day - and the fish tacos, which he enjoyed very much. The fish was fresh and sauteed with a slight crisp.

Almost every dish came with tortilla chips. There's a salsa bar with 3 salsas, one green (mild), one orange with mango (mild and our favorite), and one hot.

Will be back soon to have something with mole.

I got the Panucho (with carne asada) and when I say that I shed a tear at its deliciousness, it is not hyperbole. My friend got the empanadas (see photo below). He gives it a thumbs up (because he lost his other thumb in a seadoo accident). 5 stars would recommend.

One of my go-to spots! I'm still working my way through the menu, but my favorites so far are as follows:

- Chicken Mole Tacos
- Pozole
- Mango Salsa
- Cantarito Paloma

The staff is also super friendly!

This was our second time visiting Taquería Los Mayas, and we had a great experience. Lupita, who was working the cashier, greeted us with a big smile and made us feel very welcome from the moment we walked in. She was incredibly friendly and took the time to explain a few dishes we had never tried before, which really helped us know what to expect. She even let us sample the aguas frescas so we could choose our favorite—such a thoughtful touch.

We visited on a Tuesday, which happened to be Taco Tuesday, and we ordered four carne asada tacos, a burrito, and a panucho. Everything was absolutely delicious. We also ordered a michelada, and we highly recommend it—the mix was super tasty and well balanced.

We also appreciated how clean and well-maintained the restaurant was. Overall, we had a wonderful experience, thanks in large part to Lupita’s excellent customer service. We will definitely be coming back!

Taqueria Guadalajara|3146 24th St, San F.text:
This place was a little bit of let down. When I walked in here after other restaurant would not accept any card, I was the only diner here which should have told me what I needed to know, but I was famished.

Let’s start with the good things first. The portion sizes are pretty good. The burrito was huge and they give free chips with a salsa bar, which is a nice touch.

In terms of not a good things
1. The food tasted average. The Carne Asada was tough and not well seasoned.
2. The items in the salsa bar again, very average tasting.
3. The quesa birria was good, but they skimped on the meat.
4. The place looked dirty, and there were flies buzzing on the floor.

Our host who was also the cashier was extremely nice. I wanted some consommé for my taco, and apparently you only get it when you order two or more, but she was gracious enough to still bring me a bowl when I asked for it.
I always enjoy the carnitas tacos here. The two creamy greenish salsas are also remarkable and really make the meal. These somewhat expensive tacos are worth the $6 (per) price tag.

We came to 24th street to see if we can find a great Mex restaurant and we did.  This place is nothing fancy, but the food is fantastic.  The shrimp taco was one of the best I have had... Great combination of flavour and spice.  Service is also very good and the salsa bar is a big bonus.  come up and help yourself with any of the salsas they have to go along with the complimentary chips.  Great place.

They give you free chips and have a small salsa bar! Some people say the salsa can be hit or miss, but when we went, it was mostly good, although a few containers were empty.

We tried the super burrito with pastor meat, and it was amazing. The burrito tasted fantastic, and the pastor meat was cooked perfectly. We also got two quesabirrias, and they were just as good. The birria was yummy, and they even gave us extra soup in a cup, which was a nice surprise.

Ordered the Ranchero Burrito with carnitas. Way too many black beans, the meat was very greasy. The grease neutralized the other flavors. Couldn't taste the cilantro, tomatillo salsa, cheese. The tortilla chips were very mediocre, salsa verde was mid, salsa roja was really good. Cost was just over $16. Place could definitely be cleaner, floor looked dirty, table tops with food bits left there. The beans and carnitas grease dominated the flavor profile of the burrito. Overall a very mediocre experience. Would I eat here again? No

Donaji|3161 24th St, San Francisco, CA 9.text:
We were reminiscing about our trip to Oaxaca and immediately started craving the food. So, we headed to Donaji, a Michelin-recommended restaurant near our home, and it did not disappoint.

Ambiance: The moment you walk in, you feel like you’ve teleported back to the colorful streets of Oaxaca City. To match the great atmosphere, the service was great.

The Starters: We ordered the guacamole with chips and the Ensalada. Not only were they fresh, colorful, and incredibly flavorful, but we also had the option to add chapulines (grasshoppers) to both, which instantly brought us back to Oaxaca.

The Heroes: For our mains, we chose the Tlayuda Oaxaca and the classic Donaji Enchiladas. The enchiladas with chicken tinga were phenomenal; they're a crowd favorite for a reason. If you're looking for authentic Oaxacan (20-ingredient) mole, this is the dish for you. The real surprise of the night, however, was the Tlayuda. This has to be my all-time favorite Tlayuda. Donaji does steak well; add chorizo, Oaxaca cheese, and fresh California produce like tomatoes and avocados, all folded in a thin, crispy homemade tortilla… chef’s kiss!

The Pairing: We paired dinner with a Malbec from Querétaro (central Mexico). The wine's bright, fruit-forward tones were a perfect complement to the complex flavor profile of the traditional cuisine.

Verdict: An incredible neighborhood spot with phenomenal service and authentic flavors. We will be back to try other dishes.

Great spot for Mexican food in San Francisco. Authentic, flavorful dishes, friendly service, and a welcoming atmosphere. Highly recommend, I'll definitely be back! Don't forget to order Spicy Margarita cocktail with the real taste of Maxican Tequila.

Amazing vibes and phenomenal food & drink. We thought we had a reservation made, but when we arrived what we had and what the host had didn’t match—it was so wonderful of them to make room for us during a very busy night. We had a few dishes which were delicious and many drinks. Such a wonderful experience and excited to go back.

First off our server was amazing , very personable and made our night. Food was also wonderful! We got guac , veg mole enchiladas and veg tlyuda would recommend them all . Excited to come back and explore the mezcal list more

The best steak tacos I have ever eaten in SF.   The flavor profile of a high end restaurant in Mexico.  The chicken in Mole was also to die for.  And without the ridicules prices of some SF Mexican restaurants.

La Palma Mexicatessen|2884 24th St, San.text: Amazing pupusas, chicharon and horchata.  There are even tables outside so you can dine al fresco.

Ordered super burito, paid for extra meat,  got almost  no meat in the burito, but a lot of beans.. very small buritoin a first place, . plus left $3.50tip,
Very disappointed,  and the small bag of chips was dry old chips. Only the salsa was worth.

This is a well stocked and fairly priced spot! I just got a bean n cheese burrito w sour cream, came to $11.40. The tortillas are SO YUMMY! The hot sauce is got a nice kick to it but isn't overpowering, very tomatoe-y. The cheese was melty and everything came together so good. Plus the chips are thick and crunchy. Will be back!!!

⭐⭐⭐⭐ = Solid (1=Avoid, 3=Standard, 5=Can't Miss). There's no seating here, just pickup. But in my search for good Mexican food in the city, La Palma takes the top spot for fast, casual. The spicy refried beans here are a must if you are getting a burrito and the orange sauce is good! Prices are also fairly reasonable given the quantity, but I would stick to the burritos here (tacos and super tacos were difficult to eat). Street parking only for La Palma.

If you want masa to make your own tortillas you can order at the deli counter by the pound. It’s not sitting in the display cases. And then you pick it up at one counter and then you can pay at the third counter.

There is the “simple” kind or the tamale kind that has lard in it. You can make your own tamale with the simple kind and add oil or butter.

This is the authentic deal!

Nopalito|306 Broderick St, San Francisco.text:
We ordered the RED POZOLE, the Tamales, Guacamole and chips, and an Arnold Palmer.  The Pozole had a gorgeous depth of flavor, a savory, distinct, well made, balanced dish with perfect accompaniments to garnish it,  The guacamole was perfect with subtle lime notes and great texture.

We will order TO GO again soon!  Nopalito is one of the finest Mexican establishments in our fine city,  It never fails to execute delicious dishes that feast the eyes and the soul.  The men and women have passion for their culture and their food and it shows in every savory, delicious bite!

And added plus, they sell their cookbook at their take out window and looking through it makes your mouth water.

Well done to all!  So happy to have your fine restaurant in our amazing city showcasing best of class dishes.

Thank YOU! ✨✨✨✨✨

Great Mexican restaurant. Came with family.  Nice selection of food options. Very large portions. Good was excellent across the board. Food came very quickly.  Excellent drink options. Service was very friendly and accommodating.  Pricy, like everything else in this town. Highly recommend.

Im not very experienced in Mexican food, but this is one of the very best I've ever had. We started with a nice sweet nutty and creamy almond horchata. So refreshing. Then we ordered the fish tacos, quesadilla with chicharron and the birra main. The fish taco was sublime. Pun on the lime. It was so flavorful and delicious. The quesadilla was unusual. The soft taco layer was fried to a crisp shell with pulled pork and lovely chicharron overflowing. So juicy crunchy and umami. The birra was a pulled beef dish with rice. So good and filling. This is my favorite Mexican food place.
Great place to grab a Margarita and Tacos.  Quick service and attentive bartender.  Everything was delicious !!!

A really disappointing experience for me, despite the very nice and friendly service. I got the tortillas and enchiladas. The portions were huge, but in terms of flavors, they were severely lacking. The tortillas were easily replicable with store bought ingredients. The most shocking to me was the rice, which was really flavorless except for the starch, no matter how hard I tried to taste it. The mole was also disappointing, and I love mole. Given the price and ratings, I expected much more. I’m really sorry to say this and I mean no harm to the business, but that was my objective experience. I ate a third of my food, nicely packed the rest, and gave it away.

Primavera|1 Ferry Building Marketplace,.text:
I stopped by Primavera at the Embarcadero Ferry Building Farmers Market (weekend 4/25 menu) and tried the chilaquiles, beef flautas, and pork tamales.

It’s honestly not an exaggeration to say these were the best chilaquiles I’ve ever had and I grew up in LA. The salsa had so many layers of flavor, and the texture hit that perfect balance of crispy and slightly softened. Easily a 10/10.

The beef flautas were also excellent. The escabeche was well balanced, not too sour or spicy, and adding their homemade salsa really took it to the next level.

The pork tamale was good, though a bit on the dry side. To be fair, I ordered close to closing time, so that may have affected it.

Overall, this is a must try spot when visiting the Ferry Building Farmers Market.
If you get a chance to go to the farmers market, you have got to try Primavera! BEST chilaquiles I’ve had…EVER! The hot sauce/salsa is a must add to give it that umami boost.
Finally got to try these chilaquiles after so much hype. Did not disappoint. Pretty decent portion sizes and delicious. Long lines and cash only

Loved the Chilaquiles! Costs 15.50 a plate, but it's worth every dime. I was there early around 9am on a Saturday, so there was no queue. The queue started forming after I got my food (see photo), so you want to get there early if you don't want to spend time in the queue. The purple hibiscus drink is pretty intense for me. It tastes more like syrup than a drink. Fortunately I bought some bottles of plain water along and diluted it with a 1 to 1 ratio and it was so much better.

The best chilaquiles in SF, I love this place. It’s definitely worth the wait, just get there early since they usually run out of their fantastic salsa  after a couple of hours. I go here every Saturday and never get tired of it!! 🙏🏽

The chicken mole and pork tamales are yummy too

Il Casaro Pizzeria|348 Columbus Ave, San.text: Some of the best pizza in San Francisco! My dad works with Italians and they loved this place so I had to check it out. The dough is soft but not too thick. The pizza is cooked perfectly. Thryvhave a nice selection all day but if you want the classic marinara you can get that in the happy hour menu, which is 3 to 5. My girlfriend also liked their spritzer. For dessert we got the lemon sorbet and afagado which were both expertly made.

Everything was so good! Our waitress was super welcoming and helped us navigate the menu since it was our first time. Just a heads-up: the portions are huge. We were totally stuffed and still couldn’t finish everything—wish we had more room!
We were so hungry at first that we forgot to snap a photo of the grilled calamari, but it was amazing. It’s done in the pizza oven, so it has that perfect char without being greasy like the fried stuff.
I added the imported Burrata to my pizza, and it’s so perfect! Much better than the same thing I got from supermarket, so creamy and rich. The pizza crust was perfectly charred too, with a LOT of mushrooms and prosciutto. My partner originally said she wasn't in the mood for pizza, but she literally couldn't stop eating it lol.
The Pesto pasta she ordered was also fresh and light, which balanced out the richness of the pizza.

We ordered Tiramisu for dessert- it’s moist and light, not overly sweet (which is important) but packed with flavor.

Also, I have to mention their Calabrian chilies!! Those little peppers are salty, fiery, and have that perfect fermented tang. They remind me so much of the pickled peppers from Hunan China.

The pizza here was good — not amazing, but definitely enjoyable. To be fair, I’m from Berlin where there are a lot of excellent pizza places, so my standards might be a bit high. Compared to what I’m used to, this wasn’t better, but it was still a solid choice if you’re in the area and craving Italian food.

I had the Montepulciano wine, which was very good. I sat outside, and they had heaters, so it was comfortable even in cooler weather. The staff was very friendly as well.

Overall, a solid option for a casual Italian meal.

Girl math is Friday Night + North Beach = Pizza.  There's no better equation.

My friends and I had gone to see our friend and her band perform earlier in the evening.  Afterwards, we headed to Il Casaro for pizza.  After two sweet drinks, I was craving something spicy.  So I got the Diavola, which came with a spicy salami, Calabrian chili, and a spicy oil.  And it satisfied my craving!  It was spicy, but not so much where it masked all the flavors of the pizza.  The crust was nicely doughy with a good char from the oven.

I also got to try my friend's prosciutto pizza and that was also delicious.  It was perfectly salty and the bitterness from the arugula balanced it.

Service was great!  We got seated right away and our food came out quickly.  Since it was Friday night, the vibes inside were lively, but not so loud where you couldn't have a conversation at your table.

I'll need to come back to try the items under the Mozzarella Bar menu.

The food here is so freaking good. We ordered meatballs, burrata, pizza, cannoli, and panna cotta.

Everything was expertly prepared and extraordinary delicious. Our Every dish entered the clean plate club. We are really looking forward to coming back and trying more things from their menu.

Tony's Pizza Napoletana|1570 Stockton St.text:
Visited Tony’s Pizza Napoletana and had a mixed experience. The garlic bread was very good and definitely one of the highlights of the meal. We also ordered the La Regina pizza. While it wasn’t my favorite choice and I wouldn’t order it again, the pizza was huge and could easily feed 5-6 people.

The outdoor tables along the street created a fun atmosphere, and the servers were friendly and attentive. We waited about 20 minutes, which wasn’t too bad considering how busy the place was.

The biggest downside was parking. Finding a spot in the area was a nightmare, so plan accordingly. Overall, a good experience with great service and atmosphere, even though the pizza choice didn’t quite work for me.

Tony's is worth the wait. We visited on a packed Sunday afternoon and spent quite a while on the waitlist, but once we sat down and tasted the pizza, we understood why the place was so busy.

We ordered the Royal Margherita, the Honey Pie with vegan pepperoni, and a classic cheese pizza. Our five-year-old ate an entire pizza by himself (a first!), and our daughter surprised us by loving the more adventurous pies even more than the cheese.

The pizzas were exceptional, the atmosphere was lively, and unlike some heavily hyped restaurants, Tony's absolutely delivers. Coming from New York, where great pizza is everywhere, I'm usually skeptical of places that get this much hype—and Tony's more than lived up to it. We'll definitely be back.

Yeah, its that good. Have your pizza anu way you like it. Thin and crispy, deep and chewy etc. They even tell you how to pair the wine. Do yourself a favor and go on a Wednesday afternoon or you're going to be waiting a really long time. Say hi to Gino, he's new and did a great job on his first day!

I can see why they’ve won so many awards, lives up to the hype for sure. I gotta go back and try some other pizza styles, they had like 8+ styles to choose from. I will be back to try the New York style and see how it stacks up against one of my favorite New York spots like Lucalis. The chew and char on the crust were the perfect amount. Super light and fresh tasting, you don’t leave feeling sluggish or like you ate too much oil. Great staff too, super friendly and fun people.

We checked in 25 mins before the stated wait time. We made it back to the hostess, and she got us to sit directly at the front of the bar with a nice view of people in line for the bathroom. 😆

Service was lacking at the first 15 mins because that’s how long it took for the waiter to take our order, but it was busy so we just ordered it all at once. Appetizer & 2 pizzas. Yes they’re big so just order one for a party smaller than 4.

Mario the waiter was great, and he brought everything promptly and politely to the table. Fun environment, and great music. Just prepare to be in line for the bathroom during peak hours since there’s only 2 bathrooms.

Fun time during North Beach Festival!

Sforno Pizzeria Napoletana|514 Octavia S.text: Visited pretty late so wasn’t expecting sit down room, and the place is nicely tucked away a few steps from your main Hayes valley stops. Super tiny tables inside and outside, so if you have a party of 3+ you’re better off ordering to-go. Got the capricciosa and it was nicely sized for a personal pizza. Given I did take it home to eat, the temp didn’t bother me I still had the entirety in one sitting and it didn’t hit too heavy or leave me still hungry. Definitely would come again to try other specials

This is a charming, authentic Italian pizza restaurant. The pizza itself is fantastic: the crust is flavorful and delightfully chewy and the toppings are great. I had the pizza cheese omg and it is more than just a cheese pizza. You can taste each of the three cheeses and they are delicious. My sister had the pizza mortadella, a more complex dish, and it was amazing. We had a flourless chocolate hazelnut cake for dessert - worth the calories!

The decor is lovely: bright, modern and with lovely touches like a plate made of slate stone for the dessert, wallpaper with a pattern of olive trees, hanging lights, silverware that is
heavy and pleasant to hold. The owner chose an open kitchen style, so you can see the pizza oven.
GREAT pizza! Nice sauce, just the right amount of cheese, lovely crust, fresh basil… and at a price so low it seems like a mistake. We will absolutely be back!

Had to try a neopolitan style pizza while in SF, and came across this hidden gem! The inside is quaint with only a few tables, but the staff were all lovely.

I got a margherita pizza to go. It was soft, puffy and chewy. It was probably the freshest pizza I’ve ever had. I wish there was a tad more flavor to it, and more basil would’ve been great. It was very solid but missing that wow factor.

If I come back I would definitely get a specialty pie! Still a great spot to hit.

We grabbed the wallet Margherita pizzas to take away and eat on the green, and they were absolutely delicious.
The staff inside were incredibly friendly, chatty, and welcoming. The restaurant itself is really clean and nice on the inside, with a couple of tables set up outside if you prefer to sit outdoors. It’s also great to see they have an accessible doorway for wheelchair users.
They had quite a few orders on the go, so there was a short wait for the food, but it didn’t take long at all and was well worth it. Highly recommend!

Pizzeria Delfina - Mission|3621 18th St,.text: Really good food at reasonable prices. The food was great and so is the location, it’s right next to Mission Dolores Park so there is a trendy mostly mode 20s-30s crowd. Parking is hard so it probably best to walk. There was a large variety of foods but my one complaint is that it’s a "pizzeria" but they only had 2 options. Although both pizzas were very good with a great crust and undercarriage had a nice char I would have liked to see more options. Also good thing to know if you live in the bay, they have a location in Burlingame, it is mown to be cheaper as it is not in San Francisco and therefore cheaper. Finally, shout out to the serveur, he new every dish in detail and spent his time with every costumer insuring any question they had was answered. I saw him spend almost 15 minutes at a table to help them figure out what drink would go well with their dish and what flavors they might like, he definitely had the knowledge of some who want to work in the kitchen so give him a chance.

Delfina was absolutely divine! I had the Pappardelle & Duck Sugo (orange gremolata), and tried the Ricotta Cavatelli (lamb meatballs, dandelion greens) and literally did a little dance of joy when I tasted each. I could happily eat either dish for the rest of my life! We also shared Pane a Pala with Delfina ricotta and dried tomatoes; Warm Castelvetrano Olives. For dessert Affogato Corretto (fior di latte gelato, moka pot espresso,
Margerum amaro, almond cantucci) and Cocoa Nib Pavlova (caramel gelato, warm chocolate sauce, hazelnuts).

Every single dish that touched my lips was such an enjoyable experience. The staff was amazing, the atmosphere was amazing, and there was a line down the sidewalk waiting for the restaurant to open even though we all seemed to have reservations. This is absolutely a restaurant that needs to be on your bucket list if you're a foodie!

Thank you for the joyful experience, and I hope I get to come back in the future! If not, feel free to branch out to Bowling Green, KY!

Limited menu but very delicious thin crust pizzeria. Ingredients seemed / tasted high quality. Service was Ok but there were only two of them and it was quite busy.

It was my first time so I can't say I've tasted a decent portion of the menu. That being said, the meatball dish and all the pizzas we're good.

They have a gluten free option for the pie crusts.

Excellent pizza place with a large variety. I went with a large group and we ordered a number of them. The Panna and Margherita pizza were delicious. The pizzas with the eggs were interesting and delicious. The star of the show was the “cheese balls” appetizer though, and I feel like I can eat 20 of them. Good desserts too!

This place is small, so not ideal for large groups. This place is open all day unlike the place next door which is only open for dinner. The atmosphere is relaxed and casual, making it great to go out with friends or on a chill date.

Hands down the best Italian restaurant in town! The service is really good. They have each staff taking care per tables, foods ordered came out fast, wine was amazing. You should check it out when you’re in SF!

Pie Punks|145 2nd St, San Francisco, CA.text: Ok, ambiance 10/al gritty nyc rap vibes make be wanna work on my handles. Grandma pizzas are to die for the sausage and chopped cheese were amazing. The regular pies a little small but delicious the mauros molto meat was really good came out hot the volka martini was a little heavy on the olives

Everything is perfect at this spot. If you are a pizza lover, you can avoid it. The place mix a bar with great drinks and superb food. Broccolis as appetizers are delicious and the pizza dough cannot be better. Good beer proposals. Great time we had here. Thank you.

Highly highly recommend this pizza joint for your next dinner out. We found Pie Punks on google and were drawn to it because of its very good reviews. The decor and ambience of this place is unmatched, it’s got a great vibe as soon as you walk in. The staff and friendly and they have a great selection of drinks and food. We ordered a Detroit pizza and salad to share, both of which were delicious, fresh and presented well. Service was also very quick. Cannot recommend this restaurant more to someone who wants fast service and tasty food.

Came here with a group of 6 and was seated immediately!

Good:
- Pizza was delicious, I liked the sausage grandma the best
- the pizza tasted healthier than other pizzas I’ve had, not too oily
- Great portion sizes! We had so much pizza leftover and it was good value

Bad:
- The place was a bit dim
- Detroit pizza was smaller for the price

Overall this was so much fun and loved the pizza it was quite tasty
This place is fantastic! One of the best deep dishes / Detroit pizza I’ve had. The cheese is so good. The beet salad was good just felt like they were canned beets not fresh.

Long Bridge Pizza Company|2347 3rd St, S.text:
A fantastic pizza joint with an array of specialty pizzas, though none that truly hit my flavor profile. That said, the pepperoni, spicy sauce, and pineapple of the luau went surprisingly well together.

That said, they could really use a soda fountain or larger 20oz bottles as their price per can was quite high.

Honest opinion: the pizza was just okay. Hard crust, very oily and salty to taste. They charge damn near $1.50 for a really small container of ranch. For the price, I would have expected better quality. Lastly,  the staff were not friendly and inviting. Probably won’t return. 😢

$30 for lunch for 1.  Drunk Cluck chicken parm sandwich. Nice crispy crust and sauce was delicious. Bread was crusty on the outside and soft on the inside.

Service was friendly and prompt.  Sandwiches only available on Wednesday’s.
Good pizza I always get the loading dock and meatball. I like the sour dough crust. Steve always helps me and is very kind, will be back soon

The pizza here is an absolute must try. They do everything right using fresh ingredients and they pay attention to every detail. The owners are awesome and care about your experience. This pizza is worth the wait if there is one. I had pepperoni and pesto and it was one of the best pies in the city by far.

Cellarmaker House of Pizza - SF|3193 Mis.text: One of the best Detroit-style pizza on a thick focaccia airy dough baked with their incredible red sauce squares and or the Seasonal creations... usually a crispy cheese edge from the deep dish paired with Cellarmakers own brews is hard to beat in the entire bay area! Enjoy, d:oD°

If pizza is your thing and Detroit style is your ultimate you MUST eat here. The pizza dough is light, fluffy and melts in your mouth. While the cheese is almost fried onto the sides making it chewy and crispy. The toppings a generous and stacked. We got seated right away at the bar with no reservations. Pizza came out quick and hot. This place ranks way above Square Pie Guys for me. Oh and the hot sauce was also fire - tangy, complimentary and spicy.

What a gem. Kinda reminds me of a London pub — which is a massive compliment in my books. Very friendly service, all around great vibe. Good place for everything from watching the game to a date night out 🕺

And for the gluten free folks out there — this pizza is NOT TO BE MISSED. A divine thin crust 😍

I was visiting SF during Highlank Park Brewery’s Timbo week. Since Cellarmaker made their version of Timbo, I checked out the brewery for their Timbo and Detroit-style pizza. The place had chill, relaxing city vibes, and the Timbo was great and the pizza was PERFECT—soft inside and toasted outside. Yum!

While I enjoyed their food, I probably missed the warm human interaction that I typically feel at breweries at least in SoCal. Perhaps this is just a SF thing? (I noticed that most of the billboards in the city there was related to AI while the common billboard you’d see in LA is either entertainment or injury lawyers 😂) Don’t get me wrong—I loved the excellent description of each beer on their Toast menu. Great work!! However, as a person who’s new to the brewery, taking a long time to read through the menu and make an “important” decision on what to order could’ve been stress-free, interactive and fun. Overall, I found the interaction between customers and staff members who served food and drinks to the table was a bit transactional. That’s all, but I’d go back and order at the bar next time!

I love Cellarmaker beer but never tried their pizza. Went here on a Saturday night. Fairly crowded but found some seating. Normally I’m not a fan of Detroit style pizza but I thought this was great. I got the carribean jerk chicken pizza. For one hungry person I think 3 slices is plenty, if I was splitting it I would be fine with 2 slices plus a side.

Square Pie Guys|1077 Mission St, San Fra.text: Ordered delivery and it was pretty good. Crispy bottom, good size, and good taste. Definitely on the milder side and not too greasy, which I appreciated. It’s a typical Detroit / Sicilian style pizza and would be a good option if you’re in the area.

This hit the spot! They were close to closing but the employee still happily took my order and whipped up the Za. It’s nice you can get slices at lunch and not have to commit to a full pizza, but it was tasty so I didn’t leave any leftovers anyways :). I enjoyed one of my favorite hazy ipas there and the staff was pleasant toward a guest that stumbled in. The pizza itself was delicious, crispy crust all around, mold grease/not excessive which can be tough with pepperonis in the mix, the perfect cheese/crust/pepperoni ratio and size and price were good!
👍🏾 would go back
👍🏾 would recommend

Genuinely quite shocked at how pizza-y the gluten free pizza was. Got the veggie one with pepperoni (balance is key⚖️) and I’m happy to report they did not skimp out on the toppings.

Partner got the pepperoni and was not disappointed! Yummm

Honestly one of the best pizzas I’ve had in a long time. The crust was perfectly crispy on the outside and soft inside, and the flavors were just right. Everything tasted fresh and well made, and it was super filling without being too heavy. I don’t hype up pizza easily but this place really lived up to it. I’ll definitely be coming back next time I’m in San Francisco.
The best mushroom pizza I ever had! The cheese type(s), mushroom quality and their cooking technique seem unique. Laege portion size. The staff were quite nice and patiently answered my questions.

Joyride Pizza - Yerba Buena Gardens|730.text: I am not a huge pizza lover but every year I’m in town this place closes because the companies who are part of the conference buy it out for a whole week. This year I made it my mission. I actually got there right as they were going to close to the public for the week but I didn’t know it because they treated me so well. They took my order and let me sit inside and not once did I feel rushed to leave. When I realized it was just me and they weren’t taking more orders for the day I tried to hurry and they told me not to rush. True hospitality. I stuck with the classic pepperoni and was not disappointed; if all pizza tasted like their pizza I would definitely be a pizza lover. I look forward to making this place my first stop in years to come!
Just had the best pizza of my life! I love that we found a random restaurant in SF and it was amazing.

This place is so cute and in such a nice location that I would definitely come again! We had a party of 4 and were seated immediately at 3 pm on a Friday afternoon.

Food: We got the iron goddess of mercy and the mochi platter and the scone platter. The tea pots are a good value because you can keep filling up the pot with hot water and it lasts the whole meal! The mochi platter was the star of the meal and definitely a really nice unique dish! It had a good amount to share for 4 people and a chewy flakey consistency. The scones were also good to share and the strawberry jam and honey were my favorite!

Service: Definitely really good and the waiter kept coming around to refill our hot water!

Ambiance: A really nice place to chill and catch up with friends! We liked the outdoor seating facing the water.

Overall would come again!

Wow! Had no idea it was the old Samovar place until I walked up to Yerba Buena gardens. It's right over the waterfall and always offered stunning views of the park and plenty of outdoor seating places. The full glass windows also adds a nice transition to the outdoors.

The owners have done a great job transforming the space to a Detroit-style pizza joint. This is my first time trying Detroit-style pizza, and I loved the square slices. I ordered the Sweet Heat Pineapple, and it was so tasty. The hot honey caramelized the crust, and it was a delicious salty-sweet experience. I don't care what people say - pineapples deserve to be on pizzas!

The woman working the counter was very nice, and she accommodated the lunch hour crowd well. I will definitely be back when I'm in the area!

Staying nearby to the BY gardens here and if you like a late afternoon spring sunshine splurge with an outdoor garden setting along with pizza n a cold beer then this is the spot. Didn’t have food but a cold beer in the sunshine was bliss! It’s pretty shelters form the wind so just perfect 👍🏽

Golden Boy Pizza|542 Green St, San Franc.text:
Went here with my family and wow… what a scene. The line was LOOOONG. We waited about 1 hour and 15 minutes for our pizza. With hungry kids, that is basically a survival challenge.
But then we took the first bite… and suddenly nobody was complaining. The pizza was sooo soft, almost like a fluffy cloud, and it just melted in your mouth. The crust was thick but light, crispy on the outside and pillowy inside. Super simple, super good.
Only downside: very limited options if you are vegetarian and nothing for vegans. There was just choice between pesto veggie and cheese. Overall though, totally worth the wait. Next time I am 100% ordering ahead because now I know the pizza is worth it, but my feet cannot handle another hour-long line.

Tried to get pizza Saturday night when we arrived San Francisco but the line was so long! We can see why now since we experienced the delicious pizza today (Monday). A few people but obviously not as busy! I love the sliced pizza idea since it was just enough to tie us over for dinner. An amazing snack! We walked to the park and ate there. Highly recommend!

Visitors and locals alike should make sure to ground themselves and their evenings out in SF with a trip to Golden Boy Pizza on Green St.

Yeah there is sometimes a line but you need that line to soak up the prime North Beach evening environs: the multi colored neon lights everywhere, the bikers pulling up stereos blasting, and jazz bands honking on the corners -- all accompany the general din of people out having a great time.

And for your trouble, you get an outstanding  square slice -- light and crisp. Take out only, you can get slices or a whole pie, but take it from a local: don't sleep on the clam and garlic slice. Yes, it has cheese on it and it's delicious.

You can certainly make a meal out of Golden Boy, but I love it as a "first dinner" to take edge off while I wait for a table at Tony's around the corner, or as a last stop to soak up all of the fun I've emptied into myself.

Some of the best Pan style pizza in San Francisco, a wonderful selection of different variations and combos, very affordable, and fantastic taste, not greasy or overly salty large portions and the staff was kind and polite.

The pizza here is amazing. I tried the pepperoni slice and it was genuinely delicious with a very authentic flavor. Even late at night the queue was surprisingly long, which says a lot about how popular the place is.

It’s just a small takeaway counter, so don’t expect a premium atmosphere, more of a classic, local vibe. During peak hours you’ll need to wait in line, and once you reach the front you place your order, pay, and give your name. After that there’s a short wait until they call you, and then you get your pizza.

Overall, great taste and totally worth the wait if you’re craving a quality slice.

Outta Sight Pizza|422 Larkin St, San Fra.text: Not your plain and ordinary slice of pizza! Nice, crispy and thin slices of New York style pizza with a twist. The service and decor here was great which gave this small pizzeria good vibes. My only issue was the unexpected mustard drizzled on my fat boy bbq slice which was too overpowering and drowned out the flavors of my pizza. Overall great pizza with a rotation of choices with quirky names.

Definitely the best pizza I’ve had in SF so far, and rivals the NY ones. Love the Hot Burrata and Ruby flavors, and although on the pricier side, buying a whole pie is worth it. Amazing texture and love the grated parmesan.

Great pizza! A very solid option in the city for a New York style slice. Very thin crust which is more crackery, which is not my preference, but executed well. Good char on the crust, but a bit too much mozzarella in my opinion. The thicker layer of cheese doesn’t reheat fully and is slightly chewy. Sauced down mildly. All nitpicks, good slice, 8.7

Got a very NY style pizza vibe! Love all the pizza slices we took from here for a picnic in the park. We had the ruby, buffalo chicken and Alfredo chicken and plain cheese for kiddo. The crust was good and sauces toppings everything was good. It is perfect for a quick to go meal. Stayed well and tasty even after travelling for 20-30 mins.

The place is on a very busy street in the Tenderloin and has one of the best pizzas in town, usually, there is a line to get your slice of pizza in there and they offer the most additional types thank you of flavors. I tried the pepperoni and it was excellent for the price they sell their slices, the person running the shop gave me an extra slice without any ask on my side (maybe because my slice was small) - service is exceptional and I highly recommend it for anyone to try their pizza when you have the opportunity.

The Pizza Shop|3104 24th St, San Francis.text: So excited to see this place when I was on the 67, had to stop there on the return bus trip. Great slices and great people. It's everything I need in pizza.
Pizza was amazing. Didn't expect it to be this good. Customer service was great. The food smelled so good, I couldn't even wait until I got home; I parked and ate 3 slices.

Service is fast. Staff is friendly and considerate. Space is iconic and comfortable, spacious, clean and has some fun touches too. Pizza is affordable and very satisfying. Similar to a NYC slice, thin, crispy but with a tender mouthfeel- hot and fresh out the oven. Thanks for being a great, refreshing stop after walking dogs.

Really, really good pizza; probably one of my new favorites in SF. The dough is almost New York-y, just a bit too thin, and it’s got a wonderful SF sourdough-y twist which enhances the pizza so much.

The shop is a very simple few tables in the same room as the pizza oven. But when the slices are this good, who cares what the shop looks like

Hands down great pizza. In my opinion, the best pizza around 24th Street. I originally visited for 1 slice but ended up grabbing 2 different style slices. I first chose Pesto, Parmesan, and Tomato pizza. Wow. What an intense flavor from the garlic and perfect amount of Pesto. The second slice I chose was the "Meat" slice. I confess this is the perfect meat pizza. Definitely not over saturated with meat but with subtlety added. The crust is perfectly thin and crunchy. This is my new go to place when I return to 24th/Folsom Street.

Hi Hat|1432 Valencia St, San Francisco,.text:
* Happy Hour 4 PM - 6 PM Weekdays, $2 Off Draft beers*

Solid Slices  for less than $6 and  Diverse Beer  Selection ~ A refreshingly friendly and inviting vibe from bartenders in SF. I'm extremely confused as to how the canned/bottles pilsner can cost $13/Beer with draft beer for $10 max... That seems suspicious.. Along with other extremely high prices cans/bottles..
