Otherwise, a proper brewpub with great pizza. The Bartenders here are very good about refilling your water glass constantly, which is extremely nice and rare in this city. Well done

visited twice now, had the jersey, pepperoni, mushroom, and spicy vodka slice. i love them all. great beer selection. everything that comes out of the kitchen looks and smells amazing. great music. i’ll be returning frequently

Came in Sunday for dinner. The moment we walked in, a waiter (or food runner?) greeted us with a blunt “no slices, only whole pizza” - no hello, no apology, no questions about our party. Problem is, slices are listed right on their menu even if we wanted slices. When we asked if we could still order, he just told us to go to the bartender and walked off to chat with his coworkers.

At the bar, we asked about the slices again since we were confused. The bartender explained they typically sell out of slices by 5–6pm. So why is it still on the menu with no disclaimer if you can’t sell slices all night long? It felt a bit deceptive given there’s no additional note on the menu.

We ended up ordering a whole pizza to-go and waited 30 minutes. When the same rude waiter handed it over, I asked for chili flakes. He rolled his eyes, trudged three feet to grab a packet, then sarcastically asked “that’s all you want?” and immediately walked back to his corner - after we’d just spent $55 on a single pizza.

The pizza itself was good and the slices are generously sized. But no food is good enough to excuse this kind of service. I won’t be back, and I won’t be recommending this place. Honestly, you’re better off going somewhere else down the street, or even Domino’s, where they’re at least happy to see you and give you chili flakes

Amazing service. Our servers were kind, inviting, and happy - so cute!

We had the Vodka sauce slice and the mushroom pizza slice - Vodka sauce was our favorite for sure.
Super cool place, Aran was super friendly & attentive! Got the vodka sauce pizza and it was tasty! Lots of drink options along with really good nonalcoholic options! Great NY style pies.

Gioia Pizzeria|579 Hayes St, San Francis.text: Very good crispy pizza. The kind of pizza that doesn’t make you feel gross after. Location only has one table. Efficient little spot.

Great (but tiny) pizza spot! I tried & liked the funghi and Margherita pizza slices. The only thing I didn’t like was how some of the crust was blackened on certain pieces. I went with friends and maybe half of us had slices where the crust got burnt and we had to peel it off. Besides that, the pizza was good and I’d go back. The salads looked good as well but in my opinion, were too pricey for what they contained (not many toppings and no protein). Be prepared to stand outside and eat your slices or find somewhere else to sit down because if I recall correctly, this tiny space only had one indoor table with 2 chairs.
Glorious slices of perfection,great options with wonderful combinations that’ll satisfy your taste buds’s every need when it comes to pizza.
Will Gioia has been killing the pizza game for over a decade.  If you want a New York slice, this is the spot in SF.  It’s a hole in the wall takeaway joint that does slices, pies, and fresh salads.

This is undoubtedly one of the best NYC style slices you’ll find in SF. The crust is outstanding with good structure, zero flop, nice chewy texture, and great flavor. The sauce is well balanced, leaning more savory than sweet. There’s a small table inside plus window seating. Lots of interesting toppings but my go to is the pepperoni slice and the mushroom (funghi) slice. They offer some great salads as well and for those of you who enjoy ranch with your pizza, definitely get a side of their homemade ranch. Highly recommend Gioia if you’re in the mood for a quick, affordable bite in Hayes Valley.

Roma Antica|3242 Scott St, San Francisco.text:
We had an absolutely terrific lunch experience thanks to David. From the moment we sat down, the service was thoughtful, attentive, and genuinely welcoming.

We ordered a pasta dish to share, and David kindly portioned it for us without even being asked. He also brought out a little extra for the table, which perfectly captured the spirit of great hospitality—undersell and overdeliver. Small gestures like that make a big difference.

The food itself was outstanding. Fresh, beautifully prepared, and clearly made with care. The atmosphere was romantic and relaxed, making it a perfect setting for a long lunch.

Service like this is rare. David struck the perfect balance between professionalism and warmth, and he elevated the entire experience.

If I could give six stars out of five, I would. Highly recommended.

Our first time here and we'll definitely be back! We loved the amatriciana and carbonara! Delicious, excellent service, lovely ambiance (we sat outside).

Simone - our server from Rome - was amazing!! I had some dietary restrictions and he made sure that everything was good for me to eat. He made our dining experience A+ 💯!

Found this restaurant by accident when looking for a restaurant close to a concert we were attending. We have since returned having really enjoyed the experience. Nice wine list for such a small place. The food and service is excellent.

Absolutely phenomenal! Everything was on point. The service was excellent our waiter was charming, friendly and helpful. The food was wonderful.  I ordered pizza and others ordered lasagna. They ate it quickly so no pictures of the lasagna. We dined outside under the heaters. It was relaxing and pleasant. Definitely will be back.

A very pleasant atmosphere and a great place for dinner. We ordered truffle pasta with shrimp and pistachio, as well as a sausage pizza. Everything was delicious. Oh, and I also recommend the teramisu!

Ideale|1309 Grant Ave, San Francisco, CA.text: I went on a recommendation from a coworker as I was only visiting the city. I will be buying them a meal at some point for this recommendation alone. Let’s just say that this is some of the best Italian food I have ever had and the service is as superb. They had 2 large parties when I went so I sat at the bar but I was not an afterthought for the smaller staff working that evening. They worked tirelessly with those groups as well as checking on me to ensure I had everything I needed, answered my questions and even gave recommendations. Many of the staff were speaking Italian and that just gave it an extra bump for me with the authenticity of the ambience and the food. I am excited to go back to see a more intimate side of this place as well. Will definitely visit again. Thank you to the staff for making me feel welcome and providing a phenomenal, authentic Italian meal.

Great place, delicious tenderloin crostini, that was gods gift to the world. Good lasagna, that pork tenderloin mozzarella with. Ham dish was deep fried which was not communicated (they did say it was breaded). Pork tenderloin was over cooked. Get the pasta and the apps.

Honestly, quite mid for the price. Service was good. Food quality seems depends on what you get. Carbonara was actually decent. The amatricianas came out with the dryest guanciale I've ever had it felt like eating dry bacon bits and its buccatini was overcooked. Was quite disappointing tbh. We had the only table that night so kitchen couldn't have been busy.

One of the most authentic Roman Italian meals I’ve ever had, and one of the best meals I have ever had HANDS DOWN in general. The food was delicious, and the service was impeccable. Everyone was incredibly friendly and attentive. Highly recommend this place! 10/10 stars.

We found this place randomly when we were scrolling around little Italy. There were plenty of wine choice and the owner knows a lot about wine and wine pairing. The environment feels very cozy. The seed were big and spacious. Although it's full of guest, me and my friends still had a nice time talking and eating. The pastas were great. Very special noodle and I think it's house made. I would definitely recommend to get the bucatini and I will return to try something else

54 Mint Cucina Romana|16 Mint Plaza, San.text:
The best CUCINA ROMANA in San Francisco! 54 mint has a very cosy and inviting ambiance, with a relaxed casual vibe making it perfect for both catch up with friends over drinks as well as date night.

The team at 54 Mint couldn't be friendlier and make you feel right at home from the moment you arrive. The experience is rounded out by a menu that consistently impresses- top-notch comfort food with something for everyone, plus creative, well crafted cocktails and wine that pair perfectly with the food.

It's the kind of place that I've return to again and again. One of my favorite Italian restaurants for a reason.

A MUST VISIT FOR ANYONE WHO LOVES GREAT FOOD, COCKTAILS AND WINE!

Very tasty boutique italian restaurant! I got an off menu item the bartender recommended which was very good and filling. It was a fettuccine noodle dish with baby shrimp and zucchini tossed is a spicy sauce (just right for me and i have high-heat tolerance). The portion was perfect for an individual. I assume their other entrees are similar size. I also ordered a basket of bread (Its appetizer plate basically; cost extra; enough for 2-3 ppl). Lastly, I ordered a smokey old fashioned. Bartender did a great job with it.

Overall,  nice lowkey restaurant. I recommend!

Cute inside, great service staff. Bread from montesacro Pinseria was the highlight to us. Also liked the negroni and funghi side. Chicory & endive salad and cacio e pepe were fine. Didn't love the gnocchi or swordfish (dry/bland).

Sometimes the best experiences happen unexpectedly. My friends and I were walking around the city when we decided to give this place a try, and I'm so glad we did because everything was absolutely delicious. The lamb chops were fantastic, and the tiramisu literally melts in your mouth. The atmosphere was pleasant, and our waiter was very kind.

We visited this modern Roman-style Italian spot near downtown San Francisco for a team dinner during a recent work trip — and it turned out to be a fantastic choice. The vibe is vibrant, charming, and perfect for both casual and special occasions.

We kicked off the evening with cocktails — mine was a refreshing blend of peach purée, agave, and a few other touches that made it fruity, light, and not overly boozy. If you enjoy creative cocktails, you’ll definitely appreciate their bar menu. The complimentary bread and olive oil were excellent, setting the tone right from the start.

For appetizers, we shared the risotto croquettes — a classic Roman touch — and they were well-executed. My main was the carbonara, which happens to be my all-time favorite. It was rich, creamy, and generously portioned — so much so that I ended up taking some home. Everyone at the table seemed equally happy with their mains.

While I skipped dessert (too full!), my colleagues raved about the tiramisu, panna cotta, and espresso — all earning high marks. Service was on the slower side, but honestly, that added to the relaxed, lingering vibe of the evening — exactly what you’d want for a team dinner, a group gathering, or even a date night.

All in all, a wonderful dining experience that I’d happily recommend.

Penny Roma|3000 20th St, San Francisco,.text:
Ive been to this restaurants a few times. Love their chef tasting menu here . It has  around 7-9 courses. Everything from appetizers, salad, pasta, sashimi, fish are all so fresh and yummy. You can taste the freshness and natural sweetness from their ingredients. I would highly recommend doing the chef tasting menu instead of order on your own. There was no single bad item in their chef tasting menu, everything was really delicious!

The waiting staff were so nice and friendly. The vibe and decoration in side the restaurants were also very nice, and relaxing.

Got the prix fix menu and it was an absolute slam dunk; while I have nice things to say about everything, they Do Not play about their crudo here, wow! Glad I ignored other reviews saying their pasta was too salty/heavy because I thought both their ravioli and fettucine alfredo were superb. If I'm really plucking hairs, our server did not really know how to talk about wine, but the bottle we got was excellent, so! I loved the space, and the ambience felt refined but not stuffy. Would definitely make a return.

Had an amazing dinner at Penny Roma tonight. We started with the Arancini di Carciofi, which ended up being our favorite dish of the night, along with the focaccia — both were absolutely delicious. For pasta, we had the agnolotti dal plin and the rigatoni arrabbiata, and everything was fantastic. The staff was incredibly friendly and attentive, and we really appreciated that we never felt rushed throughout the meal. Can’t wait to come back!

Amazing spot for an Italian meal. We ordered 2 pasta dishes and 1 started for 2 of us, and it was more than enough food. I loved the beet and ricotta salad, the beets were so fresh and the sauce gave it the right amount of zing. I loved my tortellini dish, it might have been a special, but it was light and fresh, and the fresh corn brightened it up. The service was great, the right amount of attentiveness. And I loved the decor and the vibe. Highly recommend!

We loved it. Great food and service! We did the shared family style dinner party menu and loved trying all the different things! One person in our party was gluten free and vegetarian and they were more than able to accommodate completely for her.

Amarena|2162 Larkin St, San Francisco, C.text: I went to this restaurant with my cousins who are local to San Francisco. We really enjoyed the food and service. Cool vibes, good food and friendly staff. I was able to find a ample vegetarian options at this restaurant. I would highly recommend it. I would definitely visit again in future .

Came here for a Saturday dinner date with no reservation and still was able to get outdoor seating.

The bread starter was nothing special, but the green herb sauce that came with it was good enough to keep dipping into. Also got the Tortino di Melanzane. Eggplant was watery and the sauce just tasted like normal marinara. Not worth it in my opinion.

The Tagliolini Ai Funghi E Tartufo was the move. Saucy pasta with strong mushroom and truffle flavor. Really satisfying.

Outdoor seating with dim lighting, staff was genuinely helpful with the menu, but once you order, expect to wait. Two hours from starters to dessert is too long. Price felt a bit expensive for what you get.

I had a really nice meal here. The food was cooked to perfection. The service was friendly as well. I recommend this restaurant if you love Italian cuisine. My waiter was kind of hard to communicate with though. I give them 4 stars ⭐️
Wonderful, authentic Italian dining in San Francisco! Romantic atmosphere with warm lighting, amazing ambience, and absolutely delicious food and wine!

An evening at Amarena offers a quiet celebration of Italian cuisine in the heart of San Francisco. We dined indoors, though the inviting outdoor space appears well suited for larger gatherings on warmer evenings. The meal began with warm bread accompanied by a fragrant basil pesto, ideal for dipping. This was followed by a thoughtfully curated trio of shared starters - arugula salad, burrata, and bruschetta - each understated in presentation yet full of flavor. For the main course, the carpaccio , sea bass, house-made truffle mushroom tagliolini, and ossobuco stood out for their balance and depth, reflecting careful technique and restraint. A bottle of Sangiovese complemented the meal beautifully. Dessert provided a graceful finale- a classic affogato alongside gelato drizzled with cherries, offering a restrained sweetness that lingered just long enough to close the evening on a memorable note.

Marcella's Lasagneria|1099 Tennessee St,.text: Best lasagna in SF Bay Area. 6 layers of fresh pasta, well balanced flavor, and perfectly cooked. Service is warm and passionate. Such a hidden gem in the neighborhood.

Great casual place. Our son said it’s the “best lasagna he’s ever had.”  I definitely have to agree. We got the meat sampler-yes samplers! We also got vegetarian sampler. I loved all six types. Come sample for yourself

A stellar neighborhood lasangeria serving a good variety of styles at excellent an price for the portion!

Interior seating is severely limited, but there's plenty of outdoor seating and lasanga makes a great takeout dish if you live nearby.

They're also next door to deceptively massive board game store and, at least the night I went, partnered together to run food into the game shop if you wanted to dine while you played.

Drink selections are a bit limited, but there's a few wines, sodas, and a number of NA wine/beer.

Their biscotti is fantastic, though I wish they offered a coffee to go with it. That said, there are a number of coffee shops a short walk away.

Overall, a must try of you're in the Dogpatch area!

.... so sorry 🤷 again. I forgot again to take the photo of my Mushrooms Lasagna, before starting eating it. Yoy really should ordering it. It is very tasty.   But I remember share with you my Tiramisu before I finished it.
I want something light so I ordered Spaghetti alle Vongole, and the flavors are just perfectly balance! The owner was also very friendly to everyone :)

Bella Trattoria|3854 Geary Blvd, San Fra.text:
I had a 12:30 reservation on Saturday and, as always, had an amazing experience. I've been here several times before, but this time I brought my boyfriend's family. Our server was very friendly, attentive, and generous with their time helping us.

They brought us complimentary bread with tomato dip. The bread is super fresh and soft! My boyfriend and I love their raspberry panna cotta with balsamic glaze. This time, they had a new caramel lavender flavor, which was also good.

Our favorite dishes were the Pasta Norcina, Cavoletti, Carbonara Sbagliata, and Marrcune ca vodka. Every time I've come here, the food quality and service have been consistent. We'll definitely be back!

The food was amazing and fresh! The service was prompt and they went the extra mile when something didn't go as planned.  We received extra delightful foods courtesy of the chef which was a thoughtful surprise! We sat in the front room by the bar which was a bit loud and cool, but there is another room I would want to try sitting in next time.  There are options for gluten free and they ask about food allergies right away.  The martini expresso was perfect! I can't wait to go back and try more!  The experience took us back to Rome with the food!

Went later in the night since they close at 10pm. Nice ambiance with dim lighting. Service was pretty good.

Appetizers:  I thought the complimentary bread felt nice and soft, but the gf felt it was old and cold compared to hot & buttery bread served at other restaurants. Next, the Bruschetta ca' pummarola tasted the best out of all the orders.

Main: We went with the three course prix fixe meal:
1. Swordfish Carpaccio Antipasti tasted... interesting; like salty dried fish, maybe not our taste?
2. When the Squid Ink Fettuccine and Salmon Fettuccini came with "a lot of plate", the portion initially looked small, but there was actually a good amount of pasta there. But flavor for both was lacking and quite salty;  we could not finish it due to the sodium.
3. Panna cotta - it was okay.

Overall 3/5 for the food. I bookmarked this place awhile back and was glad to try it, but will likely not return.

We had the most wonderful service from the twin duo: John and Kevin today! They were attentive, gracious, courteous, and friendly. They recommended choices for us, Salatina gnocchi, carbonara, and the vodka pasta. The food was hot delicious and we definitely will come back.

Delicious! Be sure to make a reservation during peak dining hours because its popular and fills up. I ordered the Margherita pizza - cooked in a wood fired oven and topped with fresh Genovese basil leaves. Paired nicely with a glass of Barbera d'Alba. Also, in this review is the butternut squash fettuccini with roasted Brussel sprouts, Pecorino, shaved garlic and balsamic vinegar drizzle. Paired with a glass of Viognier, Chenin Blanc, Chardonnay blend. The restaurant is located in a residential neighborhood, so if you're a tourist your bus/rideshare will pass by this place on the way to or from the de Young museum. Oh, and don't forget housemade desserts - Yum :)
Great service and clean bathrooms too.

Pasta Supply Co|236 Clement St, San Fran.text:
Try it before it’s gone! The garlic crab noodle was delicious! Pasta Company definitely puts their own spin on the crab garlic noodles. The pasta was cooked perfectly, had the right amount of texture for that chew. The two butter sauces used in the dish work well with each other. The sauce has a slight kick to it but not overpowering. We wanted to lick the plate of the leftover sauce but we are not savages haha. It only comes with “half a crab” meat that was picked. I honestly don’t think it was half a crab meats portion worth but still a decent dish to feed two. We also had the garlic bread which was also good!

Going to buy my own crab since I bought the butter garlic and noodles to make at home!

2/22/2026 visit to the Clement St. location:
Food: absolutely amazing!!!! It’s soul-healing pasta. My friend & I shared 2 entrées - Hot Mess Spaghetti & Meatballs and the Reginette & Lamb Ragu, plus the sour cream & onion burrata. Oh, and the house salad?!?! A different kind of magic. In fact, the remaining salad was part of my leftovers & the next day, it still had crunch. It is important to note that their menu rotates through dishes.

Atmosphere: Cozy, warm-lighting, comforting.

Service: The staff is a very special group of people who are warm, inviting, helpful with menu suggestions, & friendly conversation. I’ve known & work with one of the owners for almost 10 years now and our main job recently underwent some major changes. A staff member took the time to come over to chat with me about how I felt about everything, which I thought was very sweet. I wear my heart on my sleeve, so that small gesture meant a lot.

Overall atmosphere and service was wonderful. However, the oven baked pasta dish was overly hard, my friend said it hurt her teeth. The sandwich was lacking a proper flavor profile and bland. Only decent item was the carbonara, but even then the meat inside of it was far too hard, tasted kind of old. Would not recommend and would definitely not come back. We politely ate as much as we thought we could and promptly left. Staff was nice, just not worth it.

P.S parking is impossible to find in the area, my boyfriend had to drive around for an additional 25 minutes when we came around noon time. :(

Sharing our experience here, so others are aware - they have serious sanitary concerns.

Came here for the first time today, found human hair in our pasta. The wait was also extremely long, it wasn’t that crowded, and we only ordered one item (rigatoni carbonara) and it took them 20-30 minutes to serve the food. After the food was served, we ate one bite and saw the hair in the food. We then notified the staff, their manager came, but she didn’t say anything other than “sorry about this”.

She then took away the dish immediately without ever checking if our preference was to get a refund or have the dish remade. She just made the decision for us (which is to remake the dish) without us even having the opportunity to give any input, she grabbed the dish and walked away very quickly.

They NEVER showed any caring for this issue, such as, offering a complementary meal, or to even serve us an appetizer or bread since the wait is forever. In addition to that, they also didn’t even cleanup our used plates, utensils, and the bites of food contaminated from the hair in our personal plates until we asked (cause we did family style to share the pasta and had personal plates).

Eventually we went over to the cashier to request the refund and cancelled the food to be remade. I’m glad they did not give us a hard time about the refund request.

All of that shows how poorly the staff are trained. Also I don’t know this kind of behavior would give customers any confidence this same issue won’t happen again in the future.

Decent food in a cute spot! I got the puttanesca salt cod plumpers and they were FANTASTIC. So delicious. I loved the capers and olives. The parm garlic bread was ok, not my favorite. It had more crunch and less cheese than I prefer. The fusilli in spicy tomato vodka was rather bland so consider adding a protein! The spaghetti and meatballs were fine, a bit spicy. The puttanesca plumpers really stole the show- I’d come back just for them!

The Italian Homemade Company|716 Columbu.text:
Wow

First thing I noticed: Eros Ramazzotti playing and I thought - either the staff is Italian or the owner dictates the playlist

I then saw a sign that literally says "Pasta Lab" - off to a great start.

Then my plate arrived: Fettuccine is yellow. First bite and... WOW. Pasta = fresh, soft, and
flavorful on its own (no hiding behind sauce).
Parmesan quality = serious business (the kind where you immediately know they're not cutting corners). Sauce = creamy, balanced, and not heavy in that "I regret this in 10 minutes" way. Garlic was definitely added but it was blended with other ingredients perfectly (you know it's there even if you ca. smell or taste it)

I ate the entire plate at unreasonable speed - partly hunger, being in a rush. The only regret? Not having enough time to enjoy every bite slowly.

This officially became one of the: "I need comfort food and don't want to be disappointed again" spots. Also I will not walk towards it next time, I will run.
5/5 stars and I would give them even a higher score.

Fantastic homemade Italian food located in the heart of Little Italy. The Veggie Lasagna and Veggie Ravioli w/ Pesto were fabulous. Staff were super friendly and welcoming and the interior has a nice cozy vibe perfect for family and friends. For our dessert we had the Panna Cotta w/ Caramel which was spectacular. Highly recommend if visiting Little Italy or you just happen to be in this part of SF! 10/10 Experience!!

The pasta was fresh and delicious. There were several I wanted to try but ultimately I went with the veggie ravioli with the pasticciata sauce. Very very good combo. My friend opted for the tortellini with butter and sage sauce. Also delish. Would def return!

table of 3 - easy access to seating and service. v lively yet chill at the same time. server was great, fast and clean, loved her accent.

food was excellent, my friend had the gnocchi with pasticciata, 2nd friend had veggie ravioli w butter and sage, and I had the fettuccine Alfredo w salsa parmegiano.

the fettuccine was immaculate, creamy, cheesy, rich, and buttery. it was probably one of the better fettuccine's I've ever had. the gnocchi as my friend describes it is very pillowy and rich, melts in mouth. the ravioli as my other friends describes is earthy, creamy, and delicate.

overall great experience, fantastic food.

A small unassuming restaurant with outdoor seating, limited seating inside.  Homemade pasta and sauces, yum!  It was cold outside, so we opted to eat inside.  Lunch was served quickly, the lasagna was a huge!  We enjoyed the food and the friendly banter of those working in the kitchen.  My daughter accidentally knocked her drink over, much to her embarrassment.  They were quick to take care of the spill and we're quite charming while taking care of it.  We would eat here again.

Cotogna|490 Pacific Ave, San Francisco,.text: My hands down favorite pasta place in SF. The menu does change but it simply never misses. The food is rich and filling but not too much. I felt pleasantly full afterwards. Kinda spendy, but worth it for sure. We have only ever sat at the bar because it is so busy, but it was still an excellent experience

Cotogna is absolutely exceptional at what they do. The menu rotates seasonally, and honestly, we are left speechless after every single visit.

One standout—though it seems to be a seasonal specialty—is the gazpacho. We still think about it constantly and hope it returns for the warmer months! Every dish we’ve tried showcases incredible technique and fresh ingredients.

Pictures are from various times we’ve visited.

Pro Tip: If you are visiting as a party of two, skip the standard table and request a seat at the counter near the pizza and salad station. It is a fantastic experience to watch the chefs at work and see the various dishes being prepared right in front of you!

We had a really exceptional dinner at Cotogna this past weekend and wanted to compliment the fantastic staff for a most wonderful time. Our niece was celebrating her impending nuptials, so we wanted to honor her and her fiance with a special family dinner. They offer a special Chef's Table for such celebrations, and it was perfect to streamline the evening for our group. Emily organized our event smoothly taking care of all the pre-arrangements with aplomb so that when the evening arrived, there was no fussing needed at all. Kyra hosted us so warmly with exuberance and the rest of the staff was so perfect and professional with the service. Most of all, each and every dish was outstanding. It was difficult to decide which one we enjoyed most. I will say the prosciutto was in a different league from all the other very good ones we have enjoyed before. The pastas were sublimely delicious and the steak was a peak above so many great steaks we have eaten. Last but not least, the gelato was revelatory. How can their dishes pop so much higher than the many wonderful times we have enjoyed these items before? Just amazing for sure. Thank you so very much to all of the Cotogna people. Can't wait to visit again!

Great experience all around. The dishes were carefully prepared and well presented. There was not a single dish we did not like. The Tortelli, Sunchoke and Rosemary pasta dish was absolutely exquisite. The staff were very attentive and knowledgeable about the dishes and wine. We felt very well taken care of.

Wow. Let me just start by saying Danny and Emile were amazing waiters. Extremely attentive to us and got the job done.

The food was out of this world. Just wow. I can not believe how good the food was. All of our dishes were amazing. The honey comb ice cream was amazing I loved it. I wish I could eat it everyday.

The bruschetta was out of this world. Wow. I could not believe it was that amazing.

Overall 10/10

Luisa's Restaurant Wine Bar Since 1959|1.text: My palét Osparonia the Fan Dancer over at the Paloma Hights Cabaret up in Pacific Hights told me about this wonderful Italian restaurant called Luisa's Restaurant Wine Bar on Guerrero St. and hell...I've never been there!  Well, I told her her I'll pick you up at 6 and we'll go there for dinner, and let me just say this...Everything about Luisa's, the ambiance, the service, the food, the patrons were amazing! ..hell even the parking was amazing!  ~no shit 5 ☆☆☆☆☆'s !

Hands down, this is my favorite Italian restaurant that I've been to in San Francisco so far. I thought I have had gnocchi before but omg when I tell you this gnocchi is so good, it's better than any that I've ever had. The pasta I had was delicious and fresh as well. The sauces were CRAZY delicious and truly I'm not one to obsess over Italian food usually but it was so so yummy and memorable. The service was so kind. She let us try a few wines before deciding on the one we were going to get checked on us regularly, but not too much and it was just really really good service. Luisa her self was there as well sitting in a booth and it was really cool to see her just hanging out while I was enjoying her and her family's delicious recipes. the ambience of the restaurant was so cute and quaint and I can't wait to come back.

Luisa is a nice neighborhood gem offering traditional Italian home-cooking owned by a lovely lady. We tried a few apps, pastas, and finished off with dessert. Everything was delicious and reasonably priced especially for San Francisco. Some fav dishes included the parpadelle which was house made, burrata, and tiramisu for dessert. We all left completely full.

I only tried the tiramisu there, but it was excellent. The vibe was so cozy, and the waitress was extremely welcoming – she made our stay wonderful! It was great to see Luisa stop by our table to check in on us. We'll definitely come back again!

One of the best Italian restaurants in the city and probably the best value Italian spot in the city.

Gnocchi was some of the best I’ve had in a minute. Lobster ravioli was also really good. Pizza honestly didn’t look the most amazing but man it tasted great. Tiramisu was a little underwhelming but still tasty, just a tad bitter and wish they didn’t use Hershey’s.

Would come back for sure.

Vega|419 Cortland Ave, San Francisco, CA.text: Authentic Italian food in the heart of Bernal heights. Went there for dinner - absolutely loved the halibut catch of the day and the ragu papadelle. Restaurant was not stingy with the sauce and it was really tasty. You know the food is good when the owner knows the regulars and greets them ! And a lot of the diners were regulars which says a lot about the food !

We had such an excellent Italian dinner here that despite being quite full we really had to finish it off with coffee and dessert. ☕

Everything was good but the clam+mussel appetizer was one of the best I've ever had.

Service was attentive and friendly and the kitchen was fast. I'll definitely come here again.

Welcoming and authentic Italian restaurant in Bernal Heights. Was moderately busy on a Tuesday evening but they had a table for us as walk-ins. Great food and service. The rigatoni with meat balls was delicious, the warm artichoke starter was tasty. Nice wine list, Valpolicella and house red both lovely. Tiramisu is not to be missed! Appreciate the soft lighting and sound proofing which creates a cozy, enjoyable atmosphere for conversations and connection.

Fantastic experience at Vega. I had the opportunity to meet the owner a while back and decided to stop by to prestige her restaurant and have a nice Italian dinner. Vega did not disappoint. It is certainly one of the best Italian places in the city, counting also with a pretty friendly and attentive staff. We had the sautéed calamari with beans, polenta with meatballs (absolutely divine), a four cheese pizza and also a seafood pasta dish with squid ink. They were all delicious. To me the highlight was the flavor and aroma from their tomato sauce used in their meatballs. Certainly homemade, fresh and very aromatic, it was delicious. The pizza had a nice crust I liked the pairing of the cheeses and the thinly sliced pears, the pasta was also delicious and had a very garlicky taste that I personally loved. As in our dessert, we really enjoyed their Creme Brûlée Cheesecake. To sum up, Vega is a gem and definitely worth your visit.

Really awesome Italian restaurant and was even able to be seated without any wait outdoors. The partially enclosed seating area combined with patio heaters were great to help stave off the SF cold air and light rain.

Started with warmed artichoke hearts as an appetizer to share with 2 people and it was delicious, albeit on the expensive side for a single artichoke heart cut in half with a salad.

Ordered the Wild boar stew special and it was fantastic. Great flavor but still fork tender.

For dessert the tiramisu and the white peach torte were both fantastic. Some of the best Italian dessert I’ve had anywhere in the U.S.

La Traviata|2854 Mission St, San Francis.text:
A stellar experience -- no exaggeration whatsoever.

I took my partner out to La Traviata for his birthday, and it was incredible. I had the Veal Traviata, and my partner had the Al Tosca. I was blown away, and it was so delicious. The lights were dim, atmosphere was quiet and intimate with Italian opera playing softly, and you can't beat the impressive walls of framed photos of celebrities from back in the day. The staff treated us like family, and sang "Happy Birthday," and even brought us a plate of creme puffs for desert -- on the house.

We will certainly be back, many times. I am so grateful to La Traviata for making our night so memorable.

We landed at SFO from New York City at noon and friends had made a reservation at La Traviata where we had an absolutely fabulous diner later that day. We started with a delicious red wine from Italy and even before we began ordering anything, our server brought out warm bread that clearly had just been baked and our palates began to savor what we realized would be a very memorable feast. While lovely operatic music serenaded us, we enjoyed delicious appetizers followed by wonderful entrees. I had the Salmone ala Wixel:  the salmon was prepared inside a parchment paper pouch and cooked with olives, tomatoes, garlic, and pesto. John, the owner and chef, brought the salmon, still enveloped in the parchment paper and placed a  plate with carrots and Brussel sprouts in front of me and  proceeded to open the pouch and gracefully place the salmon with all the seasonings on my plate. I have never tasted any salmon as delicious as this very unusual and tasty dish. I’m lacking in the eloquence to describe the  salmon but urge you to order it and savor the salmon yourself! My wife loved the seabass and my friends loved what they ordered. We shared a few desserts that were also wonderful. While we were up at 5am to make our flight from JFK, we spent hours enjoying our dinner and felt incredibly fortunate to be dining in this gem of a restaurant!

Best Italian food in SF!! They customize for Jain options too (no onion or garlic) and service is top notch.

The complimentary bread served is the BEST bread I have eaten so far - super soft  and very fresh.

Cozy, low lighting, very romantic. Its extremely intimate, be prepared to whisper. No dress code but expect to pay roughly $50 a person. The espresso was amazing. The desserts were great! Got the shrimp and scallops. Didn't disappoint. The gnocchi was just as good as the other reviews say! Overall a great hole in the wall.

My husband and I came in early on a Saturday for his birthday. We are visiting from our of town and he wanted Italian.  I'm so glad we found this restaurant, because it was fantastic!  We were welcomed warmly and the pasta was absolutely delicious!  It was so good, my husband wanted to go back before we left town.

Anchor Oyster Bar|579 Castro St, San Fra.text:
I spent the last month biting my tongue waiting to get a chance to visit this restaurant and try cioppino for the first time.

I arrived early and kind folks explained the process.

The staff was very welcoming and polite. The cioppino was so good, that first bite everything went quiet in my head. The tomato based broth...oh my lanta.... And to have all that seafood cooked in it! I just want to go back for more!

I strongly recommend taking some time out of your day and enjoying this special gem, and I cannot wait to go back.

I wish i could rate them 100 starts. The customer service is unmatched 🙌,  such attention to detail and every member of the staff is caring and kind which made us enjoy the experience.

The chef is star,the food was superb and feels fresh and there are so many options from appetizers to main courses. Ideal to share with your friends so you get to try different things. Loveeed the scallops and the caesar salad.

Please do yourself a favor and visit this beautiful restaurant,  you will definitely enjoy the experience.

We visited on a weekend evening and waited over an hour, as they do not take reservations and the space is small. It is a popular spot and still worth trying, though a less busy night may be more enjoyable. We ordered the Caesar salad and the large cioppino. The Caesar was a generous portion for two and overall good, though the dressing was lighter than expected. The croutons were excellent and easily the standout of the salad. The large cioppino was the highlight. The broth looked tomato based but leaned more toward spiced flavors than tomato. It was filled with crab, crab legs, shrimp, fish, clams, and mussels, all well prepared, and bibs were provided since it can get messy. Overall, Anchor Oyster Bar is worth a visit, especially if you can go on a quieter night to avoid the long wait.

We finally arrived at this cozy, welcoming, and authentic restaurant. There were a lot of people there, but we were the first. Everyone waited patiently, and it was worth it. The food was delicious, everything was fresh, and perfectly prepared. The oyster selection was excellent. The staff was incredibly polite and attentive. It was a great day.

Went at 2:15 on a Saturday afternoon. No wait when we showed up, but a line up formed around 2:30.
The food was unbelievable. Had a dozen oysters and the full size cioppino. The cioppino was outstanding, very large dish my wife and split. Absolutely worth the hype and any wait a person chooses to do.
The service was great, super friendly. We brought a bottle of champagne for corkage, and some places are miffed when you bring a bottle in, they were happy and very accommodating and brought an ice bucket to place it in.
Short review is definitely recommended.

Scoma's Restaurant|1965 Al Scoma Way, Sa.text:
I visited this restaurant over the weekend, and it exceeded my expectations in every respect. Making a reservation was definitely the right choice, as the restaurant was very busy.

The service was exceptional—professional, attentive, and genuinely welcoming throughout the entire evening. The food was equally impressive: every dish was of outstanding quality and fully justified the prices charged. A detail that particularly stood out was being offered the locally caught salmon, as the fishing boat had only returned to the harbor earlier that same day. It doesn’t get much fresher than that.

The location is simply stunning. Watching the sunset over the harbor while enjoying dinner created a truly memorable atmosphere that perfectly complemented the dining experience.

Having traveled the entire West Coast from Seattle to San Diego, I can honestly say that this was the finest restaurant of my trip. The combination of exceptional food, impeccable service, and a breathtaking setting makes this place stand out even among some very strong competition. Highly recommended.

This restaurant is PHENOMENAL. Came here for date night with my boyfriend and it was all around such an enjoyable experience. Make sure to get a reservation beforehand, as they can get pretty busy. The food here was amazing, it was all so flavorful and just delicious. My boyfriend got a steak dish and he said the cut of meat was really high quality and that it was cooked to perfection. I got the clam linguine and it was also amazing. Finally the service here is just amazing, all the waiters are extremely kind and respectful. I will be coming back once I am back in SF!

Pretty good experience overall. The complementary bread was so-so, but the whipped butter made it better. We started with some clam chowder. The portion wasn't that big, but the soup was creamy. We then had their oysters a la Scoma, with spinage and melted cheese. It's very different from a regular oysters platter, but it was pretty tasty, albeit a bit salty. For main courses we had their pasta diplomatica and the fish and chips. The pasta was quite good. The fish was perfect; the fries were alright. We finished the evening with a torta setteveli (chocolate cake), which was decent.

The ambience is nice, but the service is exceptional. We spent $157 for two people after tax before tip and we were absolutely stuffed, we definitely could and should have ordered less.

We stopped in San Francisco on our way home from an outing in Northern California and decided to take a walk down memory lane. It's probably been 20 years since we last visited Scomas.

Upon entrance it was the exact same as we had seen it many years ago. The service and greeting was impeccable, i love the formality of their outfits and demeanor. We enjoyed a lighter lunch, soup, couple appetizers and drinks. The food was good, definitely fresh I loved all the views, nostalgia and memorabilia inside the restaurant. Hopefully it doesn't take us years to come visit again.

It’s been a while since we ate out and everything was delicious! From the fish n chips to the cioppino, everything was full of flavor. Possibly the best fish n chips we’ve had. The lemon tart was a perfect ending to a lovely lunch.

Serving sizes are well-portioned and can be shared by most!

Sotto Mare|552 Green St, San Francisco,.text: We very much enjoyed our shared crab cioppino dish. I was definitely enough for 2 people and absolutely delicious.  Plenty of fresh bread and a bottle of wine made for an excellent meal. We sat at counter which was interesting as you had a view of everyone working very efficiently. Staff is very helpful and engaging.  My wife very much enjoyed the meal and atmosphere at this establishment.  Parking can be a challenge,  but definitely worth a visit. Everything on menu looked delicious.  Can be a Long wait line for a table. Inside and outdoors seating available.

Located in the heart of Little Italy, North Beach San Francisco.  This is my favorite place for classic cioppino, fresh oysters and marinated Dungeness crab!  They offer raw oysters either on the half shell or as shooters, west coast or east coast species with a spicy red sauce.  Be sure to ask for their fresh horseradish.   For starters, we also love their bay shrimp Louie salad, which is big enough to share with 2-3 people.  I also recommend their marinaded, cold whole Dungeness crab, which comes in an olive oil, lemon garlic sauce.  Be sure to order their marinaded”Best Damn Cioppino” , which is served in a large bowl:  Dungeness crab, mussels, clams, squid, and scallops.

A lively North Beach restaurant with tradition and character. We got a table for two on a busy sunny Sunday noon with outdoor seating, very close to neighboring tables, but that's part of the atmosphere.
The oyster plate was fresh and good; 12 oysters with minimal garnish (had to ask for extra lemon). The house cioppino was the standout: a generous, flavorful seafood stew that's clearly the dish to order here.
Service was friendly, attentive, and efficient despite a full house. Good value for money. Recommended!

Long waits on a Saturday night, but you get a buzzer so you can walk around.

The last time I was here was 2018, and the food is still excellent. I always order the cioppino and although still good, it seems like the recipe has changed a little bit. The oysters are superb as is the cobb salad.

Wines are well priced and service is great. Sitting outside is preferable to inside.

Such amazing Italian food!! Ordered a half dozen of east + west oysters. I personally prefer east cuz it’s sweater. Absolutely favorite is the seafood risotto, so much seafood and fresh. Same with their signature seafood soup(?) forgot the actual name of the dish. But it’s served with so much fresh seafood. Crab, shell, squid, mussel. Seasoning is absolutely spot on. Not too heavy or creamy. Such delicious food for amazing price! Def a 10/10!! Server was so nice. Just be aware they have added a 4% service fee. I didn’t take it out because I feel they deserve that and to better survive in sf as such an amazing Italian restaurant.

Betty Lou's Seafood & Grill|318 Columbus.text:
Mixed service snd food experience

The atmosphere was one of the highlights of the night. We sat at a window table facing Columbus Street, which turned out to be perfect for people watching. It was a lively evening.  We spent more time pointing out what we saw outside than having our own conversation.

We started with the Caesar salad. It was good, though smaller than I expected after being told the dishes were family style. The fried calamari was also good.  It came with a tarter and cocktail sauce.

The standout dish of the night was the cioppino. It came with a generous amount of seafood and the scallops were cooked perfectly, tender and not rubbery.

Food wise, everything was excellent.

Service, unfortunately, was a bit lackluster. Our waiter never refilled our water glasses, never checked if we wanted another glass of wine, and seemed impatient when we asked for more time to read the menu even though the restaurant was only about half full.

Absolutely love this place. My wife and I found this hidden gem last summer on a weekend trip to SF. So when I was in town one night for business, we made a drive intro eh city for a great dinner before our flight.

Lucky for us it was a rainy Tuesday night and we didn’t have to wait for a table.  The service here is so good! They had fresh bread (and I mean piping hot fresh sourdough straight out of the oven) on the table in a couple minutes.

I had the clam chowder and it was so friggin good! Amazing taste and chuck of clams spread out in almost every bite. Then I ordered the same thing I got last time, linguine with seafood 🍤 🍣! The pasta is done of the best homemade pasta I’ve ever eaten. They literally pack this full of clams, mussels, calamari and a giant scallop and shrimp. I love that they don’t scrimp on the seafood.

Definitely a hidden secret but my first choice for authentic Italian In little Italy 🇮🇹!

This visit was unfortunately very disappointing.

We ordered two lemonades, but both were undrinkable from the first sip. They had a strong tap-water taste, along with what seemed like poorly filtered or contaminated ice, so we were unable to finish them.

The pasta and ravioli were noticeably overcooked and kept breaking apart on the plate, which made them unpleasant to eat. The biggest disappointment, however, was the cioppino, which is advertised as the restaurant’s signature dish. The broth had the same unusual watery taste, several clams were not properly cleaned, and the seafood overall did not taste fresh—especially disappointing for a seafood restaurant in San Francisco.

For two people, ordering two pasta dishes and drinks (without alcohol), the bill came to over $140. Given the quality of the food and drinks, this felt significantly overpriced.

We chose this restaurant because it is often recommended as a well-known spot in San Francisco and expected much better. However, despite it being a Saturday lunchtime, the restaurant was already quite empty. We also noticed that all nearby tables were filled with tourists and no locals, which gave us the impression that the restaurant’s quality may have declined over time—possibly due to changes in ownership or kitchen staff.

Overall, this experience did not meet expectations, and unfortunately, it is not a place we would choose to return to.

On a seafood recommendation by a resident my wife and I had a wonderful experience here. We were visiting The City for her birthday, having lived here many years ago. So many choices for good seafood, but we will surely go here again when we return.  My wife had the seafood linguine in tomato cream and I the cioppino. Mine had 1/2 a crab and was so large I thought it was for two. I ate it all! Thankfully, they had bibs. This cioppino is finger food and will decorate the tablecloth, napkins and you! So good! We both finished our plates, which is rare these days. The staff were attentive, suggestions spot on and the owner was very nice, sharing stories of his life and career. Thank you Hector and all the staff. Looking forward to our next meal!

I like the eclectic decor. Sometimes breezy if someone leaves one of the doors open. Cioppino is definitely the way to go. I liked the large shrimp and scallops especially. Risotto was okay but a little under for the rice. Seafood was fresh. Bread was good. I could see why they charge for additional

Fog Harbor Fish House|39 Pier, San Franc.text: Red Lobster 2.0 but honestly it was not that bad. Made a reservation, came a little early but that didn't stop them from seating me exactly on reserved time. I ordered the calamari as the app, it was good some was chewy as others were soft. I wish it had a little salsa as a dipping option instead of Ranch.  I ordered the mix seafood grill, the food quality looked and tasted clean but i wish it had more seasoning to it. The flavor was a little bland but it did give me a red lobster vibe. Service was good but he did take my appetizer without asking if i was finished. I didn't fight it but it is what it is. I ate most but had leftovers that i could of used as to-go. The dessert I had the fudge cake, and that tasted really bitter. I ordered a side of vanilla ice cream and that didnt save the cake from disaster. Overall i would give this place a 6/10.

This is a fantastic family-friendly destination with sweeping waterfront views.
We just had our second visit to Fog Harbor Fish House at Pier 39, and it did not disappoint. The sweeping views while you eat make the experience so special.
If you are a fan of rich and flavorful soups, the Clam chowder is a must-try. We paired our meal with the Cali Squeeze Blood Orange beer, which is incredibly crisp and refreshing. And we can never get enough of their warm, fresh bread and butter—yum! Plus great share for our family is the calamari.
For dinner, the seafood pasta was a great mixture for different tastes, and my husband’s absolute favorite is their Surf and Turf. Highly recommend checking them out on your next trip to the wharf!

I must admit, when visiting Pier 39, I thought all the restaurants would be “commercial” and “touristy”, Fog Harbor was an outstanding exception to my own rule!! What an incredible restaurant and dining experience! I cannot recommend this place enough! To start, we were greeted by the most beautiful trio of hosts! So delightful! We had a lovely table by the window with a perfect view of the seals at sunset! Our servers, Jeremy and Luis were kind and attentive to our needs! The Food… OMG!! Where to begin! Every single bite was phenomenal! The crab cakes are the best I’ve ever had! I ordered the crab and scallop risotto, it was creamy and delicious, the sweet crabmeat and delicate scallops were simply divine! My husband enjoyed his fish and chips, the fish was crispy and perfectly cooked! If you are visiting the Bay Area and want an overall incredible dining experience, you must try Fog Harbor!

Fog Harbor Fish House was absolutely amazing! The restaurant itself is beautiful and the view or the harbor and ocean with the sunset is incredible it makes the whole dining experience feel really special.

The food was fantastic. The oysters were perfect! super fresh and full of flavor. Everything we had was delicious and you can really tell they care about quality.

Our waiter, Andres, made the experience even better. He was excellent, professional, friendly, quick, and totally on point the whole time. He made sure everything was perfect and we felt really well taken care of even offered to take our pictures!

If you’re looking for great seafood with an incredible view and top-notch service, I highly recommend Fog Harbor Fish House. I’ll definitely be coming Back next time im in SF!

This was a great place to eat while on our vacation. The location was awesome, right on Pier 39! We got a seat with a perfect view of the ocean. The seafood was fantastic, I got the lobster roll and it was amazing. My wife got the grilled chicken and it was just as good. Unfortunately the only issue was the wait time. The wait was nearly an hour even though they told us it would only be 30 minutes. That was forgiven as soon as we got the amazing meal. I would definitely come back to this beautiful place for the tasty fresh fish!

Piccolo Forno|725 Columbus Ave, San Fran.text:
From the moment I walked into Piccolo Forno, I was greeted so warmly by the Italian servers that I immediately felt like family, even dining solo. The hospitality alone set the tone for what became one of the nicest dining experiences I’ve had.

I started with a glass of crisp Pinot Grigio and the complimentary bread basket served with absolutely incredible olive oil and balsamic, simple, authentic, and impossible to stop eating. The ambiance was vibrant and lively, like sitting in a charming Italian countryside trattoria filled with energy, conversation, and warmth.

The Caesar salad with anchovies was perfectly balanced and fresh, and the special fussili with seafood was outstanding—rich, flavorful, and cooked to perfection. Every bite tasted authentic, comforting, and deeply satisfying. Their chili oil was an absolute delight with the pasta. I almost didn’t ask for it because I didn’t want to offend anyone, but the waiter jokingly said, “Maybe you like it spicy,” and I’m so glad he did. That chili oil elevated the dish to another level. I know I will be dreaming about it.

To finish, the tiramisu was heavenly: light, creamy, and the perfect ending to an exceptional meal.

Piccolo Forno didn’t just serve amazing food — it gave me a genuine piece of Italy. From the atmosphere to the service to every course, this place is truly special.

Unexpected gem! We were looking for a non-chain place to dine on a very brief visit to San Francisco, so glad we found Piccolo Forno on our Google map. The owner graciously held a table for the 4 of us while we walked there from Fisherman’s Warf after we called to find out about space. The seafood over black pasta special was absolutely delicious, as were the spaghetti and chicken parm dishes. We arrived as strangers but were treated like family. A small but special spot for great food and even better service!!!

A cosy restaurant serving genuinely delicious Italian fare. The meal began with complimentary bread and olive oil. The burrata arrived like a cloud - creamy, soft, and beautifully balanced with briny olives and sweet cherry tomatoes. The lobster stuffing of ravioli di aragosta was perfectly minced, and luxurious. The spaghetti alla carbonara was silky, and lifted by generous chunks of guanciale. We ended with an affogato that delivered the perfect finale - bold, sweet and beautifully comforting. The pastas did take a while to reach our table, but knowing everything was freshly prepared made the wait feel entirely worth it. The staff were warm, cheerful, and genuinely welcoming throughout our meal. This is one spot I’ll happily return to, no hesitation.

It was another ordinary day for me and my husband when we were craving for Italian food. We like the chicken parmigiano. Unfortunately they didn't have the lasagna that day so I had the lobster ravioli, it's a bit salty and little filling inside. The service is good.

This place is everything I like about a small neighborhood Italian spot - simple, casual, and completely unpretentious.

Food - We ordered the lobster ravioli and a pasta with truffle sauce, and both were absolutely delicious. I’m honestly drooling just thinking about them again. The flavors were rich and comforting without feeling overly heavy.

Service - The staff were also very friendly, which added to the relaxed vibe of the place. The restaurant itself is very small, so it’s a good idea to make reservations beforehand.

Nothing fancy here, just really good food and a laid-back atmosphere. If you’re in the area and craving good Italian food, this spot is definitely worth a visit.

Stella Pastry|446 Columbus Ave, San Fran.text: This was a lovely place for Italian desserts during the Italian Festival in North Beach (June 2026). It’s a quaint place along Columbus, bordering Chinatown in San Francisco. We were able to get some Tiramisu and Sacripantina. Both were excellent. It’s kind of a tiny place, along Columbus. Service was good. Definitely will try to come back soon.

The service was excellent. It was raining and the shop was completely full. There was an empty table but no chair available because the chairs had been taken by other customers. I was standing and eating my pastry at the table when someone from the kitchen noticed and brought me a plastic chair from the back so I could sit down. It was a small but very thoughtful gesture, and I really appreciated it.

Stella Pastry is a classic Italian bakery in North Beach and it’s easy to see why it’s so well regarded. The pastries are consistently well made and balanced, without being overly sweet.

The cannoli stands out for its crisp shell and smooth ricotta filling, with just the right amount of sweetness and texture. The tiramisu is also very solid—good coffee flavor, clean layers, and not overly heavy. Sacripantina is less common but represents a more traditional Italian-style cake with sponge, cream, and subtle alcohol notes.

Overall, the desserts feel carefully made, with attention to proportion and flavor rather than excess. This is the kind of place people return to for reliable, classic Italian pastries rather than trendy or overdone sweets

Tbh, this is a highly overrated spot. I’m wondering how people say it’s divine!? We had canoli, sacripantina and a few cookies, all average and good. I felt that a normal bakery in India would have more delicious food than this place. May be this is a taste loved by other country people? May be.

Stopped by this little store to try one thing, a square of their sacripantina cake. It was so delicious. Nice and chilled, a good strong rum flavor in the cake.
Many of their other cake and desserts looked delicious as well, especially the tiramisu square and strawberry shortcake.

A North Beach classic worth every bite. We walked in unsure of what to order and the attendant was genuinely helpful and friendly, patiently explained the pastries and made us feel welcome. We went with the Sacripantina cake and it did not disappoint: moist, not overly sweet, and full of flavor.

Simple, honest, and delicious. Recommended.

Dianda's Italian American Pastry|2883 Mi.text:
This is a cute little bakery and very busy most of the time. The service was great, but it felt so rushed that I didn’t get to see all the options because there was a long line behind me. Good business, I suppose.

I ordered a few slices of their cake options: strawberry shortcake, mango tres leches (not the traditional version), tiramisu, and carrot cake. My favorite was the carrot cake—it had the perfect balance of moisture and sweetness, and you could tell they used fresh ingredients. I hope my next visit isn’t as busy so I can explore all the options and try more!

One of very few places left that's survived the pandemic and cultural diversity! I'm 46 and this place has been around since as long as I can remember! Delicious cakes, cookies, and pastries especially my favorite the cannoli! Tres leches are also my go-to with mango, pineapple, guava, and chocolate to choose from! Get there early or you will miss out plus place can get pretty packed at times! Service is always great and the price definitely reflects quality!

Dianda's is definitely a quality bakery with detailed attention. You will pay for the 5 star qualities of the baked goods, variety, and charming customer service......The parking is not easy but Dianda's is well worth the stress of locating a safe parking spot.
Very impressive array of desserts! The eclair’s custard was kinda grainy but the chocolate topping was plentiful and tasty.

I am not particularly picky when it comes to dessert but this cake was just so dry and flavorless! The only good part about the cake was the filling, other than that, I would not recommend. The icing doesn’t have any taste… any cake would’ve tastes better than this, especially for almost 60 dollars for a small cake!

Cinderella Bakery & Cafe|436 Balboa St,.text:
This is a cute little bakery with Russian fare. Ordered the Piroshkis - cabbage eggs and green onion eggs. The bread was cloud like and extremely nice. Also got the  mushroom blinkchiki which was warm, and comforting stuffing. The stuffing tasted good despite not much spices, however their portion size was small for that price. To satiate the sweet tooth, ordered the almond coffee cake , which looked really nice and tasted ok.

Highly recommend getting a spot as they have limited seating

During my visit to San Francisco park I wanted a homely and satisfying meal to cap off the trip. Cinderella achieved both of those with flying colors. I got the pelmeni soup and it sent me back in time. It tasted of home in a bowl with a nice smell of fresh bread. To top it off water is complementary. I wish I lived in San Francisco just to have it again. A go to spot if you're passing through like I was.

Cinderella Bakery & Cafe is an absolute gem if you appreciate simple, well-made comfort food done right. What stood out to me the most was their bread and beef stroganoff. The bread is incredible—fresh, soft on the inside with a perfect crust on the outside. You can really taste the quality, and it feels like something made with care, not mass-produced. It’s the kind of bread you keep thinking about even after you leave.
The beef stroganoff was just as impressive. Rich, creamy, and full of flavor, with tender pieces of beef and a perfectly balanced sauce that isn’t too heavy. It tasted authentic and comforting, like a true homemade dish. If you go here, don’t skip the bread—and definitely try the beef stroganoff. It’s simple food, but done exceptionally well.

Amazing store for Russian Food. We ordered the borscht, pelmeni, olivye, and medovik. It did take a bit for our main dishes to come out but its a family run restaurant so i was a-ok waiting. We had a tub of olivye and slice of medovik to snack on anyways so all good.

The olivye was quite salty but i do tend to like my food quite salty so it was very tasty to me. The medovik or honey cake was the highlight of the meal, not too sweet, super soft and filled with pieces of what seem to be walnuts and sour cream frosting that had a great tang (what i think is often missing from american cream cheese frosting)

the borscht was earthy and comforting and also vegetarian just fyi. the pelmeni tasted nicely rich from the meat and even better when dunked into the sour cream

Me and my partner went to this bakery on the 30th of December at around 11 AM. It got really crowded in like 30 min or so. We got the mushroom and potato piroshi, sweeet cheese and beef blinchiki, strawberry cream turnover, hazelnut financier, eclairs and napoleon cake.
The sweet dishes were definitely good. We also liked the Pirsoshki. For a group of Indians, blinchiki might not hit the spot very well (obviously because we wanted some spice/ onions and green chilies probably in the beef filling), but I would definitely recommend this place.
San Francisco prices-so not super wallet friendly.

Schubert's Bakery|521 Clement St, San Fr.text:
Black Current Mousse (3.5/5): was really excited for this one but it was not as good as I anticipated. Mousse was yummy but not the perfect texture and a bit sour. Will try one of the other flavors next time.

Princess Cake (5/5): The marzipan was delicious and complemented the jam and cream well.

Chocolate Mousse Cake (4/5): Really rich and delicious almost like a fudge. The chocolate Mousse was quite dense more like a buttercream than a mousse.

I’ve been eyeing Schubert’s for a couple years now, so I probably had my expectations a little too high. Everything was good, fresh, tasty. Everything is beautifully prepared and packaged. I just wasn’t wowed, and I’m bakery spoiled. But the staff is helpful and they have an amazing selection! It really is a SF gem.

Nice little bakery. They do run out of the smaller cakes as the day goes by, so keep that in mind if you want to walk in and get one of the top seller items. They have a couple tables inside, but this is mostly a "take out" type of bakery. Huge selection of cakes, good prices. Want to come back and try some more!

I enjoy their delicate flavors and attention to detail. Ordering a cake was a breeze and every part of it was delicious! Yes, including the macarons and white chocolate signage. They make the best princess cake in the area. I had their mango cake as well, but I prefer it contrasted with the saltiness of a touch of cream cheese. Lovely local spot!

This has always been the cake for when it's a real occasion. No yukky grocery store thing ,these are fancy.  We just had the chocolate mousse cake and so rich so beautiful,and so delicious. Well worth the $.

Juniper|1401 Polk St, San Francisco, CA.text:
Juniper is a highly acclaimed, modern café and bakery located at 1401 Polk Street (at the corner of Polk and Pine) in the Nob Hill/Tendernob neighborhood of San Francisco.  Opened in early 2023, it is the sister concept to the renowned Saint Frank Coffee (located just a few blocks north on Polk). Juniper was founded with the explicit goal of bringing fine-dining pastry execution into an approachable, everyday neighborhood café setting. The name and kitchen philosophy are inspired by Brother Juniper—a companion of St. Francis of Assisi known for his open, joyful hospitality.
The interior features a clean, bright, and minimalist aesthetic with soothing neutral tones, industrial architectural bones, and large windows that let in plenty of natural light. A centerpiece of the café is the open view of the kitchen, allowing patrons to watch the pastry team roll, shape, and bake throughout the morning. It operates with a swift, bustling neighborhood energy, though seating is relatively limited inside.
The bakery shot to massive local fame after dominating the 2024 San Francisco Croissant Competition, sweeping the Grand Prix, People’s Choice, and Best Original Creation awards against some of the city's long-established legacy bakeries.
Open daily from 7:30 AM to 3:00 PM.  The Lines: Ever since their sweeping wins at the SF Croissant Competition, weekend lines can get long. However, if you are only ordering coffee, you can bypass the main pastry line and head straight to the back espresso bar.

What we enjoyed most about the pastries are these retain the crisp and the crunch of the flaky pastries like the mushroom and prosciutto danish, the mushroom danish topped them a;;

Tried Juniper and wow… easily one of the best bakeries in SF.

The croissants are insanely flaky and buttery.
Tried the pain au chocolat, banana chocolate croissant, and the raspberry almond and all of them were so good. Each one had a really nice balance of flavors without being overly sweet.

The vibe is cute and cozy, and it does get busy but definitely worth it. 10/10 recommend!

This delightful coffee and pastry shop offers a charming experience under one roof. I particularly appreciate that the pastries are made fresh in-store. Although it was quite busy when we arrived, with a line forming, it moved swiftly. The selection of pastries is extensive, featuring unique options like kimchi croissants. Seating is limited, so keep that in mind. Parking could be a challenge too.

If you're just in the mood for coffee, you can bypass the pastry line and head straight to the coffee section. Simply let them know you're only ordering coffee. We tried the latte, cortado, and matcha, all of which were excellent. Additionally, we sampled a variety of croissants and cookies, each one delicious. The service was prompt, and the prices were reasonable. This could easily become my go-to coffee shop whenever I'm in town.

Ran into this sweet hidden gem on my way to running a few errands. The pastries are delicate, unique, and full of flavor. You can really taste the love that is transpired through the art of baking. The coffee roast is phenomenal. The vides are immaculate, staff is fast paced, sweet, and welcoming.

This is one of my son's favorite spots and I see why😊.  During my visit he treated me with a visit to Juniper Cafe.  It's a must-visit, especially if you're a pastry lover! They earned their title of "best croissant in 2024" for a reason. The Traditional Croissant is absolute perfection—light, airy, with a beautiful crunch and that ideal buttery taste. Seriously delish😋.
​Beyond the classic, don't miss out on their unique creations, like the rich Black Sesame Kouign Amann, and the sweet and satisfying Chocolate and Banana Croissant. The Juniper Lemon Choux is a bright, delightful finish, and for a savory start, the breakfast sandwich is a winner with genuinely fresh ingredients. Pair it all with the refreshing Iced Strawberry Matcha Latte or a Latte for a perfect morning!
The staff is polite and attentive, clean facility, but limited seating.
Love it! 💜 I can't wait to come back! 👍👍

Turquaz SF|1198 Mission St, San Francisc.text: This place is awesome! The ambiance is fantastic and the space is huge, making it perfect for large groups. I got the salmon bowl and it was incredibly fresh, flavorful, and healthy. My husband got the meat döner wrap and really enjoyed it as well. Some of my friends ordered the Kavurma and eggs dish and loved it — it looked amazing, and I’d definitely try it next time. The tender slow-cooked beef with fried eggs looked so delicious. The vibes of the place were perfect, and everything felt warm and inviting. Their pastries also looked incredible. Definitely a spot I’d come back to!

We stopped by for lunch while visiting the city and have been amazed by the aunthencity of the food, genuine friendliness of the staff, and the coziness of the restaurant. Lahmacun was spot on (just like how it would taste in Turkey), vegetarian çiğ köfte was awesome, beyti kebab was delicious, and the few mezes that we tried (ezme, humus, smoked eggplant with yogurt) were all excellent. The diverse baklava selection along with the other desserts, which are all impressively prepared at the restaurant, served with Turkish tea, were out of this world! We can't wait to go back and try some of the other dishes. Kudos to the owners and staff for this fantastic restaurant!

I had a fantastic experience at Turquaz in San Francisco! The food was absolutely delicious, with so many authentic Turkish dishes to choose from that it was hard to decide what to order. Everything we tried was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully prepared.
One of the highlights was their incredibly rich menu of pastries, desserts, and Turkish delights. Whether you're looking for a savory treat or something sweet to finish your meal, they have an amazing variety that truly showcases Turkish cuisine.
The service was outstanding—fast, attentive, and welcoming from start to finish. The entire staff was friendly and professional, but a special thank you goes to Shahin, the cashier. His warm personality, kindness, and excellent customer service genuinely made our day and made us feel right at home.
Turquaz is a wonderful place for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Turkish food and hospitality. I’ll definitely be coming back and highly recommend it to others!

Authentic Turkish breakfast!!!
Definitely feels like home for me. We had a breakfast plate, sütlaç, and Anatolian cheese. Everything tastes great though we didn’t get our food on time. It was busy so I think they missed. But overall a really good experience.

We were truly very satisfied with this restaurant. We came both for breakfast and dinner, and each time we had a wonderful experience. The staff were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and attentive. The place itself is very stylish and has a cozy atmosphere. The food was absolutely delicious, and the portions were quite generous. Prices are also very reasonable for the quality offered. Definitely a place we would happily visit again and highly recommend. 🌸

Lokma|1801 Clement St, San Francisco, CA.text:
If you’re looking to try authentic Turkish food, Lokma is definitely a must-visit. The restaurant offers amazing Turkish food options, especially the burger and chicken bowl, both of which were flavorful and perfectly prepared. The pita bread was served fresh, warm, and soft, pairing really well with the incredibly tasty baba ganoush.

The seating inside is small and cozy, giving the place a welcoming vibe, and they also have two outdoor sheds for comfortable open-air seating. To top it all off, the baklava was perfectly crispy and delicious. Overall, a fantastic spot for anyone wanting to explore Turkish cuisine with great flavors and a cozy atmosphere.

This is the kind of restaurant that makes me consider moving to San Francisco! A perfect Turkish breakfast experience - rich, flavorful, and full of variety. The menu offers plenty of options, the atmosphere is cozy, and it wasn’t overly crowded when we visited.

We ordered the traditional Turkish breakfast, creamy mushroom soup, orange juice, and sparkling water. You can mix and match everything on the table, and somehow every combo just works! I don’t have a lot of experience with Turkish breakfasts, so I can’t say how authentic it is but I can say it was absolutely delicious, and I loved every bite.

Update: finally remember to upload photos

A story about how one of my favorite spices (cinnamon) ruined brunch on a Sunday.

I love cinnamon. In pastries, in coffee, in savory chili.i just did not expect it to hijack a savory Turkish ratatouille and dominate my entire plate.

The Good:
• For a walk-in only, a short 10 minute wait was very much appreciated
•The eggs were beautifully executed - soft, fluffy, properly folded, tender inside without being runny or rubbery. You can tell the kitchen knows what they’re doing.
• Tzatziki on eggs? Unexpected and absolutely delicious. Cool, tangy, creamy against warm eggs - amazing!
• The Arnold Palmer was perfectly balanced (not too sweet, not too tea-heavy). Clean layers, refreshing, 5/5.
• Cold water served immediately upon arrival (and properly cold). It’s such a simple touch, but always appreciated.
• Ambiance is top notch - warm, inviting, great brunch energy.
• Attentive staff throughout.

The Not-So-Good:
• The Turkish ratatouille was overwhelmingly cinnamon-forward. That was the only dominant note I could taste, and it overpowered the dish to the point where I struggled to finish it. Interestingly, the server initially mentioned they don’t add cinnamon but after checking with the kitchen, confirmed that cinnamon is indeed added. It was just far too much.
• No side plate. With oranges, sauce, and potatoes sharing space, there was nowhere to rest utensils or keep citrus from soaking into the stew. Small detail, but it matters.
• No salt and pepper on the table, so there was no way to tweak seasoning.

The frustrating part is that the technical cooking - especially the eggs - was excellent. But when one spice dominates and a few small service details are overlooked, it changes the entire experience.

Cinnamon, I still love you.

10/10
This was an exceptional breakfast. It is very rare to find an authentic Turkish restaurant in the States and this one was by far the best one I tried. Their menu is not big but everything is intentional. 10/10 would recommend.

Such a cute place. The food is mediocre. Started with a high note on appetizers, the kofta was on the dry side but it had all the right spices, the zucchini dolmas tasted delicious. The grilled chicken was also dry, the mousaka was rather bland and had not enough beef. But overall the food was gorgeously plated, the place was cute and the service was great.

Palmyra|700 Haight St, San Francisco, CA.text:
