I ordered the beef and chicken platter, and honestly the food was amazing. The meat was flavorful, tender, and perfectly seasoned. The hummus tasted incredibly fresh and rich, and you could tell everything was made with care.

I also tried another plate with ground beef, hummus, salad, and pickles on the side, and it was just as delicious. Every item on the plate was balanced perfectly and full of authentic flavor.

The service was outstanding from start to finish. The cashier, the cooks, and the entire staff were professional, welcoming, and very generous with us. They didn’t just meet my expectations — they exceeded them.

If you’re looking for authentic Syrian and Middle Eastern food in San Francisco, Palmyra is definitely worth visiting. Strong 5 out of 5 stars from me.

Highly recommend! I come here often because it has great food, kind hosts, and ample space/seating. Very comfortable place to eat, sit and read or chat with friends. I’ve tried the rotisserie chicken, lamb shawarma, and kofta platter - all were delicious, though my personal favourite is lamb shawarma. A special shout out goes to the cashier worker/server who is always warm, smiling, and extremely welcoming! Really appreciate his kindness and hard work. Owner is also lovely.
Food was hot and delicious and the service was excellent. The dining area was very clean and well kept. The falafel was the best falafel I've had.

I was really impressed with the falafel plate here. Every item tasted fresh and full of simple, clean flavor. The team working here is genuinely kind, and the relaxed counter service setup makes the whole visit feel comfortable and easy. I left completely satisfied and will definitely return.

I would definitely come back here and get the falafel wrap again! Great combination of flavors and textures, very enjoyable. I ordered online for pickup and made a note about my allergy; the man behind the cash register was good about checking in with me about this and making sure I felt secure in my order. I could tell the service is very friendly and personable. I was in a rush to something so couldn’t engage in a chat but if I had time, I probably would have. Lots of indoor seating, a bit of outdoor seating.

Just a note to the business: if you can make your signage any bigger or more visible, especially being on a corner, I think it would bring more business! I was following Google maps and still walked right past it and then had to backtrack because the signage wasn’t very visible to me. Another friend who frequents this area said she’s been around a lot of places and never knew a Mediterranean place was nearby. Y’all have great food- advertise more boldly if possible!

Bistro Mediterraneo|2955 Clement St, San.text:
Amazing food and excellent service at this cozy, family-run spot that makes you feel truly welcome. It may be small, but it’s a hidden gem well worth seeking out.

We tried the lentil soup, gyro, and falafel plates, and everything was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. The baklava ice cream was the standout and an absolute must-try. Highly recommended!

Delightful Turkish Mediterranean market and deli with a few tables for dining. The falfel wrap was particularly good! Interesting selection of packaged food. The staff was quite helpfu and pleasant in discussing the menu and offering suggestions. One suggestion to management is to use a vegan mayo in the Grilled Mutabbal dish, which is otherwise vegan. There's some great vegan mayonnaises on the market and this dish looked quite interesting. An overall fun experience for a first time visit!

The gyro wrap (pita) was really delicious. First wrap that did not feel oily, saturated, and the sauces had a nice kick. The falafels were also really delicious. However, the humus felt like it could’ve have used more seasoning/citric juices. Would definitely recommend.

A great little neighborhood gem with freshly made gyros, hummus, etc and some Turkish groceries and sweets and a counter with fresh baklava. I got the Gyro, some Turkish soda and some Palestinian olive oil. 10/10 experience

If you’re passing by this restaurant, you won’t want to miss their Lahm Bi Ajeen (see pictures). It does take some time to cook, and I was honestly wondering why it took so long — until I tasted it. Then it all made sense. Totally worth the wait.

Presidio Kebab Mediterranean Restaurant|.text:
Came here on a Sunday late afternoon close to 2p. There was just a few people and no wait at that time. I thought I was under dressed, cargo pants and a t-shirt, but thankfully it was in the afternoon. I would definitely dress better if it was in the evening. It’s a nice place. The service was great. Ambiance was nice. We got the Baba Ganoush & Greek fries for appetizer. I don’t think I’ve ever had shakshuka but it was good and seemed fresh. The pita bread was good that went with it. They did ask if we wanted more pita bread, so I guess that’s refillable. It was just enough for us so we didn’t need more. The fries were ok. There was a lot! Normally, I like thin fries but only if it’s slightly crispy. These weren’t as crisp.
For the entree, I got a Greek salad. The feta tasted a little different to me but other than that, it was good. My friend got the lamb kebabs. I tried one and it was good. It didn’t taste gamey. It was good as my friend ate all of it.
I would come back here and recommend if you’re looking for Mediterranean food.

It’s great to have a local spot for Turkish cuisine! We absolutely loved the Beyti and the Manti; both were wonderful. The Doner wasn't exactly what we were hoping for, but the rest of the meal made up for it. We found the desserts a bit rich—the Kunefe was very sweet and the Sutlac quite hearty with rice—but overall, it was a pleasant local find.
Amazing. Order combo platter, Adana kabab, lentil soup, baklava, and Greek salad. Especially enjoyed the housemade hot sauce. Wine prices are great.

Great experience at Presidio Kebab! The food was absolutely delicious—fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. Vedat’s hospitality really stands out—welcoming, attentive, and genuinely cares about every guest. You feel taken care of from start to finish. Definitely a place I’ll keep coming back to.

Great service and delicious food!
The staff was very friendly and attentive, and it was nice that the waiters spoke Turkish too. The food was full of flavor, and my favorite was the Iskender - absolutely delicious! Definitely a place worth visiting again.

Zevi Cafe & Bistro|67 5th St, San Franci.text:
Zevi Café was my first meal in San Francisco, and I couldn’t have picked a better place to start the trip. The food was delicious — a mix of Turkish dishes and American favorites.

It’s a family-owned business, and our waiter kindly shared the story behind the restaurant and even gave us a few recommendations for our stay in San Francisco, which made the experience feel very personal.

The portions are quite large — neither my best friend nor I could finish our plates — so sharing might be a good idea.

PS: The prices are also very reasonable.
Great place! I wasn’t expecting to find such high-quality Turkish food in SF. Meze, döner, kebab, and baklava were all spot on. I truly felt at home.

Avocado toast was not tasty. Looked like the avocado spread was from a package. Shakshuka was not ok either. The cheese in it smelled bad and tomatoes were not fresh. They were from a can. Shakshuka did not have cilantro. Bread was like sponge. Coffee was bad too.

Poached were good though.

Was a decent experience, but not much to write home about. The seasonal soup (lentils) was excellent while “Alexander’s Favourite” was good, but the Jasmine rice with Falafel was only serviceable (the falafel seemed to be pre-made, rather than fresh.) The quantities were quite decent and the service was ok too (though they did forget our drink order till I reminded them in the middle of the meal, despite the place being less than a third full.) However, everyone was all smiles and made us feel right at home.

Amazing spot!The dishes were bursting with authentic flavor and everything tasted incredibly fresh. The mantı had the perfect balance of spices and sauce, the cheese pide was crisp on the outside and soft inside, and the kibbeh over yogurt and tomato was honestly one of the best I’ve tried. The hummus and warm bread were also outstanding and made the whole meal even better.
Huge appreciation to Chef Saim — his craftsmanship shows in every detail, from presentation to taste. You can tell he truly cares about the quality of the food he serves. A place I’ll definitely come back to💕

Penaber Mediterranean meze house|835 Hyd.text: I recently dined at Penaber Mediterranean Restaurant and was genuinely impressed. The food was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented, and the prices were surprisingly reasonable for the quality of the experience. The restaurant was spotless, well-organized, and had a warm, elegant atmosphere that felt like fine dining without being pretentious. It’s the kind of place where you can relax, enjoy a great meal, and feel that you received excellent value. I’ll definitely be coming back.

The food was absolutely amazing, and the lamb was incredibly delicious—very tender and flavorful. It was my first time trying sarma (grape leaves stuffed with rice), and I loved it! Everything tasted fresh and well-prepared.

The owner was very friendly, helpful, and truly focused on the customers, which made the dining experience even more enjoyable. Highly recommended!

Overall good food with nice flavor. Rice was a little undercooked, calamari rings some were a bit overcooked, but otherwise tasty and fresh ingredients-decent value. Decent house red. Service was quick and very pleasant.

I enjoyed my meal at this cute Mediterranean restaurant. The food was good overall. The best item was the baba ghanoush - it was smoky with a good flavor. The hummus and the tzatziki were fine, but not as good as the baba ghanoush. The chicken skewer was a bit spicy for my taste, but the chicken was nice and juicy, and at six dollars per skewer, the price could not be beat. I enjoyed it all with a glass of sparkling wine. The service was kind and friendly. I liked the lady who was serving us. The atmosphere of the restaurant was cozy and inviting, the kind of neighborhood place where you can just drop by and have a bite.

Penaber Mediterranean Meze House is the new hot spot in the neighborhood, and it does not disappoint! The flavors are fresh, vibrant, and absolutely delicious—but the lamb steals the show. Perfectly cooked, tender, and seasoned to perfection.

The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, and you can tell the chef puts real love into every dish. 👏 Chef—keep up the great work, you’ve definitely gained a loyal customer!

If you’re looking for authentic Mediterranean flavors with a modern twist, this is a must-visit.

Ararat Kebab & Gyros|612 O'Farrell St, S.text:
Last year, during my first visit to San Francisco, I discovered this restaurant and absolutely loved it. Now I’m back in San Francisco for the second time, and on my very first day in town, I knew I had to come back here.

The food is incredibly delicious! I was so excited to start eating that I completely forgot to take a picture of the chicken soup, but I did manage to get photos of the İskender and the dessert. Everything was fresh, flavorful, and just as amazing as I remembered.

If you’re in San Francisco, you should definitely give this place a try—you won’t regret it!

Ararat Restaurant in San Francisco is absolutely amazing! The food is incredibly tasty, fresh, and full of authentic flavor. Every dish we tried was perfectly seasoned and beautifully prepared. You can really feel the love and care put into the food. This is one of the best places in SF for delicious, high-quality meals. We will definitely be coming back!” 😊

I had an amazing experience at this kebab spot. The food is absolutely delicious and you can tell everything is made fresh. The place is very clean and well organized, which made the whole experience even better. The staff member who helped me was super friendly and welcoming. This has definitely become my favorite place to get kebab and I will keep coming back.

Nice clean small European kabob style restaurant and the food that I ordered was delicious The regular wrap which mind you was about $15 but I think for San Francisco it's probably fair staff was amazingly friendly the hot sauce was incredible
Very nice place to get some good Turkish food. Pleasant people and cozy atmosphere. Tea is on the house.

North Beach Gyros|561 Columbus Ave, San.text:
THIS IS A MUST IN SF!!

When I saw over 4,000 reviews with a 4.9 rating on Google Maps, I was honestly skeptical—so I decided to try it myself. Believe me when I say this place truly deserves all 5 stars.

The food is hands down some of the best we’ve ever had. The service was also excellent—the owner is incredibly friendly and a great host, which makes the whole experience even better.

Everything we ordered was amazing, but the Mesopotamian plate was definitely the best value. The desserts were also really good.

This is not a paid advertisement—I’m just genuinely excited about this place and highly recommend it. Plus, it’s not as crowded as other spots in North Beach, which is a big bonus.

Amazing food & service. We popped in here hungry and left full, happy, and with a bag of leftovers! The portions are large and all the food fresh and flavorful. We ordered the Grilled Branzino and Mesopotamia Kebab for our mains. Our server was very attentive and gave us plenty of things to try while we were here. Great place to stumble upon!

My husband and I were visiting SF and found this place randomly. As a Turkish person, I really missed service like this in a restaurant.

Abdurrahman was very kind and welcoming. The food was absolutely delicious. The meat was cooked perfectly, and the mezes were amazing. And honestly, I still can’t believe they managed to make dolma better than my grandma’s! Also, you get extra mezes from the house if you order any meat/chicken.

Awesome turkish food, reasonable prices, and great service. We even brought our coworkers the next day! Def recommend!

Some of the best Mediterranean food! The food was super fresh. Loved everything from the beef and lamb gyro plate to the warm pita bread and delicious dips! Jose and Serhat, the waiters, were also super attentive and kind in making sure I was comfortable. They also offered me to tried their baklava dessert, which was also incredibly fresh, flaky, warm - delicious! Overall, would recommend this restaurant for quality food and service!

The food was absolutely amazing from start to finish. The falafel was perfectly crispy on the outside and soft, flavorful, and fresh on the inside. The tzatziki was creamy, refreshing, and complemented every bite beautifully. The fries were golden, crispy, and seasoned just right.

The salad tasted incredibly fresh with crisp vegetables and a well-balanced dressing, while the hummus was smooth, rich, and packed with flavor. Every dish felt freshly prepared and made with quality ingredients.

Overall, a fantastic meal with excellent flavors, freshness, and presentation. I would definitely come back and highly recommend it to anyone looking for delicious Mediterranean food! 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

Berlin Doner & Kebab House|901 Sutter St.text:
Phenomenal Döner & menu overall

The restaurant & staff ooze authenticity, warmth & a deep passion for the cuisine, they serve their guests

Went with a big group of friends and we all tried a little bit of everything wasn’t a single thing on the menu I wouldn’t be elated to try again

The döner was incredibly juicy, full of flavor, and cooked exactly the right way. You can tell the chef really knows what he’s doing — the meat was tender, well-seasoned, and not dry at all.

I’ve had a lot of döner in different places, and in my opinion, no döner in SF beats Berlin Döner. This is easily the best döner I’ve had in the Bay Area so far. The balance between the meat, rice, and salad was perfect, and everything tasted fresh.

If you’re craving real, properly prepared döner, this place is a must-try. I’ll definitely be coming back
Awesome food and menu. A friend took us there for my birthday. It was wonderful. I like how fresh the salad and how tasty the food plate is. Make sure you order the combo to try out everything.

Absolutely loved my breakfast here this morning! The staff was so friendly and welcoming, which made the experience even better. The food was incredibly delicious and authentic. And oh my goodness, save room for the desserts- they are to die for! Highly, highly recommend to everyone looking for a tasty and delightful meal.

A perfect start to the day! Will be back soon!

#TurkishBreakfast #Foodie #HighlyRecommend #Delicious #SweetTooth
It was very good experience having dinner there, the food was delicious and the staffs were nice and welcoming.

Baklavastory.|1830 Harrison St Ste B, Sa.text:
If you’re looking for authentic Turkish baklava in San Francisco, Baklavastory is a must-visit ✨
From pistachios to butter, all ingredients come from Antep and Urfa, and Chef Tolgay personally travels to Turkey every year to hand-select the highest-quality products. You can truly taste this care in every bite.

The baklava is always fresh, perfectly balanced in sweetness, and never heavy. The moment you walk in, the aroma of real butter welcomes you. Chef Tolgay’s warm smile and hospitality make the whole experience even more memorable.

If you’re in San Francisco, you have to try it. One visit and you’ll become a regular 💛

The best baklava in the city!!!!!!!!

A small, unassuming shop kind of in the middle of nowhere that I decided to check out after a day at the nearby Jewelry Exchange. They have the best baklava I’ve ever had in my life! They always give free samples (usually the walnut baklava) when you walk in. I’d recommend calling to order a day in advance, otherwise they might not have the baklava that you want available. The pistachio baklava is absolutely incredible and addicting. I usually don’t like baklava when it has goat cheese, but their baklava has no trace of goat cheese. One of my favorite desserts in SF!

Baklava Story

Absolutely worth the trip. The baklava is made to order, incredibly fresh, and honestly some of the best I’ve had. You can taste the care in every bite — perfectly flaky, balanced sweetness, and rich flavor. DO NOT get fooled by the humble storefront.

The owner was so welcoming and offered us a sample as soon as we walked in, which immediately set the tone. Friendly, kind, and clearly passionate about what they do.

If you’re in San Francisco and love baklava, do not miss this place. I promise it’s worth going out of your way for.

If you want to taste Turkey's most traditional dessert in its most authentic form, consider yourself lucky to be in San Francisco. A baklava master who truly loves his craft is working from the other side of the world to bring you the finest Turkish baklava possible. The dedication shows in every single bite. And if you need any further proof of quality — the taste has been tested and approved by an American professor from Stanford. That says it all!

Usually baklava is too sweet for me, but they really nailed the recipe. This is the best baklava I have ever had and will ever have in my life. The owner/Baker is so passionate. He literally gets his pistachios and ghee from his farm in Turkey. I don’t even normally write reviews, but here I am.

Samiramis Imports|2990 Mission St, San F.text: A large selection of Middle Eastern groceries like pastries, snacks, canned goods, pickled foods etc. I got a large plate of baklava for my friend’s party and they were absolutely delectable! Will definitely be back to try the other pastries.

This is my one stop shopping store for Middle Eastern and Palestinian products. They have excellent home made products along with an extensive line of imported goods. There selection is awesome, multiple types of breads, olives, cheeses etc! This was my go-to store during COVID when everyone was lined up at Safeway! And the proprietors are very nice too!

This is the place to come for Middle Eastern goods. The selection is wonderful, the date and olive varieties are excellent, and they have so much! Take your time, poke around, it’s fantastic!
Enjoy!

Love love this market. It has all the spices I need and the best fresh feta cheese that you will have in you life (at least in US) you can get red pepper paste, harissa, grape leaves, phyllo, dates, olives, pita bread, labne, couscous, freekeh, lentils, falafel, kibbeh, pistachios, currants, barberry, za'atar, aleppo, vanilla. If you have any Mediterranean/Arab/levantine/Israeli recipe you will need this store.
A hidden family owned gem. It's all about everything Mediterranean grocery modestly priced and more. The owner was the most generous and helpful. A truly humbiling and kind experience!

Kitchen Istanbul|349 Clement St, San Fra.text:
Great place!
The service was very attentive.
We enjoyed the delicious food.
All fresh and flavorful.
Excellent wine pairing 🍷
Especially liked the Muhammara, borek, tomato salad, fresh baked pita bread and rice pudding.
Less liked the:
The fish crudo was very good but a very small portion.
Manti (the meat had an aftertaste)
and kunafe (not enough cheese)

I had probably one of the best dinners of my life at Kitchen Istanbul, which is by far the best Turkish restaurant outside of Turkey that I have ever been to. Everything we tried, from şakşuka to mantı, from börek to Adana, were just perfect in such that they had a touch of reinvention of the traditional recipes, without removing the original essence from them. İnci profiterol, a phenomenal İstanbul classic, brought back memories from my childhood walking around İstiklal Caddesi in İstanbul, stopping at İnci after a movie at Emek theatre; reminiscent of places that no longer exist.  A short but rich menu, was  such a great representation of the Turkish cuisine as you can probably tell from the dishes that I had; whatever Turkish cuisine has to offer, from regional gems, to complexities and surprises, are all here. And  I appreciate the fact that this place is not hiding its origins; while Istanbul and Anatolia are part of its DNA, it does, at the same time, have so much more to offer. Highly recommended

Absolutely fantastic food and wine service. All of the dishes were perfectly seasoned and balanced. We especially loved the dips, borek, heirloom tomato salad, and moussaka. Will be recommending this gem to friends and returning asap!
I’ve been to Kitchen Istanbul twice, and both visits were great experiences. They have an excellent wine selection, and the food is delicious, with great flavors and an enjoyable atmosphere.

The food here is genuinely excellent, which makes this review disappointing to write.

Unfortunately, my partner became ill and spent much of the day at Stanford Medical Center. Out of respect for the restaurant, I called to cancel our reservation rather than simply not showing up. While it was admittedly close to the reservation time, OpenTable does not allow cancellations within 24 hours, and the restaurant itself wasn’t open until 5 PM, making it difficult to provide additional notice.

Despite explaining the situation, I was charged the full $50 per person cancellation fee. What was most disappointing wasn’t the fee itself, but the complete lack of flexibility or empathy given the circumstances. I was even told they would prefer I come dine alone while my partner was sick because it was better for the restaurant than giving up the reservation. “It’s a reservation business not a walk in business”…although I walked in myself last weekend.

As someone who had dined here the week prior and planned to become a regular customer, this felt shortsighted.

Restaurants are certainly entitled to protect themselves from no-shows, but there should be room for reasonable discretion when a guest makes a good-faith effort to communicate an unexpected medical situation.

The food is absolutely worth trying. The customer service experience, unfortunately, left a very different impression.

If you're making a reservation well in advance, be sure to keep track of it carefully, as the restaurant may still enforce cancellation fees even if you are a returning or prospective long-term customer.

Anatolian Table|702 Valencia St, San Fra.text:
Another amazing halal spot in San Francisco! 🇹🇷✨

We ordered the Turkish breakfast for two, and at first they moved us to a bigger table—we were a little confused… but then the food started arriving 😅 and WOW, it all made sense.

The table was completely filled with so many varieties of cheese, plus a mix of sweet and savory dishes. It instantly took me back to Istanbul on a rainy day—such a nostalgic and cozy experience ☔️

For $60 for two, it’s absolutely worth every penny. The quality, the variety, and the whole experience were just incredible.

The service is warm and welcoming, which made everything even better. Highly recommended! 🙌

Definitely coming back again to try more from the menu!

Tasty and truly authentic Turkish culinary experience along fashionable Valencia Street.

Lahmacun (Turkish thin-crust pizza) is a rare-find highlight worth a trip for: expertly hand-tossed and wood fired, making for a perfect item to share with friends.

As with almost all items on menu, eating family style is best. Meze Combo is a wonderful tour of spreads and apps; the falafel is noteworthy — crispy, crunchy, herbaceous and savory.

Adana Wrap, a doner served on flatbread with shaved beef and lamb, can be split and provides a comforting close to a meal.

There hasn’t been a line when I’ve come in the evening for dinner and we got seated quickly. There are outdoor heaters too. Their service is good and servers are all Turkish. This spot might be one of the best Turkish places in SF according to my very Turkish friend. Love the food especially the manti and pides and etc. definitely would come back.

Good Turkish cuisine, pides, kebabs dishes were tasty and fresh.

The horiatiki salad was not good; tomatoes were too mushy and ripe. We tried to get it remade but waiter availability is an issue here. They’re not checking the parklet tables often enough and getting waiters attention is very difficult.
Desserts are ok; plenty of excellent options nearby like Smitten and Dandelion Chocolate.

Best time to come here is early afternoon to avoid big crowd.

Such a wonderful place. The food was fantastic. Whether you eat meat or are vegan, you’ll find something to suit your taste.
The staff are incredibly friendly and recommended some excellent options I never tried before. We tried kusbasi, different mezze, octopus, deserts - everything was perfect. The interior is lovely as well!

Troya Restaurant|2125 Fillmore St, San F.text:
Came here for dinner on a Saturday. Got the chickpea falafel. Honestly so good and flavorful. The sauce on top was amazing and really tied it together. The hummus was very creamy and not too salty. Also ordered the chicken skewer. The chicken itself was tasty and well seasoned, but the rice stood out a lot. The raisins added a nice sweetness and it came with more of that same sauce from the falafel which was amazing. We finished with the walnut baklava and it was easily some of the best baklava I’ve had. It was nutty and not overly sweet.

The staff was incredibly kind. We came in right before closing and stayed a bit past, and they never rushed us. Even cleaned up spilled rice right away without making it awkward. Only criticism with the place is that it is pretty expensive for the quantity and the quality of the food.

We stopped in for brunch on a whim and found our new favorite Mediterranean place! The atmosphere was great and the staff were very nice. It looks like a popular place so I’d recommend making reservations. We were just two so they were able to seat us just fine.
The food was delicious and the portions were on the large side, at least for what we ordered. And there was a good variety of things on the brunch menu. We also had Turkish coffee which came with a delicious pistachio Turkish delight.
I highly recommend Troya and we look forward to coming back.

Absolutely love this place! If you are vegetarian, their meal for 2 is a must! If meat is your preference, try the braised lamb, it’s unbelievable!

Come with an appetite because the servings are portioned perfectly for a hungry patron.

Must try:

Hummus- rich, creamy, and a great appetizer to start with. For those who want to make a meal of it, try the warmed variety with braised lamb, absolutely amazing!

Labneh- better than your Nine’s, it is a great appetizer to have with their tasty salad.

Braised Lamb shank- It’s something you just have to try. It’s one of  the best Mediterranean meat dishes out there.

Moussaka- a vegetarian delight, the moussaka didn’t lack in flavor, quality, or portion size. Loved it.

Vegetarian meal for 2- can’t describe how incredible the meal deal is. Please try it and come back and let me know how it goes.
Delicious entrees and appetizers. I wasn’t the biggest fan of the Turkish delights but thats just personal preference. Possibly best Baklava Ive ever had.

WoW!!!! We just discovered this place today walking down Filmore. This was probably some of the best Mediteranean and Turkish food I have had in my lifetime and I have loved in several with large Middle Eastern populations!!! We each ordered some Brussels sprouts to start which were delicious and then the star of the show was the skewer plates. The lamb was fresh and tender and perfectly seasoned over rice that made me feel I had to gone to heaven. I want to learn how to make this rice!!! It was so wonderful. Its pricier for brunch but worth every bite. Thank you Troya for sharing this beautiful food with the world!!!

The Bite|996 Mission St, San Francisco,.text: The Bite offers authentic Palestinian cuisine made with wholesome ingredients that nourish your body and support your best health. Absolutely love this spot!

We swung by here for dinner after watching a movie at AMC Metreon. The area getting there felt a little sketchy, but it wasn’t too bad overall. Once inside, the place was clean and the cashier was friendly.

I ordered the grilled beef kebab rice bowl, and it was easily one of the best Mediterranean meals I’ve had. The rice could’ve used a bit more flavor, but the kebab itself was phenomenal.

Overall, I’d absolutely recommend giving this spot a try!

Sooooo yummy. Plenty of gluten free and vegan options. I have celiac and they wiped down the grill to make my kebab, plus the fryer doesn’t have gluten in it so celiac-safe fries!! Staff were so kind.

Absolutely fantastic restaurant with great service and fantastic food. If you are looking for fantastic Turkish | Mediterranean food, this should be your first choice. We will definitely be back soon and every time in San Francisco!

Fantastic service! The Genovese was outstanding from the sauce to the tender beef on that seeded roll , can’t go wrong! Very down to earth people . I’m from Philly and know a good sandwich when I taste one , folks this is it ! Listen I love you but Jesus loves you most ! Check out The Bite for sure without a doubt!!!!!

Himalayan Cuisine SF|1412 Polk St, San F.text: We drove up from San Jose to try this. Certainly, we have had more than our share of Indian food, and a good portion of the menu should comfort those who want some Indian. But do yourself a favor and try some of the less common dishes--the Nepali Khaja lunch set is my pick in this category. The photo in the menu is deceptively small; in reality, it's a portion possibly enough for two. The "beaten rice" is less like rice and more like muslix--it's grainy and crunchy, a delightful contrast to the spicy chicken choyela and edamame. For those who can't decide, consider the dinner plate (also maybe big enough for two), Himalayaj Thali with your choice of mest or veggie.

Nice spot that stays open later than most! Good Nepalese cuisine. Had their Chicken Tikka Masala, Lamb Curry, Momo's, Chicken Chow Mein and garlic naan. Everything tasted great! They work fast and the food arrived piping hot. Solid food option!

Fantastic food, all fresh made and authentic in a laid back environment. There was only one waiter working when we went in for lunch but he absolutely hustled around. The chow mein was the star of the show, the spices used and chicken were completely unique to anything I've ever eaten!

After craving Tandoori Chicken for about 6 months...no shit 6 months!
I left my little piece of paradise we call Napa, and headed straight into the city to the absolute best Tandoori Chicken this side of the Himalayas!  That's right Pilgrim... The Himalayan Cuisine SF restaurant, just a block off Van Ness on Polk where everyone, and everything comes with a red dot on its forhead.  The Tandoori Chicken and Rice is out of bounds, so good you'll convert to Hinduism and trade in your car for an elephant.  Everything about the place is amazing, the food, the service, the patrons, the decor, the vibe..Everything 5 ☆☆☆☆☆'s

This place has decent prices, but it’s still slightly higher than you would pay at most Indian places, especially if you compared to other cities with large amounts of Indian restaurants. The food portions are pretty decent and I have to say the food is tasty so at least you get good quality food for the price you pay. The place is a little bit dated, but it is nice overall and the service is pretty good. It’s nice because it’s in the neighborhood where you can get other food as well and walk around.

Nepa Indian Cuisine|507 Divisadero St, S.text:
Came second time and its a gorgeous place and great food.
1. We ordered Baigan Bharta and Paneer Tikka Masala
2. Great Garlic Naan and Awesome Mango Lassi
3. They always ask for feedback and so nice people my god... beautiful souls.
4. Their papad and chutney is really good
5. They also gave us complimentary deserts without asking
6. For place like SF its also easy on ur wallet.

Had a great time with food. Surprisingly good food. I think… I might have to go again lol. (I rarely go back to places idk why 😶)

They probably have the best chai in the Bay I have tried at a restaurant yet.

Recommendation: Chai, complimentary Papad and chutneys, Palak Paneer, and Naans.

Wow, talk about FABULOUS Indian food before a show. I couldn't believe how delicious everything was and now I'm already ready to return for round two.

Let's talk about their vegan friendly entrees. Their Baingan Bharta and Aloo Gobi were fantastic. I've had these two dishes a lot in my plant-based life and the way they prepare these dishes here exceeded my expectations. The flavor bomb that comes from the Aloo Gobi was absolutely noteworthy and should definitely be considered. Ten out of ten on both of these dishes.

I also ordered an Aloo Pratha which I didn't realize would be cooked with butter. I asked to have one without and they were able to accommodate me. It was a massive amount for just one order and made the perfect serving bread for all our vegan dishes.

Service was super friendly and the evening ambiance made for a great meal. Whether you're plant-based or not, there's something for everyone here and certainly worth a try.

Stopped by Nepa Indian Cuisine in NoPa for lunch and had an excellent experience! The Vegetable Chilli Momos were crispy, packed with flavor, and perfectly spiced with that signature Himalayan kick. Special shoutout to Rudhra — such a humble and attentive waiter who made the meal even better. Highly recommend!

I’ve gone back and forth about writing this because Nepa is our favorite Nepalese/Indian spots in the city. The flavors are consistently excellent, and we’ve had great meals here—which is why this is so disappointing to share.

Over the past couple of visits, there have been serious issues with dietary restrictions and allergy handling that management needs to address.

A few months ago, despite clearly communicating multiple times that I have a cilantro allergy (yes it's a weird allergy, no it doesn't take like soap), we were served two dishes with cilantro. The first (an appetizer) was sent back. The second, prepared and served after the appetizer, was a curry that we specifically double-checked before eating—we were assured it contained no cilantro. Unfortunately, that was incorrect, and I became sick after eating it. While the staff did remake the dish and comped the meal, the damage was already done.

We returned this past Sunday, hoping that experience was a one-off. Again, we clearly stated that I have a cilantro allergy and that we are vegan. The meal itself was great, and everything appeared correct. However, when eating leftovers the next day, I discovered chicken in the coconut vegetable curry (see pictures).

At this point, this isn’t just a minor oversight—it’s a pattern. For a restaurant that offers vegan options and accommodates dietary needs, these kinds of errors are serious and can make guests very ill.

I want to be clear: the food is genuinely good, and the staff has always been kind and responsive in the moment. But there are clear breakdowns in communication between front-of-house and kitchen, and/or in food prep protocols.

Management needs to urgently review and tighten their allergy and dietary procedures. This includes:

* Clear labeling and verification of dishes
* Stronger communication between servers and kitchen staff
* Cross-contamination and ingredient checks

We’ve loved this place and truly hope they address this—because right now, it’s not safe for anyone with allergies or strict dietary restrictions.

Noe Indian Cuisine|4166 24th St, San Fra.text: Me and my friends (group of 5) went to this restaurant for dinner yesterday. Food was perfectly delicious and authentic. We tried Shierly Temple, Palak Paneer,  Butter chicken, garlic naan and Basmati rice. We were welcomed by Papadum and they gave some sweets in desert as complimentry. Really enjoyed. Thank you guys for your awesome service.

Came here for a lovely Monday night dinner and and loved the food! Great value and tasty! Will definitely be planning to come here again! Enjoyed the vegetable pakora, papadum, lamb Rogan Josh and coconut vegetable curry. Subi was lovely and sweet to us!
Such a cute hole in the wall restaurant. Open late and had the best food. Ordered the garlic naan and butter chicken. 10/10 service and cozy atmosphere

The food was too good and the service was also great (Bibek)the server was doing his job so well, the environment in this restaurant is incredible and comfortable I’ll suggest you to go and check once I bet you gonn love their food , service and environment 🤟

The food was amazingly delicious I ordered momo chili chicken and garlic naan it was very delicious, naan were very crispy and big, staff was cooperative and friendly overall I had very good experience.

Diwali Indian Cuisine|1499 Valencia St,.text:
We found this place on a whim and had the most incredible meal at Diwali! The menu showcases a variety of dishes with something for everyone — beautifully crisp dosas, tons of flavorful vegetarian options, and plenty of delicious meat dishes for the carnivores in our group.

The dosa was the star for me: paper-thin, perfectly crispy, and served with chutneys and sambar that tasted unbelievably fresh. Every dish we tried was layered with spice and aroma without ever being overwhelming.

Service is warm and welcoming, and the staff really cared about explaining dishes and helping us choose. Portions are generous, prices are fair, and the atmosphere is cozy without feeling crowded. The owner, who is incredibly passionate about sharing the flavors she loves, is so kind and attentive.

Whether you’re craving comfort food, exploring new flavors, or dining with a mixed group of dietary preferences, this place nails it. We enjoyed it so much that we came back for my husband‘s birthday and tried some new things which all were a hit.

I love this place! I've tried a bunch of things from their menu (soups, veg/non-veg entrees, dosas) and they're all great. But my favorite is the gongura chicken biryani. I come in almost once a week just to have this. I come so much that the owner and staff know me and my order, even when I'm calling in a takeout order! And if they're reading this, they definitely know who I am 😁 I've been meaning to post a review for a long time but never had pictures to add because I'm always too excited to dig in!

A great place to dine in for Indian food. The masala dosa, vegetable Chettinad curry, and Vijayawada biryani were very tasty, and the gobhi manchurian was an absolute standout.

The paneer 65 was quite dry, and the chili naan was just okay in taste.

Quite a surprise. Wasn't expecting much given the very generic Indian name, and unusually high ratings on Yelp and Google. However, every single dish was tasty. The service was professional and the ambience, nice.

- Goat chukka dry: Mild spicy, goat was excellently cooked to the point of shredding
- Shrimp curry leaf masala: a little bland to our liking. Could be spiced up a lot with crushed black pepper, and shallow fried onions
- Egg dosa: nice texture, the egg is noticeable, batter felt fresh, chutneys and sambar were above average
- Spicy Bullet Naan: generous dose of butter, soft, and tasty

Street parking can be challenging to find based on time of the day / week.

Overall, works as an option to come back, but likely wouldn't go out of my way to repeat.

Ordered family style and ordered butter chicken, paneer tikka, with rice and garlic naan.

Loved the naan, super fluffy and crispy at the same time, everything came out piping hot and it was delicious. Pretty small place but decorated in a modern way so vibes were nice!

Rose Indian Cuisine|1330 9th Ave, San Fr.text: Best Indian restaurant ever! I’ve only been twice but the service is really great, the food is 10/10 i travel an hour just so i can either dine in or take out. They also have GLUTEN FREE Naan, which makes them stand out a lot because places don’t offer that. If you have an allergy, they also take that very serious as they can. This is definitely a SF gem and i recommend this to everyone, this establishment is clean too! I also took someone here and they loved it as much as me, very flavorful!

What a find!!! It was a true blessing to discover the kind people & indulging dishes at ROSE Indian, Inner Sunset. The service was attentive with strong attention to detail. The quality of the food was really great and not oily like other restaurants.
We LOVED the complimentary tea at the end of the meal. You'll be very well treated here. We cant wait to come back!

5 stars 5 stars 5 stars! I could say it x1000 this place is so good. The staff go above and beyond, my waiter Dennis and another gal whose name I didn’t catch were an absolute delight! The food? Don’t get me started. Absolutely fresh deliciousness. I highly recommend butter chicken, tikka masala veg, cumin rice, the MOMO!! And SAMOSAS!! The naan was so fresh and the size of an elephant ear haha. All so good. I couldn’t get enough!! Def will be back

This is my favorite Indian restaurant in town! The food is always fresh, flavorful, and consistently delicious. Every dish is packed with authentic taste, and the service is outstanding.

A special thank you to Dennis, who always provides exceptional service. He is friendly, attentive, and makes every visit enjoyable. It’s rare to find such great food and such welcoming hospitality in one place. I always leave happy and look forward to coming back. Highly recommend!

Stopped in for a meal whilst in SF and truly a 100/10 experience. We tried the Goat Biriyani, Pakora, Naan, Chicken Curry and the Tea and everything was AMAZING!!! The service was the definition of hospitality!! Definitely returning when back in town!

Kava Indian and Himalayan Cuisine|2151 L.text: One of the best Indian restaurants in town. The experience was good along with the service and food. I recommend

Love this place! We went there after Crissy Field and ordered Szechuan chicken, chicken momo, and biryani (food for two). They were all amazing — we finished everything. Such a great ambience, very hygienic, and our server, Sachina, was amazing. She made sure we were comfortable, and she always had a smile on her face. The food was reasonably priced too.
Excellent food and service! Highly recommended for amazing Indian food - it is solid.

Everything we ordered at Kava Indian Cuisine was really tasty and packed with flavor. The spices were just right, and you could tell the ingredients were fresh in every bite. The staff was super friendly and attentive, which made the whole experience even better. The restaurant was clean, cozy, and had a nice vibe that worked for both a casual night out and a special dinner. Kava Indian Cuisine is definitely one of the best Indian spots we’ve tried, and we’ll for sure be back.

We seek out great Indian food. This new restaurant on busy Lombard St caught our eye, and they had a menu that looked enticing. Momos, traditional Indian curries, various options like somosa chaat for appetizers.

Trust this reviewer... You will be very happy eating here! We are vegetarian and had Paneer tikka masala, saag paneer and naan/onion kulcha. Huge taste, super organic and savory flavors and spices. Will be back!!!

Kolapasi South Indian cuisine|3465 19th.text:
Authentic, unapologetic Indian restaurant with outstanding food and bold, intense flavors. Every dish had its own distinctive taste and texture, making the meal a great experience from start to finish. The service was prompt and friendly, and the ambience perfectly complements the overall experience.
The prices are very reasonable, which explains why the place is so popular and hyped, completely deserved. A cool spot with excellent value for money.

We absolutely love Kolapasi—it truly feels like family to us.

The food is consistently incredible—delicious, full of flavor, and so authentic to South Indian cooking. It means a lot to us, especially since it reminds my partner of home.

It’s also one of the few places in San Francisco where you can find this level of authentic South Indian cuisine, which makes it even more special.

The team is always warm and welcoming, and we’re so grateful to have this place in our neighborhood.

We’ll definitely keep coming back ❤️

We wanted to have authentic South-Indian food but couldn’t find any that we liked in SF. Saw Kolapasi’s menu and decided to try and it did not disappoint. We ordered Gongura Chicken Biryani & Vijayawada Mutton Biryani. Both were delicious and had generous servings. Also ordered Salted Lemon soda & Mango Lassi. Everything was very good. It is right at the intersection and not too busy surroundings so finding parking spots shouldn’t be a problem.

10/10 flavors, definitely recommend !!!

ghee dosa 5/5- best dosa I’ve ever had

viyajawai chicken biryani 5/5- best biryani we’ve had also! huge portion, flavor bomb, saucey

coastal masala shrimp 5/5- came out first and we devoured it so fast

paratha 5/5- I prefer this over naan

kerala chicken fry 3/5- wasn’t my fave
Sooooo good. Every dish we got was delectable. I will say the lamb dishes were spicyyyyy but were also spice wusses. Service was wonderfully warm, friendly and attentive. Will definitely be back!

Udupi Palace|1007 1/2 Valencia St, San F.text: 5 🌟 Rating from my experience. Its so amazing to find a Pure Vegetarian Restaurant in the Heart of "San Francisco". The taste of Food is just Amazing, Price is affordable and quantity is good. Everything we ordered tasted nice and staff are very helpful. Who ever the owners are just wanted to say keep up good work and thanks for operating a Pure Vegetarian restaurant which is hard to find in other cities around US.

So good and cheap! We even tried DoorDash the second time knowing the dosa won’t be crispy - but it hit the spot. Besides the dosa and uttapam we also love the samosa chaat and dahi vada!
The dosas are better than dosa corner imo

such a wonderful cozy and casual dinner spot!! this was my first time trying an uttapam, idli, and medu vada and they were all great. my photo is of my plate partly eaten because it looked so good I forgot to take a picture before digging in. they were busy on a Saturday night but the staff was very nice and attentive. we had a delightful time here it feels like a true gem of a restaurant. would absolutely recommend

Recently a friend and I had made lunch plans for the end of the year (we work together and we don't usually see each other again for months). She recommended Udupi Palace and I'm so glad she did- I've had Indian food before when I lived in LA but not since I moved back to the Bay. I'm pescatarian so a veggie friendly menu that is also budget friendly. WIN WIN!

We ended up inviting other colleagues and we were a group of 14 in total on a Monday afternoon. We called ahead of time to make sure they could accommodate us and it was no issue at all.

The ambience gives very casual and cozy.

The service was 10/10- Jacob was our server and although I didn't ask I almost felt like he could have been the manager or owner. He gave us full attention, made sure our water glasses were full and when I say as soon as we all ordered- the food was coming out piping hot and fresh! I was impressed.

Location: It's located in the Mission off Valencia- a short 10 minute walk from the BART station (24th/Mission). Lots of culture all around.

I took pics of everyone's food including mine:
Some our selections were the paper dosas (which is like a thin like crepe fried up)- they were huge in size...well very long. See the pics
A number of people ordered different types of dosa combos, we had veggie byrani and I had the lentil/veggie soup which was perfect on a chilly day.  One of the dosas came stuffed with potatoes and another combo had some sort of salsa on top and came with a mini fried donut of sorts. Great variety of options to choose from.

I would highly recommend checking this place out for great hospitality, good warm food served with care and a price menu that won't break the bank.

I see they have a location in DT Berkeley as well.

One other thing to mention- online it does mention if you are part of a large group (over 6 or 8)- they automatically charge gratuity. However, when our receipt came- that was not the case. We assumed it was added in and realized quickly it was not automatic so just check your receipt.

ENJOY!

I was recently in San Francisco for the Snowflake Summit 2026 and ended up eating breakfast at Udupi Palace three times during my trip. Everything about this place—the food, the environment, and the reasonable prices—is absolutely superb.
I tried a variety of their tiffin items over my visits, including the Tiffin Thali, idli, puri, and pongal. Everything was incredibly delicious and fresh, but my absolute favorite was the pongal paired with their sambar—it is a must-try!
If you are looking for authentic South Indian comfort food in SF, this is the spot. I’ll definitely be back the next time I'm in town. Highly recommended!

Namaste SF Indian Cuisine|2848 23rd St,.text: Great new addition to the neighborhood! Really nice ambiance and friendly service. Food was fresh and tasty. Loved the momos and avocado chaat for appetizers, and the lamb chops were excellent as well. Highly recommend!

Namaste SF Indian Cuisine is an ideal place to savor a delightful fusion of Indian and Napoleon cuisine. The ambiance is both relaxed and sophisticated, making it an excellent choice for a romantic date night or a gathering with friends and family. The staff were exceptional—polite, kind, welcoming, efficient, and knowledgeable about the menu. They were more than happy to recommend dishes and answer any questions we had. I highly recommend this restaurant!

Stumbled on this newly opened Indian restaurant and it did not dissappoint. The ambiance is very nice, but with a smaller neighborhood feel to it. Our server was very attentive to our every need.

Everything we ordered from the momos, to the royal chicken curry, and daal makhani were flavorful. We finished with the ras malai for dessert and it was also a hit. Note: we also tried the cilantro pesto naan, which was good, but not a standout.

We’ve been on the hunt for authentic Indian food in the city (hard to find!) and are stoked to have this new neighborhood gem. We’ll be back soon!

Huge win for the Mission that this Silicon Valley staple finally landed in San Francisco! Namaste Indian Cuisine brings a level of flavor depth that sets a new bar.

The menu is a fantastic mix of Indian and Nepalese classics. If you eat meat, you must try their goat—it’s incredibly tender and rich. Highly recommend the Malabar and Himalayan Goat Curry. My wife was also blown away by the vegetarian selection; it’s massive and clearly prepared with just as much heart as the meat dishes.

Don’t miss:
The lentil soup: great to start any meal on a crisp S.F. night. Hearty, earthy goodness.

The Momos: Super succulent and the perfect starter. The dipping sauce is next level.

The Goat: Any preparation they offer is top-tier.

The Drinks: They’ve curated a great wine/beer list, and their non-alcoholic cocktails are actually thoughtful and complex, not just sugary soda.

The ambiance is great for a date or a simple night out during the week but it’s the super inviting staff that really makes the experience. Every dish is layered with deep, authentic flavor. Highly recommend!

Beautiful new Indian fine dining spot in the Mission! Namaste has a modern, elegant ambience with thoughtful décor and a relaxing vibe. Loved their creative mocktails and the delicious food, especially the chicken momos, avocado chaat, pani puri, and crispy gobi Manchurian. Great beer selection too, with both draft and bottled options. A perfect place for anyone looking to enjoy upscale Indian flavors in San Francisco. Highly recommended.

Aaha Indian Cuisine|3316 17th St, San Fr.text:
Absolutely delicious! And that’s coming from someone who is very picky about south asian flavors. Anil at the restaurant was the kindest gentleman and made our experience a 10/10.
The pomfret fry, and the daal tardka are must trys!

Love this place in the mission. Got the chicken pepper fry and chicken chettinadu and chapati and chili garden naan. All was great, loved how many spiced and chopped onion are in both dish. Chili garlic naan is life changing, best naan I've ever had

Today I visited Aaha Indian Cuisine on 17th Mission, and the experience was amazing! We ordered the Pomfret Fish and Chicken Tikka Masala — both were absolutely delicious and highly recommended.

A special shout-out to Anil, who provided exceptional service. He was incredibly caring, attentive, and made sure we were comfortable throughout our visit. I truly appreciated his hospitality.

We highly recommend this place — definitely worth a visit! ⭐️🍽️
By far the best chicken biryani I have had in SF. Strong flavors, perfectly cooked, all I could ask for in this dish. I am super happy to have found this restaurant!

Today I ordered Green Chicken Fry and Egg Biryani, and honestly both dishes were very disappointing. I couldn’t even finish a small portion of the food.

The Green Chicken Fry had no salt and almost no flavor at all. It looked like a proper fry item with all the ingredients and appearance, but the taste was completely missing.

As for the Egg Biryani, the gravy/sauce used in it was not tasty and gave the entire dish an unpleasant flavor.

I’ve eaten at many restaurants before, and this is the first time I’ve had such a bad experience. Spending $38 on food like this was definitely not worth it. This is one of the worst places I’ve tried.

Trisara|211 Kearny St, San Francisco, CA.text:
Come in around 9pm on a Friday night, not a lot of people in the place and it was very easy to order. My food arrived fairly quickly as well.

Food was yummy, i had goat biryani, lamb curry and garlic naan. Each one was wonderful and complemented each other.

Service was great, the servers were pleasant and prompt with the orders.

Atmosphere is good, quiet and good for groups of people to have a chat in.

Oh. My. GOD! I was in San Francisco for a night with my best buds, and we were craving Indian food. We saw this place was near our hotel, so we thought we’d give it a try. I never once had food in my life that left me feeling the way I did after I left Trisara. I started out with the Mango Lassi for my drink, something I had never had before. It was SOOO creamy, thick, and sweet with mango. It was delicious! I then ordered the Chicken Tikka Masala, with a Garlic Naan, and Rice. I had never eaten Indian food before, but my buddies showed me how to properly eat it. The Naan, along with the Rice, and the Chicken Tikka Masala: it was an explosion of flavors! It felt like an experience in my mouth, that could have only been compared to an energetic music festival. I HIGHLY recommend this place, to ANYONE: residents of SF, or just some dudes that spent the night there for fun, who are looking for food that will leave their tongues buzzed with flavor, their stomachs full with warmth, and their senses pleased beyond what they could have ever dreamed of!

The food at Trisara Indo-Nepalese Restaurant was overall okay. The fried rice was good, but the noodles and Manchurian had a slightly sweet taste, which took away from the authentic flavor. The mango kulfi didn’t taste very fresh, which was a bit disappointing. On a positive note, the service was good and attentive. Overall, an average dining experience with room for improvement in authenticity and consistency.
Lovely, authentic food! The lamb biriyani was excellent and could easily feed 1-3 people, the portion was big! You can also amend the spice level. Would recommend

Super friendly service and delicious food! They will do non-spicy if you want, but will add the spice if you do. I stopped here randomly and will be back. They were also super quick with the food which I appreciated.

Sunset Indian Cuisine|642 Irving St, San.text:
Sunset Indian Restaurant, has taken over the former Naan N' Curry location, and the transformation is impressive. The interior has been thoughtfully updated with attractive décor, a carpeted dining room that softens the space, and the welcome addition of two bathrooms — a definite improvement for guests.

I especially appreciate that they now offer beer on tap, which pairs nicely with their menu. The wine selection is also solid, providing good options to complement the meal.

As for the food, the Lamb Biryani is easily a 5+ star dish. The rice is light, loose, and beautifully aromatic, with just the right level of spice. The addition of cashews adds a subtle richness and texture that really elevates the dish. I also thoroughly enjoyed the Palak Paneer, which was prepared with a creamy, well-balanced sauce and a generous amount of paneer.

An added treat was the plain naan — simple yet perfectly done — which complemented both dishes wonderfully and rounded out the meal.

Overall, Sunset Indian Restaurant is a strong addition to the neighborhood, offering flavorful dishes, thoughtful updates to the space, and a comfortable dining experience.

Sunset Indian Restaurant is a hidden gem that blends Indian and Nepali cuisines. I often finish work late and with little energy to cook, so knowing it stays open until midnight makes it my go-to for a satisfying, home-style meal.

Whenever I crave Indian flavors, I enjoy Saag Paneer — it's creamy and perfectly spiced. And when I feel like having some non-veg options, I usually go for chicken curry by a side of mixed vegetables. It always hits the spot!

They also serve an authentic Nepali menu, which adds a special touch. The Momo is delicious—soft, juicy, and truly satisfying.  Chicken Chowmein, It has a comforting, home-like taste. The noodles are nicely wok-tossed and enriched with Himalayan spices that give it a wonderfully smoky flavor. On top of the great food, the service is super friendly and the owner is genuinely open to suggestions and feedback, which says a lot about the care they put into this place. Keep up the great work, Sunset Indian Cuisine team — I'll definitely keep coming back for more!

Plus, try Nepali beer Barahsinghe, which tastes more like a German-style Pilsner.

Had a wonderful experience here! The Indian and Nepali dishes were full of authentic flavor and perfectly cooked. The MoMo was juicy, Staff were welcoming and the service was quick. The restaurant had a warm and comfortable atmosphere. One of the best places for Indian and Nepali food. Highly recommended for anyone who loves authentic South Asian cuisine!”

Very delicious and authentic! Wonderful and attentive crew, very clean, professional! We will be back! Highly recommend!!! The food was very delicious..

Food:5/ Service:5
Atmosphere:5
Noise level:Quiet, easy to talk….

Sunset Indian Cuisine on Irving Street in San Francisco is a welcoming spot for authentic and flavorful Indian dishes. The food is freshly prepared, with well-balanced spices and generous portions that stand out for both taste and quality. The restaurant is clean and casual, the service attentive, and the menu offers a great variety—perfect for both dine-in and takeout. The waiter here doesn’t understand English well, make sure you ask them to repeat the order before confirming.

Roti Indian Bistro|53 W Portal Ave, San.text: Really tasty warm spicy food for a rainy Sunday night in the city. My wife has celiac disease and they confirmed all the curries are gluten-free. We had the paneer jalfrezi and palak dal, both of which were fresh and scrumptious. Other people in our party had a variety of things, including the gobi special, which they all loved. Great for a big group, easy to access via Muni, what's not to love?

Great spot for date night on a lively street surrounded by other restaurants and nightlife. We made reservations and had a smooth, welcoming experience from start to finish. The service stood out, our waitress was friendly, attentive, and helped guide us through the menu.
We chose the pre-feast option, which was a fun way to sample multiple dishes in one sitting and is perfect if you want variety or are new to the menu.
Unfortunately, the food itself was underwhelming for my taste. I usually love bold Indian flavors, rich spices, and vibrant presentation, and the dishes here felt a bit muted and washed out. We requested one dish to be spicy, but it barely had any heat. I had high expectations based on the experience and setup, but the food didn’t quite deliver for me.

Excellent if you are craving for Indian food. Their special lunch combos are awesome. Have been to many Indian restaurants in the Bay. This was by far the best for combo choices offered. We ordered four different combos and all were finger licking good. One extra star for being open to serve all day long, unlike most who close in the afternoon. Great and polite staff too.

We have been waiting to try this place, as it’s always busy, so of course we assumed it was good. Yes, the food was good, but they really need to have more work on service. We called to say we would be ten minutes late to our reservation, but once seated saw no one except for menus and water for 20 minutes. A very nice and attentive younger waitress helped us promptly once I asked her about it, but she was clearly just helping cover whomever should have been there. We actually were unsure who our waitstaff was since there were several different people that attended to us. We then waited another almost 40 minutes for our food. As said the food was good, but we cannot recommend the naan bread. The owner checked on most tables and was kind and considerate, so that was a plus.
I’ll also mention another time I stopped in one afternoon to ask about ordering take out, but the two people in the back just looked at me and no one came out to talk, so after five minutes I left. Not sure what happened there.
They are very popular, which I’m glad for, but just wanted to leave an honest review from our experience. Also, it’s less noisy to sit outside.

Lunch combos were amazing
We had chicken korma , Chiken Makhni in the combo and it was delicious.
The Salad portion they gave with this combo was good too. Mango lassi can be better but no complaints
Their appetizer combo is a must try which has onion pakora, Samosa and papadum
Overall we had an amazing lunch experience here and would definitely recommend to try this place out.

Amber India Restaurant|25 Yerba Buena Ln.text: I came to this restaurant because I love Indian food. The chicken I ordered was sizzling hot when they brought it over to me. The service was very friendly an attentive to my needs. I highly recommended this place. I give them 5 stars ⭐️

The food at amber was extremely mediocre. What you are paying for is mostly location. However, the interior which was once nice is showing signs of dilapidation.

My review is for the buffet, but I would think it translates to ala carte on the matter of food quality.

The naan and roti were both puffy and doughy. This is a problem in many of the North Indian restraunts the bay area. My go-to place for good naan is bundu khan! Go there!

The buffet offered 5-6 vegetarian dishes of which a couple were barely Indian like sauteed broccoli and corn.

There were 4 non vegetarian dishes- butter chicken, mutton and fish. There was also tandoori chicken. The tandoori chicken was again not juicy and did not have the smokiness or charring you'd expect from a tandoor. Go to bundu khan, waah ji waah or kuku da dhaba for your kebabs instead. You'll thank me later!

The chicken in the butter chicken was overcooked and the gravy was excessive sweet. Similar issue with the mutton. The only meat that was not overcooked was fish but for a tawa fish it was lacking in flavor. You are better off going to zareens or pakwan for curries.

The desert was the only thing that was remotely tasty. The gualb jamun and barfi were ok but nothing to write home about.

The service was the only thing that was good. It was prompt and attentive.

We had the Jain ala carte menu which had surprisingly enough variety to pick and choose. Fried palak starter was the best item of the night.
Desserts- Gulab Jamun and Kulfi were tasty 😋Rotis and lacha pratas were dry 🤢and lacked buttet/ghee toasting. Servers were quick to refill glasses, clear cutlery, offered fresh plates and packed leftovers— above and beyond! 🥇

Exceptional and authentic Indian food prepared with fresh ingredients and presented in a highly professional manner.  The service was amazing.  Large vegetarian options and the dishes were innovative with great flavor and taste.
Bougiest Indian food I have had. Excellent service. Great flavors and texture. Ambience was fantastic as well.

Shalimar Restaurant|532 Jones St, San Fr.text:
This no-frills Pakistani eatery in the Tenderloin is open late (till 11 PM on the Tuesday night I stopped by for a quick bite). It has the standards including tandoori meats and naan breads as well as curries (meat and vegetarian). The chai tea hits the spot on a cold night too.

I ordered at the counter, and one of the waiters brought the food to my table. There were lots of single diners (all men, as were the staff this evening) on when I stopped by at 10:30 PM on a Tuesday night.

Not fancy, but filling, tasty, authentic (I haven’t been to Pakistan, but it remembers me of many eateries in North India), and reasonably priced.

There are two other locations as well, though I’ve only visited this one. My partner and I used to eat at this spot two decades ago when one of us lived in Chinatown, and I’m glad to see that this place is still serving up hot Pakistani food and chai!

They take card payments including Apple Pay. A saag gosht (spinach lamb curry) with naan bread, chawal (a rice pilaf), raita (lightly spiced yogurt), and a cup of sweet hot chai cost me about $25 with tax but before tip.

I’d come back when in the Tenderloin, especially when looking for something hot to eat late at night or craving Indian/Pakistani cuisine.

I just visited Shalimar restaurant and ordered Nihari with one naan. Honestly, this was one of the worst Nihari and naan I’ve ever had. The naan was poorly made (almost like cold naan) and not enjoyable at all. On top of that, the cashier was extremely rude, which made the whole experience even worse. I definitely do not recommend Shalimar restaurant for Nihari and naan. Save your time and money and try somewhere else.

The food is great. It's one of the few Pakistani BBQ spots that I've seen. They have authentic tandoori ovens,  and you will smell like BBQ smoke when you leave. Everything is spicy. Great flavor. It is in the tenderloin area, so do with that info as you will. The food is 5 stars for Indian/ Pakistani food (they serve both).
Fantastic food the flavors really stand out.  And plus they have a very tasty Chai.

Yoooooo! Im writing this as i clean curry from my teeth. Wow. I didn’t know i needed this meal but it blessed me. The food was HOT, and very tasty with the right amount of spice. Amazing, will be thinking about this for a long time!

Keeva Indian Kitchen|908 Clement St, San.text:
An amazing Indian restaurant.

Since step into the restaurant
The owner so energetic and funny
She was so nice and lot of sense of humor

My husband and I get a lot of food
We order Samosa to start

And our Entree is LAMB SAAG

BUTTER CHICKEN (the signature dish on the menu).  for myself I don’t like yogurt that much but this dish is a perfect curry you must try!

PRAWN BIRYANI
It’s like Basmati fried rice Indian style thats full of flavour and has lots of herb. It had lots fried garlic on top which made the dish so special

Special dish of the night is PALAK CHAAT
Chickpea batter fried with baby spinach with mint sauce. My husband loved this dish and so did I!

CHILLI CHEESE NAAN
Unbelievable!!!!

Great service
Quick food

This was my first time trying Indian food and I absolutely loved it.
Everything was amazing — the food, the energy, and the warm reception from the staff.

The flavors were rich, fresh, and perfectly balanced.
You can really feel the care and passion behind every dish.

The atmosphere was welcoming and comfortable, and the service made the experience even better.

10/10 — I will definitely be coming back.
Highly recommended.

This is by far the best Indian food in the city. It’s so authentic and home made. Rita is the owner and it’s her family recipes. When you visit you feel like a guest in her world. The food is amazing. She even has things that aren’t on the menu that she will make special. My kids love it and my wife and I love it. When my son comes to town from college it is his first ask of where to eat.

We stumbled upon Keeva while walking along Clement today. We loved every single thing we tried and the owner was so welcoming. Thank you!!! I’m so glad to finally have a favorite Indian restaurant in SF 😭😭😭

Amazing hidden gem in the Richmond!
The food here is absolutely outstanding — every dish was fresh, vibrant, and perfectly spiced. You can really taste the quality of the ingredients and the care put into each plate. The flavors were rich but balanced, and nothing felt heavy or overly oily like at some other places. Every bite was satisfying and left us wanting to try even more. We came to celebrate a friend’s birthday, and she brought her toddler along. The owners were incredibly warm and accommodating, even bringing out a book to help keep the little one entertained. The service had a wonderful, family-friendly vibe that made the whole experience even more special. A cozy spot with amazing food — we’ll definitely be back!

Pakwan Restaurant|3182 16th St, San Fran.text: Their mutton Biryani was very good however, the rice were a little less serving. Chana dal was very authentic and pure Pakistani style. I recommend for Takeaway since the ambience of the restaurant is average.

We visited on December 24th after reading so many rave reviews, and Pakwan completely delivered. The consensus is right—this is some of the most flavorful food I’ve had in many years. Also, it is halal.

We ordered Goat Biryani, BBQ Chicken Legs and Breasts, Chicken Shikh Kebab, Chicken Boti Kebab, Garlic Naan, Cheese Naan, and Plain Naan. The goat biryani came in a huge portion—definitely enough for two people to share. It was incredibly aromatic, with perfectly cooked rice and tender meat. The Chicken Shikh Kebab was okay, nothing extraordinary. The Chicken Boti Kebab was good, but for me, the BBQ chicken was the star of the show: very flavorful, moist, and tender, checking all the boxes for truly great food. All the naans were fresh, fluffy, and delicious.

The service was efficient, and the food came out quickly. As many have noted, it’s a no-frills, casual spot with an average ambiance, clearly focused on fantastic food and great value. There are plenty of seating arrangements available, suitable for both small and large groups, and portions are generous for the price.

It can get very busy and loud during peak dinner hours, but that’s a testament to how good it is. This is now my go-to spot for a satisfying, flavorful meal in San Francisco.

Highly recommended!

Got Uber eats delivery.
Very quick to deliver.  Like 20 minutes!
Stellar authentic Pakistani food flavor

A little extra greasy though

Loved - must do again

* Nihari
* Palak Ghosht Mutton
* Naan

We love Pakwan. I mostly order to go and I am always happy with the quality of food and the consistency. I like their chicken tikka masala with garlic naan, and also the tandoori chicken leg.
I wasn’t the fan of seekh kebab, but overall a good experience.

Food came out quickly, the naan is very good and freshly made, super buttery. The curries all have distinct flavors. The place is self serve so get your own everything from the front, but they do clear the table for you after you finish.

Dumpling Story - Valencia|694 Valencia S.text: The food was ok. I feel like it gets high rating because of you give it 5 stars then you get free items so beware. The dried garlic noodles was bland and over cooked with no flavor. The soup dumplings was super fatty. The best thing was probably the string beans but it was a slightly over salted. I wouldnt come back again. Definitely a one time experience.

Everything was excellent! The soup dumplings were perfect- dough was amazing. The pan friend soup dumplings were so incredibly tasty. And the curry lamb steamed dumplings… man, so good. Idk what else to say, great overall!!

Without intending to, I ended up at this place to enjoy my New Year's lunch. I had an enjoyable experience.

The staff are friendly, and although it looks small from the outside, it's pretty spacious, clean, and tidy inside. Also, you can see the kitchen in the back and watch your order being prepared.

I can say that the dishes' flavors and seasonings are on point, and they have a good selection of vegetarian options at affordable prices.

If you're in the area, you definitely have to try this place.

The best dumpling spot (IMO) in SanFran!
Found this gem on Google Maps and it was such an AMAZING experience.

EVERYTHING-EVERYTHING on this menu was flavorful, cooked to order, and fresh!

I had the Dan Dan noodles, green beans, fried beef dumpling and boiled pork dumplings- ALL OF WHICH WERE AMAZIIIIIING!!!!

I will post pics as well, but trust…. You need this experience in your life!

The staff was super friendly and helpful (especially my waitress) in narrowing down my choices as I came alone… but so glad I came!

Highly recommend- can’t say much else about this place because I was so wrapped up in the food- there could have a flame throwing monkey bar tending and I would have never noticed!

Trust me- go and check them out, you’ll thank me later!

Simply sublime
When a steamed dumpling oozes richness and provides instant dopamine, you know you’re in for a treat.
The noodles had a freshness and texture that one can only dreams about eating again.
Highly recommend ~

Dumpling Zone|408 Dewey Blvd, San Franci.text: Literally some of the best XLB i might have ever had. We ordered the crab and pork xiao long bao , pork bao , chili oil noodles and green onion pancakes. Everything was extremely flavorful and perfectly cooked. I cant wait to go back!! No wait on a weekday.

I like the pork and cabbage dumpling and pan fried pork buns. I couldn't believe how much soup was in there and the outside stay so crispy! I really like the crispy texture. The green onion pancakes were good but really oily.

I came here for a Tuesday afternoon relaxed lunch. I got a spicy fish noodle soup - which was decently spicy but I felt the taste wasn’t   extraordinary to give it a 5 star rating . So giving it 4 stars for the food.

The custard lava bun was very delicious and deserves a 5 ⭐️

I just back from Taiwan and was craving red braised beef noodle soup. This was better than most of the ones I had in Taiwan! Flavorful broth, chewy noodles, and tender meat. Highly recommend. I’ve already ordered it for delivery a few times and recommended it to a few friends.

On Saturday around 7 PM, we stopped by Dumpling Zone after reading solid reviews online. The wait for our party of five was about 30 minutes, but parking was easy since it’s located in a residential part of Midtown.

The restaurant is small—maybe a dozen tables—and was packed with families and a large birthday parties. It’s a casual spot with one main server and one runner. You can order food online or directly through a staff member, and they’ll bring everything to your table.

We decided to try all the most recommended dishes from the review sites and the photos displayed on their window—and we were so glad we did. Every dish we ordered was delicious. Prices were very reasonable—actually more affordable than many other dumpling places we’ve tried, probably because of its unassuming location. Portions were generous too, which made it feel like a great deal overall.

The vibe is super casual—nothing fancy—but the service, while basic, came with a smile. We really appreciated the personality of our server; he was friendly, attentive, and made sure we were updated on our table status. He was clearly keeping an eye on everything and even watched for when guests were wrapping up so he could manage the waitlist efficiently. No reservations are taken, but he kept names on a handwritten list.

We were so stuffed from our meal that we didn’t make it to dessert, but we’ll definitely be back to try more items next time we’re in town. Highly recommend!

Yuanbao Jiaozi 元寶餃子|2110 Irving St, San.text: Their house special beef noodle soup was delicious.  Brisket was very tender, noodles are springy and hold the soup great, the broth is very flavorful.  The pork, shrimp, chive and egg dupmplings were also very good.  Nice thin skin, great filling.  At 3pm, no problem getting a table for one.  You will also get your food very fast.

I haven’t visited this local gem for almost a year since my spouse doesn’t eat beef and he has been constantly having home cooked dumplings for lunch. I was constantly craving for the spicy pickled beef noodle soup and their seaweed salad so I came on a Saturday morning, waiting for them to open so I can skip the line.
