I was disappointed to find that they have taken the seaweed salad off their menu but the beef noodle soup was still promising like it used to. The line started forming oriented door at 11:45 pm and my husband wanted to speed finishing his food and leave cus he felt pressured to make all the guests waiting outside. For people who want to skip the line and wait, I suggest going to the one in San Mateo since there will be more seatings available in a large storefront.
Hidden gem. The dumplings were yum! The salads were so flavorful I would come back just for that. Highly recommend SO YUM

The restaurant is very famous for its dumplings and is recommended as one of the best in San Francisco in their cuisine, so I decided to try out. They usually are very busy in their small place, so be prepared to wait for a few minutes for a table.  Their service was OK, given that they were in a rush to close the restaurant when I got there. Talking about their food. It’s very good. The dumplings are very well-made and their beef noodles are also worth trying. So overall good Chinese food, but the experience of eating there requires some patience so be prepared.

That was an exceptional place to eat the freshest dumplings you will ever eat! The flavor of the soup is outstanding and the ladies work endless to provide the tastiest food there is for fresh homemade dishes!

Dumpling Time Design District|11 Divisio.text:
Pretty good soup dumplings, friendly service and nice outdoor location with a covered patio.

Got the wood ear mushroom salad and the 10pc combo. Salad was great. On the dumplings: tom yum, pork, pho are all excellent. The wagyu was also fine. Great if you enjoy heavy mushroom flavour, but pretty regular otherwise. The kimchi jigae was the only one that fell flat.

Overall, it was a fun, colourful meal!

I don't live in the city but I've had to come up here every day because my wife had been transferred to a hospital in San Francisco. She went through a spell of being terribly nauseaus and only certain foods could be kept down. She's a fan of dumplings so we found this place on door dash and tried it.

The Wagyu XLB is one of the best things I've ever eaten. And no that's not hyperbole.Everything we had from this place was great but those specific dumplings were amazing.

My wife also was a big fan. She had the vegetable dumplings and not only was able to keep it down with her nausea, but would also get it again and she is a picky eater.

The worst thing we ate there is something I would descriebe as "good but I don't need it again." I won't name the item because it really wasn't bad at all, it was just outshined by everything else.

I have since recommened thist o a few different doctors and nurses at her hospital and two have reported back to us that they loved it as well. In fact one nurse's wife now wants Dumpling Time at least once a week.

When I die, if the Wagyu XLB is the last thing I eat, it would not be a wasted life.

If you ever wanted to try soup dumplings, look no further. I was new to this cuisine and this place had the perfect offerings. We tried a few things on the menu and they were all amazing. Their outdoor patio is great where you can spend some quality time with your friends/family.

Service was fantastic, did I say service was good.  In todays world, in the service world, service is what counts most.  Then comes the so many different chioces of dumplings, lamb, beef, pork and fish, it was a party eating there.  Loved it and will be back and they are reasonable on the pricing.

We enjoy dining at Dumpling Time.  Their food tastes good, my favorites are soup dumplings and string green beans.  You ought to try the super sized soup dumpling once, you drink the soup inside with a straw which is fun.

Dumpling Home|298 Gough St, San Francisc.text:
Best dumplings I’ve ever had!! Such good food!
We had the pork xiao long bao, pan-fried juicy pork bao (my favorite) and the dry-fried chicken wings. That was perfect amount of food for 2 people

Nice outdoor seating which was covered
Pretty popular spot. We waited for about 20min for a table

Highly recommend this spot!

Michelin 2025! The flavors were amazing. Had the Fresh cucumber with garlic sauce (just wanted to eat the whole plate, couldn’t stop eating them), pan fried dumplings (crispy on the bottom and steamed on top; different crunch profile with good flavor), soup dumplings were very good, had nice big red spoon to eat the dumplings, they a soy sauce, ginger, vinegar dipping sauce. Overall, it was amazingly good. We were able to come and had no wait for a party of 4.

Dumpling Home remains one of the premier destinations for authentic dumplings in San Francisco, offering a high-flavor experience that justifies its popularity. While you should expect a wait time of around 60 minutes, the kitchen’s efficiency is unmatched; once seated, your order arrives incredibly fast. The Juicy Chicken Bao is the undisputed star of the menu, delivering a literal burst of savory broth and intense flavor in every bite, while the Mongolian Beef Dumplings are equally impressive and packed with seasoning. Pair these with the solid Sesame Paste Noodles for a well-rounded meal, and be sure to utilize a pro strategy: add yourself to the Yelp Waitlist in advance and always ask for a side of their signature chili sauce to elevate the heat.

Only order their dumplings, don't order the other dishes.

We ordered the Dan Dan noodles, the Juicy Beef Bao, the Beef Xiao Long Bao, the Season Beef Pockets, Green Beans, Green onion pancakes, and the vegetable bao.

Worst order: DO NOT ORDER THE DAN DAN NOODLES. They came out cold so it was basically inedible, and there was very little sauce and seasoning so it was extremely bland. IT was defintely the worst dan dan noodles I've ever had. I was warned by a friend to not order their dan dan noodles because they also came out cold and bland when she ordered it previously, but I was assuming that was a one off instance. But it seems like their noodles are always cold and tasteless.

Best order: The Juicy Beef Bao was really really good. The soup inside of the bao is really rich and flavorful. The bottom of the bao is pan fried and the top is steamed so each bite is a really satisfying texture. If I came again, which I probably won't, I'd definitely order this again.

Their beef xiao long baos were also pretty good. The soup inside was also tasty.

The Green beans also came out pretty cold, so I wasn't a fan. The taste and texture was good but any dish being cold ruins it.

The vegetable dumplings were also pretty yummy! I will have tp say they get bonus points for having many vegetarian friendly options (I'm not vegetarian but my friends are). The green onion pancake were alright.

Service etc:
We came on Sunday 12pm and had to wait 20min for a table because it was pretty packed. The food came out pretty quickly but that's because the food was cold already, so I would've preferred if they took longer with the food if it was warmer. All in all, I probably wouldn't come to this spot again and would keep looking for a better dumpling restaurant. But if I came again I would only order the dumplings and get none of the side dishes.

Steamed dumplings (Xiao long bao) are amazing. We ordered one spicy and one squash/shrimp.
Pan fired dumplings are not the best in Bay Area, I’ll give a 4.
A localized dumpling place in general. Good vibe and atmosphere.

HK Lounge Bistro|1136 Folsom St, San Fra.text:
The dim sum here was pretty good. Every dish was prepared well and tasted fresh. I'm not sure why the restaurant's ratings are so high though. While the food was fine, the prices were exorbitant, especially given the small portions.

Perhaps the greatest offender was the $18 plate of black and pepper pork spare ribs. What we got were 8 little pieces of deep fried pork that were mostly just bones.

The soup dumplings were very average. The wrapper was too thick and the meat filling wasn't terribly flavorful.

The lotus-wrapped sticky rice was also very average. The small piece of pork in the middle was tasty, but the sticky rice lacked any real fragrance or flavor.

The truffle salt shrimp toast was our favorite dish.

Service was great - attentive and friendly .

The restaurant is very small inside and can get quite noisy. The location is strange, in a non-descript, somewhat dodgy part of Soma.

Great gem in the city. Service was top notch and the intimate setting is great for parties of 4 so enjoy a small gathering. The food I had was also was some of the best I've had in SF. The crab was fresh, dumplings delicious and pork belly so succulent. We also ordered a plate of shrimp noodles, but that wasn't the best. (too buttery)

Oh damn, this place was so delicious. Came here for lunch and had the pan fried noodles, shrimp crepe rolls and the eggplant was AMAZING. Also had the egg custard and I’m like damn I wish I lived in San Fran. I will just have to make the trip from Seattle more often! 😌

Wwnt to this place after seeing the good reviews. The crystal skin on the shrimp dumplings and rice noodle roles are really good and chewy. Those are the 2 dishes I'd recommend. The rest are just ok. For e.g., there is absolutely no reason why they should be charging $14 for siumai when it is typicalylly $7 to $8 in other dim sum restaurants and it taste just average.
ugh preorder their live fish. it’s so fresh & silky smooth. this city is riddled with sichuan restaurants, so a cantonese spot that does everything perfect is like a diamond in the rough.

Dragon Beaux|5700 Geary Blvd, San Franci.text: Great dim sum, great menu with lots of dishes we are interested in. We tried the special dish the soy sauce frog meat and the Malay (馬拉盞) okra, and recommend both. The lunch menu has attractive pricing.  We came on a Friday and found a good parking space in the parking lot right next to the restaurant. There are dishes we would like to try for our next visit.

This was such an excellent Dim sum restaurant. We came here to celebrate Mother’s Day and it was packed but we had a great table from a reservation. Everything we ate was amazing and where we originally thought we’d over-ordered it turned out we couldn’t stop eating.
The dumplings were great, the daikon radish cakes were also superb. We ordered a Peking duck which I thought was excellent. The soup dumplings and the walnut shrimp stood out to me as the bests.
Highly recommend this spot for any occasion

This has been my favorite dim sum restaurant in the city in the past 10 years.

The food was always super fresh, and flavorful. You can taste it in the shrimps, the fish, and everything you ordered here. With their top notch quality and i really mean it, their price is very fair and reasonable. They never tried too hard to become those ridiculous crazy expensive upscale restaurants like those in downtown either , even though their food is much better.

My pride restaurant to take visiting friends, and family here any chance i could. Make sure you have a reservation ahead of time.

The food was hit or miss. The grouper was good but was not cleanly deboned. The chow fun lacked seasoning. The soup dumplings were tasty. As was the squid.

The service wasn’t great. Our servers did their job but they were neither attentive nor friendly.

The ambiance wasn’t bad but it wasn’t particularly good. There were workers trying to fix something on the side. We were seated by the entrance so it was quite chilly.

Probably won’t return.
Went here for Dim Sum.  This place offered some dim sum i have never had before. Quality and taste was very good. Even the tea was delicious. Some pics we started to eat before i could snap.

Nan hot pot SF|501 Broadway, San Francis.text:
Absolutely one of the best hotpot experiences I’ve had. The broth was rich and flavorful, especially the signature spicy base — perfectly balanced with depth and aroma without being overwhelming. The ingredients were incredibly fresh, from the thinly sliced meats to the seafood and vegetables. You can really taste the quality.

The service was attentive and welcoming, and the staff made sure our soup was refilled and everything was taken care of throughout the meal. The atmosphere was lively but comfortable, great for gathering with friends or family.

If you’re looking for authentic hotpot with premium ingredients and great service, this place definitely delivers. Can’t wait to come back again.

Had such a fun experience at Nan Hot Pot in San Francisco! If you’re looking for one of the best hot pot spots in the city with authentic flavors, modern ambiance, and amazing service, this place is definitely worth visiting. The restaurant has such a cozy and trendy vibe with warm lighting, modern decor, and a fun interactive dining experience that makes it perfect for date nights, group dinners, or even a casual foodie outing in San Francisco.

The food quality was incredible from start to finish. Everything tasted super fresh, flavorful, and high quality. We loved all the different broth and topping options, and the sauce bar lets you customize everything exactly how you like it. The meats were tender, the vegetables fresh, and every bite was so comforting and delicious. Their desserts and soft serve were also such a fun sweet ending after the hot pot experience.

The staff was extremely friendly, attentive, and welcoming throughout the whole visit. They were helpful explaining the menu, quick with service, and constantly making sure everything was perfect at the table. You can really tell they care about creating a great dining experience for guests.

Overall, Nan Hot Pot is a must-visit restaurant in San Francisco for anyone craving authentic hot pot, delicious fresh ingredients, excellent customer service, and a beautiful modern ambiance. Highly recommend for food lovers looking for a unique and memorable dining experience in SF!
So fun!! They just opened and are a modern, authentic hot pot. The food is really high quality. Best hot pot in the city for sure!

Loved this place! The food was fresh, the soup base was amazing, and there were tons of options to choose from. Service was quick and friendly, and the vibe was really nice. I’ll definitely be coming back!
Such a great hotpot spot! The staff here are genuinely so nice and welcoming. The food was delicious and fresh, and the atmosphere felt really cozy. Highly recommend!

QUACK HOUSE aka Hing Lung Company|927 Po.text:
We ordered takeout and the food was ready right when we arrived, which we appreciated. The service was smooth and efficient — no complaints there.

The portions were lit of rice, smaller portion of meet and equal veggie. fAt about $50 for two plates, it felt a bit pricey for what you get, but that may just be the reality of San Francisco.

Taste is subjective, everyone’s preferences are different. Overall, solid service and a convenient takeout experience.

Uber eats - the poached chicken is dry and over done,
Shockingly bad.
The roast duck tastes OK except it’s literally swimming in its oil.
This place is not ready.

There are four chairs and no public restroom if you want to eat here. $24 for combo duck/chicken noodles. My friend had the bbq pork noodles. Both of us thought our dishes were way too salty and actually had to stop eating it. Portions are big. They should offer a half order for half the price and not use too much salt. I’ve been to Go Duck Yourself. Menu similar and didn’t have salt issues there.

Excellent Roasted Duck over Rice Plates to go.   Good prices.   Pickup, Devlivery only.   If you find better Roasted Duck I the city, Please Tell Me about it, but I doubt you will find better duck.    I loved their old location and I was miserable for 2 years when their old location closed before they opened this new location.   If you like Duck, you will like Quack House!!!

Inflation sucks!

Bought 3 meat rice for 23 bucks.

Cost only $13 two years ago.

That's 10 bucks increase!

Ordered pork belly, Roast duck and char siew. Came with rice and veggie and 4 sauces.

Roast duck was good. Char siew was yums.

But for USD23 bucks......a bit pricey...

Maybe just roast duck or 2 meat rice next time...can forgo the pork belly.

Maybe Hing Lung should put up the prices for 1 meat, 2 meat and 3 meat rice next time, like they used to do. Just a suggestion.

bao|590 Valencia St, San Francisco, CA 9.text: Such a great dim sum spot—I’ll definitely be back. The chicken pot stickers were a standout, super flavorful with the perfect crisp, and the fried sesame balls were easily my favorite. Portions are generous, so you honestly don’t need to order as much as you think. Overall, really good dim sum and great value. Just make sure to come early—it gets busy fast!
The baos were amazing!!!! Not a fan of the coconut bunnies for dessert. They were coconut jello basically and a weird texture. Glad we tried it though!

soooo so yummy with lots of veg options. got the veg xioa long bao, shanghai xioa long bao, steamed veg bao, stir fried green beans, kung pao veg. these are my fav green beans ever, just wish the portion was bigger

We ended up at bao unexpectedly and ended up having a scrumptious feast. Everyone wanted to try out different dishes so we ended up getting pretty much everything we wanted.

None of the dishes were disappointing. Some bao and dim sum plates were cooked with original Chinese seasoning, which may be an oddity for people who are unfamiliar with them.

This was a great find as you also get a ton of value in terms of quantity and different unique dishes to try out.
WOW!  What a Wonderful Experience!  The Soup Dumplings, the Bao, the Noodles and Amazing Vegetable options too...You can taste the Homemade Freshness in Every Bite.  A MUST Try!!!

Mama Ji's|4416 18th St, San Francisco, C.text: Came here with modest expectations but was very pleasantly surprised. All the dishes were packed with flavor and they did not hold back on the spice. The fish fillet was probably our favorite but everything was excellent and authentic. Will be back for sure.

Classic chinese dishes but done really well. A few elevated dishes you dont normally see. A table of 9 coworkers really enjoyed almost everything we ordered. Good veg and tofu options. Meat eaters enjoyed themselves too

I haven't visited Mama Jis in some time but gee Mama Jis you must have left the building . What happened? This place used to serve up tons of good Chinese dishes but my experience  was everything but. The authenticity of the dishes wasn't there and I don't think that anybody was doing quality control in the kitchen.  Every dish show cased lots of bell pepper, onion and too much garlic. Even the simple stuff, aka the rice was off. It was dry and under cooked.   The greens with garlic was almost the highlight.  Whereas the lotus root was drowned in a garlic chilli sauce, not a bad idea but the garlic overpowered the chili and it kinda resemble a hot mess.  The cumin lamb featured more bell pepper than lamb and tasted almost the same as the Wowo Tou besides the hint of cumin.  Bummer as there aren't tons of Chinese restaurants in the Castro to start with.   Oh well let's just hope that Mama Jis finds her way back from her stroll.
I tried the Triple C tofu after reading reviews. It was really flavorful and delicious but just a bit too sweet for my taste. I will be going back to try other stuff!

Mama Ji's is a great place in San Francisco if you’re craving authentic Sichuan flavors. The dishes are bold, spicy, and full of flavor without being overwhelming.

I tried a couple of their classic Sichuan dishes and was impressed by how well balanced everything was. The food tasted fresh, and the spices really stood out in a good way. The restaurant itself is small and cozy, which gives it a nice neighborhood feel.

Service was friendly and the atmosphere relaxed. Overall, it’s a fantastic spot for flavorful Chinese comfort food in the city

Dim The Way|1109 Ocean Ave, San Francisc.text:
Is my first time here.  The ambience is more South Florida key west with the color schema and vibe but is definitely a local Cantonese spot.  We’ve tried the beef stew noodles with wontons, the pork congee, and the steamed rice rolls.  All were great with the rice rolls being a “great find” even better than some sit-down fancy dim sum places anywhere.  The texture of the rice rolls were perfect: fresh made hot, with cilantro and spring onions, and plenty of fresh shrimps.  We’ve also had the steamed beef rice rolls-just as good.
The beef stew noodles with wonton were also great.

Best price!!!!!
I really like this place!
The foods is very delicious and not too salty.
The staff is very friendly and they work hard.
The shop is clean and cute. It is a great place to go with friends. I will come back again!

2026 update: Dim The Way was busy at noon on a Sunday. I had to take my food to go as there were no place to sit. Wonton noodles remain excellent. It is nice to see a family owned restaurant busy. The chef is the dad, his children are waiting on tables.

Updated review: Heads up, for the rice rolls, there are two free toppings available out of three. Pick from lettuce, green onion or coriander/cilantro. The toppings however, don't make it less plain as it is a plain dish. Same with the +$1 egg option. It tastes the same as before for my pork rice roll $6.80 +$1 egg without the options in a previous visit. At least it looks better. For the bbq pork wonton lo mein $13, this was OK as in my previous review. Service was meh. I was the only customer at 7pm. Didn't get a tea refill or perhaps I should have asked. The food came out fast.

Original review: No more 21 Taste House. Their pork shoulder ramen will be missed. I asked the new owner if Susan opened another restaurant somewhere and the answer is no.

Dim The Way is a daughter and father team. The food was OK. The beef stew lo mein $13 had OK flavors. That sauce they used made it OK. The noodles were too soft. Good lo mein noodles tend to be more chewy and bouncier. The chef cut the beef stew onto smaller than usual pieces. Four were meat pieces and the rest were tendons. The minced pork cheung fun $6.80 was a plain dish. The young daughter waitress will ask if you want chopped green onion with your rice roll. The soy sauce they used weren't the sweet ones normally found at dim sum restaurants. My suggestion is to add more flavor by using a sweet soy sauce with sesame oil to add some aroma to this dish.

Things I loved about Dim The Way are: No annoying music. Food came out fast. Simple menu. Free hot tea was fragrant. Clean. Genuinely nice people. Chef speaks Cantonese and Mandarin. House branded plates. Good job team at Dim The Way.

Ordered takeout from here and it was really good! The rice rolls were super tasty and smooth, and the soy sauce was perfectly seasoned—so flavorful and well-balanced. I also loved their taro dumplings， soft taro filling. Definitely worth trying. Will order again!

The rice rolls seems like their signature dish and it did not disappoint. The noodle soup is also big for its price tho the broth lacked a bit of flavor. We were already full by the time we got our shumai so wasn't able to enjoy it at the restaurant but reheating at home wasn't a problem. Overall, Dim the Way is a great addition to the neighborhood and I hope they keep their prices low.

Henry's Hunan Restaurant|1708 Church St,.text:
From time to time, I visit this restaurant and I am always delighted that they remain in business.

Maybe it's because I'm a longtime San Francisco resident and this brand has been around a long time. I started eating Hunan province style Chinese food back when it was in the Chinatown/Barbary Coast locations.

They've been true to their original recipes going back decades, not succumbing to latest trends. And so, part of the allure for me regarding my fondness for this place is its continuity and consistency from the past.

Yes, the food can range from "not-hot" to raging inferno, so be careful how you order your spiciness level. Their portions are generous, but they are also on the pricey side for Chinese restaurants.

However, they are located in a section of town that's not hurting and pricing reflects that. While I do praise them for keeping consistent in their menu through all these years, they can be a bit of a hit & miss from time to time. Sometimes they're great and at times, they miss the mark, I'd have to say.

All in all, it's a great place to support.

This is definitely one of the best Chinese (Hunan) restaurants we've ever been to in the San Francisco Bay Area. A lot of Hunan food out there tends to be greasy, with sauces or chili oil that taste artificial. But the food at Henry’s Hunan Restaurant is high quality, tastes great, and isn’t greasy at all. Be sure to try the hot and sour gizzards — they’re a must!

Henry’s Hunan is a SF institution that used to have locations across the city. COME GET DIANA’s SPECIAL!!! An invention of the founder and named after his wife. It is my favorite dish in the world and Henry’s is the only place you can get it. Everything is fabulous, from their homemade dumplings, to string beans w meat sauce, to chicken salad, to other entrees like Henry’s shredded pork. You can choose your spice levels, from mild to kick your butt spicy. I cannot recommend this piece of SF history enough.

So delicious! We ordered the sweet and sour chicken and the Mongolian beef and it was amazing. The food is high quality and the menu is extensive with many appealing options. Already can’t wait to come back!

The place is top notch as always! Got to see Jeff, the owner, today for the first time in a while. Plenty of table space for those lookin to sit down and relax, as most orders are pick up these days. #1 rec for anyone is Diana's Delicious Meat Pie!! The ground pork has the slightest hint of heat, then you get a familiar salty kick...PARMESAN CHEESE! Picks included...

ChinoYang’s Hunan and Mandarin Cuisine|1.text:
The food at chino yangs was fantastic.

I got a dry spicy pot with pork spare ribs, celery, tofu skin, potatoes, lotus root, woodear mushroom. This was very addictive with its numbing and chilli spice. The meat / veggie ratio is good, personally I would prefer more veggie.

Also got

Hunan fried rice noodle which was less spicy with a nice smoky aroma. It also had some tripe.

Ginger chilli free range chicken which was very spicy with jalapenos and bone in chicken. It has chicken elbow, skin, feet and white meat. This was delicious.

Spicy chilli fish fried fish. The batter was extremely  crispy and the fish was nice and soft inside. This was a touch on the saltier side.

Everthing was delicious albeit a little pricey.

The service was very helpful and efficient.

The chrysanthemum tea helped to wash down the chilli oil and really hit the spot.

All in all a great meal and I will be back!

Had a fantastic meal here. Everything we ordered came out hot, fresh, and packed with bold flavor. One dish arrived on a sizzling plate and smelled amazing the moment it hit the table—super satisfying with a great spicy kick. The other was rich and savory with that classic wok-char taste, and the ingredients were cooked perfectly.

Service was friendly and efficient, and the overall vibe was comfortable. If you like authentic, flavorful Chinese food, this place is absolutely worth a visit. Will be back!

The best spicy Chinese cuisine in the city!
As a guy from Hunan I guarantee you this is the most authentic Hunan cuisine I have ever had in SF. The spice level is spot on- not just hot, but well coordinated with the food itself. The stir fried beef is just like the one I had in Hunan - spicy with all the rich flavors. Dry pot chitins is phenomenal as well, crispy and delicious. I’ve been eating here for a year now, and I have not had any food which is not authentic and delicious. Shout out to chino and the Hunanese chef man, appreciate yall for bringing authentic food from my hometown to the city. Definitely a 10/10, a must try Chinese restaurant!
The General Tso's chicken was satisfying along with the stir fried string beans. Their cooking seems very top tier. They do a lot of spicy Hunan style too. Definitely worth checking out.

As a Hunan-native, I can confidently say this is the MOST authentic and the best Hunan cuisine in Bay Area! Fresh veggies, flavorful fried pork and beef, home-made seasoned pork, and clean cooking oil. Delightful experience!! Also super friendly host! It definitely gonna be our go-to place.

CHEF XIONG KITCHEN 川香園鉄木真|2428 Clement S.text: Best Chinese food I’ve had hands down. The folks there are super kind and will serve you a great meal. Can’t remember many meals that hit the spot like that!

Chef Xiong Kitchen is a fantastic spot that really delivers on flavor and convenience. The atmosphere is no-frills and casual, it feels like a genuine, family-run eatery, which adds to its charm. The service was quick and efficient, perfect for a late-night craving.

The food itself is what really shines. The scallion pancakes were crispy and delicious, a perfect appetizer. But the real stars of the show were the main dishes. The spicy wontons were mouthwateringly good, with a perfect balance of heat and flavor. The large bowl of beef noodle soup was incredibly rich and hearty, loaded with tender beef, fresh bok choy, and plenty of cilantro. It tasted incredibly authentic, reminding me of the food I've had in China.

it's not a place for a fancy dinner, but for an authentic, delicious, and convenient meal, especially since they're open late, it's a solid choice. It's a lifesaver when you need a great meal long after other places have closed.

I had dinner with my family at Chef Xiong Kitchen yesterday and we ordered several dishes: green peppercorn stewed fish, stir-fried potatoes, pig feet and chicken skewers, cold spicy noodles, beef noodle soup, and stir-fried green beans. Everything was absolutely delicious, especially the cold spicy noodles, which were a standout.

The portion sizes were very generous and offered great value for the price. Each dish was packed with flavor and had its own unique flair. The service was impeccable; the staff were attentive and made sure we had everything we needed to fully enjoy our meal.

I’ll definitely be coming back, whether for dine-in or takeout! I will also be telling friends and family to try this place out!
We stumbled upon this place randomly during our trip and it was a hidden gem! Really authentic Chinese food and friendly service! I will be coming back!!
This is our go to place for Sichuan food. We had some special local dishes that we typically don’t find in the Bay Area, 魔芋烧鸡，乐山甜皮鸭. Super tasty. Highly recommend!

Hai Ky Mi Gia Chinese Cuisine|707 Ellis.text:
This noodle place is the real deal! The couple running the restaurant are the absolute sweetest. They were so patient with us, taking their time to explain the menu items and give great recommendations.

I ordered the #2 (wonton noodle soup) and it was absolutely delicious plus the portion size was great. I also had the Thai iced tea, which was perfection because it wasn’t overly sweet, just the way I like it. Exceptional food and even better hospitality. We will definitely be back!

I've been here several times and they are extremely consistent in taste and portion size. Prices are reasonable for the portions you get and the service is always very efficient and friendly. Sure it's messy in the TL, but the restaurant is a homey spot with lots of customers and comfortable on the inside.

Things I've ordered:
House special ho fun (soup and dry) - this is the must order here in my opinion, it is full of toppings over the noodles (shrimp, char siew, pork slices, ground pork, chicken strips, fish cakes, beef balls, bean sprouts, pork liver if you want it). I've only gotten the rice noodle version and like it dry with soup on the side. It is well seasoned on its own but you can jazz it up with pickled jalapenos and other sauces that they provide on the table. The fried garlic oil they put in it is delicious and I've never regretted ordering this.
Golden fried fish cakes - about 3 whole fishcakes in each order, cut into half and fried to golden perfection. They are a little greasy but tasty with a nice bounce, they taste great dipped in the hoisin sauce. Served with pickled vegetables on the side (cucumber and carrot) which cuts through the grease perfectly.
Wonton soup - the wontons are just ok, there's no shrimp in it that I could discern and is mostly just wrapper and ground pork. It's seasoned well and comes in the same soup that they serve with the house special noodles.
Beef stew ho fun - this was more soupy than stewy in my opinion, with a strong tomato flavor. The beef was alright and pretty chewy and tough, it would have been nice if they had stewed the meat a little longer to get it nice and soft. The rice noodles don't come with anything else except beef and bean sprouts. It's an ok option but I would opt for the house noodles over this anytime.

The crowds here seem inconsistent, I had to wait once for over 20 mins for a table and it was packed to the gills, and other times I got a table right away and there were a decent number of customers but no wait. The tables turn over quickly though since people are here to eat, not sit around for hours.
Braised Duck Leg Egg Noodle Soup(Dry) was delicious. Fast service. Homemade Soybean drink was good too.

The dry noodles are thoroughly infused with the delicious flavor of the broth.
As for the accompanying soup, it is richly aromatic with the savory essence of offal.
The area outside the shop is unpleasant, as there are quite a few homeless people to the left, and locals let their dogs defecate indiscriminately without cleaning it up.
Thank you, Democratic Party.

This place is amazing. I love the soup, braised duck, and also the noodle. Very flavorful and I like noodle texture is fantastic. I like the combination noodle between the thick and thin egg noodles. The best one ever. House special also great. Don't forget to try the soymilk. Also good.

Kung Food|1615 McAllister St #4413, San.text:
Food is excellent and finely prepared with savouriness, freshness and balance of spices rendered supreme.

Personally, the beef texture could be improved with more marinating and select cuts. They target deliveries within two miles and with casual dining room with no fine dining pretenses.

I would recommend for vegans and any hungry diners alike. Excellent friendly staff who will go out of their staff duty calls to please dining experiences; one of my favorite Chinese restaurants.

Definitely an easy 5 star!
Food is amazing as always, comes with delivery, fair prices. Super authentic Chinese food! I recommend everything on the menu especially the Korean bbq wings and the general chicken. The chef knows what’s he doing hundred percent. Also the owner chino is an amazing guy, always gives back to the community- again, reason why I recommend this restaurant to everyone.

Ordered takeout and got the General Tso’s lunch special and the salt and pepper tofu. I know SF has everything from dim sum to Cantonese clay pot rice, but there’s something about American Chinese food that really hits the spot - and this was absolutely delicious.

The portion sizes were great - the lunch special came with an egg roll and hot and sour soup on the side. I highly recommend paying the extra $1 for the chow mein.

Tasty food, generous portions, and one of the best lunch specials I’ve had in the city!

I only had take-out and delivery but everything was delicious! The pork wonton soup was amazing. I was expecting pork wontons but there were several meaty dumplings (obviously from scratch) with plenty of BBQ pork and veggies. I was sick at the end of my trip and this place got me through it. The combo rice, shrimp noodles, spring rolls, crab rangoons, fried chicken, and soup was the best I've ever had. Three out of six days we ordered Kung Food and were pleasantly delighted every time. There is nothing like this place up in Washington state.

Delicious. If you've ever had Panda Express and thought: I wish this tasted like it was made with love.

Then this is the place for you! High quality Chinese-American food. Delicious, fair priced, and quick.

LetsSweet Kitchen|1010 Bryant St, San Fr.text:
beef brisket curry was so good! Good spice, good depth, very tender.

Wonton noodle soup also tasty. Everything had a good clean taste. Yuen Yueng drink was great!

Egg tart also good, not too sweet. Overall food was solid, excited to come back.

Found this spot recently and I’m already hooked! We tried the combo (rice rolls, siu mai, curry fish balls) and teriyaki chicken egg rice - everything was delicious and super authentic. Perfect if you’re craving real Hong Kong flavors! Service was incredibly warm and friendly too.

Oh and they had super cute HK themed merch you could purchase as well - egg tart towel, packaged noodle towel, egg tart magnets, etc.

Such a hidden gem, def worth a visit!

The food was crazy good. The sauce on the curry chicken was unbelievable... It was sweet but not overly sweet, savory and smooth... With a little heat kick at the end. Compliments to the chef and I highly recommend. One of my new favorite spots to go.

Due to a flub at work I had no lunch today, so I decided to check out LetsSweet. I live in the south bay so I usually go to HK cafes there and this was my first in SF.

I ordered the brisket noodle with round rice noodles. The broth was so clear, not overly seasoned or salty, but still tasty, and the noodles were perfectly cooked. The brisket was also just about perfect. Tender but not too soft, some chew. Also seasoned well with good flavor.

I also ordered the HK milk tea. It was actually a more floral tea than I'm used to (I'm not an expert but I think usually the south bay cafes use a black tea?). However it was also fairly tasty and not overly sweet.

This spot will definitely take a place in my office neighborhood restaurants rotation and I'll be back again.

So glad to know that our favorite Hong Kong style noodle place is back at the same location as the late Beyond Cafe with apparently the same chef but new owners and an expanded menu! Their wonton noodle soup (exquisitely complex broth with taste of dried scallops, springy noodles, and perfect wontons with juicy ground pork and plump shrimp) and spicy beef brisket noodle soup (tender chunks of gelatinous beef in a nicely balanced spicy beef broth and springy noodles) are so good it will be difficult to try other delicious looking offerings on the new menu!

Wing Lee Bakery|503 Clement St, San Fran.text:
Located on Clement St between 6th and 7th Ave.
This spot still has the best dim sum to go.
Always a line during lunch time, but it moves quickly.
Cash Only!
Food is always fresh and hot.

Great dim sum champ on Clement Street that stays open later than the others, for people who like to have dim sum after work! Delightful staff, delicious dim sum, and low prices and make this the go-to spot on Clement Street for the afternoons!

Purchased an egg tart this afternoon. Ate 3/4 of it and notice mold on the crust. I have not had issues with the food in the past or just haven’t noticed. For mold to grow, it must have been sitting around for a while yet it was still sold to customers. I will not be returning even though I have purchased from them and their deli for over 15 years. Just hoping I don’t get sick later. Also noticed that there is less shrimp in the har gow and that the steamed rice balls are smaller.

The har gao were delicious, dough was very soft and nicely warmed up from the steam. Ones not in the steamer were cold but tasty.

$7.90 for a light lunch

Take-out only. Wing Lee is a good spot to get buns and dim sum. Their line in the mornings is not bad, compared to the other dim sum restaurant on Clement. We had some egg rolls, pork dim sum (siu mai), pot stickers and a porridge. They didn't have Chinese donuts to go with the porridge. Awh. Since there aren't any tables inside, we resorted to sta

Go Duck Yourself 去你鸭的|439 Cortland Ave,.text:
If I could give 3.5 starts.
Had a lot of SF Chinese BBQ growing up, so this review comes with over 50yrs of perspective..It's a restaurant, so food must be the main focus. Food was "alright".  We had their duck and combo meat with roast pork and BBQ pork.
Duck was meaty and skin was crispy, but lack the intense Chinese BBQ roast duck flavor.
Roast pork:  skin was very crispy (a hint of torch taste). Again, lacking the salty 5-spice flavor.  But this could depend on the section of the cut of meat.
The BBQ pork:  beautiful bark color. Meat was tender and flavored "evenly throughout".  "Evenly throughout" is where I have issue with. I like it to be kinda "hard" on the outside but tender on the inside, and the endpoint almost crunchy and full of BBQ flavor.

The service is excellent and presentation was upscale.

This would be a place I might take an out of town visitor, where we want an upscale feel to chat is focus.

Go Duck yourself is casual csntonese bbq restaurant at Bernal Height. It is small restaurant with unique interior. The menu is simple.
Popular dishes includes
GDY Cantonese Roast Duck: The signature dish, served with duck au jus and plum sauce.
Crispy Skin Pork Belly: A whole pound of pork belly with crackling skin.
Honey Glazed Barbecue Pork (Char Siu): Sweet, bite-sized pork collar.
Garlic Greens: A popular vegetarian side of stir-fried seasonal greens.
Good Ducking Noodles: Wok-fried egg noodles with tender duck chunks and duck au jus
The food is yummy. The noidle us a bit greesy.

The food is authentic and amazing.  Service is a let down.  We’ve been going for several years now and go about once a month.  My girl loves the boneless duck with plum sauce.  It’s Smokey and crisp, full of flavor from the crispy skin.  I like the bbq pork char siu ,  it’s really tender with just the right level of sweetness.  Sticky rice scrumptious and  duck noodles always has good texture and flavor.  Finally the roasted port is dazzling, crispy skin and so tender and juicy.

We usually go with the grandmas and my daughter so need a table for five or six.  Really is it that much trouble??!!  Where is the basic hospitality?  Getting a tea refill or a fork isn’t that much trouble.  We are not demanding but I shouldnt have to feel like an unwelcome guest after spending 250 and then having to push 20% for a tip.  (Sigh). I just hope they improve.

I celebrated my birthday here and the food and service were fantastic. Loved the Cantonese Roast Duck, Pork Belly two ways, garlicky greens, noodles, fish sauce chicken wings, and dessert. I'm definitely coming here again.

The name alone is enough to catch the attention of any passerby, but once you learn a bit more about this place, you'll definitely want to stop in and try the food. We did, and it was absolutely delicious! We ordered the famous Cantonese Roast Duck, bao buns with hoisin sesame paste, OG Charsiu noodles, and garlic  greens with jasmine rice. The Cantonese Roast duck paired with the bao  buns is an absolute must-have. The noodles were incredibly flavorful with a smokiness that I really enjoyed. We only got the garlic greens and jasmine rice because they came highly recommended, but they weren't really our thing. I rounded out my dinner with a nice glass of Umbriasonwhite wine. The service was excellent. Pro tip: if you're coming for dinner, make a reservation for a table because they fill up fast. They have very little room for walk-ins.

Good Mong Kok Bakery|1039 Stockton St, S.text:
A Chinatown hole in the wall dumpling spot that makes great dim sum dishes! Not a dine in spot. Only take out.

There was a line as I arrived in the morning, but very minimal and the wait may have been a total of 5 minutes. Three cooks were behind the counter taking orders and preparing the food.

I ordered the Steamed Pork Bun, Har Gow, Shu Mai, and Pot Stickers. All savory and all delicious as any dim sum restaurant.

The Steamed Pork Bun was large and it had more dough than pork. But the dough was very fluffy and flavorful. The pork was tender, sweet and savory.

The pot stickers were traditional and flavorful. Crispy on the outside as they're meant to be. The fillings in the Har Gow and Shu Mai were spot on when it comes to consistency, flavor, and texture. If you want the popular dim sum dishes, this is the spot to go to.

Long line. It took about 15-20 minutes to get in. Line actually moved pretty quickly. Thank goodness they accepted Apple Pay, I didn’t have enough cash on me. Since they did accept cards with a 3% charge, I kinda splurged a bit more with my selection. I got the Turnip Cakes, Chicken Siu Mai, and Chives and Shrimp dumplings. They were good. Servings were large. Ambiance, you can’t sit down, it’s take out only. It’s a hole in a wall sorta place, but serves lots of dim sum dishes. Great place to go for fresh dim sum when in Chinatown in San Francisco.

Dim sum and buns here are big enough and yummy enough 🤩🤩
Dim sum skins are little bit thick but you won't even care about it when you taste the fillings at such a low price.
Pork and shrimp dumpling was amazing, and steamed BBQ pork bun was impressive, not to mention sesame balls.
You might think the staff is not that friendly and the kitchen looks messy, but come on... with their tasty dumplings and buns at such a low price, I dare not to expect fancy service from 5 star restaurant 😂😂

This was a popular choice on Google so we came here for breakfast around 10am. The line was short so we joined. It moved insanely fast. Just make sure you know what you want to get or order. The ladies can understand English and speak Cantonese or you can simply just point at what you want to order.

The food was warm from the steamers so temperature was nice. The only thing is that it dont actually taste good. You get what you pay for. This place is just popular because it's cheap. Its not popular for excellent dim sum.

The shrimp dumpling had a ton if shrimp but you dont taste any shrimps flavor. All you can taste is just the dough. Would not recommend getting it.

The fried meat dumpling was crispy but you just taste the oil. There isnt much pork  or filling inside. It’s mostly like the dough you are eating. Average.

The "chinese donut," you tiew, or fried dough was cold and dry. It wasnt fresh. Nobody wanted to finished the one stick and it was tossed in the trash.

3 orders for $9.80.

Go here if you want cheap dim sum takeout but do not go here for cheap and quality dim sum.

Dim Sum Takeout in Chinatown. Prices are still reasonable for Dim Sum, cheaper than most sit down places, and fair quality for takeout. Going in early morning seems to be less crowds and more freshly made food for the day. They now accept credit cards, but 3% additional charge compared to cash.

Best that I've tried include the clear shrimp dumpling, egg shrimp/pork dumpling, egg tart, and sesame balls (when available). These dumplings are absolutely filled with shrimp and taste really good. The egg tart is smooth and custard-y without too much egg and the sesame balls are chewy and mildly sweet.

Pork buns, while large, don't have too much meat filling and sometimes are a bit wet from leaving in steamers. But for $2 as of April '26 I can't complain too much.

Now, the ladies behind the counter are straightforward and want the line to move along smoothly. Some days they do run out of certain items (no egg yolk bun or sesame balls at different times 😭). So go in with some idea BEFORE entering the line and ordering at the counter.

Also, don't feel too rushed if ordering multiple items. They started to ring me up before I finished and I just asked for the rest. On one occasion they misheard my order for 2 shrimp dumplings for 6 shrimp dumplings and I didn't realize till after packing my order and paying. Not the end of the world I just had a larger than expected breakfast 😋.

Cheung Hing Restaurant|2339 Noriega St,.text: The braised tofu party tray was very good the last time we had it at a party.  But the latest one is not as good.  The braised tofu wasn't as tasty and it was loaded with big bok choy instead of button mushrooms like I was told when ordering.  Way too much sauce.  Won't be getting it again.

The roast duck chicken and pork are to die for!!! No wonder they always have lines stretching Noriega St to Sunset Blvd!!!

I had a half chicken and half duck and I was more than satisfied! The quality 👌 the atmosphere chaotic but in a good way! You can even pick out your own bird or pig!

The one downside is cash credit. Like most gas stations you pay more for credit and there's no advance sales...OTHERWISE BON APPETIT!

THE BEST DUCK IN SAN FRANCISCO POSSIBLY THE ENTIRE WEST COAST! DO NOT MISS!!!🤤🤤🤤❤️❤️🤭🤭😃😃

Service was rude the second time we came. The lady at the cashier spent her time talking to her friend and complaining about how the customers before us didn’t follow the “ordering etiquette” therefore slowed down her work. An easy remedy would be you stop talking and get back to work? In terms of the food, we especially loved the roast duck. The skin is glossy and crispy. We tried the double roast combo on rice and got roast duck and roast pork.

When I asked if they sell half a pound, they said yes. But when I was about to pay, they suddenly said the minimum is $9 for this type of box.
Such dishonesty. I even wonder if they properly pay taxes to the government, since they only accept cash.
Who would want to eat something like what’s shown in the picture? I threw it all away as soon as I got home.
I won’t be setting foot in this shop again. Thanks!

A lot of locals say this place has the best Hong Kong style barbecue meats in the city, that is when I had to make a visit and see for myself. Upon arrival, the lines was out of the door and that kept me wondering to see if what they did was true. That was not really the case because they just had one person chopping for all the customers. Also the store is very compact, so not much people can just wait inside to crowd the whole place up. They offer roast duck, chicken, soy sauce chicken, chau siu, roast pork, wok fresh items and much more. They used to have dine in, but after Covid they removed that option and it is strictly for to go now. When it was my turn to order, I just went with my normal go to which is roast duck, because if you mess that up then no need of a revisit. The skin was semi crispy, meat was slightly warm and a lot of fat than meat ratio. To conclude people’s opinions and facts, this is definitely not the best Hong Kong barbecue shop in the city. I know where the best one is and most people agree with my option. I do have to say the staffs here were welcoming and well trained which made my visit an issue free one. I recommend this place if you are in need of a quick bite, to anyone on a limited time or if you live nearby. I might be back in the future to tryout the other options they have to offer.

IPOT|1420 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 9.text: Liked the place very much. We got two different kinds of soup, the tom yum was delicious, the kimchi soup was meh. Everything else was fresh, nice selection of sides, perfect for a big group or a date. Service and wait may vary depending on the day of the week. Ambience is good, can be a little loud because people seem to come in groups but that is a great thing if youre coming with a group. Servers weren’t being fake nice and pretentious. Overall, loved the place and food. Will definitely go back again

Great value for an all-you-can-eat hot pot spot! The price is very reasonable for the amount of food you get. There was a huge selection of meats, vegetables, and other hot pot items, and everything tasted fresh and high quality. The portions and variety definitely make it worth the price. The broth was flavorful, and the staff kept everything stocked and running smoothly. If you’re looking for a satisfying hot pot experience without spending a fortune, this place is definitely worth checking out. I’ll be coming back!

Had an incredible meal here today! The quality of the meat was outstanding—fresh, tender, and beautifully sliced. There was a great variety of ingredients, from vegetables and mushrooms to noodles and tofu, all of which tasted fresh and delicious.

The broth was rich and flavorful, and the dipping sauces added even more depth to every bite. The portions were generous, and everything was served quickly and neatly.

What impressed me the most was the overall dining experience. The atmosphere was warm and welcoming, the staff were attentive and friendly, and the ingredients were consistently fresh throughout the meal.

If you’re looking for a place to enjoy authentic hot pot with family or friends, this spot is definitely worth visiting. I’ll definitely be coming back!

I can't believe the chicken wings were burnt on the outside but still raw inside. The beef was even worse; the BBQ pieces were completely charred on the outside, but still frozen in the middle.  There were five of us, and we placed our order, but the internet wasn't working, and our order wasn't sent to the kitchen. This is the second time we've been here, and both times have been the most disappointing dining experiences of my life. The first time I thought it was just an internet issue, but it happened again this time. I'm very disappointed with this place. I really didn't want to leave a bad review, and I've never given a bad review before in my life, this is the first time, because I'm so upset that I paid money but didn't receive fair treatment. The restaurant is truly unprofessional in this regard. I really don't understand why this happened, but it's unfair to me. I wanted to enjoy a meal with my friends and family, but both times I've been here have been disappointing, especially considering the amount of money we paid for five people, and both experiences were the same. I apologize to the restaurant for giving a bad review, but there was truly no fairness for me. I hope in the future the restaurant will fix these problems so that all future customers can enjoy a pleasant time with their friends and family at this restaurant. Our entire family experienced diarrhea and severe stomach discomfort after eating dinner at your restaurant 😢😢😢😢😢

I’ve been eating at Sunset IPOT for over ten years, and I’ve shared my favorite parts of their menu with you. They have so many options, but I think it’s the most cost-effective Chinese hotpot and Korean BBQ buffet. Their $6 sake is such a great deal. My companion and I use our Amex Platinum cards every quarter and get a few free meals.

The Pots Hot Pot|2652 Judah St, San Fran.text: Very cool place. We have nothing like this in Montana that I have seen. Thanks to the nice man working! He was very patient with us. We had no idea what we were doing and he walked us through the whole process, very informative, very pleasant. It was a great anniversary dinner!

This place is great, we came in on a Saturday around 2p, got seated fast, our broths and first serve of meats came out immediately, and we loved the variety at the self serve area. Lots of veggies, fish, seafood, sauces, etc.

The broths were tasty, the meats were nice and fresh, everything was so good, and there was a person who kept around in case we needed anything 😊
90 minutes all you can eat for $30 is not bad at all. The meat is decent quality and honestly the broths taste really good. They don’t dilute over time and honestly taste better the more you cook it.

I enjoyed the food at this hot pot restaurant. Most of the meat and vegetable items were pretty good. I also liked the individual hot pots for each person. However, the ordering process was very confusing. We thought we had to pay extra for the meat, but apparently the cost was all you can eat for everything. You just have to order the meat separately. The service was, on the one hand, good (this guy showed up and gave us some pointers on how to order), but on the other hand, not so great (they never brought our egg that we ordered). The price was affordable and a good deal. The atmosphere left a fair amount to be desired – the no back stools were uncomfortable, and the tables were jammed together.

I’m giving this place five stars. It’s not the absolute best hot pot I’ve had, but it’s consistently good, and that’s important. I’ve been to other AYCE spots where the meat was chewy or just average, here, the meat quality is not bad. I’ve tried almost everything (except the wine-marinated one), and I even like the lamb, which I normally don’t.
Service is attentive and hospitable. Just a heads-up: it gets loud, so it’s not ideal for a date. The condiment area can get a little messy when busy, but overall it’s a great spot.
If you’re in the area and craving hot pot, I definitely recommend trying it.

M Pot|832 Clement St, San Francisco, CA.text:
M Pot is opened by the same owner as 千王府, and you can really taste it. The spicy beef tallow broth is super fragrant, and the duck soup is rich and comforting.

Meats were fresh (loved the ribeye), and all the classic dishes like tripe and goose intestine were on point.

Don’t skip the desserts — the brown sugar rice cake and iced jelly were AMAZING.

Even the fried rice was surprisingly good.

Definitely coming back!

Just checked out this hotpot place and the flavor was absolutely amazing! You can find a variety of ingredients for hotpot which is classic in China but rare finds here in the US. Honestly I haven’t seen anywhere else serving bamboo mushrooms for hotpot, and gladly I find it here, and it tastes great. The base was so flavorful and the owner told us they cooked the base in house. We also really enjoyed our dessert, 冰粉and糍粑. Both business owners came from Chongqing and they brought their passion into serving customers with good food and service, they helped us clear the foam on the surface as well, which was very nostalgic to me. Excellent service with clean and cozy environment!

If you want a delicious hot pot with really great quality ingredients, and no frills come here. The service was amazing, and everything was fresh. Prices were awesome., and they have a bright clean space. Would 10/10 recommend. Leelee (LiLi sp?) Was so welcoming and was really helpful. Take her tips about items on the menu. She did not steer us wrong.
Came for our first Hot pot  experience and owners made sure leave with most wonderful experience. They went above and beyond to make it a great experience. Definitely recommend it.

Currently visiting from NYC and this place was absolutely amazing
This place was definitely the best hot pot I’ve ever been to !!! Everything tasted super fresh & the people were so nice 🥹
Definitely gonna come back here when I visit again
We even got a cute rice cake desert !!! Definitely recommend you eat here

Boiling Hot Pot|5512 Geary Blvd, San Fra.text:
January 2025:
Came here for dinner on a Friday night. Got here early because I heard it gets busy. This is an all you can eat hot pot place that has a 90 minute limit.

Overall, food was pretty good. We had 4 soup bases: miso, chicken, tom yum, teriyaki broth. I liked the chicken base the best.

We had a wide range of meat: chicken, beef, lamb. We also tried the egg noodles and vermicelli noodles. Everything was pretty tasty 😋.

Beer options complimented the food well. Would come back again for more hot pot.

Love coming to this hot pot spot. They are extremely efficient, you order off an IPad and get to choose 4 broths (such variety!). Their service is very prompt, you get your food within 10 mins of sitting down and take your time waiting for the broths to boil.

Their selection of hot pot items is great for the price- my favourites are the bean curds, seaweed, beef tendon and tripe. They give generous amounts of each item with each order, which is nice so you lessen the amount of time you would need to re-order.

A note that their AYCE price does not include any desserts like most other places do. This might contribute to their slightly lower pricing but not a big deal if you care more about quality hot pot.

This place is fantastic! 90mins of all you can stomach. Good gosh! Roll out of this place. The food and selection are excellent. The prices are very good. Will go there many times in the future. The experience of cooking your food in the center of the table is fun and a great place to take people visiting. There is something on the menu for everyone!

Great service! We love Boiling Pot, the food is amazing for a cook yourself experience. The selection of ingredients is very good, there's meat and vegan options.  The servers are really nice and attentive. The atmosphere is okay,they have a range of music playing, it does get steamy!!!

Happy 4th of July.
Normally we don't eat AYCEhotpot in SF (mostly because the price). But it was holiday so why not. Many other hotpots in town were closed so we'd like to try this.
It was around noon, we didn't have to wait and were seated immediatly. The servers were very nice and fast. We could chose up to 4 soups. You can order as many as you can (as long as you can finish them), no limit to 5 dishes per 10 mins or 1 call. There is a small sauce bar with some nice recipes there. Food is quite nice, and I really like the beer beef. Some dishes that I really like in other places aren't here, but they have enough good food for you to try.
The only thing that make me confused was when we sat down, the server said $28.99 for AYCE. Later she told other customers $35.99. In the end we were charged 35.99 each too. I guess that was holiday's price but she should have told us from the beginning.

Overall, I like the idea of 4 soup bases. You don't have ro refill broth so the flavor stays the same. Service is fast, environment is fresh and friendly. The price is just similar to others in SF. I Might come back but if I have a chance I either would try other new AYCE in the city or just go to other cities in The Bay Area for much more value.

Sam Wo Restaurant|713 Clay St, San Franc.text: Sam Wo was exactly what we wanted when we were looking for a restaurant in Chinatown! The food was amazing!!! We enjoyed beef fried rice and sweet and sour chicken. I was not particularly impressed with the spring rolls but they were still tasty! The service was very fast, we only waited a few minutes after ordering for our food to begin to arrive.  The overall atmosphere is a little cramped and the chair are stools, but it did not dull our overall experience! I only comment on the atmosphere in case (you) are wondering if it is casual or fancy… definitely casual!

Sam Wo is one of the oldest restaurants in San Francisco. Their menu has the right amount of selection and from what I’ve tasted…like their restaurant it has classic food tasting. Simple service but very attentive. Bathroom is clean with reminders how old the restaurant has been running via news clippings on the wall. It’s in SF Chinatown so you already know parking is a challenge. And yes…for traditional and classic tastes Sam Wo is a place worth coming back. 👍🏼

If you want a taste of "Old San Francisco," Sam Wo is a must-visit. Dating back to roughly 1907, it stands as one of the oldest establishments in Chinatown.

​The restaurant is split into two levels and is quite compact.
​Watch Your Head: The second floor has notably low ceilings. If you’re tall or struggle with tight spaces (claustrophobia), it’s something to keep in mind, though it adds to the quirky, historic charm.

​While the menu is smaller and more focused than your average Chinese restaurant, it hits all the nostalgic notes:
​The Legend: They are world-famous for their BBQ Pork Rice Rolls—a mandatory order. Instead of thin rice noodle rolls, this one is thick.

​You’ll find traditional staples like wonton soup, savory porridge (congee), and various noodle and vegetable dishes.
​It’s simple, no-frills, and steeped in history. Go for the rice rolls, stay for the century-old atmosphere.
Overall the foid is just ok. It is the history that make it attractive for local and tourist.
At last, don't expect a nice service.

The food was not surprisingly bad :))))). The iconic BBQ Pork Roll was a huge letdown. The filling tasted like a strange combination of BBQ pork and bacon, and the flavors were bland, greasy, and completely unremarkable. In fact, most of the dishes we ordered lacked seasoning.

The service was even worse than the food. We tried to get a staff member’s attention 3 times. Each time, they looked directly at our table and simply ignored us. No one came over to take our order, and we were left waiting while staff attended to other tables around us.

After ignoring us throughout the meal, a staff member suddenly became concerned about the tip and pointed out that no tip had been added. Lmao :))))))).

Got there around 5:30 on xmas eve with no line. Menu is def more simplified but has many of the signature dishes as before. I just remembering it being more flavorful, larger portions, and still missing of the rice plates. We had some of the classics. Roll, wonton soup, egg rice plates, and fried rice. Good but not great in my mind. I still want to go back and try again. If you order egg, ask to make it runny.

Rice Roll Express|1131 Stockton St, San.text: Ordered to go #1 Rice Roll Special with cilantro and T5 Signature Shrimp and Roe Wonton in Chili Oil. The #1 Rice Roll Special includes a choice of one vegetable wrapped inside, the options are bean sprouts, lettuce, corn, scallion, or cilantro. The rice roll rice was paper thin and the meat inside was very good, a mix of beef, pork, bbq pork, and egg. It’s very light and easy to pull apart to eat, good with soy sauce. The T5 Signature Shrimp and Roe Wonton in Chili Oil was not too spicy, it’s a light spicy oil base around the wonton that you can dip your wonton in. Despite how red the chili oil looks, it’s not as daunting. The wontons were a nice size, not too small and a good portion, wrapped nicely and very juicy inside, the shrimp pieces are noticeable. Service was very fast, ordering was seamless though cash only and the food was ready soon after. The store inside is very simple and plain with plenty of menus on the walls and a simple setup of chairs and tables.

The food was absolutely delicious.  The shrimp wontons in the soup was very tasty.  The fried shrimp dumplings in chilli oil was my favorite dish and absolutely amazing.  The rice rolls were also very delicious.  It was crowded and tables were not cleaned I am assuming because of how busy.  Other than that I will be returning soon to enjoy the delicious food.

We were in Chinatown this past Sunday 5/17/26 and decided to try this place.  We ordered a BBQ pork rice roll, a shrimp rice roll, and a bowl of beef stew and wonton soup noodles.  This is a cash only place so make sure you have cash, and you place your order with the cashier before you find a table and wait for your number to be called.  The rice rolls were good but they gave us a beef rice roll instead of a BBQ pork rice roll.  We were in a hurry to an appointment so we didn't want to bring it back to the counter for an exchange when we realized it was beef instead of BBQ pork inside one of the orders.  The beef stew and wonton soup noodles was just average, since the beef stew was not very tender so I recommend just ordering the rice rolls.

This restaurant's main focus is rice rolls, so it is not your typical Chinese restaurant. The rice roll combinations here are out of this world! A visit to the sauce station is a must to get the full-on rice-roll experience. An endless supply of chili oil, peanut sauce, sweet sauce, soy sauce, cumin, and sriracha is available to you to achieve the best flavor. The place is quite small, and you can feel quite trapped between the lines, people waiting for orders, and the odd seating arrangement. Not the vibe to lounge; it's more of a scarf down your food and go.

We had lunch here after walking around for hours and it was very filling. We had the beef stew wonton noodle soup and shrimp rice rolls. They are a perfect pair! The stew was hearty, flavorful and warm. The rice roll was savory and delicious.

The place was crowded for lunch service and rightfully so because they’re so good! Service was quick, we got our food in less than 5mins.

New Fortune Restaurant|815 Stockton St,.text: Great hole-in-the-wall Chinese restaurant with a solid menu, many Chinese American dishes, speedy service, and complimentary tea. I went on a Friday afternoon for lunch and, I ordered the seafood plate with crispy noodles. This was my first time dining here, and I appreciated how quick my food was delivered. There aren’t many vegetarian or plant-based options, and I didn’t see tofu listed at all. But prices are pretty affordable, and I recommend paying in cash due to the credit card fee. Indoor seating is limited, so plan to go during slower business hours or order takeout. Overall, I would recommend!

Mama’s and papa’s Chinese comfort food.
Had Singapore style noodles and eggplant tofu with rice. Complimentary tea on the house. Be aware, there’s a fee for using card so bring cash.

Been ordering secret menu item:
Sweet and sour tofu with green beans and mushrooms. Served with steamed or fried rice. A must for vegetarians and vegans.

Generous portions. Bring cash if you're buying something less than $20 or order something worth $20 because that's their minimum for credit cards. I got the HK noodles with pork chops for $13 (w/tax) and was not able to finish it for lunch and had to finish for dinner. Totally worth it. It's definitely for sharing and absolutely delicious typical hole in the wall Chinese fast food.

Very homestyle Chinese food spot in the city - the menu has both typical (chow mein/chow fun) and atypical dishes (salted fish fried rice for example) and a wide variety of wonton/noodle soups. I think it was definitely very authentic tasting, but I do feel like it was missing that little extra edge to give it top marks. Reasonable pricing (not cheap not expensive) but the only real downside is the space inside is *limited* and they get *busy* - from the time we asked for a table to the time we ate, it was about 45-50 minutes on a Sunday lunch day. They can’t help it, the space is small - but just be aware if you’re starving. Also the middle table is like family style so you sit with strangers next to you.

Very busy and crowded eatery. There was a queue during dinnertime, it's better to go early as the restaurant is small and closes by 7pm. Although it's a Chinese restaurant, there was a proportion of non-Chinese customers. Ordered the wonton noodle soup and mapo toufu and the noodle was simply delicious.

Taishoken San Francisco|665 Valencia St,.text: Came here after seeing a video about it on Instagram. I think it has the best tsukemen I’ve had outside of Japan! The gravy/soup part is actually served super hot and is tasty. The normal tonkotsu ramen is also decorous. I liked the cucumber appetizer with some kind of chili garlic onion topping - refreshing and a nice starter to a heavy carb meal that follows.

Taishoken has great appetizers and ramen with a rich hearty broth.

The calamari and karage were very crispy. This is one of the better karage I have had. The calamari came on a bed of chilli oil that was also yum.

The ramen pork broth is like marufuku. I got the spicy miso ramen. The chasu could have been a little more tender. The ramen unusually was topped with red onion. The noodles are very al dente. I like this but it might not appeal to everyone.

The side of chilli oil was good.

The service was efficient. The atmosphere was cozy and the bonzai in the glass tubes were a nice touch.

Loved this ramen spot. Walked out of the way to try, and place was packed (in a good way...let's you know it's good). Staff was friendly and turning tables quickly. Had the Strawberry Yuzu drink with Tonkatsu Pork Broth Ramen. Nice portion served hot and fast. Saved to favorite; will be back soon!

Grilled eggplant was the best. Agadashi tofu was delicious as usual. Our sashimi platter melted in mouth.
Ramen broth was tasty - soft boiled egg was cooked to perfection. Service was amazing. High recommended this place.

Very good food and fast service. Packed cause was rainy day. Loved it. Definitely recommend. If can just say something about their Sangria is ok. ( lived 15 years in Barcelona ) that’s why I guess I had better ones. But loved their twist for sake.

Mensho Tokyo SF|672 Geary St, San Franci.text:
Mensho Tokyo SF is a cute little ramen spot in the Tenderloin. Went here by 5pm on a Tuesday, and was able to get easy seating for two people. Street parking was difficult to find, but thankfully there was a parking garage right next to the restaurant (Temple Garage). By the time we left by 6pm, there was a long line out the door on this rainy day. Food speaks for itself.

Ordered:
Spicy Enoki chips
Corn wings
A5 Wagyu sushi
Signature Spicy Toripaitan Ramen
Spicy Sichuan Pepper Style Vegan Miso Ramen
(With a side of chashu pork and ajitama egg)

Absolutely loved our appetizers! Was surprised that the corn was sweet, considering we’re in the middle of winter. Wagyu sushi was fun and delicious - pretty sure it was real wasabi instead of horseradish-wasabi.

Ok so for ramen -
Their signature ramen was playful, with their rich and creamy broth. This was our first time trying duck chashu. Usually not a fan of duck, but this was good! LOVEDDDD the fried burdock root in this. Will plan to order extra next time, if possible.

We also tried their Spicy Vegan Miso ramen. **disclosure: I am not a vegan.** But wanted to see this style of ramen because the reviews kept raving about how much people loved their Vegan options. && it does not disappoint! I was surprised by the delicately creamy, sesame & porcini mushroom broth. The noodles are perfectly chewy.

My only negative thing is that the Sichuan Pepper Style makes the broth taste saltier than it actually is. When I come back, will plan to order the non-spicy version. Other than that - I can see why this little hole in the wall made the Michelin list.

Will plan to come back and order:
Corn wings
Signature Spicy Toripaitan Ramen
Vegan Miso Ramen w side of ajtima egg & pork chashu

Tried Mensho Tokyo in San Francisco and it absolutely lived up to the hype.

We ordered the Spicy Lamb and the Extra Spicy GKO both were incredible. The broth is rich, thick, and almost curry-like, packed with deep, bold flavors that really stand out from typical ramen.

They’ve got a great variety of veg and non-veg options, which makes it an easy pick for mixed groups too.

Definitely one of the best ramen spots I’ve been to, highly recommend checking it out!

Mensho Tokyo SF is truly one of the standout places in the city for anyone who loves ramen 🍜. It’s not just another noodle shop — it feels genuinely unique and deeply authentic, the kind of spot where you can tell the chefs care about craft, flavor, and experience 🎌. Because of its popularity, I ended up waiting in line for a little over an hour ⏳ before getting in. Honestly, the wait itself becomes part of the ritual; everyone in line knows they’re about to enjoy something special.

Once inside, the service moves quickly ⚡. After placing my order, the food arrived much faster than I expected, especially considering how busy the restaurant was. I ordered the spicy VGKO 🌶️, the spicy enoki mushroom 🍄, and a Japanese beer 🍺 to go with it. I usually have a pretty high tolerance for spicy and hot foods, but the heat level of the spicy VGKO caught me off guard. It’s intense — delicious, but definitely not for the faint of heart 🔥. If you’re thinking of ordering the super spicy level, just be prepared.

Overall, Mensho Tokyo SF delivers a memorable ramen experience with bold flavors, efficient service, and a vibe that makes the wait worthwhile ⭐. I’d absolutely return — though next time, I might think twice before going for the highest spice level.

Hands down the best ramen we’ve ever had.
The signature bowl is rich and deeply flavorful, while the classic is a great option if you prefer something lighter. The GKO and lamb cumin ramen bring in Chinese-inspired flavors, which is a unique and creative twist. Everything we tried was delicious—highly recommend!

Hands down the best ramen I’ve ever had. The broth is incredibly rich, creamy, and packed with flavor—every spoonful feels carefully crafted. The noodles have the perfect texture, and the toppings are high quality and unique.

This isn’t your typical ramen spot—it feels elevated, almost like a Michelin-level experience in terms of technique and flavor. You can really tell there’s a lot of thought behind every element of the bowl.

If you’re into bold, intense flavors and don’t mind something a bit heavier, this place is a must-try. I’d absolutely come back

HINODEYA Ramen Japantown|1737 Buchanan S.text:
this was honestly one of the most disappointing ramen experiences I’ve had.

I ordered the duck ramen, which was nearly $40, and it was not worth the price. The duck tasted mostly like burnt seasoning, and the broth was surprisingly bland and lacked the depth and flavor you’d expect from a good bowl of ramen. The karaage was actually the best part of the meal.

The service didn’t help the experience either. After we had already been seated and settled in, we were asked to move to another table to accommodate a larger group. While we agreed, it was inconvenient and not something you expect after you’ve already gotten comfortable.

The restaurant was also extremely loud, making it difficult to enjoy the meal or conversation.

To top it off, the end of the experience felt rushed and unfriendly. The waitress came over, handed us the payment device and bill without asking how the food was or whether we needed anything else. It felt more like we were being rushed out than treated as guests.

For the price, quality of food, and overall service, I left very disappointed. Unfortunately, I won’t be returning.

Ramens amazing. S tier Ramen. The meat is tender/Delicious. The broth is better tasting and perfectly picked. The ramen noodles is just right. Food came fast. Service is nice and helpful. Place is cute and fashionable highly reconmend!

Guy next to us had his folding chair collapse. He fell to the ground and seemed ok. I prefer to eat without worrying about the structural integrity of the chairs. Nice to sit outside though.

House ramen was good, subbed wheat noodles with spinach noodles, al dente. Vegan ramen was ok, strange to drink savory soy milk. Fried garlic and onion toppings were delicious.

I came to Hideyama Ramen today with my partner, and this visit was really disappointing, especially considering I’ve been coming here for years.

On a positive note, my Tokyo Duck ramen was actually very good great portion and rich, flavorful broth. We also had gyoza and hot sake, which were enjoyable.

However, my partner ordered the beef sukiyaki ramen, and when it came out, there was a thick, short, wavy hair in the bowl clearly not something that should ever be in food. This is a serious contamination issue.

We tried to get our server’s attention, and I understand it was busy, but being busy is not an excuse when it comes to food safety. Eventually, another server came over. We showed him the issue, expecting at least a proper apology or some explanation of what happened. Instead, he just took the bowl away and said a quick “I’m sorry” no explanation, no reassurance, nothing.
