They brought out a new bowl of ramen, but at that point, the experience was already ruined. It’s hard to continue eating normally after something like that, it honestly just stays in your mind.

What makes this more frustrating is that I’ve been here many times before with friends, family, and coworkers, and when everything goes right, it’s great. But when something goes wrong, the response is just a simple “sorry” with no accountability or communication. Even on my last visit a few months ago, the broth was bland and the response was the same just “I’m sorry.”

At a minimum, for an issue this serious, there should be clear communication, reassurance about food safety, and a more professional response.

Because of both past and current experiences, I don’t think I’ll be coming back. I really hope they improve their service and train staff to handle serious situations like this more appropriately.

I had dinner here with my friends, and overall it was a great experience.
Our server Isaac, along with all the other staff, was super friendly and made us feel very welcome.
The atmosphere was lively and really felt like being in Japan, which made the experience even better.
The food was amazing, and even the desserts were delicious. Definitely a place I’d love to come back to.

Marufuku Ramen|1581 Webster St #235, San.text: Ramen bowl with pork belly. Very hearty, delicious ingredients. Asked for medium spicy, it wasn't spicy at all. The whole soft boiled egg in the soup was amazing. Overall the soup was excellent except for not being spicy when it was supposed to be medium spicy. The chicken karaage was good not great. Really good quality chicken meat, the coating could definitely be better. The dipping sauce with lemon was very good. So stuffed I couldn't finish my soup. Service was very good. Great place for a date night. I recommend this business

Marufuku Ramen SF absolutely delivers! The tonkotsu broth is rich and silky, the noodles have that perfect bite, and the chashu melts in your mouth. Even when the place is busy, the staff is friendly and efficient, making the whole experience smooth and enjoyable. The karaage chicken is a must‑order side, and portions are generous enough to leave you satisfied but wanting to come back. Easily one of the better ramen spots in San Francisco!

I’ve heard about this place for a while and finally had the unexpected opportunity on Christmas Eve. A friend and I decided to meet at Japantown for dinner. We walked around and I remembered this place. We decided to eat here. We didn’t even have to wait 5 mins and we were called to our table! Our waitress was SO nice! She was above & beyond! Gave us recommendations and answered our questions; and staff was so attentive!
I ordered the vegan creamy broth ramen. It was good; however, I liked Hinodeya’s better as theirs was more creamy and I liked the taste better. Marufuku’s was still good though.
My friend had the Hankata Tonkatsu DX (The DX just means it has extra toppings). Apparently, this is one of the popular dishes. He said it was really good. We both finished our ramen so highly recommend.
We ordered the chicken Karaage as well. I have to say this was SO good!!! For those wondering, I know I ordered the vegan ramen but it’s because I don’t eat that much meat (obviously not 100% vegan or vegetarian).
Street parking can be tough but you if you’re lucky, you can find free residential parking or metered parking. Otherwise, there is an underground paid parking garage. If you take public transportation, the 38-Geary or 22-Fillmore (you’ll have to walk a few blocks, not bad as there’s plenty of shops & restaurants along the way).
I highly recommend this place!

We arrived around 6:30pm on a Wednesday and was told we would be seated within 30 mins, which was to be expected during dinner time. The inside was packed with every table full, making it quite loud, but not too much that you couldn’t have a conversation with your table. Staff was quick and attentive.  Fun fact: you can ask for a bib so you don’t splash yourself when you eat.

The ramen broth was rich and flavorful. The egg was cooked pretty well too, the yolk was still runny and the whites weren’t too soft. The meat was tender, able to pull apart with the chopsticks.

The takoyaki was also very good and tasty as well. Not too soggy, but still crisp and creamy on the inside.

Overall this was a good experience and would come again, but perhaps a bit earlier to avoid the dinner rush.

Marufuku Ramen in San Francisco serves up some really good, flavorful ramen! The broth is rich and well-balanced, and the overall taste is definitely worth the visit.

That said, be prepared for a wait—especially on weekends. It can get pretty busy, so plan accordingly if you’re going during peak hours.

Overall, a great spot for ramen in SF. Just expect a bit of a line before you dig in!

Taniku Izakaya|1035 Geary St, San Franci.text: Every item we ordered was magical. Absolutely loved all their small tapas style dishes, scallops, yam, pork belly, chicken, beef. Scallop and sweet shrimp (ebi) handrolls were great. Fine dessert choice as well. Get there early as there was quickly a line out the door with 20-30 minute waits throughout the night after we arrived.

Really enjoyed a 2 person meal here around 6:30pm. Loved all the various grilled skewers. From the scallops, yams, pork belly, chicken thigh, steak. Loved the ramen as well. Then went back for a few more appetizers of tofu. Then went for some handrolls with scallops and ebi and it was fantastic. Ended the meal with 2 desserts and saw there was a small line after 7pm so it was probably 20-30 minute wait after.

Taniku Izakaya is absolutely worth the wait. They don't take reservations and there's no waiting area, so you'll need to wait outside, but trust me, it pays off. Everything on the menu is seriously delicious and clearly made with care. Standouts for me were the takoyaki, the wagyu beef sticks, and the chicken katsu, all perfectly cooked, flavorful, and incredibly satisfying. Once you're seated, the food and overall experience make you forget about the wait entirely. A must-try spot if you love quality izakaya food and don't mind waiting for something great.

I finally made it to their happy hour, which has some great deals.

Their happy hour menu contains some of their ramen bowls off their main menu for $13. I got the garlic pork ramen, and they did not skimp on the pork. There were two thick pieces of soft pork belly that was coated with garlic and seared to perfection. And the meat melted in my mouth.
The bok choy was crunchy and its bitterness was tamed with the flavorful pork. The noodles had a good bite while the corn added some sweetness. And the marinated egg also added another flavor profile. Not sure what it was marinated in, but it had a nice tea flavor. And the yolk was perfectly jammy.

And then there's the broth: full of umami, pork, and garlic flavors. I have been incredibly stressed from two projects at work and the moment I took a sip of the broth, I immediately felt at ease. The ramen was so comforting, especially now that we're experiencing autumn weather in San Francisco. I tend to eat fast, but there was no rushing this. The world and my worries melted away with each bite.

I also got the amaebi shrimp hand roll, which came with a yuzu sauce. I actually found it to be a bit gingery, which was a nice surprise in flavor. It seemed the rice was also marinated in perhaps soy sauce for that umami note.  And the shrimp was plump. It was a good bite.

This is a popular spot with a small dining space, so a line can form pretty quickly outside as the night progresses.  But that ramen is worth the wait.
One of those places you go to because the reviews are so great and you discover that every single person was right. This place has the most delicious food.  What a treat.

Ju-Ni|1335 Fulton St Unit 101, San Franc.text: My wife and I went as our Valentine's date night, expecting maybe a quiet-ish time at the bar and some polite banter perhaps with our chef and maybe the couples next to us. What we got instead quite by happy happenstance was a rollicking good time chatting with the super friendly couples next to us at the bar, while our chef JK obliged with friendly comments within our conversation while still preparing the delectable courses of the omakase. Of course, the happenstance of the chatty friendly couples sitting next to us can't be replicated, but our chef JK really rolled with it and knew how to adjust the service to the cadence of our conversation -- our lucky accidental group ended up being the last guests at the restaurant, at which time the chefs broke out some japanese sake for us on the house, and we proceeded to chat another 20 minutes before leaving with the distinct conclusion that this was unquestionably the best time we have ever had at an omakase and that we shall return!

man i’m so disappointed that i’m disappointed. Other than Ken, there just doesn’t seem to be any good omakase in the city. for $238/head the fish they offered for the menu was puzzling. uni was an add-on and they smoked it. not a fan. why ruin the pure, sweet buttery taste of uni? wine/sake list was underwhelming. biggest faux pas for me was putting sesame oil on one of the sushi. that was a frustrating bite. i had brought a guest to try omakase for his first time, hyping it up immensely, and i left feeling slightly embarrassed.

Sushi will never be the same for me after this. This was my first omakase experience, and it was nothing short of amazing. Geoffrey’s technique is incredible, he brings out the perfect balance of flavor in every piece. The attention to detail and care in each bite made the entire experience unforgettable.

16-course omakase service in this tiny location. The fish was outstanding, half of the courses were interesting, memorable, and outstanding and only two courses were "just fine" but that's gonna come down to personal preferences. Limited drink options, and service was fine but nothing to write home about. Come for the fantastic fish and creative courses. Was pretty loud whole I was there for to a business dinner that was very boisterous, but seems like that's probably rare for this place.

ju-ni focuses on the fish with some delicious nigiri pieces, but overall the ambiance & service was distracting and overpriced for the number of pieces served. overall, glad I tried it and loved the standout pieces, but wouldn’t recommend.

service: our chef wasn’t bad but the difference of service between our seating and the one next to us was night and day. ended up listening to the other chef more since he explained each dish with detail while ours mumbled his way through.

standout dishes: fatty tuna, red snapper, salmon roe with monkfish pate

KEN|252 Divisadero St, San Francisco, CA.text:
KEN is a hidden gem in San Francisco and absolutely worth the effort to book. I used to work in SF before the pandemic and was a regular at Kusakabe, where Chef Ken previously worked, so I was excited to finally visit his own restaurant.

The restaurant itself is very intimate, with only a 7-seat counter, which gives it a warm, homey feel. Chef Ken’s hospitality truly stands out. He took the time to introduce every fish, explain how each piece was cured, and recommend the best way to enjoy it. He was also incredibly patient and accommodating with allergies and preferences, and somehow managed to make all seven guests at the counter feel equally cared for throughout the evening.

As a Hong Kong native, I especially appreciated that Chef Ken was happy to chat with me in Cantonese, which made the experience feel even more personal.

The quality of the food was outstanding. Some of the highlights for me were the Hokkaido scallop sashimi, the otoro nigiri, and especially the nodoguro add-on at the end, which was unforgettable. Every course reflected the passion and attention to detail Chef Ken puts into his cuisine.

Because of the small size of the restaurant, reservations can be difficult to secure, but it is absolutely worth planning ahead. Parking in the area is also limited, so I would recommend taking a rideshare or using Muni, which conveniently drops you off right at the corner.

Dinner at KEN was one of those rare experiences where everything is considered, not just the fish, but the rhythm, the temperature, the ceramics, the space, and the human touch behind it all.

Each course felt perfectly calibrated: cool against warm, delicate against rich, flavors building intentionally. The fish itself was exceptional, pristine and expressive, but what stayed with me was the balance, nothing  felt rushed.

The setting is intimate in the best way. Friendly, welcoming, and deeply personal, without ever feeling performative. You feel cared for, guided, and included. The attention to detail extends beyond the food to the vessels it arrives in; beautiful, tactile pieces that elevate each bite and make the entire experience feel composed and intentional.

The sake also deserves special mention. Thoughtful throughout, and the dessert sake is an absolute must, unexpected, rich, and the perfect closing note.

This is the kind of meal you keep thinking about days/months/years later. A meal that lingers in memory, not just on the palate. Thank you, Ken I’ll be thinking about this fabulous evening for a long time to come.

We recently celebrated my partners birthday at KEN and had the most phenomenal night. We had a lot of interaction with all the staff which made the experience memorable and far from boring. The food from start to finish was out of this world and every flavor profile was *chefs kiss*. There were details throughout from the bites, to the dishes, to the sake pairing... couldn't have asked for a better evening. I had asked if we'd be having Uni because it is my absolute favorite and not only did Ken deliver, I think it was the most wonderful bite of Uni my mouth has ever tasted. Would definitely go back in the future for another celebration and have already recommended it to several friends!

P.S. the drink coasters are all handmade + gorgeous and I really wanted to buy it but they are 110% not for sale.

The best way to describe Ken is like having a good friend, who is also a chef, prepare a exceptional food experience for you. The space is beautiful and intimate. The staff were very attentive and kind. The menu was expertly crafted. I appreciated how there were warm dishes interspersed with the cool sushi and sashimi, as the weather is cold at this time. Makes me wonder what menu chef would come up with for spring as the weather changes.
It was an amazing experience, one you'll be thinking about for a while after. Absolutely recommend.

By far the best and most memorable omakase experience I’ve ever had! The atmosphere, food, and storytelling came together to create a truly unforgettable evening.

Not only was it incredibly fun, but also educational. This is absolutely a must-visit spot, and I’ll be sure to come back every time I’m in San Francisco!

Omakase|665 Townsend St, San Francisco,.text: This restaurant was amazing! The food was delicious, the service was excellent, and the chef was friendly and fun to talk to. I visited on my birthday, and they truly made me feel special. Everything exceeded my expectations, and the whole experience was wonderful. I highly recommend this place and would definitely come back again.

Had such a fun Omakase experience here! The chef was not only incredibly skilled but also super funny, making the whole dinner entertaining from start to finish. Every course was fresh, beautifully presented, and full of flavor.

We even got to see the chef’s amazing sushi knives (some worth thousands!) and his hilarious cucumber 🥒 “snake” cutting skills — unforgettable moment 😂.

Great food, great vibes, and a truly memorable night. Definitely recommend if you want amazing sushi with a fun atmosphere!

Really delicious and fun omakase experience. Loved it and thought the food was good and the chefs were chatty and fun to talk to!

Ambiance and service were wonderful and they accommodated our last minute reservation for my birthday.

I had the pleasure of dining at Omakase, and it was an unforgettable experience! The chef’s attention to detail and the artistry in each dish were absolutely breathtaking. Every course was perfectly paced, showcasing the freshest ingredients and creative flavor combinations that delighted the palate.

The ambiance was intimate and elegant, making it feel like a truly special occasion. Chef Jackson was incredibly knowledgeable, attentive, and passionate about the food, which added to the overall experience. He was also very entertaining and funny too.

If you’re looking for a culinary journey that is both refined and exciting, Omakase is a must-visit. I can’t wait to return!

I absolutely love our dining experience here. Picked this gem out to celebrate us getting married. And it did not disappoint! The food was amazing. We wanted to cherish every bite! The service was wonderful. Everyone left fulfilled and happy, laughing as we all walked out the door. I would come back in a heartbeat💓

Chīsai Sushi Club|3369 Mission St, San F.text:
Chisai Sushi Club is a small but cozy gem in San Francisco. The intimate setting makes the experience feel special, and they offer omakase-style dining at a very reasonable price. I had the Chisai course while my husband ordered the Oki, and every piece was outstanding. The fish was incredibly fresh, flavorful, and perfectly seasoned.

The chef was super friendly and took the time to introduce each dish, which made the experience even more enjoyable. The nigiri was so good that I couldn’t resist ordering extra pieces at the end.

Definitely make a reservation — it doesn’t seem like they take walk-ins. This is a new favorite spot that’s officially going into my go-to restaurant notebook. 🍣✨

Went there for my wife's birthday dinner with our oldest kid. We got two chisai and the oki (more expensive option, by $30). The place is small, and nice. Good spacing between tables. The service was great, both the servers would let you know where the fish from and the sauce.

For the price, the quality and amount of food was totally worth it. My only minor quibble is that most of the nigiri and sashimi also felt a bit more oversauced. However, most of the sauces were quite unique and different, so a different style that kinda worked. My kid loved the meal

Definitely worth a visit

Fantastic experience! Treated my girlfriend here for her birthday and got "The Chisai" omakase set menu for $120 which included oyster, salad, sashimi, 12 piece nigiri, temaki, and dessert.

Favorites:
1. Seared salmon
2. Sea bream with sea salt flakes
3. Albacore

We sat at the counter. The restaurant had a dimly lit, intimate ambience, and the chef was very nice and quiet. The restaurant only sat about 16 people. Would definitely come back!

This place is special. I’m so used to saying in SF, “was pretty good but paid 50% too much.”

The same cannot be said about this place. We had the Chiisai omakese and it was expertly plated and service was phenomenal.

From the plating, to the quality, to the ambiance, you can tell this place takes extreme pride.

Will be coming back!

We went to Chisai in May 2024.
It was amazing.
My wife is vegan and I was apprehensive about a sushi place doing vegan but she agrees it was amazing.
We got seating at the counter which gave us close access to the chef's who were super knowledgeable and answered any questions we had.
Food was great with the right mix of different cuts of fish alongside amazing accompaniments which helped to elevate every bite.
For the price this was back in May 2024 it was a steal and really kicked above its weight.
It is a small venue and got fairly loud, but you could still have a conversation with the person next to you.

Can't recommend it enough.

KUSAKABE|584 Washington St, San Francisc.text:
Kusakabe creates the kind of dining experience that feels carefully designed from the moment you sit down. The restaurant has a refined but welcoming atmosphere, with the sushi counter being the main focus where the meal unfolds course by course. There is a sense of calm throughout the space, allowing the attention to stay on the chefs, the ingredients, and the details behind each dish.

The omakase experience balances traditional sushi with more creative touches. Each course brings something different, from fresh sashimi and nigiri to warmer dishes like chawan mushi that add depth to the progression of the meal. The sushi highlights the quality of the fish while incorporating thoughtful flavor combinations, with dishes like scallop and the dry-aged fish courses standing out. The sake pairing also fits naturally into the experience, adding another layer without overpowering the food.

What makes Kusakabe memorable is the craftsmanship and pacing of the meal. The chefs take time to explain each course, making the experience feel personal rather than rushed. Sitting at the counter adds to the atmosphere because you get to see the precision and care that goes into every piece.

Kusakabe feels like a place made for special occasions, celebrations, or anyone looking for a more elevated Japanese dining experience. It combines technique, hospitality, and creativity in a way that makes the entire meal feel like an event rather than just a dinner.

This is by far the best Japanese restaurant I have been to in the United States. What an experience! This restaurant offers two tasting menus.  I had the "petite" omokase, which is a sufficient amount of food to feed anyone other than a football player. The quality, the attention to detail, and the service are all world-class. Pricey, but it's worth it!

So far the best omakase I’ve had in SF. I really like the flavor design of their sushi and the pacing of the entire corse. It’s exciting and satisfying. Really love the contrast of the dry aged fish sushi and the fresh fish sushi. And the appetizer is a omg level of surprise. Wonderful experience.

Ugh, really really wanted to love this place. This was my fifth omakase (not at this restaurant); the staff were super friendly and attentive but the sushi and flavors were lack luster to off. I also got very very sick as soon as I walked out….that has never happened to me before with sushi and like I said I have sushi from a wide range of places often (and I actually had sushi 3 times this very week already). The space is really small and it doesn’t feel like there’s any special effort to make it fit the price tag…just dim lights, wood chairs, not even a minimalist edge or anything chic. This would not be a place to dress up. The super high rating is a bit confusing. I paid $400/pp for the sushi and sake pairing, which I was happy to do but I do wish the sushi had been worth it . The sake pairing was excellently selected, the flavors went together very well. Altogether, a bit disappointed and very confused as to why I essentially threw up most of it up upon leaving. I’m typically an easy sell and I wish this had been great so I could come back often. I parked in a garage pretty close by, there were a few.

Good omakase place in SF. I'd say the portion size of the regular omakase is too large for me but the petite looked small.

I enjoyed the sushi pieces, but didn't love the other courses as much. Drink menu was nice and the service was good.

Rintaro|82 14th St, San Francisco, CA 94.text: My kids gifted us a certificate to dine at Rintaro. Luckily we were able to reserve it for our anniversary dinner. We were seated in the room further back from the entrance. At first it was quiet with 2 other couples. Then 2 tables of 3 and a table of 6 girls were seated and with the background noise of one of the staff slapping and cutting the udon it quickly became pretty noisy. Our server and the team that brought us beverages, dishes and cleared our plates were very professional, well-informed about sakes and suitable pairings and looked happy to be there. This made dining there so wonderful! They confirmed that we were celebrating our anniversary and gifted us glasses of sparkling after confirming where we were planning to imbibe alcoholic beverages that evening. So sweet! The sashimi , salad and grilled mushrooms (yakimono) were 5 stars. Oden and fried items were 4 stars. The desserts were a mix - one so good and the other just okay. Overall it’s a good place to eat and experience authentic Japanese eats with a California flourish.

So cozy and delicious. From the exterior to the delightful interior to the restrooms, this is a beautiful restaurant. Wonderful service and the food was all delicious. We loved the ume welcome drink. We tried the sashimi sampler, the gyoza, duck mizuna salad, trumpet mushroom and chicken thigh sansho yakitori, matsutake udon and passionfruit profiterole.  Our favorite was the mushroom yakitori. If we came back we'd try more yakitori and the whole fried fish. Tricia and our server were awesome. Excited to try recipes from the cookbook!

Rintaro SF has been on my list forever, so we planned ahead and reserved 2 weeks in advance for a Sunday dinner at 7:45pm. Definitely needed it as the place was busy and buzzing (typical izakaya energy). The space is beautiful, probably added to my expectations going in. And maybe that’s why the food didn’t quite land the way I hoped. It wasn’t bad by any means, just… underwhelming for how hyped it is. A few dishes stood out, but overall it didn’t feel as memorable as I expected for the price ($90 per person spring menu set)

What I had:
Ocha no ponchi cocktail: We started with some alcohol to warm ourselves up as it's cool outside. Light, refreshing and easy to drink. A nice start to the meal.
Haru no shira-ae: First dish, seasonal veggies with tofu sesame sauce, nothing particularly exciting.
San ten mori (sashimi plate with bluefin, yellowtail, halibut): Fish quality was mid, slices were very thin and small. We added uni and sea trout (+$10), was just ok
Hitokuchi oden: Had simmered daikon, lingcod hanpen, and sweet potato satsumaage fishcakes. The broth was bland (or maybe because we had a very great oden at a omakase place at lunch that same day, so this broth flavor was less comforting.
Yakitori tsukune: One of the best dishes of that night. Juicy, smoky, and very fatty dipping into the egg yolk. Would love to try it again.
Chiizu tori katsu (chicken and cheese) & Sakana furai (fish): We had 2 people so we ordered both to try a bit of everything. I prefer fish katsu
Chikara udon: Comforting broth and chewy noodles. I like the texture of the grilled mochi.
Hojicha panna cotta: My 2nd favorite dish of the night. Smooth, nutty, not too sweet (if you don't pour the syrup - I don't of course). A perfect finish.
We also get a free strawberry jelly as a birthday present, so very appreciated the staff!
Overall, I’m glad I tried it, but for $100++/person and lacking flavor, I wouldn’t rush going back soon.

April 2026:
Enjoyed a wonderful dinner. Reservations seem mandatory here.

We tried the tasting menu. Had a lot of variety. My favorites include the sashimi (like the yuzu paste with the tuna, yellowtail and trout), the sakana furai (yellowtail katsu), and the oden (daikon, fishcake with sweet potato, lingcod in a delicious broth).

Next time, I would like to try the pork tonkatsu.

Service was friendly and attentive. Would definitely come back again!

A beautifully designed space with a great ambiance, Rintaro delivers the feel of an upscale izakaya serving traditional Japanese dishes like katsu and oden. I have heard about this place for years and was excited to finally try it, but I left feeling it was just alright. You can clearly taste the quality of the ingredients, but nothing truly stood out or felt imaginative. I appreciate the simplicity of Japanese cooking, but several dishes leaned too far into blandness. The cold udon, for example, lacked depth. Aside from a strong hit of ginger, there was not much flavor to pull from. The katsu, made with Berkshire pork, had good meat quality, but the breading did not hold together, which was surprising at this price point. As someone who cooks at home, I made katsu this week and my breading stayed intact, so that was disappointing. The mini cold crab dish was enjoyable, but the portion felt unbalanced. There were too many pickled cucumbers, which overshadowed the crab and left each of us with only a few small bites. I did like the chicken yakitori with sansho, though it is hard to fully appreciate it given how expensive it was for what you get. I understand that dining in San Francisco is expensive, but the pricing here set expectations for creativity and innovation that the food did not meet. I left feeling underwhelmed, and my wallet definitely felt it. I really wanted to like this place, but it did not live up to the hype. I do give props to the service. I really appreciated their attentiveness and willingness to explain the menu to you.

Moku Yakitori-Ya|312 8th Ave, San Franci.text: A great, local izakaya that really shines with kushi (skewer) cuisine. In addition to kushi, they also have several other dishes that are delicious, but especially the chicken-skin gyoza and their karaage. They also have a solid sake selection. I’ve been here several times and am always eager to come back. Heads up that if you come hungry then leaving full will probably run you at least $50 per person without drinks.
The yakitori was cooking to its highest quality!! I got the wagyu yakitoki (skewer) and I was cooked very well!! I highly recommended this place to eat Japanese skewers!

Was in the area and found this restaurant online. Low-key casual little izakaya, off the main street. Atmosphere is cozy and relaxed, service is friendly and helpful, but the food just isn't there.

Almost everything was a little over-seasoned for my taste. And the quality just wasn't what I was hoping for. Gyutan was very tough and dry, skirt steak didn't have the deep beefy flavor I was looking for, and the chicken thigh didn't have much flavor. They also wait to serve all the skewers together at once, so it's not as fresh when it hits the table.

On the bright side, fried squid was good, scallops were flavourful and perfectly cooked, and the portion sizes are generous. I loved the eggplant and their sauce, but they drenched the eggplant with the sauce which made it too salty.

Overall the quality of the ingredients and attention to detail (seasoning) could use some work, but friendly service and comfortable atmosphere. (3.5/5)

Awesome awesome spot. Really incredible vibe in there and great music.

Food is clean eating and delicious, we ordered a ton and loved everything.

The mackerel, mushrooms, scallion chicken, and short rib were incredible. Got one of their highball cocktails as well which was insanely refreshing and a great balance of sweet and sour.

Highly recommend this spot! Seating is limited.
Love it! All the skewers and other dishes we ordered were so so nice. We liked the mochi cakes, fried oysters (such lovely tartar sauce) and the zucchini was amazing.

Izakaya Sozai|1500 Irving St, San Franci.text:
I absolutely love Izakaya Sozai- this place never disappoints! Every dish is consistently delicious, but my personal favorites have to be the ramen and the cartilage (seriously, so good every time). Honestly, you can’t go wrong with anything on the menu, and my whole family always finds something they love whenever we come here.

It’s also an amazing spot to meet up with friends - the vibe is cool, casual, and welcoming, making it perfect for a fun social outing or any kind of get-together. Whether you’re a local looking for a go-to favorite or hosting out-of-town visitors, Izakaya Sozai is always a hit. Such a gem in SF! 👌🏼

Sozai has been a staple in the Sunset for over 16 years and honestly, it never gets old. The menu is carefully put together — every dish earns its place. Some have been here since the beginning and for good reason, they're that good. Not kept around out of habit, just genuinely that delicious. Love that they also switch things up with seasonal specials so there's always something new to try.

If you don't know where to start, the ramen, nankotsu, and maitake fritters are a few must-orders — dishes so good I'd gladly bring them to a deserted island. Trust me on that.

But the food is only half the story. The staff is always warm and attentive without being overbearing, and the vibe just feels like home. This is the kind of local spot that makes the neighborhood what it is — the kind San Francisco doesn't make enough of anymore. If you haven't been, go. If you have, you already know.

What a hidden gem! I came here with some family and we ordered several things from across the menu, and at multiple points in the evening. Every single thing was beautifully presented, stunningly delicious, and we stuffed ourselves. They also have an exceptional collection of Japanese beers and sake that are all divine. Be aware this small place fills up every night of the week, so either plan ahead or get there early. I will absolutely be back the next time I’m in town. The agedashi tofu is the best I’ve ever had.

First time walking it, it felt small but had character, you can tell this was a local's favorite. Only once have I seen this place not packed. Service was great, knowledgeable and kind. Chef was attentive to care to see if any guests had any food restrictions/allergies.

The food was amazing, not the run of the mill Japanese dishes you'd find typically. Many of the dishes I was unfamiliar with. Lotus chips was one of the groups favorites, served with tuna. French fries! Not your normal fry, Japanese yam imported from Japan, everyone loved it. Just thinking about it makes me want some. Ramen bowls were great, flavors were roasted and peppered which is quite unique. You can tell the Chef is not scared to experiment. Bone Marrowww, can't go wrong with that. So much more....Savory and Sweet.

Great experience, Great Staff, Great Food. Definitely should give it a try, I will be back soon.

Now I have to say, for a small joint like Izakaya Sozai, they can sure serve up mini courses that will absolutely get your eyes wide open. Most of the dishes were very delicious and for a very decent price. Izakaya style, of course. My top favorite dishes were the Tako Wasabi, bacon wrapped enoki mushroom and mochi, and Ankimo pate. If you are a foodie like me and are adventurous, I recommend you come here to try it.

Kizuki Ramen & Izakaya（Stonestown Galler.text: Great selection of ramen broth choices. Also a less sodium option is available. Noodles were prepared perfectly. Broth was rich with a lot of flavor. It could have been served at a higher temperature because it tends to become a bit oily once it cools down. The chichen karaage was excellent as well as the Takoyaki which i didn't expect. The geso or squid karage was good. A bit chewy but still good. All in all this is a solid ramen place to go to if you are in the area.

Delicious ramen spot with exceptionally efficient service. Was pretty loud when it’s busy which can be a chaotic dining atmosphere.

Very filling and creamy broth, such yummy flavours! Would definitely come back to try other menu items.

Food is great! The pork Tokusua? is very crispy, the dumpling Gyoza sauce is perfectly seasoned, the noodles have a great chewy texture, and the broth is rich and flavorful. The only downside is that the black pepper flavor in the curry is a bit overpowering. I hope they can tone it down a little in the future.

It was our first time trying this restaurant, and we were genuinely impressed. The ramen was absolutely delicious..rich, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. The ambiance was also very pleasant, making the whole dining experience even more enjoyable.

A special thanks to Edwin for recommending the lower sodium option, which turned out to be just as tasty without feeling too heavy. His service was exceptional..friendly, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. He really made the experience stand out.

We will definitely be coming back and highly recommend this place to anyone looking for great ramen and outstanding service!

Good food. A bit salty for my taste but everything was very tasty. I appreciate how the Kaarage had a good balance of breading and chicken, as a lot of Kaarage I have usually has too much breading and grease. The gyoza (not pictured) was also delicious and juicy.

Curry Hyuga - San Francisco|525 Valencia.text:
Came here for a nice catchup with a friend.

Good:
- Food came out super quickly!
- I liked the special noodles -I got a creamy udon and it was so good and it came with soup and salad too. I liked the miso soup a lot but the bowl was pretty small.
- The staff were nice and speedy with ordering

Bad:
- Super casual vibes
- The portion sizes are on the smaller side

Overall this place is a nice quick food spot, you can get a lot of different food items and I'd try more next time

i thank god every day that curry hyuga exists and is walking distance of me. i eat here every single chance i get. i walk in. their servers are incredibly nice. i order my tonkatsu. the tonkatsu is good. its meaty. its breaded well. the tonkatsu sauce is actual tonkatsu sauce. the miso soup is good. the rice is good quality. my only complaint is that i am not able to eat curry hyuga for every meal every day.

Killer casual Japanese curry in the Mission district.  The signature curry bowl was a slam dunk (we tried both the pork cutlet and chicken katsu — slight preference for the chicken). It was pretty sweet but the table had five different spices to add in, making it easy to get exactly the level of fire you want.

The portion was generous and since they don’t have a large menu, the food came out quickly. It’s a killer spot for a warm homey meal if you need food and you need it STAT.

This place is consistently delicious and never fails to hit the spot, with generous portions that are perfect for sharing, or for keeping leftovers. I especially love their corn potage soup; it’s incredibly comforting and warming, making it an ideal choice on a chilly San Francisco day. Beyond the soup, everything we’ve tried from the menu has been flavorful and satisfying, with just the right balance of spices and richness. The cozy atmosphere makes it a great spot to relax and enjoy a hearty meal, whether you’re stopping by for lunch or dinner.

Easily one of the worst Chicken Karaage I've ever eaten. Greasy, way too much coating, not seasoned well and NOT CRUNCHY at all. Didn't taste fresh at all. Very first bite I got a mouthful of mushy coating and tons of grease with no chicken meat. Six pieces in the order, ate 3 and threw the rest out. Waste of money.
The tartar sauce wasn't good either, tasted like pure mayo. I do not recommend this business

Muracci's Japanese Curry & Grill|307 Kea.text: Got a medium spicy katsu curry and was fairly spicy! So if you are unsure, err on the lower side! Food tasted good, nothing super special. Not the worst curry I've had, but also not the best. Fairly liquidly curry, not thick at all. This is also one of those pick-up only locations, so don't expect to be able to sit down and eat. You order and pick up at the window.

The vegetarian curry sauce is good. However asking about if the tofu and vegetable curry has potatoes the clerk said "I don't think so, but you can order them as an add-on." I did that but it seems from other photos potatoes are already included so I paid the extra charge for no reason. Overall it was pretty expensive for curry ($25).

I don't like that it's takeout only and you can't really tell that from the photos of the food on the reviews.

I got chicken Karaage and ramen with hot curry and fried gyoza.  The ramen and curry were great and the curry was nice and spicy and the gyoza were fresh and flavorful but I would have liked them better of they were pan fried on the bottom instead of deep fried.  I would try other things here for sure so I would recommend ordering ahead of time.

Quite delicious curry and katsu! Ordering takeout at the window is easy, and the portions are large and price is reasonable. The katsu was crispy and not dry, and the curry was flavourful with medium spice being a good level

Ordered a Katsu Curry for dinner via DoorDash.

The curry was subtle and good. However, the katsu was flavorless and unseasoned. I loved the pickled vegetables though!

I probably wouldn’t order again.

Volcano Curry|5454 Geary Blvd, San Franc.text:
The Japanese curry is delicious.  It cooks after  you ordered, so the pork katsu is hot and fresh.

I ordered it medium-spicy, and it wasn't too spicy luckily.  The portion size is huge.

No frills japanese curry place. Curry is EXCELLENT. First bite and it brought me right back to when I was in Japan, 10+ years ago, purchasing my ticket from the vending machine and sliding it over to the chef.

I got a chicken katsu curry and added a potato croquette. Portions are very generous and the curry is to die for. Chicken katsu was juicy and crispy.

I hope I'll have the opportunity to visit SFO again!

I actually really like this place. The curry is very good and they give you veggies along with your protein choice. Came back here after a long time and the flavors are just as I remember it - delicious. What surprised me and caused a 4 star vs 5? I choose to dine in, because I dont want to eat out of plastic. What did I get? Great food in a to-go container, now plastic utensils and they still ask you to put the used plastics in the previous dining wear wash bins. What is even the point? Might as well tell us to throw it in the trash. At least theres still regualr cups for water vs paper cups...

This place is great! Very simple establishment with a few tables, but quick on the orders and you can get up to an insane level of spice! Their prices have steadily risen in the past few years, so I hope that means they are paying their employees well. I recommend their Burger Steak Curry and Onion & Ginger Pork Curry. Beware that parking in this area is considerably hard during lunch time.

Volcano Curry is a causal, counter-serve Japanese curry restaurant in Central Richmond, San Francisco. In addition to curry, they also serve sandwiches, soups, and side orders. The menu is on the smaller side.

The restaurant has signs asking patrons to not play cards, to not bring in outside food, and to not put the to-go boxes in the trash can (there’s a separate bin for those). There’s also a sign to order at the counter before taking a seat, then another that they reserve the right to refuse service to anyone. Lots to read!

Whether dining in or not, everything is served in a microwave safe to-go container. The veggies that come with the curry are carrots and potatoes. I admittedly don’t have much experience with Japanese curries, but this is similar to the few I’ve had in the past. I enjoyed the sauce-to-rice ratio, and it was filling.

For a quicker bite, this would be a good option. It wouldn’t be good for groups or hanging with a friend for a while.

Yoma Cafe - Japanese Bento Box, Sushi, S.text: My husband and I absolutely love Yoma Cafe, this is our favorite bento box spot in the city. They also have some of the best sushi rolls around. no matter who the staff is, they’re always super nice. Mind you, we’ve been here early when they first open, mid afternoon, and for dinner time. We get takeout and order DoorDash frequently, but we like to come and eat inside every once in a while .

Delicious food. Very kind staff.
The sushi burrito has an excellent filling to rice ratio.

It's a TINY place with limited seating, not ideal for seated dining with young kids used to hichairs. But the food is wonderful. Can verify that two year olds will eat it. Was it worth the hassle of keeping my toddler contained? Yes. Will I be ordering takeout next time? Also yes.

Had a lovely lunch here. Delicious cooked sushi, and super fresh sashimi! The sparkling juices were also super tasty. Would highly recommend ordered the fried chicken plate with takoyaki. For $25, that plate could easily feed you for lunch for 3-4 days! Would highly recommend coming here any time. I for one, will definitely plan to come back.

We ordered sushi platters for an event.  The sushi platters were requested for Monday at 4:45pm.  I called the same day to confirm that they would be ready and was reassured that they would be.  We were so pleased with the sushi quality, the presentation of the platters, the timeliness of the service.  Our guests were happy with the quality of the fish and many complimented the choice.  Thank you for making our celebration joyful and for exceptional customer service and presentation.   We will definitely recommend you to others.

I absolutely loved this place. We were hanging out in Golden Gate Park and got hungry, and this was the nearest place that popped up in our radar, and we are so grateful that I did. We went on a Sunday noon for lunch, and it was fully packed. Thankfully, you were able to get a seat in no time. The place is tiny and absolutely cracked, but I think that is what makes this place special. The menu is mostly street style and everyday Japanese fair. You can place your order through the kiosk or at the counter. I got the Bainter box and some sides with it, and my friend got the sushi burrito. The food came within 10 minutes and was piping hot. The portions are insane, and I definitely had over ordered. I had to take away the leftovers. The Test was absolutely spot on, and everything was fresh and delicious. They also came around and gave everybody a free bowl of me so soup, which was such a amazing touch. What stood out for me was the hospitality. The hostess had learnt My name and she addressed me by that name when she came around to give my order as well as in other interactions.

I definitely recommend this place to everyone, and I will also bring my friends here.

Udon Mugizo - SF|1581 Webster St #217, S.text: This restaurant is tucked into a mall in Japantown and really delivers on flavor! The udon was fresh and delicious and the onigiri appetizer was one of my favorite things anywhere. I got the nabeyaki udon and really enjoyed the mushroom in it too. The portions were generous and service was so friendly. The wait time can get long (we waited 30 minutes) but we put our names on the waitlist and explored the mall in the meantime. I would go back!!

Peak lunch was about a 30min wait so we were shopping diff stores in the mall. The food came out quick. I def recommend the mentaiko creamy udon which is light and has seafood taste and the curry udon for a beefy rich taste. The noodles were a yummy texture. The tempura was good, but i prefer more of a light airy batter. Jeff was our waiter and was excellent, super nice!

It is better to get your name on their list! With our name on the list, a party of 5, we had to wait 40 min! It was middle of holidays, the mall was full.
We had three different udons and all of them were really tasty! All ingredients were fresh, the noodle was cooked perfectly and the broth was amazing delicious! Our appetizer was the takoyaki, we forgot to take pics, we ate them too fast, so good they were!
The service was very good, all attendants were polite and they made sure to accommodate well.

Went here on a whim and was very satisfied.

Food: I got the salmon spinach cream sauce udon and the broth was soo good. I don't like cheese and not a fan of green onion eitger, this bowl came with a lot of both and I wish I had scooped it out but I figured I'd eat it as it's supposed to and I ended up pushing them to the side as I ate. It wasn't bad, I just don't prefer them. Noodles were chewy (in the best way) and the salmon was also well seasoned. I nearly drank up the broth by itself.
I also ordered the lychee soda that came in a pouch with some kinda light up device inside. I didn't care too much for the presentation, but the drink itself was good. Not too sweet and good flavor.

Service: service was quick, I got seated right away. The host asked if I needed recommendations, but I already knew what I wanted.

Atmosphere: the layout was good, nice flow from entrance to seating and kitchen. Everything looked clean and well kept. Noise level was moderate, you can talk in a normal voice and be able to hear each other if you're with a group.

I'd come back again to try the mentai cream. Or stick with the spinach because it really was delicious

One of our go to spots for udon. Their signature mentai cream udon is my favorite. Thick saucy cream sauce on the udon is the best. A bit on the heavy side but then it was very tasty. I also like their katsu donburi. Comes with a mini udon so it will definitely be very filling. Our server was super nice and tentative as well. His name is Jeff. Iykyk 😆

Came back a week later (doctors appointment at Kaiser). Had the curry udon and it was decent. Portions were big and Janny was such a nice and attentive server as well!

Marugame Udon|3251 20th Ave, San Francis.text:
We stopped in before heading to the crystal fair happening at Golden Gate Park. This was our first time here. Wow! Just wow!

#1 Fresh Made Udon Noodles!!!! Woooooowwwwww!!!! And Yuuuuuummmm!!!

#2 The menu looked so delightful, we had a difficult time figuring out what to order.

#3 It is a lunch line set-up. First, order your meal, then choose which toppings to add to your dish. THEN, check out the grab-ems -- deep fried seafood,  chicken and veggies -- to add to your meal tray. Then add beverages when you pay.

#4 The tables were clean and seating was ample -- both inside and outside.

#5 The food was AMAZING!!! Perfectly seasoned and cooked. Tasty!

Yuuuuuummmmmm!!!

Service with a smile.

Oh.. and one of the families that were there had more small children than adult hands so staff helped carry their food and drinks to their table. Above and beyond!

We will definitely go back.

Highly recommend.

Always enjoy trying new soup at Marugame Udon in Stonestown even the location in Los Angeles. Never disappoint. Highly Recommended to anyone in NorCal or SoCal area. It’s a good quick fix lunch or dinner if you don’t like to eat heavy.

In terms of affordability, this is the best bang for your buck udon you'll get in town! The food is good, I also love how they cook the egg to perfection. The quality overall dropped a little in the past few years, I miss the days when they would all scream "irasshaimase" whenever you came in. But still a great comfort food spot, especially if you are at the mall running some errand. Expect self-serve kind of service, you go through the counter choosing your food in a kind of "assembly line buffet style" and pay at the end before sitting. Don't skip the counter with some extra toppings and cutlery!

my friends were raving over this as their favorite udon spot. I don’t really see the hype but maybe I need to try a different bowl. It is cafeteria style and I like their add on options. The dining area and where you get your utensils was very dirty. I don’t think I saw one person cleaning the dining room the whole time we were there
Food is always on point, top choices as comfort food. Trying their seasonal tomatama curry for the first time, will definitely be eating this again next time.

Yo Yo's|318 Pacific Ave, San Francisco,.text: Incredibly huge portions at an extremely competitive price point! This whole container cost me maybe $14. You don’t see prices like these in SF often, especially close to FiDi where most restaurants charge an $25+ for a weekday office lunch. It’s takeout only since it’s a small counter space. The couple running this restaurant is so sweet and warm. There will definitely be a line during lunch rush, but they are fast and diligent so the wait goes by quick. I really enjoyed my chicken curry also. The carrot was so sweet and flavorful. Office lunches are a little less bleak with Yo Yo’s around.

A lot of people ask me is this the absolute best restaurant you’ve ever been to in your life? Yes it is. This is the best lunch restaurant I’ve ever been to in my entire life. The food is cooked with love and you can taste the quality in the ingredients and in the recipes. If you start coming here every day, your life will improve.

Yes. Just YES. This place is amazing.

Roughly $10 for a healthy portion of food. Friendly owners. Can pay by venmo.

Parking does suck, but this is one of those ~must try~ places in SF. Plus you’ll save so much money.

Btw, like one table inside. Definitely a grab-n-go spot.

I couldn’t imagine rating this place anything less than 5 stars!! The owner’s personality ALONE was worth the visit; it’s truly an experience that appeals to all six senses!!

As for my udon, it was so delicious I slurped it down before I was able to get a pic! Very generous portion, delicious, and I will definitely be back for sushi next time!!

I’m not used to feeling full for under $10!!!!! 🤩🙏🏽

Service was quick and friendly. Udon was ok for the price. A bit sweet for my taste. Noodles are thinner than usual udon noodles. Portions are big though and can't beat the price.
I will try their curry next time.

SHOWA Le Gourmet Tonkatsu|1550 Howard St.text: Refined, thoughtful, inventive restaurant, service and food. From the restaurant decor's details, the serving platters, plateware, entrance design and sleek design it was exquisite experience. The service from Joe was next level and immaculate. His attention to detail and care of ensuring each guest has a memorable and enjoyable experience alone is worth a visit. The food was refined, delicious and well balanced with delicately fried tonkatsu and freshness from fish, vegetables and somen. Thank you Chef Koji-san for a delicious and memorable food experience!

Celebrated friend's birthday. One host for about five parties so the service was slow at the start. Eventually it became alright once the faster eating parties were established from the slower eating parties and pacing was set. The biggest wait was waiting for the various Tonkatsu - which I assume is due to the cooking time.
Not all the food were to my taste but I ate everything since the food is pricey. I like the Kani Negitoro Tartar Chips and Suika Shave Ice. One of the Fresh Yasai Sticks tasted bitter which I didn't like at all. There was second serving of Donabe Dashichazuke if we were still hungry. I regret add-on Sparkling Lychee Stone dessert since it wasn't worth $25 to me.
Overall, I think I like the stuff the food was served on more than the food. But I've been told that I have a cheap tongue so I don't appreciate these fancy food/experiences.

I genuinely don’t know how they make this profitable at this price point.

The quality of the food and the level of service are honestly surprising for what you pay. Every course feels thoughtful and refined, yet the atmosphere remains relaxed and comfortable rather than overly formal.

One thing I especially appreciated was the pacing. Tasting menus often suffer from long gaps between dishes, which can make the experience drag, but here the flow was smooth and efficient. It never felt rushed, but it also never felt slow or boring.

The portions were perfectly designed. Instead of a few heavy plates, they serve multiple smaller dishes, which keeps you engaged and comfortably satisfied from start to finish.

If there’s a sushi add-on option, absolutely get it. It was one of the highlights.

Even the smallest details are clearly intentional. The restroom is stocked with Le Labo hand wash, the amenities are meticulously curated, and the all-black aesthetic throughout the space shows how much care has gone into every element of the experience.

Right before the dessert course, the chef personally came out to greet the guests, which added a warm and memorable touch.

The only downside? It’s incredibly hard to get a reservation😅

Came here for a birthday celebration. The restaurant is beautifully decorated. The meal starts with a treasure box of small bites. All the dishes following were delicious and light. Moving to the deep fried dishes became heavier. Bites were more filling. The fry was still light. Drinks both alcoholic and non alcoholic were good. Service was amazing an attentive

My first time dining at Showa and it did NOT disappoint. The staff curated the whole dining experience and made us feel welcome. The 12 course menu is an experience that you savor so dont be in a rush. There's something unique about each dish. The Sake pairings were excellent.

Kawanoya|1689 Church St, San Francisco,.text: Love this spot. First time here we only got skewers which were great. This time we tried the katsu, eggplant, and yellow tail and it was all amazing. The atmosphere is very cozy. This is a place you should sit back and relax for your meal— not really the spot for quick in and out.

Amazing! Love the fresh sesame seeds that you can ground to top your katsu and the cabbage was super yummy with the dressing. The teas were really yummy too! First time we tried going for lunch for 6 ppl and couldn’t get in then we went right at 4:30pm on Sunday without reservations and was able to get in! Highly recommend

I’ve dined here 8+ times, and every visit feels like being transported back to Japan. The wabi-sabi inspired interior and decor feel thoughtful and intentional without trying too hard. You can tell a lot of care goes into both the atmosphere and the food. Every dish feels refined over time, like the team is constantly improving and paying attention to the details

Some of my favorites:

• Fried Chicken Cartilage [$9] 10/10
Absolutely addictive. Perfectly crispy with great texture, especially with a squeeze of lemon and their sesame dressing

• Okra Skewer [$4] 9/10
Charcoal grilled perfectly with a nice smokiness. Tastes even better dipped in the sesame dressing with a sprinkle of Japanese chili powder

• Miso Black Cod [$26] 9.5/10
Consistently cooked perfectly every time. Super fresh, buttery, and generously portioned

• Tiger Prawn Katsu Combo [$30] 9/10
They weren’t exaggerating about the juicy tenderloin pork. Crispy outside, tender inside. Add mozzarella cheese on top to take it over the top

Overall, this is one of those rare spots that stays consistently good no matter how many times you return. Between the warm atmosphere, attention to detail, and quality of the food, it’s easy to see why I keep coming back. Whether it’s your first visit or your tenth, this place genuinely delivers every time.

food was excellent, especially grilled hamachi collar and sea bass, house made tofu dishes, and various charcoal skewers. very warm vibes without feeling too fancy. pleasantly full but no wait to sit at the bar for 3 on a thursday night. service was friendly and efficient.

Really amazing food with great attention to detail. The flavors were spot on and the food cooked perfectly. I especially enjoyed the way the dishes were served in high quality cookware and unique serving plates. Servers were friendly and accommodating, but a little on the slow side so give yourself extra time if you might be in a rush. Still very worth it!

Nippon Curry San Francisco|3347 Fillmore.text: Yummy japanese curry and rice. If you're not too hungry, getting one dish and adding extra protein is perfect.  Inside of the restaurant is simple, not many decorations, can get loud when it's busy.
Pretty good taste for the curry, wish I had the pork lol because the crab cakes too small for me

Absolutely delicious Japanese Curry.  Had the chicken katsu.  Chicken was crispy and did not get soggy, curry was flavorful, rice was well cooked and sticky.  Great service as well.  Bonus points for the $4 Japanese IPAs (all day happy hour).  Will definitely be back!

Delicious food and fun beverages at a reasonable price. Shown is chicken katsu curry, and triple croquette curry (crab, pumpkin, and potato - pumpkin was my favorite and the crab was very flavorful). Fast and friendly service.

Would absolutely recommend and would love to come back next San Fran visit.

Overall it was good, I just wish they had a few more small bite options like shishito or agedashi tofu, also tofu curry would have been nice, the aji fish were kind of small. The curry had good flavor though

Matcha Cafe Maiko|1581 Webster St #175,.text: I love coming here for the soft serve! It’s always rich, creamy, and full of flavor. Definitely worth the price. This has easily become one of my favorite spots for ice cream, and it never disappoints.

If you want to try an interesting, weird, but good dessert, check out Cafe Maiko. I had their special sundae with matcha ice cream, corn flakes, adzuki beans, caramel, etc. It was bizarre but fun and tasty.

Wife and I went to this ice cream spot while walking around Japan town in Sf. I'm not a huge fan of matcha, but this ice cream was pretty good! Will be going back the next time we are in town to try the other available favors.

Me and my friend got a duo Thai tea/ube swirl! It was delicious and the cone was a nice sweet balance to the soft serve.

Would come back if ever in the area! :)

Stopped in for the Golden Sho-Gun (24K Golden Soft Serve). The screen in the shop shows a cone fully draped in gold leaf; what I received was a small patch of gold on one side, with the rest of the swirl bare. The matcha itself is creamy and pleasantly bitter-sweet and the waffle cone is fresh, so flavor-wise it’s solid. But the draw here is the gold, and the presentation didn’t match the advertising. At $11.90, it feels overpriced for what’s essentially a gold-leaf accent. Either apply the gold as pictured or update the menu to show what’s actually served. I’d come back, but for the regular matcha—it’s the better value.

Stonemill Matcha|561 Valencia St, San Fr.text:
I was so sad when Stonemill Matcha shut down during COVID, so it was such a great surprise to see it reopened now that I’m back in the Bay Area!

It’s fall season, which means pumpkin spice everything 🎃 We ordered the seasonal Pumpkin Spice Hojicha Latte, the Halloween special Hojicha Pumpkin Pie, Matchazuke, Tamago Sando, and a classic Matcha Latte.

Everything was really satisfying. The Tamago Sando tastes about as close to a Japanese konbini sandwich as you can get in the States (minus the price point). The matcha was just as good as I remembered.

It does get pretty busy around noon, so it’s best to arrive a little earlier to beat the lunch rush. So happy Stonemill is back!

Came here with my partner when we were craving a Japanese style breakfast and this place totally delivered. We ordered the veggie miso soup, rice porridge, teriyaki katsu sandos, along with matcha and hojicha lattes. Everything was comforting, flavorful, and really satisfying. The atmosphere is pretty cool, very traditional feeling with a tea tasting area. All-in-all I'm glad we chose this place for breakfast this morning. I highly recommend to anyone.

I highly enjoyed my iced matcha tea! Very balanced early flavors. The cafe environment was very calming and relaxing. I had a lovely morning just sipping on my drink and people watching. There’s plenty of seating and I could see this as a productive place to get work done or meet up with friends. Agreed with some other reviews that service could be better.. I JUST walked in and one of the male servers said “do you know what you want?” Like no? I just walked in! Will still be back next time to try the matcha latte and matcha pastries.

A great restaurant tucked away in the mission district. I am visiting from NY and love visiting local eats like this.

The food and coffee were delicious! I hade the Japanese Breakfast and the Lavender Matcha Latte with almond milk.

The portions were generous. I left feeling satiated.

The restaurant itself was cozy and laid back. I will be coming back!

Absolutely loved this place! Such a gem for matcha lovers. We tried the hot Ginger Matcha Latte with oat milk and the Matchacano with a shot of hojicha, both were excellent. The matcha was smooth and well-balanced, and the hojicha added a beautiful depth and warmth that took me back to Japan. You can tell they truly care about quality and flavor.o Definitely coming back to try more! 🍵✨

Miyako Old Fashion Ice Cream|1470 Fillmo.text: I visited recently and was pleased to see so many ice cream options. He sells lots of awesome candy that I haven't seen in years. He also offered ice cream samples. He is a very nice person and great to talk to. Be sure to bring cash. Will be back soon to continue supporting a Black business owner.

The shop is run by a very sweet older man, I enjoy every visit. It really feels like a shop you could have been to in the 80s. The menu online is often inaccurate and the hours fluctuate slightly around open and closing times, but I definitely recommend it. Remember to bring cash, it's cash only.

The prices have gone up quite a bit but they definitely needed to, I'm sure they were too cheap to make a living before.

Current flavors on my last visit, if the menu isn't accurate:
Chocolate, vanilla, chocolate chip, mint chocolate chip, strawberry, rocky road, cookies and cream, grasshopper, mocha fudge, lucuma, macapuno, rum raisin, butter pecan, black walnut, caramel praline, New York cherry, rainbow sherbet, green tea, avocado and what looked like ube (it was unlabeled).

Owner is super kind and allows you to sample flavors. Definitely recommend this ice cream spot, there’s a variety of flavors and they’re delicious. 10/10 mom and pop vibe. Cash-only payments accepted.

$4.5 for a hot freshly roasted beef sandwich, with the side of nachos and potato salad in San Francisco is unimaginable! The pistachio ice cream was really good as well. Tom is a really nice man who is dedicated to his ice cream shop and the customers. Come and get yourself a sweet treat!
The owner Tom is so sweet! We had the macapuno ice cream and it was delicious! Definitely recommend stopping by for quality ice cream. They only accept cash.

Origin Lab Coffee & Matcha|211 Sutter St.text:
This spot has such a great vibe and the perfect balance when it comes to its menu selection. There are plenty of delicious options to choose from without feeling overwhelming, and everything feels thoughtfully curated instead of trying to do too much. You can really tell care was put into every detail!
One of my best best!!! Choices was deciding to try the citrus matcha cloud, and wow — it was easily the highlight of my visit. We got more options with that touch of creamy silky velvety texture in the palette it was incredible!!! And I’ve had yuzu and matcha before, but this combination was something special. It was light, refreshing, and incredibly smooth, with the matcha perfectly balanced by a bright, citrusy kick that made every sip exciting and refreshing.
The ambiance is also fantastic — bright, welcoming, and full of good energy. The music playing in the background added such a fun, relaxed, uplifting vibe without ever feeling too loud or overwhelming.
Since it’s a newer spot, everything feels super clean, fresh, and beautifully maintained, which made the entire experience even more enjoyable. Overall, this place feels warm, happy, and very sweet team big shoutout to the great staff behind and from the front scenes super sweet thank you!! definitely somewhere I’m excited to come back to again and again ….

I stopped by Origin Lab on their soft opening day and was seriously impressed. The space feels fresh, lite, thoughtful, and inviting without trying too hard.

The coffee is excellent, smooth, balanced, and clearly made with care. You can tell they’re paying attention to quality and detail. I also tried their matcha, and it was one of the best I’ve had, vibrant, not overly sweet, and really well prepared. It’s rare to find a place that does both coffee and matcha this well.

The owner and team are friendly, welcoming, and genuinely excited to be there making coffee and matcha with expertise, and it was especially cool to see the craft going into it. Highly recommend checking it out!
It’s my new favorite spot! Absolutely love the vibe and the staff!! They have so many options of matcha that I want to try all of them! Definitely recommend this place 🤍
owner and staff are soo sweet! drinks are delish and well worth the wait. very cute inside and out. love the vibes already went back twice (in one day) 🫶🏽

I can’t lie, this was the best matcha i’ve ever had. I got the strawberry matcha and it was amazing. The strawberry cream was something i’ve never had before, normally it’s just a strawberry syrup, but the cream was game changer! I highly recommend coming here! This is the second time this month i’ve come here!!

OISHII MATCHA|2299 Market St Apt A, San.text: My friends and I stopped by OISHII Matcha and had such a great experience. We tried the ube drink and the ice cream, and both were absolutely delicious. The ube drink was perfectly balanced, and the ice cream was rich, creamy, and full of flavor. What really made the visit stand out was the service—Youcef was incredibly friendly, helpful, and took the time to answer our questions and make recommendations. The whole experience was welcoming from start to finish. We’ll definitely be back the next time we’re craving a sweet treat in San Francisco! 🍵💜🍦

One of the best boba places in the SF city! A must stop by if you’re in Castro!
Their soft serves are to die for♥️ my favorite is the ube. The owner is super friendly and gave us a sample of matcha soft serve.

The matcha soft serve is very yummy! Not too sweet, and you can really taste the matcha flavour, not watered down or too milky. The ube matcha was just in general bad and watery. Staff was really kind and accommedating. No space inside the store to sit, but the two tables outside are good for when it's nice out.
very cute small business! the matcha tastes wonderful and the ube is so good. 10/10 would recommend.

Amazing. Both workers on 1/19/2026 were so nice. Someone had taken my drink or we had just missed it and he remade it for me including an extra special treat on the side. They also gave us a free sample of their matcha. The matcha soft serve and strawberry matcha latte were both so delicious I am going to come back another time.

Constance Tea & Matcha|3512 Balboa St, S.text:
What an incredible experience! Today was their soft opening, and this is truly the first store I’ve ever seen serving freshly milled matcha. This is not your typical matcha shop—it’s something completely unique and on another level.

You can actually taste the difference when the matcha is freshly milled. The flavor is richer, smoother, more aromatic, and far more complex than the matcha served at regular cafés. Every drink feels like a premium tea experience rather than just another trendy beverage.

We ordered the Freshly Milled Matcha Latte and the Black Matcha. From what I understand, they may be the first shop serving Black Matcha, which made the experience even more special. The Black Matcha was rich, bold, flavorful, and unlike anything I’ve ever had before. It was easily one of the best tea drinks I’ve ever tasted.

Even on their very first day, people were waiting over an hour for their drinks, which says a lot. Normally, I would never wait that long, but after trying it, I can honestly say it was worth every minute. The quality, craftsmanship, and attention to detail are exceptional and unlike anything else I’ve experienced in the Bay Area.

If you’re a matcha lover, this is a destination worth visiting. A truly one-of-a-kind concept and a refreshing addition to the tea community. Congratulations on the soft opening—I can’t wait to come back and see what’s next! 🍵✨

Constance Tea & Matcha is an absolute game-changer for the SF Bay Area. They are the first and only tea studio in the area to freshly grind Tencha leaves into matcha in-house using commercial-grade stone mills. Getting to watch the traditional matcha-making process right in front of you is incredibly cool and unique.

The Vibe
The space itself is super clean, beautiful, and radiating calm, Zen vibes. It’s the perfect place to unwind and enjoy a high-quality brew.

The Drinks
We ordered the Reserve Matcha and the Black Matcha. Both drinks were absolutely delicious and packed with that rich, savory umami flavor. Too often, matcha at other shops tastes watered down, but here, the exceptional quality of the tea really shines through. You can taste the freshness in every sip.

The Service
While waiting for our drinks, I had the pleasure of meeting one of the co-founders, Bill, and his lovely wife. They are incredibly passionate about their craft and so knowledgeable! I walked away learning so much about the art of tea just from chatting with them.

Final Verdict: Exceptional quality, beautiful space, and wonderful owners. This is officially my new go-to matcha spot!

If you are a matcha lover, this place is an absolute must-visit! What makes this shop so special is that they serve freshly milled matcha, which is something I have only experienced in Japan. You can immediately taste the difference. The matcha is incredibly fresh, smooth, aromatic, and full of rich flavor. It has a depth and complexity that you simply don’t get from regular matcha drinks.
Every sip feels like a premium tea experience. The quality, craftsmanship, and attention to detail are outstanding. It’s clear that a lot of care goes into every cup they make.

This is not just another trendy matcha shop—it’s a unique concept that brings an authentic Japanese matcha experience to the Bay Area. The freshly milled matcha latte was exceptional, and easily one of the best matcha drinks I’ve ever had.

Congratulations on the soft opening! Wishing the team great success. I will definitely be back, and I highly recommend all matcha lovers come and try it for themselves. 🍵✨

very pretty cafe ! the matcha is freshly milled and one of the employees gave us a tour of their process and milling machines

i got the fresh milled reserve matcha latte which is pretty expensive (~$12) but it’s really good! there is a more normal priced matcha latte as an option as well. would come back, highly recommend 🤍

Checked out the newly opened fresh milled matcha spot in Outer Richmond. Glad I did it.
A wonderful cozy spot. Bench seating along the wall and a small counter.
Many matcha options including fresh milled matcha, stone ground teas, freshly ground teas pulled to order, brewed teas.
Friendly and efficient service.
I chose the classic fresh milled matcha latte and it was perfect. No added sugar to allow me to enjoy the matcha.
Wonderfully made and worth a future visit.
Check this place out.

Asha Tea House|17 Kearny St, San Francis.text:
Stumbled upon this with my friend, I got the regular iced matcha latte and she got the blood orange matcha latte. The iced matcha latte was perfect~ the blood orange matcha latte was interesting in a good way! You taste both the orange and the matcha, it was a perfect  balance of the two.

It’s a super cute matcha cafe. They sell matcha here and other teas. Service was fast and they were all very helpful.

I really thought this place was cute, and their menu was amazing with so many delicious options. But when I got my drink, the matcha wasn't very strong, there was a lot of ice (I got light ice), and the Asian pear I got in it was chunky and not very flavorful. The size was also pretty small, and this whole drink cost $8.15!! I also asked for it 'for here,' but it came in the same cup as the 'to-go' cups. Overall, it wasn't that good and I would likely not come again. Maybe their other drinks are better though!
Excellent tea shop. Lots of cute drinks and a tea bar. Also excellent HK iced tea with great caffeination.

Very fun tea shop in San Fran. Their matcha was wonderful, smooth, lots of flavor and a bright green. They have a lot of options to add as far as additional flavors, I got the lavender with no additional sweeter and it was perfect. If you appreciate good matcha but also like to change up from the basic plain matcha, this is a great stop. The barista was very friendly and there is lots of great indoor seating options.

Dropped in for the Strawberry Matcha and it was awesome I came back later in the day for another. Really sweet and beautiful Tea house with nice staff. The Matcha is just perfect and doesn’t taste like supergrass. But is very tasteful and taste well down. I recommend the Strawberry Matcha that comes with strawberry puree with real fruit unlike other Matcha places that may only do syrup. They also have huge pastries that look delicious too. Give this little tea house a try. You won’t regret it.

Doppio Coffee & Brunch|1551 Mission St,.text:
Seriously incredible brunch spot in SOMA.

Came with my family and got to try a few different menu options, and they were all incredible. The best thing I had was the doppio crepe, which is a turkey, egg, and avocado savory crepe. It was the perfect blend of flavor and the perfect amount of avocado and cheese.

We also got a salad and avocado feta toast which was also great. They allow you to customize your sides for your salad which is really nice and there are tons of vegan options.

Will definitely be back!

I visited Doppio Coffee & Brunch today and had a great experience. They offer delicious vegetarian breakfast options, and the coffee was excellent — they use Lavazza beans, which made the coffee really smooth and flavorful. The café was very clean, calm, and relaxing, with beautiful Turkish music playing in the background. It’s a perfect place for chatting and spending time with friends. The staff were also very friendly and welcoming.

New favorite breakfast spot. The tomato mozzarella toast is hands-down one of the best I’ve ever had. Fresh, perfectly balanced, and so satisfying. Paired it with a matcha and it was the perfect start to the day. Highly recommend

Yummy toasts! I went with the caprese and it was chefs kiss. Their Gluten free bread option was actually yummy and soft. Iced coffee was deliciously dark and strong. Enjoyed the vibe of their quirky tiled tables. Theres nice natural lighting from the storefront windows. Baristas were friendly 👍👍 got to come back for a cappuccino!

Spent a week in SF and right across from our daughter’s apartment we tried Doppio on our last morning in town. We should have come here everyday!!!
We drink half caf coffee and this was hands down the best cup we have ever had from a coffee shop. Bold and tasty. Not watery and lacking flavor like most.
The menu is huge so we struggled with what to order. Got the parfait which has Greek yogurt, fruit and granola. Very good.
The avocado toast with an extra egg was delicious.
Place is super clean and reasonable.
Definitely a great place!!

Sophie's Crepes|1581 Webster St #275, Sa.text:
exactly what i needed!! they were very generous with the strawberries 🍓!!

i asked how long to wait, they said 5-10mins but i got it less than 5mins!
i got the strawberry + nutella for $10.97 (including tax). in my opinion it’s a bit pricey, but considering there were so many strawberries, it’s kinda okay. would go again but not often cuz of the price lol

SO GOOD. We got the mixed fruit crepe and it reminded me so much of the fruit sandwiches we enjoyed in Japan. Quick service and friendly staff too. The price wasnt bad and it was wonderful that they were open later.
So yum! My friends and I all tried it for fun and we enjoyed it. Who knew icecream in a crepe would be so yummy! Our favorite flavors were black sesame and the banana walnut!
