When asked for a cup to get water, they charged me $0.48 and gave me a cup and told me to get water from the water fountain outside. The water fountain was out of service . So I went back to let them know. Someone looked like manager or owner told me, you can buy a water ，，we normally don’t give out water here. I said but I got charged for the tiny paper cup. She then got me some water without simile , hello ? Will not be back for the service . Not to mention the ice cream scoop was from someone else already melting a bit when they handed to me .

Tucked away in the heart of San Francisco’s Japantown, Sophie’s Crepes is a long-standing favorite that’s been serving delicious Japanese-style crepes since 2000. The cozy little shop, with its bright yellow trim and casual charm, has an inviting atmosphere that feels both nostalgic and welcoming.

I ordered a Japanese Special crepe, matcha ice cream with red bean paste, and it did not disappoint. The crepe was freshly made, warm, and slightly crispy on the edges while soft and chewy in the center—just the way it should be. The matcha ice cream had a rich, earthy flavor that paired beautifully with the sweet and mildly nutty red bean paste. It wasn’t overly sugary, which made it even more enjoyable.

Even though the shop was fairly busy, the service was quick and friendly. There’s a small seating area inside and a few tables outside where you can sit and enjoy your crepe while people-watching or just soaking in the Japantown vibe. It’s a fun spot to take a break, especially on a warm afternoon.

Whether you’re in the mood for a sweet treat or just looking for a little comfort food with Japanese flair, Sophie’s Crepes is definitely worth a stop. The quality has remained consistent over the years, and it’s clear why this place is a local favorite.

Mochill Mochidonut|1737 Post St #395, Sa.text:
Really delicious Japanese style doughnuts in Japantown. Being from the East Coast I’m a big doughnut guy and these mochi doughnuts are legit. Super flavorful and the perfect crisp/chewy ratio.
Dos doughnuts up  🍩🍩

We love their donuts so much. The textures were so good, soft, fluffy, a little crispy on the outside and the sweetness of the glazing was perfect. We got Matcha and cookie and cream donuts. There was no waiting line. There were a promotion for a half price for their anniversaries on that weekend. I want to go back to try more of their flavors. It is located on the second floor.

📍 𝑴𝒐𝒄𝒉𝒊𝒍𝒍 𝑴𝒐𝒄𝒉𝒊 𝑫𝒐𝒏𝒖𝒕, Japantown 🍩
Always loved this place, their mochi donuts are delicious! 😋

What I ordered:
White Choco Berry
Matcha Oreo
Black Sesame
Hojicha
Cinnamon Sugar
Matcha
Taro
Fruity Pebbles

Mochi donuts are made with rice flour which gives these donuts their light and chewy texture. The flavours lean towards more typical Asian flavours which I love! For instance you’ll find flavours like taro, black sesame, and matcha.

6 for $18, otherwise the donuts are $3.30-$3.50 each.

No wait at all during the week around lunchtime.

Mochi donuts are a unique type of donut, super soft, and fluffy and there are over 25 flavors to choose from. A higher price point than your average donut but is worth it. Highly recommend it on any day of the week!

Japan Center Malls|1737 Post St, San Fra.text: We bought some delicious ramen and some stuff at Daiso. The ramen was delicious and still retained it's original flavor. I got a wooden alarm clock and some Chocorooms at Daiso. The shopping experience is easy and convenient, with a lot of bathrooms. The experience was easy because short lines and convenient times. The staff around stores were nice and friendly. But the east 2nd building is usually empty at 4 or 5. I liked the visit, and there were some waterless yrinals. The prices were fair.

Cool plaza. It was quiet when I visited so would like to see a peak time. The Peace Pagoda is still under refurbishment, so don’t make a special trip until that’s completed.  Would return, particularly for a festival or community celebration.

This is a fun place to visit. Lots of shops and restaurants that will fulfill your Japanese cravings.

Parking is always a consideration and keep in mind that tall cars may not fit in the parking structure as the ceiling is very low.

I thought parking was reasonable.

During our visit, we ate at Benihana and enjoyed everything about it. The hostess even took a photonof us, printed it and gave it to us for a souvenir.

Some of the shops have very unique and interesting toys, momentos, food, and Japanese pop culture items. Very nich and fun just to walk around.

I did not get the chance to try the bakery, I was full from Benihana.

Best of luck on your trip.
Very beautiful and clean Japanese mall. You can get everything here . My nieces favorite mall.
Very nice experience with alot yo watch.  Will definitely go back there. Very good to go with your family and friends. Good food and you can find lots of cute things. And kids will love it

Brothers Restaurant|4128 Geary Blvd, San.text: A friend from Seoul recommended this place and it was an absolute delight to experience Korean food with friends. The service was perfect! I had tons of questions on recommendations and how to eat certain things and they were very kind and patient explaining things to us. ALL the food was really amazing and it felt and tasted authentic! Strongly recommend this place whether you love Korean for or open to try!

I knew from the moment I stepped into this restaurant it was going to be awesome. The air was smoky, the food smelt incredible and the atmosphere was lively.

We cooked the meat at the table on coal, and there were heaps of complimentary sides that were amazing. Everything was delicious, I can’t wait to go back!

Highly recommend this place!

came here with a group of 8 ppl on a sarurday night at like 8:15. we waited maybe 10 mins. the staff is incredible, like truly a highlight of our night. thank you for being so accommodating and warm.

the food is incredible and the charcoal/ wood stoves are harder and harder to come by. i would recommend the pre-fixe + adding a few more dishes on the side depending on group size.

wear woven fabric, the smoke will stick to your knits. overall 10/10, fav kbbq in sf hands down

We got the tasting menu for 2. Perfect portion size and really good quality meat. The spicy tofu soup and the spicy pork were a standout. The wood used in the barbeque is also excellent. Amazing staff and amazing service.

It had been a while since I had Korean BBQ, so I was really looking forward to it. This was my second time at this place, and the food was just as good as I remembered. We got the two-person tasting menu, plus the Korean corn cheese and steamed egg. The ribeye was super tasty—especially wrapped in lettuce with all the side dishes. The corn cheese and steamed egg were pretty good too. The vibe was nice and laid-back. We showed up a little after 5 PM and it wasn’t busy at all. The staff was super friendly, and overall it was a great experience. I’d definitely come back, especially with a bigger group next time.

Han Il Kwan|1802 Balboa St, San Francisc.text:
Han Il Kwan is a traditional Korean restaurant that has been a staple for Korean food in the area, focusing on classic dishes and Korean barbecue rather than trying to modernize the experience. The restaurant has a casual, comfortable atmosphere that feels built around bringing people together for a full meal. It is the type of place where ordering several dishes for the table works best, especially if you want to experience the variety of flavors that Korean cuisine offers.

The Korean barbecue is the main attraction, and it delivers with flavorful marinated meats cooked to a good balance of tenderness and seasoning. The galbi is one of the standout choices, with a sweet and savory marinade that pairs well with the grill flavor. The bulgogi and other meat options are also worth exploring if you are dining with a group since sharing multiple plates gives you a better sense of the menu. The portions are generous, which makes the overall experience feel like a good value.

The banchan selection is another highlight and adds a lot to the meal. The side dishes come with a variety of textures and flavors that balance out the heavier barbecue dishes, and they feel like an important part of the overall experience rather than just an extra. Beyond barbecue, dishes like tofu soup, seafood pancake, steamed egg, japchae, and cold noodles make Han Il Kwan a good choice even if you are not specifically coming for grilled meat.

Service is generally efficient, with dishes coming out quickly even when the restaurant is busy. The popularity does mean waits are common, especially during dinner hours, so arriving earlier or planning ahead is helpful. The restaurant works especially well for groups because the menu is designed for sharing and the atmosphere naturally fits a longer meal.

Overall, Han Il Kwan is a reliable spot for traditional Korean comfort food with strong portions, flavorful dishes, and a menu that covers all the classics. It is not trying to reinvent Korean cuisine, but that is part of its appeal. If you are looking for a satisfying Korean meal with great barbecue, generous banchan, and a lively dining experience, this is a place worth adding to your list.

The menu is variety of the items. Very traditional, authentic and super delicious. The owner lady was very friendly and accommodating. She offered us some dessert sweet fermented rice drink was amazing. We will come again. Thank you

The food was great—especially the Korean BBQ, which I really enjoyed. The service was good overall, but I wish we had a bit more attention from the waiter. At one point I wanted to order more food, but the waiter wasn’t around. I’d recommend ordering everything you want right away. Still, the food itself was very good and worth it.

Very tasty and great service! I love the side dishes so much. I highly recommend coming here :)

The tofu soup was amazing. The amount of food is really a lot! We left super full and very satisfied.

Thank you so much!

The best Korean food I had in the Bay Area. Great portion for the BBQ, every dish is so flavorful, I absolutely love everything including the free small dishes.

Just be aware it’s kind of hard to find parking in the residential area.

um.ma|1220 9th Ave, San Francisco, CA 94.text:
The Korean food here is really good. The flavors are a bit on the sweeter side compared to my usual preference, but overall still very enjoyable.
The kimchi soup was super good — definitely highly recommend it.
The outdoor seating area could be a bit more organized, but everything else was nice and comfortable.
Overall, a good spot if you’re craving Korean food.

We attended a wedding dinner here, and it turned out to be such a wonderful experience.

Food: A variety of dishes were served family-style, including chicken wings, rice, kimchi, soup, and beef. Everything tasted fresh and flavorful. The wings were perfectly cooked and full of flavor, and the kimchi really stood out as our favorite part of the meal. It was all so enjoyable that I’d happily come back again just to have those dishes one more time.

(I Can remove all Negative rev from your business. If you want then please let me)

Chicken wings were very yummy and extremely crispy, tteokbokki was average. Overall, food was good but not life changing. Patio was warm and cozy, but indoor vibe was not great. Would recommend for a yummy casual dinner with a nice vibe and good value.

Best Korean food in town! And at a decent price! Highly recommend their bibimbap, corn cheese, dak galbi and kimchee prawns. Most of the seating is at the back of the restaurant, each table sits 4 comfortably. Consider a reservation, especially during weekends. This place gets packed. They also have the BEST side dishes.

Came here on a weeknight, never had trouble getting a table. The food is good but not great, partially due to the prices to portion ratio.

We ordered teokkboki, LA galbi, beef bulgogi, pork belly, kimchi fried rice, bibimbap. Everything was well cooked. Galbi wasn’t too fatty and was delish, bulgogi was slightly too sweet but still tasty, kimchi fried rice lacked seasoning or taste, bibimbap had a unique western take on it, pork belly was mid.

One disappointing thing - banchan (small side dish) isn’t free flow - it’s just season quails eggs, spiced broccoli, yellow pickled radish and kimchi. It’s disappointing considering these are inexpensive sides even from a restaurant owner perspective.

I’ve dined here on multiple occasions, I’d personally recommend: LA galbi, scallion pancake, soonduboo jjigae, rock n roe rice and charred gai lon.

Service staff were good! We enjoyed our food and service, just oddly underwhelmed by needing to pay for side dishes which are free flow in majority of the Korean joints I’ve been to. It’s not a big deal, just in principle?

Would come again if I’m in the area. Total came to about $50+ per person with tips.

Kogi Gogi BBQ|1358 9th Ave, San Francisc.text:
10/10, 5 stars, so amazing. We went on a Sunday evening around 5:30-6pm and it was quite busy with a few parties ahead of us, but it was well worth the wait.

We got spicy beef bulgogi, marinated galbi, garlic chicken, and ribeye. I would definitely recommend choosing marinated meats as the bulgogi and galbi were our favorites, but I also really enjoyed the other two using the provided dipping sauces. All the sides were really tasty as well and we also got the corn cheese because duh and it was delicious.

We waited for a bit when we arrived due to them being so busy, but I took that as a sign the food would be really good and I was right. Once we were seated, we received prompt service the entire meal getting drink refills and more entree options. All of the wait staff was extremely kind and continuously checked on us the whole time to see if we needed anything else.

Very, very good 👌🏼

The premium for $60+ isn’t worth it imo but there are goods

The goods:

Service is so good. Our waiter was fast and attentive.

Enjoyed the Kpop songs along with the mv playing

Ac is strong and cooling

Lots of meat variety and sides. The corn cheese is unique and yummy. The sweet and spicy rice cake has a chewy and nice slight crunch from deep fry but there’s a bit too much sauce that’s overwhelming

You can ask for half orders by asking your waiter directly

The bads:

Although it had A5 wagyu and filet mignon, 1 person can get a maximum of only 2 small A5 wagyu pieces and 1 small filet mignon piece. It wasn’t very obvious since the tablet said 2 per person with a maximum of 4 orders. The filet mignon wasn’t worth it but the wagyu was good

The truffle beef had no smell or taste of truffle and the meat tasted average quality. Would not recommend tteokbboki. The sauce taste similar to watery ketchup with just a smidge of gochuchang. The rice cake also wasn’t chewy

After ordering, you can’t add or look at any food items for 5 mins and you’re limited to 4 meat and 4 sides at a time. It would’ve been a bit easier if you can at least look and add but not submit the order

Came here for a friend's birthday dinner with a party of 15.
We had a reservation at 6:30PM and we didn't get seated until around 6:50PM. The tables can't be combined so there were 2 separate tables for all of us. Layout could be a problem if you prefer to all sit together in a larger group.

The food is great. They are tentative and will constantly change the burnt griddle plate. They constantly take our order so we can keep the flow going. The quality of the meat is great and flavorful. We enjoyed the overall experience. The place is a little small and I would come here with a smaller group of friends.

Me and my wife stoped here after visiting the Japanese tea garden!
We got quickly welcomed and seat, we got 5 dish to star and were delicious,  after a quick change of grill from the staff, we went w second round of dishes, the corn side dish was  super tasty, but what stand out to us was the fried calamari, they were stunning and tasteful ( I’m still dreaming about that calamari ) , little gem in the sunset district!
We definitely enjoyed ourselves in this place and we will be back!!

Time of review 03/10/2024. The prices are based on time of review

~ Hands down my favorite. We go here so much, the owner recognizes me ~

Meat: 5 out of 5 ⭐️ $42 a person
You can either order the regular or premium menu. We usually get regular, but have tried the premium. Both are really good. Meat is good and they give you a lot.

Sides: 10 out of 5 ⭐️
The sides are why I keep coming back. The rice cakes are phenomenal. The corn cheese is superb. Soups are delicious. I’ve tried MANY Korean bbq ayce, Kogi will always be #1 when it comes to the sides.

Staff: 5 out of 5 ⭐️
Great staff, always changing the grill even before you can ask. Attentive and gets your orders fast.

This is a safe pick whenever picking Korean ayce. They get busy during the weekends. They have a special Monday-Thursday, I believe it’s $32 a person.
Overall I spent $91 + $10 = $101 for 2 people

SAN HO WON|2170 Bryant St, San Francisco.text:
San Ho Won! truly sets a high standard. The quality of the meat and ingredients is excellent, and you can really taste the freshness in every bite.

Everything we ordered was absolutely delicious, and the flavors were authentic and well-balanced.

The service was outstanding — attentive, friendly, and professional from start to finish.

Highly recommend if you’re looking for high-quality Korean BBQ with amazing service!

I’ve been tracking San Ho Wan for a while now, but I always seemed to remember too late, leaving only the "late evening" slots available. I finally got on the ball and scored a prime-time reservation, and the experience confirmed exactly why this place holds its Michelin Star.

The Vibe & Value:
What’s refreshing here is the casual atmosphere. In a city where some 1-star establishments are pushing the $300+ price point, San Ho Wan is a breath of fresh air. It’s accessible, yet maintains a standard of technical excellence that some luxury tasting menus miss. While S.F. has plenty of good Michelin choices on the less expensive end, this is a standout for modern Korean BBQ.

The Technical Audit:

The Gyeran-jjim (Steamed Egg): Absolute technical perfection. The rise was consistent and the texture was soufflé-light.

The Galbi / Short Rib: The Maillard reaction on the beef was lacquered perfectly. You can see the quality of the sourcing in the marbling—rich, rendered beautifully, and handled with real precision.

The Banchan & Sides: Every detail, from the vibrant ferments to the crispness of the ssam (lettuce wraps), was handled with care. The kitchen operates with a visible discipline that you can see in the cleanliness of the stone pots and the clarity of the presentation.

Pro-Tip on Access:
If you can’t snag a reservation through the system, they do have counter space for walk-ins. It’s a great fallback if you’re in the Mission and want to bypass the online calendar.

The Verdict:
If you can navigate the competitive reservation system, it’s a must-visit. It delivers a high-functioning kitchen performance without the "stiff" service often found in the Mission or SoMa fine-dining scenes.

The food was amazing! We were celebrating my partner’s birthday, and the team made the experience even more special. We also have shellfish and dairy allergies, and they went above and beyond — they modified dishes and even provided us with a separate menu to fit our needs. Everything we ordered was delicious. We had the glazed short ribs, BBQ prime ribs, white rice, and the beef potstickers — all SO good! They also serve a lovely tea at the end, which was such a nice touch.

And I have to mention — the bathroom is insane. So beautiful and such a vibe.

We’re very excited about this place, and it didn’t disappoint!

We sat at the bar counter which you could see the kitchen, it’s kinda cool and has that chill vibes perfect for a date night.

We had beef tartare and egg soufflé as appetizers, I wish the beef tartare has a bit more acidity in it, also lacks creativity in the seasoning. Egg soufflé was pretty impressive, the spicy anchovy broth that goes with it was really good.

We ordered galbi and beef tongue as main along with rice. Meat texture was excellent, yet it would be better if there are some variations in marinate.

Dessert was impressive, it’s structured like a tiramisu but with korean ingredients and flavors in it, very well balanced and perfect end to the meal.

Service was okay, for a Michelin restaurant I expect more interactions with servers. Several servers also seemed not very knowledgable about the menu and dishes.

Overall pretty good value for the quality of food, recommended!

I had such a lovely time. Not only was the food delicious but the staff was so kind and attentive. I emailed a few weeks in advance to inquire about my food allergies and when I arrived, the team remembered and gave me a marked-up menu of items I could avoid. I then asked for their recommendations for the best experience and everything was incredible. I ordered the Kimchi and Bacon pancake, Baek Kimchi, Prime beef rib cap and grilled tteok. Couldn’t tell you what I liked the most because each item was perfectly plated, deliciously seasoned and the prime beef was so tender. 10/10.
MJ also helped make the experience top tier. He was frequently checking in, quick to clear the table, and guaranteed my tea was always warm.

K Soul Chicken|1710 Polk St, San Francis.text: K Soul Chicken is the perfect place for a little Korean fried chicken and snacks pick me up! Their chicken is fried to perfection, and offer so many different flavors. Posted right along Polk St, so it is the perfect place to get the party started over a little beer & casual dinner with friends before hitting up the bars. Aside from the fried chicken, the bbq pork plate and mandu were also delicious and filling. They’ve been serving the community for 35 years, and the owner is so kind and welcoming. Definitely hit them up if you’re in the mood for some delicious KFC & Korean staples.

I’m just gonna say it: it smelled weird in here. Damp, like a locker room. It wasn’t bad enough to deter us but enough to color the experience negatively — I imagine I’d probably rate this high if I had it for takeout (and, it seems like most people coming in were there for pickup).

Still, sometimes these rough-around-the-edges mom and pop shops can be surprisingly good, and that was true for the Korean fried chicken. Spicy, crispy yet super saucy, and mixed in were these lovely, chewy little rice cake tteokbokkis.

I’d pass on the bibimbap, as the bulgogi was chewier than expected and the oil was a little heavy handed.

I have a feeling I would’ve liked this more after a drunken night out, so perhaps this great Korean spoon is best saved for late nights with friends.

This place was so delicious! This place was always recommended from our friends. Finally got to try it. We had a kimchi fried rice and the fried chicken was so good. The owner was super nice and he treated us so well we will for sure. Be back our new favorite Korean spot of all time.

A nice place to get Korean snack foods. The proportions are enough to easily feed two people and it's pretty good. The restaurant has a very casual atmosphere, and it being in a nice neighborhood, it's nice to hang out for a bit.

Korean Soul Chicken, very clean and crispy chicken. Great service and a cozy place to sit. The #10 plan wings and the #1 Galbi boneless chicken were great! Will, a local of San Francisco, helped us make a great selection and the wings were awesome!  Our daughter loved the Korean corn dog and said it was one of the best she's had. Maid with beef sausage! Thanks so much Korean Soul Chicken and "Will" for the great hospitality in San Fran!!! Loved it

Jijime|5524 Geary Blvd, San Francisco, C.text: Came by around 7:30pm, the day before Thanksgiving and was seated right away. Ordered a yuzu jello alcoholic drink, soju, okonomiyaki fries and the chocolate tarfufo for dessert. The yuzu jello was fun to eat. The fries had the flavors of okonomiyaki, it even came with sliced cabbage at the bottom. Fries were crispy. We came to celebrate a friend’s birthday and the staff brought a birthday candle with the dessert. The chocolate tarfufo is basically chocolate ice cream. What made the night special was the staff. She was very welcoming and sweet. They also have a happy hour menu which looks intriguing.

We had another fantastic meal at Jijime - try to stop every time we are in town. Both of us had SIZZLING BIBIMBAP - one chicken and the other bulgogi. Arrived at table sizzling making rice and veggies crunchy on the bottom. Very yummy. We also tried the corn cheese. It arrived sizzling and was very yummy.

The staff was very attentive without being overly invasive - dirty dishes were picked up immediately and my water glass was never empty. We will be back the next time we are in The City.

I just had a really pleasant experience eating lunch at this restaurant. The chicken dish I had was mildly spicy 🌶️ just like the way I like it, the ambiance of the place was very pleasant and the service was very friendly! I give them 5 stars 🌟

Bibimbap flavor was good, though spicy pork was not very spicy.  Also, it could have been more food for the price.  No free small plates like in other Korean restaurants, so an order of kimchi was extra.  However, that maybe just for barbeque places which they don't have.

this place has great korean food if youre not looking for a kbbq spot! we’ve come several times, and we definitely leave satiated. However, i would have liked to get side dishes with our main dishes as i’ve had in other korean restaurants before; some kimchi or pickled radishes would be great. I did ask for some fresh kimchi, but it was only brought out after asking for it, and when i tasted it, it wasnt as spicy as i expected kimchi to be.

the spicy kimchi soup (with added silky tofu) was also not very spicy; maybe my tolerance to spice has increased? 😅nonetheless, the flavor of the soup was really good. and the texture of the tofu was soo silky smooth. my brother who doesnt typically like soup, was eating it uppp.

Kokio Republic|711 Geary St, San Francis.text:
Food was great.
Pork tacos - good flavors, a little sweet
Bulgogi taco - really nice mixture of Korean flavors in a taco

Chicken sandwich (fire) - for me it was ok 3/5. I feel like the coleslaw mixture needed a little more flavor. And the breading needed a little more flavor. Chicken texture was nice. Bread was nice and soft.

Chicken tenders  - great selection for my kids. They were crispy and very flavorful.

One of the best Korean spots in the city for a quick meal! If you’re looking for a late night place to eat… this is your spot. Service was friendly and nice, you can see them cooking your meal in the kitchen. Very little seating but this place is meant for a quick go! The tacos were delicious and juicy… I still think about them right now. 10/10 will come again.

This is a great spot for some quick Korean food. It is located in the tenderloin district.

If you’re staying in a hotel, it’s likely you’ll be in this general area. Most of the hotels seem to be located here.

I lived in San Francisco for years. I moved away last year. But, this is a good little quaint spot to check out.

You get a lot for you money here and the quality is great. My favorite item on the menu is the kimchi rice balls. They're perfectly fried and have a great flavor. They have menu items I havent seen anywhere else. I have only ordered delivery from here but I would really like to eat in to get the best experience.

Late night Korean fried chicken cravings? You've come to the right place! They deliver on Doordash too, which is even better.

I ordered the Boneless Soy Garlic Fried Chicken, which was okay. The 6-piece was quite expensive and honestly, the chicken was a little dry. I did enjoy the sauce though! Maybe I'll try the wings next time.

For sides, I also tried the Disco Theok-bokki Skewers and their Plain Risotto. These are DEFINITELY unique options for a KFC spot. The skewers were a new take on the "sotteok sotteok", but instead of sausage, they use fishcake! Pretty tasty and VERY saucy. The risotto really surprised me! Though it wasn't super traditional, it sure was delicious.

Kingbob|645 Clay St, San Francisco, CA 9.text:
Absolutely loved this place. The spicy pork with rice was fantastic — the addition of glass noodles was something I’d never seen before, but it worked incredibly well and was such a pleasant surprise. The ddeokbokki had the perfect slightly sweet flavor that I personally love, while still having a nice kick.
The kimbap was also excellent, especially paired with the spicy mayo sauce that came with it — such a good combination. Everything tasted fresh, flavorful, and comforting. Great portions, great value, and an overall great experience. Will definitely be coming back!

Arrived at 5pm on a Saturday, the place wasn't busy and i was able both order quickly and get my order quickly too.

Food was solid, i had a bulgogi gimbap, shrimp gimbap and yangnyeom korean fried chicken. The taste was good, but i need to dock a point foe the chicken being overcookee and tough on some pieces

Service was great. Server was observant and provided a knife as i was trying to cut some of the tough pieces. Fairly efficient as well with all the delivery orders coming in

Atmosphere was solid, background kpop but nothing intrusive to potential conversational chatter of groups of people

We had late night dinner reservations elsewhere and needed a little snack to hold us over. We got the bulgogi kimbap and boneless KFC with the sweet and spicy sauce. While waiting for our food, we were pleasantly surprised when they served us each a hot cup of broth. The chicken was delicious and they were big pieces of breast meat that weren’t overly breaded, but still very crispy. The kimbap was also great. The man who helped us was very nice and the restaurant was clean.
Stumbled into this spot in SF and left so happy! The Korean fried chicken had that perfect Kentucky-style crunch, and the dumplings were seriously delicious. Can't wait to come back!
The kimbap was DELICIOUS. I loved the filling to rice ratio. I got the Bulgolgi kimbap and it was SO flavorful. I also got the fish cake soup and it was super tasty.

Muguboka Restaurant|401 Balboa St, San F.text:
This is one of my go-to spots!
Everything is always so delicious, and whenever I bring friends here, they always love it too.

This time I ordered takeout, and I was honestly so impressed I had to leave a review.

I got the kimchi pork belly and added tofu. When I opened the package at home… wow!!
They packed the main dish so generously and included so many side dishes too.

And of course, the food tastes amazing!!
Compared to other places, the prices are really reasonable, and every single side dish is good.
The kimchi and kkakdugi taste super fresh — you can tell they make them in-house!

Pretty sure I’m about to completely overeat tonight 😂

This restaurant is located on Balboa and 4th Ave with a variety of Korean dishes at an affordable price. It is well known by the locals. The food is tasty and has a well balanced Korean flavor. The seafood and kimchi pancakes are one of their signature dishes. Highly recommend their chapchae and pulgogi or marinated meat dish.
The staff is very friendly and go above and beyond l , ,whether it is crowded or slow they will always help you to serve the food in a timely manner.
Highly recommend this restaurant for families and small groups.
It gets very crowded during the weekend and you may have to wait to be seated.

Very bad experience ordering through DoorDash here. I ordered the seafood tofu soup, Bulgogi and Dae Ji Bulgogi. The meat taste expired, doesn’t seem hygienic. It is not edible and the seafood tofu soup had only one shrimp in it.

Always a joy coming here and one of my favorite food spots in the city. Korean food with a touch of homeliness to it. There’s also always a mystery freebie item they give out (could be dumplings or soup). The seafood pancake and bibimbap is to die for.

This is one of my favorite spots for Korean food. The atmosphere inside is cozy and comforting, making it the perfect place to relax and enjoy a meal. The food is always delicious, fairly priced, and served in reasonable portions. I’m usually not a big fan of tteokbokki, but this place cooks it perfectly every time. The staff is incredibly friendly and attentive. They even give me a free plate of gyoza whenever I visit. I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for great Korean food, affordable prices, and excellent service!

Manna|845 Irving St, San Francisco, CA 9.text:
I love this cute Korean spot!!!!
Even though the restaurant is on the smaller side, it’s worth the wait and it’s always busy even on the weekdays.
My favorite thing to get here are the seafood pancake, the galbi and the fried chicken. But my newest favorite is the kimchi fried rice, it’s so gooood!!!

The fog in the Inner Sunset doesn’t just roll in; it conducts a hostile takeover of your morale. It’s the kind of damp, bone-deep San Francisco cold that makes a man reconsider his loyalties and start confessing to crimes he only thought about committing. I was marooned on Irving Street, dodging the screech of the N-Judah and seeking a safe house. I found it at Manna. From the outside, it’s an unassuming storefront, the kind of joint that doesn’t need a neon sign to prove it knows its business. Inside, they offer a very specific, beautiful paradox: a modest, softly lit dining room that serves weaponized heat.
Before the main event, the table is populated by the banchan—small, cold side dishes of fermented defiance. Radishes with a sharp, garlicky bite, and seasoned greens that taste like they were plucked from a much brighter, more optimistic climate. They are the opening arguments, a cool, crisp misdirection before the kitchen unleashes the heavy artillery.
Then, the photographic evidence arrives. The bibimbap was delivered not on a plate, but in a black, cast-iron stone bowl hot enough to forge a counterfeit coin. This wasn't just dinner; it was a localized geological event. Look closely at the egg, resting on the summit of marinated beef and julienned vegetables like an unblinking, innocent yellow eye. It sits there, completely unaware of the searing violence about to take place. You are forced to become an accomplice, plunging your chopsticks into the center and frantically folding the raw yolk into the scorching rice before it sets. The sound is a violent, beautiful sizzle. What remains at the bottom of the bowl—the nurungji—is a structural masterpiece of caramelized desperation, a layer of scorched rice that crunches with the satisfying finality of a closed case.
To my left sat the stew. A soft tofu jjigae boiling with the sheer, unadulterated hostility of a jilted lover. It arrived actively raging in its own cauldron, spitting red, microscopic droplets of thermal warning across the table. Ordinarily, soup is meant to soothe. It is the food of convalescence. But this molten, crimson broth was a full-blown interrogation. It demanded your sweating, undivided attention. You don’t just sip it; you negotiate terms with it. Yet, beneath the fiery chili oil and the bubbling surface, the tofu itself is impossibly silken, a quiet mercy hiding in a bowl of fury.
Providing a necessary alibi to all this aggression was a plate of japchae. It’s a deceptive tangle of translucent sweet potato glass noodles, tossed with cabbage, carrots, and ribbons of beef. Slippery, slightly sweet, and aromatic with toasted sesame oil, it acts as the diplomat of the table. When your palate is exhausted from the blistering masochism of the stone bowls, the japchae pulls you back from the ledge.
Five stars. It’s an exercise in delicious, calculated trauma. In a city increasingly obsessed with foam, tweezers, and edible flowers, Manna is the only place in the neighborhood where the dinner actively, gloriously fights back.

Came in for late 3p lunch-dak bul kogi, no wait. Chicken & onions arrived on sizzling cast iron plate. Banchan of broccoli w/sesame oil, potato strips w/ garlic were tasty, kimchee was a bit too sweet for my taste. Overall good service & generous portions, unlimited banchan.

Tried the kimchi pancake, tofu soup, seafood bibimbap and pork bulgogi. All are great but kimchi pancake is def standout! Came at peak dinner time on a Friday and wait time was 30-40 mins but was worth it

Great food, good for family style and decent portions. Overall I’d recommend the dumpling soup and soft tofu soup. Decently prices but it’s walk in only. Expect a wait! There’s indoor and outdoor seating.

My Tofu House|4627 Geary Blvd, San Franc.text: A go-to spot for serious Korean comfort food in SF. The soondubu is deeply savory with a clean, well-balanced broth and silky tofu that melts in your mouth. Banchan are thoughtfully prepared and always fresh, not an afterthought. Everything arrives bubbling hot and perfectly seasoned. Consistently satisfying and worth coming back for again and again.

Menu is straightforward & well organized. So it's easy to navigate.

But food is just ok. Nothing stood out. Not sure if it's a recent change, or only temporary--they no longer offer the fried fish banchan, or nurungji (crispy rice in bottom of pot).

Dining room is cold, so bring a warm jacket.

When I need a comforting meal, I come here. Delicious banchan, rice made fresh in a stonepot and they even make you nurungji at the end of the meal 😍 The Oi Muchim and fried fish banchan are my fav. The stews come out boiling hot with the most amazing silken tofu. Bibimbap loaded with healthy veg, BBQ plates steaming with piles of juicy marinated meats, and thick crispy pancakes stuffed with goodies.

If you are still waiting in two-hour lines for Korean food in the city, you’re doing it wrong. You need to head to the Inner Richmond and look for Tofu House on Geary. It’s a no-frills, legendary neighborhood spot, but there is a specific strategy to eating here if you want the San Francisco neighborhood locals experience.

First insider tip: hit the ATM beforehand because it’s cash-preferred and you don't want to deal with card minimums when the craving hits.

Drop into this cozy spot right on Geary and 3rd avenue where even though the pacing is quick, they don't skimp on hospitality. The staff are incredibly attentive where it matters most: your banchan bowls will rarely sit empty, and if you treat them with respect, they treat you like family.

The moment you sit down, they hit the table with the Banchan. Unlike places that skimp, their side dishes are top-tier—the potato salad is elite, and yes, they still serve the classic whole fried fish as a side!

But the main event is the boiling stone pot of Seafood Soon Tofu. The second it lands on the table, you have a 10-second window to crack your raw egg straight into the boiling broth so it poaches perfectly. I order mine at "medium" spice—it gives you that rich, deep, fiery red color and a perfect kick without completely overpowering the silky, pillowy texture of the fresh tofu. Altogether  this is  a great spot for those "Don't feel like cooking" nights.

This place has a special place in my heart. Just classic soon tofu and what I like most is that they do the nunungi, not sure how to spell that. But it’s a good spot. It feels like home if that makes sense. I’m definitely bias because I’ve been coming here for all my life but I just love this place. It definitely is not the best Korean food or the best soon tofu or anything spectacular but you know what you’re going to get and I like what I am going to get. My Beli gave it a 7.5, I think it’s more like a 8.5 because I’d prefer this place over so many others even if they are more expensive or fancy. This is definitely an experience and nostalgic place for me.

Ollei|2127 Polk St, San Francisco, CA 94.text:
This place is my go-to spot whenever my sister stops by my work since it’s so close to the area. The service was pretty good, and the portions were generous, each order could easily be shared by two people. Since my sister and I like trying different dishes, we decided to order three items.

First, we got the Soy Garlic Ollei Chicken Wings. They were flavorful, crispy, and perfectly coated with sauce. Next was the Beef Bibimbap. The beef was well-marinated, and it came with a good amount of veggies and rice, making it a really satisfying and well-balanced dish.

Lastly and definitely the star of our lunch was the Creamy Ttok & Pasta. We originally expected something like a regular creamy carbonara, but we were pleasantly surprised! It was incredibly creamy with a nice kick of spice without being too overwhelming, and it had a delicious drizzle of bacon on top that made it even better!

I would absolutely order the Creamy Ttok & Pasta again, and honestly, I’d recommend trying all three dishes. Everything we had was delicious!

Ollei is a fantastic neighborhood find that brings a warm, welcoming vibe and top tier Korean comfort food to the bustling Russian Hill area. The atmosphere is cozy and character filled, making it a perfect spot for everything from a casual dinner to a late night craving. The service is consistently praised for being friendly and attentive, with thoughtful touches like hand drawn happy faces on takeout orders that show they truly care about their guests. The menu is packed with standouts: the Army Stew (Budae Jjigae) and Seafood Pancakes are absolute must orders for their rich flavors and generous portions, while the Soft Tofu Soup and Bulgogi consistently hit the mark for quality and authenticity. Don't miss the Ollei Chicken Wings perfectly crunchy and available with a delicious soy garlic or spicy glaze or the inventive Creamy Ttok & Pasta for a unique fusion twist. Whether you're diving into a hearty bowl of Beef Ramen or sharing a plate of Corn Cheese with friends, Ollei delivers a satisfying, 5-star experience that feels like a warm hug in every bite.

December 2025 Informal and casual Korean restaurant in general downtown area.  Food is standard with a bit of authenticity lacking as flavors are there but ingredients are just average. Meat is less tender than traditional and appetizers can be improved.

Service is standard and efficient.

Place is a bit aged.

All in all a good option if you need a quick Korean meal.

My favorite Korean restaurant. The soft tofu soup is fabulous. The bibimbap is great too.
The truffle fries are delicious and a very healthy portion  but it will fill up up so to  leave room for your dinner,  share it with others.
The service is friendly and efficient

The restaurant is  small and cozy. K Pop videos are projected on a wall.

No steps so accessable

This isn’t a place you’ll write home about, but if you’re under the weather or just craving something hearty, the Army Stew is a must. It’s a big bubbling pot of everything—including the kitchen sink: ham, sausage, Spam, baked beans, kimchi, instant noodles, gochujang, mushrooms, and rice cakes. Super filling—enough to feed 4 people easily. It was just my husband and me, but we still ordered it and took half home.

We also loved the seafood pancake—crispy, flavorful, and one of those dishes that’s best eaten fresh (definitely don’t save it for the next day).

These two dishes are the standouts and would get 4 stars on their own. That said, we’ve tried other items before like the fried chicken wings and bibimbap, and they didn’t hit the mark—lacking authenticity and flavor.

Stick to the Army Stew and seafood pancake, and you’re in for a comforting, satisfying meal.

Cocobang|550 Taylor St, San Francisco, C.text: It’s a dope spot I’m from Texas and it was so damn good fried chicken. It’s small place but I’m not use to the tall buildings but this is a dope city. I got the chicken cutlet and my gf got the fried chicken a half order. I also got a Korean beer and the misses got the strawberry slurry soju.

This is my second time eating here and it did not disappoint. It’s a cool & comfy city vibe. They’re really efficient on service too so your food comes out right away after you order. We did find the Army soup salty, and that’s probably because of all the pork that’s in there…they don’t skimp on the meat. You can’t go wrong with the kimchi pancake, tofu soup, fried chicken, and the beef kalbi (I like the spicy over the original). I just wish they served a plethora of banchan with the main dishes. That would seal the deal here!

Coco Bang might not look super fancy from the outside, but don’t let that fool you. This place is awesome! As soon as we walked in, the staff was super friendly and made us feel welcome. The vibe inside is really cool and relaxed. It’s a great place to hang out and enjoy a meal with friends or family.

We ordered six different dishes and shared them family-style. Every single one was delicious! The flavors were amazing, and you can tell the food is made with care. It’s hard to find a place where everything you try tastes this good.

If you’re looking for great Korean food and a fun atmosphere, Coco Bang is the place to go. We had a great time and can’t wait to come back!

Super tasty late night dinner place! No reservation so we waited around 30 minutes to get a table. Service and atmosphere was okay, very busy vibes around the time we ate (8PM).

Food was so delicious and filling for the price! The corn cheese was so good and cheesy, it went well with everything. The kimchi fried rice was also delicious and all the ingredients came together perfectly. The BBQ combo is the best deal for the price and the meat was flavorful and tender, with an extra bowl of white rice to go with it. I would recommend ordering any of these items because they were all so tasty! We still had leftovers too.

Overall, I would definitely recommend coming here for a cheap, tasty, late night dinner. I do wish there was some dessert on the menu though!

This was my first meal in SF after travelling over 15 hours, and it was SO good!!! It was healing. I got the spicy tofu soup (with pork) and the fried dumplings. My soup was excellent, the perfect level of spice for me (medium) with lots of meat and tofu. It came with a bowl of rice and three banchan dishes. The banchan offerings could be improved (I was also surprised that kimchi wasn’t offered as a banchan) but the soup was a 10/10. A lot of the other tables had BBQ dishes, which seems to be other popular item on their menu.
The restaurant is small and popular, so be prepared to queue even on weekdays. The restaurant has a waitlist system that you can register on though, which makes it more efficient.

jina bakes|1581 Webster St #150, San Fra.text:
We were in Japan Town one late afternoon and was having boba sundae when I suddenly realized that Jina Bakes was just across the store we were. Items were 50% already since it’s almost closing time. We got the Injeolmi Croissant, Kalbijjim Croissant and Hotteok Kouign-amann.

The Injeolmi Croissant was my favorite! It was a great balance of the soft, chewy mochi and the crispy, flaky croissant. The Hotteok Kouign-amann was sensational! The sweet gooey filling was balanced by the nuts and croissant itself. The Kalbijjim Croissant was ok but not worth the original price of $8.50 (since I got this for $4.25 only, it was ok). The center filling was more of an empanada filling (like potatoes and peas) and you can’t taste any kalbijjim. It’s still good for a savory pastry but maybe put more of the kalbijjim.

We had these to go and ate it a day after. The breads are still good upon heating it a bit in the toaster.

Injeolmi croissant: flaky, crispy, buttery pastry with a soft and chewy mochi, and a light sprinkle of injeolmi on top. 10/10
Salt bread: crispy bottom, soft and fluffy inside, perfect amount of salt. Also 10/10
Einspanner: cream top was delicious, can't say anything bad about einspanners tbh

They only let in parties of 3 at a time but the wait wasn't too bad at all!

First time at a super busy spot. Casual spot with no dining options. Nice variety of baked goods and drinks such as the honeycomb toffee latte. We picked up the kouign amann and the match cream puff. Both items are sweet, larger than expected, and a definite must share. Once your order is placed, your pastries are handed to you while you wait for the drinks to be made.Overall, another good bakery option in SF

Stopped by on a Saturday morning after seeing this place on TikTok and arrived right around opening. There was already a small line, but the wait was only about 5–10 minutes. They limit the shop to three customers at a time, so I can imagine the line getting much longer later in the day. The bakery is located inside the Japantown mall and has a very minimal look to it. Staff were friendly and efficient.

I picked up five pastries: almond croissant, orange chocolate bun, injeolmi croissant, hotteok kouign-amann, and a danish. The orange chocolate bun was the standout, super unique and absolutely delicious and would highly recommend. The injeolmi croissant was also excellent, the rice cake was soft and chewy with a nice soybean powder coating, though most of the powder fell off during transport, so I’d recommend eating it right away. The hotteok kouign-amann was pleasantly surprising with a nutty, sweet flavor and it felt really well balanced. The almond croissant and danish were solid and exactly what you’d expect from a top tier bakery.

Everything held up well even when eaten later at home, and surprisingly tasted great the next day too. Prices felt very reasonable for the quality. Definitely worth checking out if you’re in the area and don’t mind a potential wait.

The prices here really make it hard to frequent but my friends and I really enjoyed trying Jina bakes! I got the injeolmi croissant and it was so worth it. I’ve never come across anything like it before and I really adored it. I loved eating the tapioca / mochi texture in the croissant. My friend also got the strawberry matcha cookie and said it’s one of the best cookies he’s ever had. This place is definitely worth the try. — And for those that don’t know, the store is very small so there’s no seating in it. Thankfully it’s in the japan town mall, so there’s some seating right outside of it! — I’ll be trying my best to recreate the injeolmi croissant now…

Jasmin's|809 Bush St, San Francisco, CA.text: These were the best burgers I had during my trip to San Francisco! The buns are incredibly soft and the combination of flavors, from the sauce to the cheese and the juicy meat, is absolutely delicious. The service is very friendly as well. They even serve water in the cutest, most charming little cups! When we visited, it was just the cashier and the cook, and both were incredibly kind. I absolutely loved this place and it was conveniently located just one block from my hotel!

Amazing service and a small, clean atmosphere. The restaurant seats 18 people max, with one table seating 6, 2 tables sitting 4, and 4 single spots to sit facing the window. The patty was slightly disappointing on the “Cheezburger”, but the fries made up for it. Would definitely go again!

If you’re looking for a fast and yummy place, this is it! My husband and I are not from SF and we were craving a burger around our hotel. We are so happy we came here. The people at the front were lovely and our food was awesome.

I got the double cheeseburger and it was so juicy but it also didn’t make me feel overly horrible after. My husband got the Mcbangbang and he loved it.

Highly recommend for a quick stop when you’re in SF!

We had a good quick lunch here while walking around the city. The burgers were delicious and service was quick. The burgers come with grilled onions and banana peppers on them, which was a unique and delicious combo! The tots were also crispy and freshly fried.
I love this place! It is a staple burger joint for me while I am living in the city. The service is exceptional!! Love the cheeseburger and tots!

Surisan|505 Beach St, San Francisco, CA.text:
Surisan is located right by Fisherman’s Wharf, with a great view and a cozy, stylish atmosphere that feels relaxed and inviting. The service was solid and professional — everything we needed was taken care of without being overly chatty or intrusive, which I personally appreciated. It made the whole experience feel comfortable and well-paced.

The food was mostly very enjoyable, with one absolute standout: the Poke Salad. It was genuinely outstanding — fresh, flavorful, and beautifully layered. The sweetness from the mango added a perfect contrast, and the poke was served inside a giant crispy shrimp cracker “bowl,” which was both creative and delicious. This dish alone is worth coming back for.

We also tried the stir-fried Shin ramen. The presentation was cute and playful, and flavor-wise it was exactly what you’d expect — a solid, comforting fried noodle dish. The raspberry lemonade was rich and refreshing, and the dessert (I unfortunately forgot the name) was nicely balanced and not overly sweet.

One dish I would strongly recommend skipping is the Loco Moco. It was overcooked, and the patty was extremely tough and dry — easily the weakest dish of the night.

Overall, Surisan is definitely worth a visit, especially if you’re already in the Fisherman’s Wharf area. It’s a chill, pleasant place with a great location and some genuinely excellent dishes — just be selective about what you order.

I recently ate at this Korean restaurant in San Francisco and ordered their Korean fried chicken, expecting something authentic after my excellent experiences in Seoul. What I received was a massive disappointment. This so-called Korean chicken didn’t even come close to the real thing — it wasn’t even in the same league. It tasted exactly like regular KFC chicken: same overly thick, soggy breading, generic seasoning, and zero authentic Korean spice, crunch, or flavor.
After having incredibly delicious, crispy, perfectly seasoned Korean fried chicken in Seoul, this version felt like a cheap fast-food imitation. For over $30, it was a complete rip-off. At that price in San Francisco, you’d expect something special, but instead I got something that wouldn’t even stand out at a KFC drive-thru.
Honestly, if you want Korean fried chicken, skip this place entirely and just go to an actual KFC — you’ll get the same taste for a fraction of the price without the disappointment. I was genuinely shocked at how far off the mark this was, especially after knowing how amazing real Korean chicken can be.
Save your money and your expectations. This place is a hard pass.

Amazing food and good portions to boot! We sat outdoors and enjoyed the shade and the well kept area. The food is so flavorful - definitely my favorite meal of our whole California trip, not to mention of SF.

Came here right at 9 a.m. on a Monday for breakfast. The interior has a nice modern café and homey feel. Unfortunately, the service wasn’t the best. Our server forgot to order my food, so I had to wait until everyone else was finished eating before mine came out. They offered me a specialty fruit drink and 20% off my dish, which ended up being about $5 off. I appreciated the gesture, but considering the situation, I think comping the dish would’ve been more appropriate, especially since I don’t drink juice or soda, so the fruit drink went untouched.

As for the food, the French toast was definitely the highlight with a crispy outside and creamy center that was really well done. I ordered the Surisan Scramble, which was decent, though the bread that came with it was a bit burnt. Overall, the food was pretty good, but the service missed the mark and made the experience just okay.

We came for breakfast, the ambiance of the restaurant was great! Has indoor & outdoor seating.

Food was great, my parents typically aren’t very fond of fusion Korean food but they enjoyed everything! They own restaurants and could tell the owner took great consideration as far as small details. Food was played nicely & seasoned well. Definitely recommend!

Kiss My Seoul|15 Boardman Pl, San Franci.text: This small cafe is on the alley directly across from the hall of justice and has four tables and another dozen seats along a wall. The menu is limited but the food is good and the quantity is large. There’s free water with glass containers you can bring to your seat. We ordered two bulgabi bowls, one a mini and the other the regular.  Both had broccoli, chap chai, brown rice and a lot of beef. It came with sauces as well. The food was good and a mini would’ve been enough for both of us. Service was friendly but the beef was a bit tough to chew. The place is large but spartan but clean.

Both got the cupbap. A decent portion of food but super average taste-wise, no sauce in it so pretty flavorless, atmosphere is so weird. Combination of Mexican Hawaiian Korean. None of the bowls come with kimchi which is crazy for a place called kiss my Seoul. Bulgogi just tastes and looks like beef teriyaki. Don’t think I’d ever come again and shocked at the other ratings.

Excellent food, portion, and service. Was in the area for a meeting and the price to portion ratio was excellent. The restaurant included a side of chicken because they accidentally input it wrong, even though I said it was okay.

Both the chicken and the beef bulgogi are excellent and I'd highly recommend them! Was fast to order and prepare!

Excellent cupbap with generous portion, taco was not great.

I had the regular size bulgogi cupbap ($16) and a single chicken taco ($5) just to try it. I've been meaning to visit this restaurant for years but it just wasn't convenient for me until today.

The cupbap container was filled to the brim and food inside was packed in. I didn't have breakfast today, so I was pretty hungry when I came here for lunch, but I still didn't fully finish it. I recommend ordering the mini size if you're not that hungry and you don't want to have leftovers.

Inside the paper bowl, there was plenty of bulgogi on top of broccoli, zucchini, carrot, cauliflower, glass noodles, and white rice mixed with a little bit of black rice — this dyed the rice purple-ish. It was garnished with green onion and sesame. Everything seemed to be seasoned with black pepper. The bulgogi was tender with some browning and tasted perfect. The glass noodles seemed to have been stir-fried with a sweet soy sauce. The vegetables still retained a bit of a crunch. The rice was a little mushy like it was just slightly overcooked, but I guess that's to fully cook the black rice.

I don't recommend getting a taco. There was a single tortilla. It was slightly toasted but still got pretty soggy from the salsa and chicken juices. The flavors didn't pair well. The chicken tasted like bulgogi and it just didn't work work well with salsa. The salsa verde and salsa roja were also a bit bland. You can get much better tacos in the Mission for less money.

There are plenty of seats here. There's also a small area with packaged Korean snacks like Turtle Chips.

Maybe the other dishes are good, but the smash burger was a huge disappointment. I saw a couple specks of red pepper in the aioli, I assume that’s the “kimchi.” Just a regular burger, not at ALL with any kind of discernible Korean influence.

Pho 2000|637 Larkin St, San Francisco, C.text:
Whoever said this place has good bánh xèo: apologize to me, and I will take you to a better place. It was the worst bánh xèo I’ve had in my entire life. It was thick, wet, undercooked and unseasoned dough. Slimy and completely flavorless. I was genuinely confused. I’ve never had bad bánh xèo before, and I order it at every Vietnamese restaurant that serves it. How do you mess up a fried pancake?

As for the bún riêu, everything else was fine, but they used tofu that had clearly gone bad (2 out of the 4 pieces were distinctly sour, disgusting, and slimy inside). The bún bò Huế had good broth, but the amount of meat was disappointing, with very little variety in protein. I had been looking forward to coming here. I won’t be back.

Our favorite pho in town even in the world!!
We usually stop by when we are in the area and craving for some pho.

For the appetizer, we had bbq pork rolls.
It’s our first time trying it and it’s really delicious.

The chicken pho is just amazing.The portion is good and the broth is flavor.

I highly recommend this place and definitely will be back again.

The spring rolls were quite nice, and the spicy noodle soup was exceptional. The pho was rich, comforting, and full of deep flavor. After trying it here, I feel it will be difficult to choose any other place for my next pho craving. I left very pleased with the entire meal.

Everything was amazing my aunt and mom are crazy about Vietnamese food so we walked to this one after seeing some photos online. They served us tea the moment we sat down, it was amazing. The portions are large, despite being labeled small. Imperial rolls, pho ga, and a mi xao bo. The prices were just right. My aunt really love their shirts Pho nomenal, had to snap a photo. Accidently pinched the guy trying to get a shot of it. Thank you again for doing that fo us. She loved it!

I went here for the first time and it was an amazing experience. The food was incredible , especially the pho. The broth was so rich and flavorful, it really stood out.

What impressed me just as much as the food was the service. Our server carried the hot bowl of pho like it was nothing , I touched it for a second and it was really hot, so I was honestly amazed!

The staff made us feel so welcome and taken care of. Our server was very attentive, and even the manager came by to check on us, helped fix our plates, and made sure everything was perfect. That level of care made us feel really special.

Yummy Yummy|1015 Irving St, San Francisc.text: Pardon my photo quality, I got excited and only remembered to take one after digging in. This might be the best bun bo hue I’ve had. The broth was perfectly balanced with just a touch of sweetness that created the most savory umami flavor bomb. It’s a very rich broth that’s meaty in flavor but not overwhelming. There was so much tender beef shank in this bowl. I kept finding more and more. The noodles were perfectly soft yet chewy. Service was quick but still polite. I loved the miniature collection at the front door. My boyfriend’s pho was also a huge and satisfying portion full of tasty flavor. Can’t wait to come back!!

Some of the best Vietnamese food I’ve had outside of Vietnam. The salt & pep crab was absolutely sensational - I went in with both hands, knuckles deep, without a care in the world. The bánh xèo was the perfect appetizer. Perfectly cooked noodles in the pho/BBH. Service was friendly and attentive. A most memorable meal and certainly will come back next time I visit SF!

We ordered a salad with chicken, beef, squid, and vegetables, along with regular pho and spicy beef pho. Everything was delicious and unique. The spicy pho had a tomato-based broth. The restaurant was very busy on Friday evening, but the service was very friendly. A lovely family-owned restaurant!
BEST pho i’ve had in the city!! so yummy & amazing customer service. also served food so fast (big plus for those hungry after a hike)!!

I had an excellent dining experience at an authentic and delicious Vietnamese restaurant. The food was incredibly flavorful and genuine, transporting me directly to Vietnam with every bite. The staff was efficient and friendly, ensuring all our needs were promptly met. Additionally, the atmosphere was nice and comfortable, perfect for a relaxed meal. I will definitely be returning soon.

Turtle Tower - Marina|3145 Fillmore St,.text:
My go to spot when I crave chicken pho!! Turtle Tower Marina is sophisticated, sexy and most importantly, so so delicious!! I came here with my family so it's kid friendly and I've also partied here with my high school girlfriends on a Saturday night! It's a perfect spot for all group sizes and vibes. My oldest loves the Beef Pho and my two little ones enjoy the Chicken Pho Noodle soup. Something about their meal is so heartwarming and filling.
The decor is very beautiful, like a fine dining establishment. Very modern and clean. The service is always impeccable! Smiles and warm greetings when we enter every time and quick service when we need it.
So excited and happy for this Marina location!

A review from KQED on late night food places in the Bay Area brought me to this new gem in the Marina district. The pho was excellent, with rich broth and fresh ingredients. We tried the basic beef and veggie pho, and both were delicious. The service was great, with friendly staff who offered stories of the restaurant’s origins (i.e. The name Turtle Tower deriving from a landmark in Hanoi.) We spent about $45 after tax and tip for two bowls of pho, which seems standard for dining out in SF these days.  Would come back anytime to try other options.

This newest location is a massive glow-up! Their full bar, decor and ambiance make it a perfect spot for dinner and drinks.

My absolute favorite rotating must order soup trio are the Pho Tai (rare beef with fresh noodles), Pho Ga (free range chicken with fresh noodles) and Bun Thang (vermicelli soup with fancy pork, chicken and thin slices of egg). Their broth has remained consistent through the years: clear, savory and flavorful.

And for hot summer days, go for the banh mi or bun cha (cold vermicelli with grilled meat and fish sauce).

Don't forget to finish with the pandan waffles!

I'm really hoping they eventually open for late night hours on the weekends like their FiDi location. SF definitely needs more delicious pho options for late-night crowds after hitting up the bar and their newest location would be perfect for that!

Welcome to the Marina Turtle Tower!
We had dinner in this restaurant during our 3 days stay in San Francisco. The food was excellent, the choice quite wide. Friendly service and nice location. Prices are also good. Recommended!

This location is stunning! From the moment I walked in, I was wowed by the decor and ambiance; the vibe is classy yet welcoming, making it a great spot for any occasion.

The service perfectly matched the atmosphere, as the staff were both friendly and helpful.

Of course, the highlight was the food. As a big fan of Hanoi-style phở, I can say that TT does it right: the noodles are silky, the broth is clear and flavorful, and the meat is incredibly fresh. I also really enjoyed the imperial rolls, fresh spring rolls, and bánh mì. And let's not forget the drinks, the cà phê sữa đá (Vietnamese iced coffee) is wonderfully strong and delicious.

I love TT pho-eva!

Golden Star Vietnamese Restaurant|11 Wal.text: I highly recommend this small restaurant and its signature Pho: the broth is perfectly seasoned (with extra salt available on the table), it’s packed with meat, and the noodles are excellent. I love soup and would recommend this spot to tourists; the prices are great, and the place is clean and cozy. The fact that locals dine here is the best guarantee of the food's quality.

Pho was better than most places I’ve been to. Portion size equals to portion amount. Other places give to more noodles when you order a large but the meat amount is the same as a regular size. The rice noodles doesn’t have a starchy smell, meaning they soak and rinse the rice noodles thoroughly.

The restaurant is clean and spacious.  The restaurant server quickly ushered me to my table and politely asked if I would like tea, if not water. The welcome drink is then served in a paper cup.

I ordered my usual Beef Special Pho which comed with beef steak, tendon, balls and stomach, and also a hot viet coffee.

The pho comes in huge serving and lined with hugh generous portion of steak slices and other ingredients. The highlight of the pho would be the  steak slices. It was fragrant and is tasty like the steak you would have ordered at a steak house.

The Vietnamese cofffee was strong and also have a balanced taste with the amount of condensed milk and hot water combination. Highly recommended as well. Beats blue mountain and star bucks hands down. 😄

Very authentic, very popular, and very tasty!
The service was fast and efficient, the Pho was a good portion, fresh, the meat was plentiful and the broth delicious, and without another thought I had eaten it all!! No photo! I can highly recommend.

This is a fantastic Vietnamese noodle restaurant; I've eaten here many times. The location is easy to find, not far from the Garden Corner parking lot. Their beef noodle soup is incredibly flavorful, with plenty of beef, tripe, and beef balls—you won't need to order any extra meat to feel satisfied. Topped with basil and lemon, it's a truly delightful taste experience！

Bodega SF|138 Mason St, San Francisco, C.text:
Bodega SF had been on our list for a while, especially after seeing it ranked in The Infatuation’s top 20 restaurants in San Francisco, so we were excited to finally try it. We made a reservation for a Wednesday night and were seated quickly upon arrival, while there was a noticeable line for walk ins.
The space has a trendy, lively atmosphere that sets the tone for a fun dining experience. We ordered a variety of dishes: steamed rice rolls, tiger prawns, stir fried pho noodles, mushrooms with quail eggs, and crab fat fried rice.

There were definitely some highlights. The tiger prawns and mushrooms with quail eggs were standouts, and I especially loved the stir fried pho noodles since they felt unique and creative compared to typical noodle dishes. However, not everything landed. My sister and mom found the filling in the steamed rice rolls to be a bit dry, and the crab fat fried rice did not quite work for us as a combination of flavors.

Service was the main downside of the evening. Although the restaurant was busy, our waiter passed by our table multiple times without stopping to take our order, and our food took over 20 minutes to arrive.
Overall, Bodega SF offers a stylish setting and some memorable dishes, but the experience was mixed due to inconsistent execution and service.

Dined here for lunch with my colleagues recently since I’ve been wanting to try this place and it was great! Our reservation was at 11:30a and by then, they had a couple of customers. Since it was earlier in the day, service was slower, but that’s to be expected as they start their day.

We decided to order two appetizers: banh cuon & grilled lemongrass wings. My colleagues ordered the dry noodles, pho and I had the lemongrass pork chops and sub garlic noodles for rice (which was the move).

We were all happy with our orders & would definitely return!

Server took a while to take our order after we sat down so the service wasn’t as attentive. The bun cha and nem cua were delicious! Although the serving portion were small(especially the rice vermicelle) for these. Bo tai chanh was delicious and authentic! Bo luc lac was pretty dry. The bo luc lac and vegetarian thit kho mushroom didn’t come with rice or any sides which I find is a bit odd for Vietnamese food. The pandan coffee tonic was not a highlight of the meal, the pandan and coffee flavor did not go well together in my personal preference.

A recommendation I would add for the restaurant is to serve some of the dishes with side of chilies, siracha, soy sauce, fish sauce, etc. Maybe a spice rack would be nice. I think this would elevate the flavors of Vietnamese food to the another level.

This restaurant has a different interpretation on Vietnamese food. Some people raves about it and others complain.

There’s different menus for lunch and dinner. Lunch has Pho, chicken wings, spring rolls etc.  Dinner menu omits all that’s offered at lunch, and offers more sophisticated dishes.

Tried the special of the day and it was not great. Angel hair pasta fried with seafood and topped with crab meat. It was too dry to eat!!

Service was ok. Atmosphere is ok… nothing outstanding. Food was lukewarm.

Came here after a long day of work and it was really solid food!

Good:
- This place is beautiful! It's so nicely lit
- Great for a date or 1-1 catchup
- The crab fat fried rice was amazing!
- I also really liked the vermicelli dish but it was so small - the sauce was so good!

Bad:
- Pretty expensive food, ended up paying $88 for 2 people
- Took a while to get our food
- The portions are really small for how much you pay

Overall wouldn't come again unless I had a gift certificate - it's so expensive.

Saigon Sandwich|560 Larkin St, San Franc.text: There's always a line and no place to sit, but the wait is worth it! The banh mi sandwiches are the BEST period!  I love the pate and my husband always gets the pork. No matter what you get, one bite and you'll be a fan forever.

Best Vietnamese sandwich in San Francisco! The sandwiches are not as good as the San Jose one, but in term of the prices, quality of bread and the portions, it’s worth to give it a try even though it’s located in Tenderloin. Combo and pork are just ok. Chicken is the best. The bread is fresh. I like how soft it’s, not too hard as others. Cash only. Open at 7am.
Be cautious to be there because there are many homeless around.

It's cash only, so make sure to have that before coming. There's a few ATMs that are nearby, if you didn't know like I did. This is probably one of my favorite banh mi sandwiches that I've ever had. It doesn't look like much from the outside, but it had a small line going outside of the restaurant, which is usually a pretty good sign the place has good food. Sort of a sketchy area. The line moved pretty quickly. I heard people ordering a  combination sandwich and was intrigued by that, but I decided to just keep with a pork sandwich. The bread was soft and crispy, and I think they made it right in the back! The ingredients were very fresh too. It had everything I look for in a banh mi sandwich. The ingredients were very fresh, and the jalapeños gave me a good punch across the face. The sauce really complimented the pork too, and crunchiness from the carrots is always great. Last but definitely not least, the value is crazy. It is such a good deal for a sandwich this good to be less than 8 dollars. I'd eat these far too often if I lived nearby. I see why the Infatuation holds this place in high praise.

CASH ONLY. Stumbled upon this unassuming banh mi spot after staying at the Motel 6 around the corner for Santacon. WOW!!! The bread here is PHENOMENAL, baked fresh right behind the counter! The banh mi ranges from $5.50-$7. I ordered the Special Combination (thit cha pate, which has roast pork, Vietnamese ham, and pate) for $7 but added extra pate for an additional $1.

WHEN I SAY EXTRA PATE, THEY PUT EXTRA PATE!! There was the THICKEST LAYER of pate I've ever seen in my life here, probably a little more than half an inch thick, THIS IS WORTH $1 EXTRA!! The pate was savory, nicely seasoned, and just overall tasty. The roast pork was very tender and slightly sweet, definitely not your usual banh mi filling in America, but made the entire sandwich really savory. The cha lua (Vietnamese ham) was cut a bit too thin for me personally but the amount of pate they put really made up for everything.

The star of the show is absolutely the bread. Crispy on the outside but extremely soft and warm on the inside, the bread itself has a nice flavor just on its own. Its thicker than what I'm used to so there's more pillowy goodness inside. They make the perfect bread here!

Craving another banh mi with extra pate from here as I write this. Absolutely coming back
Actually crazy how fresh and tasty this was. Bread has perfectly crunchy exterior and soft interior - wasn’t difficult to bite. Not overly crumby. Meat was tender, sauce was so good. Excellent.

Cafe Bunn Mi|417 Clement St, San Francis.text: I’m really frustrated. We specifically asked for no cilantro. The cashier confirmed it. When I opened my to go order, there was so much cilantro in my pho. If it wasn’t frigid in the restaurant itself we would’ve opened it up and caught in and returned it immediately. Unfortunately that was not an option today. Please have your kitchen staff be more mindful of dietary requests.

Came here bc of GrubwithMike on Insta, and got their Crispy Duck Bun Mi. The most expensive sandwich on the menu and it was only okay. Bread was a little stale. Got two pieces of duck and it was mostly skin and fat which I guess is all duck in a way. But $19 for this, not worth it. Not coming back. The restaurant also smelled like old bleach when you walk in.

Maybe they had an off day? I did go at 3:30pm on a Monday…

Wednesday is the dayyyyyy
Perfect balance of meat and veggies 🔥
Grilled pork banh mi also amazing (not pictured cuz I devoured)

Casual Vietnamese joint, with a large menu. We came here for lunch and sat near the window. They have a couple tables outside for seating as well. There is only one washroom. Basically you pay and order, and you get a number while you wait for your food to arrive. Service is very no frills.

We got the five spice chicken pho, five spice chicken Saigon noodle vermicelli, and steak Saigon noodle. They didn't include too much meat for the vermicelli dishes. I liked that the spring rolls were fresh. Keep in mind that they do contain pork. Unfortunately, I was disappointed that they didn't include any of the usual Thai Basil and other herbs in the vermicelli dishes.
The pho broth was ok, nothing outstanding.

A few weeks ago, I came across this place while I was around Clement St. I've walked by it a few times before but always noticed that it always had a line and busy around lunchtime. I am one who never likes to line up for food in SF due to the fact that we can get good food anywhere we turn in this city. However, this particular day, I realised
it was empty, but I found out later that I was lucky I came in a few minutes right before the lunch rush. I got myself a window seat facing the street and enjoyed the food that now, a few weeks later, I kept going back on a weekly basis a few times twice a week. Now it's like a Friday and sometimes Monday ritual to go walk around Clement St, pick up my sauce and yummy food around the area, and have something at Cafe Bunn Mi. Place is clean, Menu has variety of choices, Staff are amaizing and the food prep is super fast and my order was out within 10min in my case. The sandwiches are to die for eventho a little more expensive than a few of the places around the Avenues, but it's so worth it with the portions. And the noddle bowls are now becoming my favorite. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a fast Vietnamese place around Clement St where food tastes good and the service is fast. I'd recommend going in a little early or after a lunch rush if u wanna take your time and enjoy your food. A 5star in my book, and I am glad I decided to walk in to try it out cause in 3 weeks, I think I have done 5 dine in and 3 times to go.

Banh Mi Viet|518 Divisadero St, San Fran.text:
Ordered takeout - there’s no seating inside or outside. I got the cold cuts banh mi and shrimp spring rolls. The banh mi good, but it could have used more pate (it was a little dry) and more pickled veggies. The shrimp spring rolls were tasty, and the portions were generous. There are definitely some better spots in the City, but no major complaints.
