As a heads up, the payment process is a little strange. They clearly prefer cash and would probably be cash only if they could. You can pay by Venmo or card, and when I asked what they preferred, the cashier said Venmo but they still add tax to your total through the register.

Overall, a solid takeout option at a fair price!
Got the beef banh mi and wasnt super impressed. The beef tasted a little sour (which I'm sure is because of the marinade they use) and was dry, and the bánh mì as a whole just didnt feel cohesive
Ordered through doordah..Pork Bahn Mi was delicious, the spring rolls were super fresh and the scrumptious peanut sauce that went along with them was just made perfect.

Got the #1 (dac biet) and #4 (tofu) banh mi for $21 even if paying by cash/Venmo. Came on hot, fresh, super crunchy baguettes with generous helpings of pickled carrot and radish.  Also generous portions of tofu.  No seating area inside, so we enjoyed our sandwiches across the street at the Muni bus stop.  Next time if it’s an option, I’d opt for less hoisin and more pate.  Fast and good quality—would go again.

I ordered this through door dash so I know that all this food would be better if I ate it in the restaurant. That just means I HAVE to eat in one day because I absolutely loved my Fish Cake Banh Mi. I grew up eating fish cake and this was exactly what I expected. Tasty fish cake and fresh veggies and herbs came together to create a perfect bite.

My wife got the tofu banh mi and I have to say I would not have missed the meat if I got this one instead. The tofu had great flavor.

I'll be going here again.

Mộng Thu Cafe|248 Hyde St, San Francisco.text:
Came here twice in two weeks, I enjoyed the flavor and the warm vibes. First time I came I got their chicken pho (delicious). Second time I wanted to try their Banh Uot Cha Lua, it’s like a Vietnamese churng fun. I enjoyed the cured pork and the crispy shrimp cake. This place is cash only so don’t get caught lackin. They also have a $3 taro sago drink in the front. Affordable and delicious, check em out ✌🏽
-IG@BlackTigerEats

wow what a wonderful spot this is! we came for soup and had the hu tieu mi. so good! really excellent quality broth and ingredients. the inside is so cute, pristine, and food was ready quickly. can’t wait to try something else next time!

I can call this place an oasis in Tenderloin! The shop is clean. The staffs are friendly. The food is delicious and cheap in the SF standard with a big portion. I got a kind of noodles that I have never before. It is not Pho or Bun that you find in any other places. It fills with fish, shrimp, and pork for only 15 dollars! Not counting fresh vegetables and unique deep flavor broth! This is will be my standard of Vietnamese food from now on! The dessert here is quite good as well.

The food is just taste like instant ramen to me. I order Pho Ga, but the broth just taste like hot water, i have to put more flavor by myself. The chicken taste good, but it seems like its just a cold chicken from last night. The service is pretty good and the astompheres is not too bad. Highly recommeded working on the food more.
The food was fantastic, practically brought directly from Vietnam. Definitely worth visiting again

L & G Vietnamese Sandwich|602 Eddy St, S.text:
I'm pretty biased since I live on the block, but this is my go-to when I'm wanted something cheap and delicious. Their prices are extremely wallet friendly, and their meat portions in the banh mi sandwiches are also very generous. I come here almost once a week simply because I can't find anything else for this price to quality ratio.

Tip: I recommend bringing cash as you do save a bit

Coming from SoCal up to SF, this was one of the best Banh Mi sandwiches I’ve had in a long time! It’s a small hole in the wall so it is limited seating, but it’s just perfection in every sense, great price point at only $6 a sandwich, and loaded with meat and veggies inside. Everything is so fresh and the bread is incredibly crisp yet soft. Can’t wait to come again!

This place is a good substitute to Saigon if you want an equally great sandwich without the long lines. My combination sandwich which I didn't know what was in it and didn't ask lol was crunchy and soft at the same time. The vegetables weren't overbearing and the meats were tasty, full of flavor and savory. The speed of service wasn't as fast but still an overall Good sandwich.

This spot is the GOAT Bahn-mi and idc what anyone else says. Been going here for lunch for forever, and the kind lady making all the food is the absolute best. Her name is Ki and she always greets me when I come in. Beyond the Bahn-mi, get the green honeycomb cake (western name I think) since it’s the BOMB. Not overly sweet and an amazing texture. Can’t go wrong with this place, and make sure to tell Ki that she’s the best since she grinds through crowds of hungry patrons like no tomorrow. She’s the best ❤️

Hands-down best banh mi value in SF! Reviewing because I live for this shop and have never wished a place to survive so much in this economy. For $5-6, you get the most loaded banh mi with the juicest portions of meat and stuffed full with pickled veggies too. They're so stuffed, I can barely hold them tight! My favs are the Chipotle Pulled Pork and the BBQ Pork.

The bread is super soft and even great the next day if you toast it! I usually buy several at a time and portion them throughout the week. The shop is in the tenderloin so be aware the area can be a bit seedy. Don't recommend indoor seating either since it's small, but pickup is super fast if you call ahead to order. I always ask fo extra pickled veggies and the front guy is super nice about it! The sticky rice with sausage is great too.

Imo, this shop is much better than the more famous Saigon Sandwich across the street. But see for yourself!

Tú Lan|8 6th St, San Francisco, CA 94103.text: tu lan is a legend. have been coming here for over a decade for tasty cheap eats. i keep forgetting to take a pic before bc i can’t wait to dig in. their bun cha gio thit nuong is so perfect, so tasty, perfect. and everyone there is always so nice. it’s a few blocks from powell station, so conveniently located, definitely stop by next time you’re in the area
It’s a hole in a wall. I didn’t get the hype whatsoever. My food was fine. A bit watery but nothing to write home about. I’ve had so much better Asian food in SF.

I have been here many times, usually when I am in the area with a hungry appetite. The thing that driven me for my first visit was, I was watching a video on YouTube about this place showing their homemade and delicious foods along with their famous eggrolls.  Their menu consists of many authentic Vietnamese dishes to choose from, soups, rice plates and much more. You can either sit on the bar top that gives you a view of their open kitchen when they are preparing your food or you can choose to sit at one of their tables. Everything is made to order just the way I like it since I never mind the wait because I am a very patient person. At times it can be crowded where the best options is for takeout or if you do not mind waiting just like me. The staffs here are welding, well trained and understanding where I never had an issue at any of my visits. You may not notice this place at first while driving by unless you come to a complete stop, because this place is basically the definition of a hole in the wall spot. Lastly this place is good for all group sizes or if you want to go solo that is good too. I highly recommend this place to anyone on a limited time, if you want to try something new or if you just so happen to be in the area. I definitely will be back to tryout the other delicious options they have to offer.

Great cold vermicelli here. Grilled pork is pleasantly charred and the imperial roll is aggressively seasoned. Bun Rieu wasn’t bad but somewhat different (less fishy) than I’d prefer. Tried the shrimp bean cake curry last time and absolutely loved it. The curry combination fried rice is satisfying and enormous. I imagine everything on the menu is delicious—look forward to trying it all.

Save room for the coconut pandan flan which has a nice ginger syrup.

Delicious hole in the wall restaurant. The imperial rolls are amazing. We shared a dish simply listed as "marinated fish." It was absolutely scrumptious: large portions of fish, lightly battered and fried in a slightly sweet sauce with ginger, peppers, and pineapple. Service was quick and friendly. Don't be scared off by the neighborhood, just make your way there. You won't be sorry!

Sai’s Vietnamese Restaurant & Bar|42 Col.text: We had a great meal and service at Sai’s. Our waiter was extremely friendly and made the dinner we had a truly special experience. He said he has worked at the restaurant for over 40 years (I think he and/or his family owns the restaurant). Their 5 spice chicken banh mi was truly delicious (we forgot to take a picture before we dug into it. So the picture is after we took a few bites). The bread is nice and crispy and the filling was very tasty. We also loved beef tenderloin pho. We also came back a couple of days later and once again our experience was great. I highly recommend Sai’s for a great meal and great service.

Walked in during the final quarter of the NBA WCF game 7 and was able to get a table in front of the TVs. Owner was very nice and welcoming. Saw he greeted a lot of customers on their way out. Seems like there are many returning customers. The interior was clean and nice. Food was great. Love that they have us free hot tea as we felt pretty cold during that day. Love that we were able to watch the game with great warm vibes after a long day.

THE BANH MI IS TO DIE FORRRR!!! Please whatever you do get tha banh mi. Ur life will change. I keep coming back to eat it. Also the server is so kind he knows the best food and got us some stuff on the house. Love this place I highly recommend!

The best Pho Bo so far! Very nice brouth! Also there are many sauce bottles on the table, ypu can add them to make your Pho more special. I made mine more spicy. My husband ordered curry with lamb, it was awesome! Very deep and correct flavors. I wish you could add Tom Yum in menu, I've never tried a good Tom Yum in United States, to be honest. Neither in SF, nor in NYC or Sacramento. Maybe you could make the best one, since you're so skilled with soups! We will definitely come back.

Took my Vietnamese brothers here cause they were craving pho in San Francisco. Was a big mistake. Not good, not authentic. Broth not flavorful. Very little meat for the dac biet bowl. Do not recommend.

Lily|225 Clement St, San Francisco, CA 9.text: Wow. This was fine dining experience and excellent food! Huge portions too :D the egg rolls were heavy

This is the type of place you need a reservation because the menu never misses, service is amazing and the experience gets better EVERY SINGKE TIME !  The menu changes often which keeps it interesting. Each dish is flavorful, made with fresh ingredients, herbs and spices.  After 4 visits, this review is long overdue.  ROB is friendly, engaging and personable. The Expresso martini is loaded and will get you going. This week was restaurant week and the prefixe menu did not disappoint!!! It’s fun bringing friends from out of town here. Small, intimate and amazing.  Will be back- much love, Sunshine ! ☀️ Xoxox

I feel honored to have sat at the counter to watch the chef in a true flow state. Every single thing on the menu had a unique approach and was done artfully and masterfully. You can’t go wrong ordering. Cocktails were creative and well done. Dessert (hot chocolate torte) absolutely blew my mind. This place has a Michelin Star in its future.

Pretty good! Got the beef pho, garlic noodles, and orange slushie. Wish the garlic noodles had a bit more flavor but they were definitely filling! They have a decent deal for lunch but I’m sure this place has a way cooler vibe at night. Food came out instantly though.

Gorgeous food, fresh, delicate, and soft atmosphere. The chef is so sweet he greets you and checks in on you during dinner. We enjoyed everything we tried, especially the fried dishes which are so light and crispy. Fried fish and the shaved ice were my faves from the night. Not an every day spot but perfect for when a nice dinner is in order. Coming back again for sure.

Bac Lieu Restaurant|3216 Mission St, San.text: Vermicelli noodle bowl with grilled pork and egg roll was really good! The grilled pork flavor was strong and delicious. The interior is also really clean and relaxing. Seems like most of their business is takeout orders. We just wandered in but would definitely come back!
Delicious food and great dining experience! The pho has delicious broth and the tofu has lots of flavor. Everything tastes very fresh!

The pho here is absolutely delicious, with a rich, flavorful broth and perfectly cooked noodles. The beef is tender and fresh, and the herbs add a wonderful aroma. The service is friendly, quick, and attentive, making the whole dining experience even better

The food was flavorful and the service was top tier. I had the curry soup and my friend ordered the oxtail stew, both great recommendations from our server.  Would definitely recommend this place to anyone Looking for good food.

Bun bo hue is good. It's very red but don't let that scare you. They also give a lot of veggie sides. Lots of meat and noodles too. Watch out the noodles are slippery!

Ordering is easy and quick. You should pay by cash.

Comfortable table seating for single dining and easily accessible by 14 or 49 bus.

Thanh Long Restaurant|4101 Judah St, San.text: I’d been waiting to check out Thanh long for years and it didn’t disappoint. The Roast and garlic noodles were amazing!! The crab was very fresh and perfect for two people. It came out to $100 per person but both my friend and I walked out stuffed. I don’t say this a lot but it’s definitely worth the wait.

​A San Francisco Legend: The Ultimate Roast Crab & Garlic Noodle Experience 🦀✨
​If you are looking for an iconic dining experience in the Outer Sunset, Thanh Long remains the gold standard. This isn't just a meal; it’s a culinary pilgrimage for anyone who appreciates the perfect fusion of Vietnamese flavors and fresh California seafood.
​The Highlights:
​The World-Famous Roast Crab: The Dungeness crab is the undisputed star. It arrived perfectly cooked—meaty, sweet, and drenched in that legendary secret garlic sauce. The rich, savory aroma hits you the moment it reaches the table.
​Signature Garlic Noodles: You cannot visit Thanh Long without ordering these. The noodles are chewy, buttery, and packed with deep garlic umami. They are the perfect companion to the crab, soaking up every bit of the flavorful juices.
​Shaking Beef (Bo Luc Lac): The beef was incredibly tender, seared to perfection with just the right amount of char, and served over a crisp, refreshing salad.
​Appetizers: The Mussels served on toasted baguette slices were a highlight—the broth was fragrant and vibrant. The Fried Calamari was also impressive: light, crispy, and seasoned beautifully without being greasy.
​Atmosphere & Service:
​The ambiance is warm and upscale yet remains inviting and unpretentious. Despite the restaurant being busy, the service was attentive and professional. The staff knows the menu inside out and ensures the pacing of the meal is spot on.
​Verdict:
​Thanh Long continues to prove why it has been a San Francisco staple for decades. While it is a premium dining experience, the quality of the ingredients and the consistency of the flavors make it worth every penny.
​Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Pro Tip: This place is popular for a reason—be sure to make a reservation in advance, especially on weekends!

I used to eat at their An the Go trucks back in college and suddenly had craving for their noodles and beef. Thought id finally make the trip to their restaurant. Althought pricing May be on the expensive side, the service and taste is spectacular. Food is serve just a few minutes after ordering and their wait staff is very attentive. Just watch for the receipt as service charge is already included.

A 10 year Anniversay Celebration here was absolutely 10/10. I highly recommend the crab and garlic noodles, and if you still want more in the photos are some other great choices. I couldn't get enough of my waitress smile everytime she checked on us. I love coming back to my city where I was born and raised for good food. Dessert was chefs kiss... fried banana and ice cream. Yum yum good!

Great as always. Went on a Saturday night.. Party of 3 + a newborn. Called around 6, and they weren't taking walk-ins anymore... Went on open table and booked a reservation for 8:30 lol

Got there early, which wasn't a problem. Drank 2 beers at the bar while we waited. We actually ended up getting sat about 30 ahead of schedule 🤞🏽

Got seated upstairs, which was a first for us. For some reason the vibe is a lot more laid back up there I feel? Could just be me, not sure, but it was more my vibe for sure.

We ended up getting a couple items that the entire table shared. Shaken beef, wok egg noodles + shrimp, bowl of white rice, as well as the roasted crab.

Everything on point, everything delish. Ended uo spending around $230 I want to say, with tip included.

PPQ Dungeness Island - San Francisco|582.text: This is my first time at this PPQ location. We've been to the old one on Clement. This one is much nicer. They may have changed the recipes for some of the dishes like the peppercorn crab. Regardless, they are still really good. Love the garlic noodles. The restaurant was full at 6pm on a Saturday night. Service was excellent.

Had the crab dinner for 10 and a few extra dishes at PPQ and it was fantastic! The food came out fast, everything was fresh, and the garlic crab was the star — messy but totally worth it. The portions were generous and perfect for sharing. Service was friendly and attentive, and they made sure we had everything we needed the whole time. Great spot for a fun group dinner — we’ll definitely be back!

Came in about 30mins before closing on a Thursday evening. We called ahead to see if they would let us order. The staff was super nice and accommodating. The food was served fast and there was no wait time. Everything we ordered in the peppercorn category was amazing. I will definitely be coming back here and I highly recommend this spot if you’re looking for good Chinese food.

Garlic butter crab - 5/5 delicious! Incredible sauce. Friend who tried all the crab flavors says this is the best one

Garlic butter noodle - 5/5 so buttery 😌

Green beans - 4.5/5 great per usual

I went to PPQ Dungeness Island with my friends and we had such a great time! I’ve known Charlie from Millbrae years ago, so we decided to check out the San Francisco location — and it did not disappoint.

The food was absolutely amazing — from the famous garlic butter lobster 🦞 to the wings and shaking beef 🥩, everything was cooked perfectly and full of flavor. To top it off, I even ran into my friend Doji here, which made the night even more fun.

Great food, great people, and a warm, welcoming vibe. Definitely coming back soon! Ohhh, did I mention that they do have Kareoke and private  Space upstairs, which is great for family function or holiday parties🙌✨

Ly's Vietnamese Cuisine|1779 Lombard St,.text: Fantastic Vietnamese food, the pho and vermicelli were both top notch. Friendly service, and a laid back vibe. 5/5
The beef was very tasty and tender. The pho was really good, it is a must try.

We loved this place and have ordered many times. Unfortunately tonight we found a dead bug in our vermicelli bowl (luckily had not eaten any yet). We suspect it was from the lettuce not being washed properly, and had to throw out all of the food. Really unfortunate.

first of all, this is probably the friendliest vietnamese restaurant i've been to in all of California, and i've been to a lot of restaurants ! i was leaving the beach, passed by while driving, and decided to park, drop in and check out their menu.

i spent some time chatting with Sammy (the owner) and her son. while i was waiting for my to-go food, they gave me 2 BIG slices of watermelon and a sample of their pho bo vien (beef meatball soup). i didn't expect it at all and it was probably the warmest welcome i've received from any restaurant.

the butterfly shrimp and strawberry lemonade here are great. if you like your drink sweetness at 0-25% like me, ask them to adjust the amount of strawberry preserve in the drink. also, they speak vietnamese and cantonese here.

special thanks to sammy and the other staff - will definitely drop by again the next time i'm in the area :)

Crowded on a Sunday at 8pm is always a good sign - no wait though. Excellent pho (we had the chicken and the five spice chicken) and quick service with a nice and chill atmosphere. Definitely coming back

Saigon BBQ Noodle House|1936 Irving St,.text: Very good pho. The pho broth was very flavorful even before I added the herbs. The noodles were all broken apart, but not too bad. We also tried the fried wings with fish sauce. It's sweet, crunchy, and tasty, but the wings were kinda small.

I like limes in my pho, but they only had lemons (verified with server). I like chili oil to make my pho spicy, but they only had siracha and hoison sauce. I love adding a lot of spring onions and cilantro to my pho. There was not much cilantro on the herb platter and very little spring onions in the soup (count how many you see in my bowl). I get skimping on expensive meats, but why are so many pho places skimping on herbs that are relatively cheap. For southern style pho, I wouldn't recommend this place.
Nice staff and clean restroom. Service was good, but the food was a bit disappointing. The egg rolls were tasty, but the pho wasn’t very flavorful.

Best bahn xeo I have tried in a long time! Better that Madame Vo’s in NYC, which until today was my favor In the US. A smiling waiter wanted to make sure that I knew how to eat it. Thank you! ♥️ Informal but nice environment.
Great atmosphere delicious food will definitely eat there again seafood noodles so good excellent Thai tea

Le Soleil Stonestown|3251 20th Ave 221 (.text:
We had a delicious dinner at Le Soleil. The food was fresh and tasty and the service was friendly and attentive.

The taretare was light and fresh, while five-spice roasted chicken.... all flavorful and well-seasoned.
Our favorite, the pho risotto was especially buttery, served with a FAT bone in rib.
The star of the show- the curry crab, was delicious and very tender.

Overall, a great experience. We’ll definitely come back.

I tried their clam soup which was cold and I wasn’t expecting that. It had good flavor but I think it would taste better warmed up. We tried the aguachile shrimp and it wasn’t what I expected. I expected more lime flavor and for the shrimp to be cooked in more lime or lemon. The shrimp was more raw than regular aguachile. We tried their garlic roasted crab and it was good. The brown butter sauce gave it a more nutty taste. It was a bit too salty but I asked for lemon and it really rounded out the flavors.

1. The lunch set is a great option for those looking to save money, but it’s also important to make sure the food is good and the place is clean. They do have decent food quality, and their private toilets are spotless.

2. Their homemade curry crab is definitely one of the best Vietnamese-style restaurants in San Francisco. You really can’t go wrong with the garlic bread; it’s a fantastic match for the curry. Plus, they have their own toilet, which is a huge plus. You can easily wash your hands whenever you need to.

My favorite was the miso mushroom!  Big portion too.  Next was the beef.  So tender!  The squid ink rice had crunchy torched rice and a bit of tobiko.  Garlic noodle could use more garlicky flavor and not be so cut up into shorter pieces.  Excellent service.  Food came out fast!

Delicious garlic noodles! Would come again just for the noodles. Friend also got the squid ink fried rice! Great server with blue hair! I am vegetarian and needed a veggie option for myself so the garlic noodles were a good choice. Happy with my meal and free Thai tea!

Cà phê Việt|121 New Montgomery St, San F.text: The vegan banh mi was tasty but kind of dry. I wish there were more dairy-free drink options instead of just pourover Vietnamese coffee, like a vegan coconut cream drink would be amazing! Beautifully decorated but no space to eat since you'll need a table for the sandwich crumbs.

Had the classic iced coffee with grass jelly. Perfect amount of condensed milk (not too sweet but sweet enough for a classic Vietnamese iced coffee) and the grass jelly adds a refreshing touch. The coffee itself is strong and fruity. Very good! Shop is still in the process of unpacking and boxes and cables were piled around. Would get 5 stars atmosphere if it was completed. Food would get 5 stars if it wasn't so expensive for one drink. Barista was very friendly!
Good coffee and also have some seating which is nice

While I only tried the Viet Iced Coffee, I felt it was expensive and too sweet for what it was. I love a good ca phe Sua da, but it didn’t feel as bold in flavor as I was expecting. While there was an option for no jelly, it wouldn’t lower the cost.
I have yet to try to egg cream or food though!
It’s a small interior, with just enough space to sit to wait for your order, but not for socializing with friends.
A nice and tasty Iced coffee with egg cream foam!

Ilana Coffee|2314 Clement St, San Franci.text: Pork and pate sandwich! Yummy sandwich! Bread was fresh slightly crunchy and soft inside. The lady there was very nice. Gonna go try their other sandwiches!
Very friendly staff and a delicious bagel and Vietnamese iced coffee! Came here two days in a row and will be coming again soon ☺️🥯🧋

I had a mixed experience here. One staff member, a Chinese lady, seemed very strict about rules. I had already placed a $17 order, but when I wanted to add a separate drink that was under $5, she would not allow it.
I also noticed the restroom was marked “Out of Service,” but when I asked, it turned out it was actually available for use.
Additionally, while I was taking a short break on the bench and playing on the phone, she approached me and said, “You can’t sleep here,” even though I was not sleeping. The interaction felt a bit unfriendly and made me feel unwelcome

A hidden gem, one of the best coffee shops in San Francisco. I came here on a whim one day and it’s super clean, the coffee tastes delicious, the service is excellent. I will happily walk by five other coffee shops just to come here. Owners are great!

There are so many reasons to like Ilana Coffee.
- It opens early and its service was very friendly, efficient and fast.
- It's Vietnamese sandwich is great. I had a combination sandwich, with has ham. pate and headchese. It’s a junior size but good for the price and perfect as a breakfast.
- It has a lot more than Vietnamese sandwich. I also tried an egg, cheese and ham breakfast sandwich and for $4.25 it has excellent value.
